title: Purchase an IAM Plan
text:
Learn how to purchase an IAM plan, or upgrade from a trial account.

As a new customer or trial user, you can review and purchase an IAM plan.

1. Review the plan options on the [Plans and Pricing](https://ecom.docusign.com/plans-and-pricing/iam) page, and select Buy Now for the plan that you want.
   1. You can switch to a monthly billing cycle by selecting the Monthly toggle.
   2. For a plan not listed, select a category from the Enhanced plans menu, and select Contact Sales.

   The checkout page opens.
2. (Optional) Select the Billing options or User licenses dropdown to make changes to your subscription.

   The totals will update automatically.
3. (Optional) Select the Included features dropdown to review features included in the plan.

   Overage pricing applies if subscription limits are exceeded.
4. Select Overages to open the feature overage pricing  window and review overage prices.
5. Enter your email address and select Continue.

   The Add billing details page opens.
6. Select a payment method and enter your billing details. ![The screenshot shows an example of the Add billing details page with the option to edit your subscription.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Add-billing-details-checkout-IAM-starter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4OTIsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.DmJBMleplMhprGw7KfI6wE1DGEMfNYEgsNbycch8DVE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the Add billing details page with the option to edit your subscription.")
7. Select Purchase.

   The confirmation page opens.

You have purchased an IAM plan, and an order confirmation is sent to your email.

Note: If you are a new customer, activate your account with the link in your email.

title: Bulk Actions Log
text:
Read about the Bulk Actions log and how it tracks the actions that an administrator has
taken on an account. Administrators see the results of an action and see details about any
errors that occurred.

This Bulk Actions log page shows a list of bulk actions, such as uploading multiple users and
envelope transfers for your account, and the progress of the actions. You can leave this
page while the processing is in progress. If you choose to remain on the page, you must
refresh the job display to see an updated status. Select the **refresh icon** in the
Actions column to update the job status.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/bulkactionrefresh.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.uL4ZTbDkRlIyNpgI-CA70cwHIq_MSIwhlFpmu657ld0&_LANG=enus)

When processing is complete, the Status column shows the
general results of the bulk action:

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/bulkactionstatus.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.uL4ZTbDkRlIyNpgI-CA70cwHIq_MSIwhlFpmu657ld0&_LANG=enus)

To review a summary of the bulk action results, select the menu in the Actions column
and select Details.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/bulkactionsdetails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.uL4ZTbDkRlIyNpgI-CA70cwHIq_MSIwhlFpmu657ld0&_LANG=enus)

The details dialog box provides information on how many users were successfully added
and a summary of any errors.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/uploaduserdetails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.uL4ZTbDkRlIyNpgI-CA70cwHIq_MSIwhlFpmu657ld0&_LANG=enus)

For users that were not successfully added or updated, the message provides a short description of the problem and allows you to download the CSV file to correct the issues. The downloaded file includes a "ProcessingResults" column that specifies the issue for any row that failed to process correctly. After correcting the issues, the file can be uploaded and resubmitted for processing.

If there is a problem with the CSV file, processing might fail. If this occurs, download the file, then review and correct the contents. Alternately, you can create and upload a new CSV file.

title: Reminders
text:
Read about how account administrators configure reminder emails to signers.

(Default: Off; Values: 1-999 days)

You can turn on reminders to send follow-up emails to signers automatically. When you enable
reminders, you specify when and how often to send notifications. The number of days before
sending the first reminder should be less than the number of days before the request expires.

title: Purchase Extensions for an IAM Plan
heading_path: Purchase Extensions for an IAM Plan
text:
Learn how to add feature extensions to your IAM account.

As an administrator of an existing IAM plan, you can add feature extensions to your
account.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

   The Subscription and Billing page
   opens.
2. In the Add feature extensions section, review available
   features.![The screenshot shows the Add feature extensions section on the Subscription and billing page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/IAM-add-feature-sub-billing.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.ZPjsrTFFOuFDONE8tD_HLIPlJ4GdJ1xNmP2t_wAOySc&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Add feature extensions section on the Subscription and billing page.")
3. Select Buy Now for the feature that you want to
   purchase.

   The feature purchase window opens.
4. Choose the quantity of the extension that you want to purchase.
5. Select Purchase.

   The confirmation window opens
6. Select Done.

Your extension is purchased and added to your
account.

title: Undo Pending Changes to Your Subscription
heading_path: Undo Pending Changes to Your Subscription
text:
Learn how administrators can undo subscription changes that are still
pending.

When you reduce the number of seats, the change is pending until the renewal date of
your current subscription. You can undo the changes while they are still
pending.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Your Subscription > Edit Subscription Details.

   For eSignature accounts, Manage Your Subscription is in
   the Edit subscription details section.

   The Edit your subscription window opens. ![The screenshot shows the Edit your subscription window with Undo Pending Change.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Edit-your-subscription-window-pending-change.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NDQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.JGagPsQs4jcH3YQBxP-5q-HRZf5xVvwYoY4w16Fn6_A&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Edit your subscription window with Undo Pending Change.")
3. Select Undo Pending Change.

   A message confirms that you want to undo your subscription changes.
4. Select Yes, Continue.
5. Select Done to close the window.

The pending changes are removed.

title: Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure
text:
Read about how account administrators can enable an optional Electronic Record and
Signature Disclosure to obtain legal consent from signers before signers interact with
documents. Administrators can customize fields in the form.

If enabled for your account, the Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure (ERSD) is shown
to each new recipient who is going to sign or add other information, or is required to view
the documents you send to them. Recipients must read and agree to the terms of the disclosure
before they can access and take action on the documents you send. The ERSD does not apply to
copy-only recipients but does apply to recipients who must sign or view your
documents.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/disclosuresigning.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.PnXIdVwuizJEwsbThMD84FduqmTWi4UfPpvEMSK2jFg&_LANG=enus)

Docusign provides a default ERSD disclosure for the convenience of U.S.-based customers only.
This default disclosure is only valid for transactions between U.S.-based parties. [View the sample legal disclosure.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=cwz1663702052434.html&_LANG=enus "Read the default legal disclosure that is available to administrators who want to customize it for their business.")

**Customize the default ERSD or create your own**

As an administrator, you can customize values in the default disclosure or create your own
free-form disclosure from the Legal Disclosure page. You can provide
custom values to tailor the default disclosure for your company. Or you can create a new
disclosure with your own text and HTML formatting. Changes to the legal disclosure are
captured in the account audit log.

Docusign does not automatically translate your custom disclosure. You must create your own
custom disclosure for each language used by your signers.

If you have questions about using a disclosure, contact your legal counsel.

title: Custom Logos for Sending Brands
text:
Read about how administrators create a sending brand with logos and colors.
Administrators can customize the Docusign environment for senders.

When you create a custom sending brand, you can add logos and specify colors. You can add
your logo to the sending experience for your users see when using their Docusign account. The sending brand also defines the email logo in the user account activation email.

![The screenshot shows a Docusign account with a custom logo defined in the sending brand.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/sendinglogowithdefaultbackgroundcolor.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNDMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.2M7XaA8LEHCrUy_gTUEcjyQqSafBCgFPv2PL_0Vqm3I&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows a Docusign account with a custom logo defined in the sending brand.")

If you modify the header color for the sending brand, the color is applied to the Welcome back banner
area on the Home page.

![The screenshot shows a Docusign account with custom colors defined in the sending brand.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Logo-header-banner.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNDMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.2M7XaA8LEHCrUy_gTUEcjyQqSafBCgFPv2PL_0Vqm3I&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows a Docusign account with custom colors defined in the sending brand.")

title: Deactivate and Activate Identity Verification Configurations﻿
heading_path: Deactivate and Activate Identity Verification Configurations﻿
text:
Read about how account administrators deactivate and activate identity verification
configurations.

As an administrator, you have the ability to manage the usability of
configurations within your account for both senders and for use through API. By default,
all configurations on your account are set to active so that they can be used. You can
also choose to restrict the usage of a configuration by deactivating it.

When you deactivate a configuration, it is labeled deactivated and is no longer available
for use. To reactivate a previously deactivated configuration and make it usable again,
you can select Activate. This provides flexibility and control over the Identity
Verification configurations within your account.

title: Change Account Plan Type
text:
Learn how administrators change account plan types. The Plan and Billing page provides detailed information about the available plan types.
From this page, administrators can select a different plan that best meets the needs of
their organization.

If you are currently on an eSignature plan that was purchased online, you can change your
account plan from the Subscription and Billing page. You can also opt to downgrade to a free
account.

Note: If you purchased your plan from a Docusign sales
representative, contact Docusign Support to make any
changes.

Changes to the account plan will occur at the end of your specified renewal term and
will not be prorated.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Your Subscription > Switch Plan.
3. Review the account plan types and select the one you want.

   Note: If you are downgrading
   to a Personal or Free plan and your account has multiple users, before
   proceeding, close the other users.
4. Review the billing and user details for the new plan, and select
   Switch Plan.

   Note: If you are upgrading your account,
   review your payment information, and select Purchase.

Your plan is scheduled to be updated. You can cancel or
adjust the change anytime before your next billing cycle by selecting Undo Plan Change.

title: Set Account Reminders and Expiration Defaults
heading_path: Set Account Reminders and Expiration Defaults
text:
Learn how account administrators set default reminders and expiration time frames for
envelopes sent from an account.

As an account administrator, you can set reminders and
expiration time frames for envelopes sent from an account.

1. From the Accounts view, select Reminders and
   Expirations.
2. To allow users to change the account defaults on the envelopes they send, select
   Allow senders to override account defaults.
3. To enable and set automatic reminders:
   1. Select Send automatic reminders.
   2. Enter values for how many days to wait until sending the first reminder and how
      many days to wait between subsequent reminders. Use integers from 1-999.

      If you enter 0 for the number of days between reminders, no reminders will be sent
      after the first one.
4. For envelope expiration, enter how many days after sending an envelope expires and how
   many days before expiration to warn signers. Use integers from 1-999.

title: Email Security
text:
Read about how administrators can enforce Transport Layer Security (TLS) encryption for
outbound emails from the account.

This option enables Transport Layer Security (TLS) encryption for all outgoing emails from the
account.

- Enforce minimal TLS requirements for outbound emails. When selected,
  the Docusign mail server will attempt to encrypt connections with
  the latest version of the TLS protocol, TLS 1.3, and will go down to TLS 1.2, which
  is the minimum version required to establish the connection. Any recipient's server
  that doesn't support TLS 1.3 or 1.2 won't receive outbound emails from that account,
  and the sender will be notified about the undelivered email when the email delivery
  fails.

  Accounts enabled with Custom Email Domain have TLS enforcement by default.
  In this case, the setting is enabled and locked from making changes. This
  feature is available for Enterprise and Business Pro eSignature plans.

title: Shared Access
text:
Read about how administrators use the shared access permissions and setup setting to
allow users to manage sharing access to their own envelopes.

This setting controls whether users who need shared access to another user’s envelopes can
request it from that user. This bypasses the need to rely on an administrator to fulfill
the requests.

- Allow to edit shared access permissions and setup: When
  selected, the Shared Access My Preferences page shows a button for end users to
  share access to their own envelopes. This setting is checked as the default
  behavior. To restrict this capability to account administrators only, uncheck
  this setting.

title: Recipient Roles
text:
Read about how administrators enable recipient actions that senders can select when adding recipients. For example, administrators can allow senders to use the Bulk Send and the In Person Signer features.

These settings enable additional recipient actions that senders can select when adding recipients.

- Enable Witness Signing (Allow sender to assign a witness for signers): When selected, senders can assign a witness for signers. For more information about witnessed signing, see [Include a Witness for a Signer](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=vwq1578456557055.html&_LANG=enus).
- Enable bulk recipients: When selected, the account is eligible to use the Bulk Send feature. The Allow sending to bulk list option must also be enabled in a user's Permission Profile to allow them to use the feature. See [Using Bulk Send](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=wtn1643071711000&topicId=kjz1578456386508.html&_LANG=enus) for more information about using this feature.
- Enable needs to view role: When selected, senders are able to use the Needs to view recipient action. With this action the recipient must receive the completed documents for the envelope to be completed, but the recipient does not need to sign, initial, date, or add information to any of the documents.
- Enable specify recipients: When selected, senders are able to use the Specify recipients recipient action. This specifies a recipient who can fill in the name and email address of the role recipients who are at their same position or later in the signing order.
- Enable allow to edit recipient: When selected, senders are able to use the Allow to edit recipient action. This specifies a recipient who can make recipient and document changes (correct) to an in-process envelope. This recipient must have a valid Docusign account.
- Enable update recipients: When selected, senders are able to use the Update recipients recipient action. This specifies a recipient who can edit recipient details for any remaining recipients in the signing order.
- Enable in-person (hosted) signing role: When selected, senders are able to use the In Person Signer recipient action when they prepare documents for signature. If this recipient role is enabled, the additional option Present this information request to in person signers is available. If selected, this option requires the in person signing host to collect the requested information from the signer before the signer can begin signing. A free text field is provided to enter the information request to be shown to in person signers.

  All in person signers are shown the same request and must provide a response. The response is saved and included on the Certificate of Completion in the In Person Signer Events section.

  See [In Person Signing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=hak1578456316043.html&_LANG=enus) for more information about using this feature.

title: License Management in Account Administration
text:
Read about license information shown in the Users page.

For accounts on IAM plans, account administrators can view user license counts and assign licenses to users in the License section on the Users page. Administrators can track license assignments without assistance from Docusign Support or account team. Administrators can view license counts in the License section on the Users page in the Accounts view.

Note: All existing customers that purchase an IAM plan will have their users migrated to Full licenses.

The license information includes the following details for each license:

- License Type: the IAM plan license type, such as Full - CX License or Full - Sales License.
- Assigned: the number of licenses assigned to users
- x of x available licenses for your account: the number of licenses assigned to users compared to the total number of licenses purchased under all accounts in the subscription.

Note: Customers may use an unlimited number of free licenses. Free licenses do not count toward your paid licenses.

![The screenshot shows the license information on the Users page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/new-license.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMTUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.wh-5ThT3iaO1j7z2uN1bJORRtMTHn3EyAYGxrbZ7l0s&_LANG=enus)

The following license types are available based on the IAM plan purchased:

| License Type | Plan Variants | Paid or Free |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Full license | - Full - Enterprise license - Full - Professional license - Full - Standard license - Full - Sales license - Full - CX license | Paid |
| Free license | N/A | Free |

The following functionality is included for each license type:

|  | Free / Free User | Paid License User |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Admin | No access to administration features. | Can assign permissions.  Can add users. |
| eSignature | Sign only. | Can send for signature. |
| Agreement Manager | View agreements signed by own user.  View agreements individually shared by other users. | View all agreements in Agreement Manager.  Can edit and manage extraction field.  Can mass upload third-party docs. |
| Workflow Builder | No access to create/run workflows. Note: a workflow [participant](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) (for example, a customer in an onboarding workflow) doesn't require a Docusign account. | Can create and run workflows. |
| Agreement Desk | Requestor Features.  No playbook access. | Approver Features.  Playbook Access.  Agreement Desk admin. |

Each user can only be assigned one license per subscription. User access to Docusign features is dependent on the account plan, the license type, and the user permission profile.

For more details about the permissions required for using Agreement Manager, see the [Agreement Manager Capabilities by Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=vhx1743523538507.html&_LANG=enus) topic.

title: Update Multiple Users through a Bulk Action
heading_path: Update Multiple Users through a Bulk Action
text:
Learn how administrators use a comma-separated value (CSV) file to update multiple users
simultaneously.

To update information for multiple users simultaneously,
upload a comma-separated value (CSV) file that contains the updates to the user account
information.

Tip: To save time preparing the upload file,
download a CSV file of existing users in your account. Update the user information in the
CSV file as necessary and upload the modified file. See [User Lists and Permission Profiles Downloads](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=sfp1583277438486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators download assigned permission profiles and permission settings for account users as a comma-separated value (CSV) file. Administrators can download just the permission profile name or a full list of permissions for users.").

1. Create a CSV file with the user information as described in [CSV File Format for User Updates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=shx1583277429436.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the format of the comma-separated value (CSV) file administrators use to upload updates to multiple users.").
2. From the Accounts view, select Users.
3. Select Bulk Update.
4. In the Update Users dialog box, select Upload
   File.

   Select the CSV file that you want to upload.
5. Select Submit.
6. Confirm that you want to continue.

   The file is placed in a queue for processing. The system displays the
   Bulk Actions log, which lists pending and completed bulk actions.
   The system processes the file and checks for errors in the formatting of the file and user
   information.
7. Select the refresh icon in the Actions column in the
   Bulk Actions log to update the job status.

title: Sending Settings
text:
Read about how administrators use Sending Settings to set the default behavior for
documents sent from an account.

These settings set the default behavior for documents sent from your account. In most cases,
you can choose to enforce these settings for all documents, or allow account senders to modify
the values for each document they send.

You must be an account administrator with All Administration Capabilities to manage these
settings. See [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") for details.

Note: The information in these topics covers all
signing settings available in Docusign. Some advanced features and options
are supported only in certain Docusign plans. Your account plan might not
support some options discussed in these topics. For more information about which options are
available for your account, check your account plan or contact Docusign
Support.

title: Signing Groups Permission Access Levels
text:
View the levels of access administrators can grant through Signing
Groups permission.

The following table describes the level of access provided by the Signing
Groups permission.

| Administrative area | Page | Level of access |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Users and Groups | Users | None |
| Users and Groups | Permission Profiles | None |
| Users and Groups | Groups | None |
| Users and Groups | Signing Groups | Edit |

title: Bulk Transfer of Envelopes and Templates with a CSV File
heading_path: Bulk Transfer of Envelopes and Templates with a CSV File
text:
Read about how account administrators can take bulk actions on envelopes and templates by
using a CSV file.

You can transfer envelopes or templates in bulk by using a comma-separated value (CSV) file
containing the IDs to be transferred. The CSV file must contain only one column with each ID
(envelope or template, depending on which you are transferring) entered on a different row,
and no other data.

A common workflow for bulk transfer is:

- Use the Custody Transfer page to filter for the envelopes that you want to
  transfer.
- Download the list of envelopes using the download CSV option.
- Review the download file and remove any unwanted data and remove all columns except for Envelope ID.
- Use the transfer using CSV option to transfer the envelopes in bulk to the new owner.

title: Purge Queue and Notifications
text:
Read about how the purge queue functions and how account administrators can change the retention period for documents.

If you have set a document retention period, when that number of retention days is reached, the envelopes are placed in a purge queue. Under normal queue processing conditions, the queue lasts 14 days after which the documents within the envelopes are permanently deleted.

- If the remove metadata option is selected in your document retention settings, then all field data and any API attachments are also deleted.
- If the redact personally identifiable information option is selected, Docusign redacts all personally identifiable information from the envelope, certificate of completion, and history.

**Notifications**

As a default configuration in the Sender’s account, when an envelope enters the [purge queue](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=rlk1578456343945.html&_LANG=enus), two separate email notifications are sent as follows to the sender and any recipients associated with the documents who have a Docusign account:

1. When the envelope first enters the purge queue (in 14 days prior to purge, under normal purge queue processing conditions), a warning email notification is sent informing that the documents will be deleted. The email provides a link to the documents, providing an opportunity to view, print, and download the documents before they are deleted.
2. A second email notification is additionally sent, generally 7 days later, prior to expiration of the purge queue period as another reminder of the upcoming purge.

Note: If any party on a Docusign transaction deletes an envelope before it is purged, they will still receive the purge warning notifications. The only way for a sender or recipient to not receive these notifications is to disable the notification option in their My Preferences > Notifications.

**Changing retention period**

If your account is using document retention and you decide to change the retention period, all account envelopes, including those in the purge queue, are affected as follows:

- **Disable document retention**. Any envelopes in the purge queue, except those placed in the queue using Targeted Purge, are removed and the documents will be retained.
- **Increasing the retention period**. The increased retention period is applied to all envelopes, including those in the purge queue. Any envelopes in the purge queue remain in the queue, but the purge queue start date is delayed to reflect the new retention period.

  Note: Increasing the retention period only delays when envelopes are purged. It does not prevent envelopes from being purged. The only way to completely stop purge is to disable document retention.
- **Decreasing the retention period**. The reduced retention period is applied to all envelopes. Any envelopes that have met the new retention period are moved into the purge queue. Decreasing the retention period does not affect the purge queue start date of items already in the queue.

title: Manage Your IAM Plan
text:
Learn how to edit your account subscription, add user licenses, upgrade your plan, and
change feature capacity.

As an administrator of an existing IAM account, you can manage and edit your account
subscription. You can add additional user licenses, upgrade your plan, and change
feature capacity.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   The Subscription and Billing page opens.![The screenshot shows the Subscription and billing page for an IAM Professional account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/IAM-Sub-and-billing-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4OTcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.xPz7oE5KWsFvLt4_io1s0vFeB_lXcOW8jVEJMaR2ddM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Subscription and billing page for an IAM Professional account.")

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Your Subscription > Edit Subscription.

   The Edit your subscription page opens.
3. Edit your plan by selecting the following options:
   1. Select the Annual or
      Monthly toggle to change your billing
      cycle.
   2. If you have an IAM Standard Plan, select Upgrade
      Plan to view plan upgrade options.
   3. Select Add Capacity or edit the feature capacity
      total to include a new add-on or additional capacity to an existing
      feature.
4. Select Next.

   The Confirm your subscription change page
   opens.
5. Review your changes and select Confirm.

Your IAM plan has been updated. Changes apply on your
renewal date.

title: Create and Manage Agreement Rules
text:
Read about how account administrators create agreement rules to determine actions for completed envelopes. Actions include Archive to, Start workflow, and Export to spreadsheet.

Before creating agreement rules, you must connect your application to Docusign. Connected applications for agreement actions are managed in Docusign App Center. For more information on creating connections and managing connected apps, see the [Use Docusign App Center to Manage App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=con5c7c664f-0f3a-482b-89d9-0766a9544b4c.html&_LANG=enus) topic.

Agreement rules determine actions for completed envelopes. Rules consist of two main components:

- **Conditions**: Determine when an agreement rule should be executed
- **Actions**: Determine what should happen in connected applications

Rules follow a pattern of 'If **condition** then **action**.' Rules can range from simple to complex, and you can include one or more conditions or actions in a single rule.

You might start a workflow by creating a rule like the following example:

If a completed envelope is sent from either the U.S. Order Form or from the U.K. Order Form templates, then start the New Order workflow in Docusign CLM and export the envelope data to a Google Sheet.

Note: Rules and actions cannot be migrated across eSignature accounts.

title: Change Brands Assigned to Groups
text:
Learn how administrators add or remove brands that are assigned to groups.

As an account administrator, you can add and remove brands
from groups.

1. From the Accounts view, select Groups.
2. Locate the group that you want to assign a brand to by searching or scanning the list,
   and select it.
3. Select the Brands tab to open the list of assigned brands.
4. To remove brands already assigned, select Remove.
5. To assign additional brands to the group, select Assign Brands,
   select the brands to assign, and select Assign.

title: Add a Signing Brand for Recipients
heading_path: Add a Signing Brand for Recipients
text:
Learn how administrators add a signing brand for senders to select when they send an envelope. A signing brand can include a custom logo, color scheme, brand name, and company information.

As an administrator, you can add a signing brand for senders to select when they send an envelope. A signing brand can include a custom logo, color scheme, brand name, and company information.

1. From the Accounts view, select Brands to view the Signing tab.
2. Select Add Brand, enter a unique brand name, and select Save.

   The Branding page for your new brand opens. The brand shown is the same as the default Docusign sending brand.
3. For the Info section of your new brand, set the following options and values as needed:

   - Brand Name. The brand name is how the brand is identified in Brands administration and to senders when they select a brand to use for the envelopes they send. Enter a distinctive name to help senders select a brand to use.
   - Company Name. (Optional) Enter a company name to use in email communications and on the Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure. To employ the Company Name value entered in a brand, you must enable the Use brand company name option in the Legal Disclosure page and utilize a [custom email resource file](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=yum1583277332870.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators that have XML and HTML skills customize resource files to control text elements in an account. Administrators can customize one or both of the resource files available for Docusign eSignature. One file is used for signing and the other file is used for email messaging."). See the [Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=aqg1583277336549.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators can enable an optional Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure to obtain legal consent from signers before signers interact with documents. Administrators can customize fields in the form.") topic for more information.
   - Use sender's company name. (Optional) Select this option to use the company listed in the sender's Preferences > Personal Information. If this option is selected, any entry in the Company Name option for the brand is ignored.

     If neither the Company Name field is completed nor the Use sender's company name option is selected, then the account name is used for email communications and the disclosure.
   - Set as Sign Default. (Optional) Select this option to make the brand the default brand used for all documents sent from the account.
4. Under Customize What Recipients See, select Edit Your Theme.
5. To add a custom logo for signing or email notifications, select one of the following:

   - Upload Signing Logo. Displays a custom logo in the signing view when recipients open your documents to view and sign.
   - Upload Email Logo. Customize the logo used in email notifications sent to envelope recipients.

     Select a logo file that conforms to the image requirements guidelines.

     Maximum file size: 300KB

     Dimensions (recommended): 296x76 pixels

     File formats: JPG, GIF, PNG

   ![The screenshot shows the Customize Signing & Email window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Customize-Signing--Email-window.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwMjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.rloVqYYzh-RpB7tTw-oh4fq64af-c7E1A_35v2i-4l0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Customize Signing & Email window.")
6. To specify colors:
   1. Select the color box of the Header or Button background color that you want to edit. The color controls open so that you can select a new color.

text:
e Signing & Email window.")
6. To specify colors:
   1. Select the color box of the Header or Button background color that you want to edit. The color controls open so that you can select a new color.

      ![The screenshot shows the Header Background color picker.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Header-background-color.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwMjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.rloVqYYzh-RpB7tTw-oh4fq64af-c7E1A_35v2i-4l0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Header Background color picker.")
