   3. For text, you can select either black or white only.
7. Use the Preview views to check what recipients will see for both the signing experience and email notifications: ![The screenshot shows the Preview view of the Customize Signing & Email window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Preview-Customize-Signing-Email-logo.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwMjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.rloVqYYzh-RpB7tTw-oh4fq64af-c7E1A_35v2i-4l0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Preview view of the Customize Signing & Email window.")
8. Select Apply Brand to close the window.
9. To save your changes, select Save.

The brand is added as tile on the Branding page. The signing brand is available for senders to select when they send an envelope. Brands can be applied to templates and envelopes generated by your account.

title: Domains
text:
Read about Step 1 of the process to set up and enable SSO for your Docusign organization and additional information about reserving domains.

Note: This guide is for Docusign
administrators. Single Sign-On requires Docusign Admin.

This section covers **Step 1** of the process to [set up and enable SSO for your Docusign organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=njp1583359138365.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how Docusign administrators can set up SSO at a global level to manage users in all their Docusign accounts.") and provides
some supporting reference information about reserving domains. This step requires that you
have already created your organization as described in [creating an organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=tif1583359135245.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators manage multiple accounts at once by creating an organization. Docusign administrators can manage users and settings for multiple accounts from one central location."). An organization can claim the same domain in both the demo
and production environments.

Note: Claiming a domain is also part of the authorization process for connected applications.
For more information, see [Connected Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ynk1583359206013.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators manage the applications that can access your Docusign organization. Applications are authorized for all domain users and access is limited by the permissions you specify.").

Best Practice:
Docusign recommends setting up and testing SSO in a demo organization first.
When successful, repeat the steps in your production organization account.

As a Docusign administrator, you can claim domains for use with Docusign through the Domains page of your organization. When you claim and
verify an email domain for your organization, you can manage all users for that domain across
all accounts linked to the organization.

You can restrict users from creating personal Docusign accounts using an email address from a
claimed domain. You can also grant administrative consent for connected applications on behalf
of domain users.

Important: A domain can only be claimed by one Docusign organization. If one organization has claimed and verified a domain,
another organization cannot claim it. An organization can claim the same domain in both the
demo and production environments.

To start, you'll initiate a claim for your organization from the Domains page in Docusign Admin. Docusign generates a special token that you
add to the Domain Name System (DNS) for the domain. Once Docusign verifies
this token in the DNS, the domain is registered to the organization.

Note: You can choose to add a CNAME record or a TXT record to
your domain’s DNS. Docusign recommends using CNAME record when claiming a
domain.

Note: All users in the organization might have access to the
organization's reserved domain and identity provider information.

title: Stamps
text:
Read about how Docusign stamps allow the administrator to assign users or groups to a
stamp as a way of giving signing authority on behalf of others. A stamp may represent an
organization, a part of an organization, such as a department, or the identity of another
user.

Docusign stamps allows the administrator to assign users or groups to a
stamp as a way of giving signing authority on behalf of others. A stamp may represent an
organization, a part of an organization, such as a department, or the identity of another
user. In certain countries such as Japan, stamps are used in place of signatures. Docusign stamps provide a higher level of control for sensitive stamps like
company or departmental eHanko.

Historically, whoever has access to physical stamps can approve official documents. Account
administrators can useDocusign stamps to create a set of stamps for the
organization and manage access to use those stamps.

Note: Stamps are only supported on Business Pro and
Enterprise Pro plans.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations﻿ for Liveness Detection for Simple and Advanced Signatures
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations﻿ for Liveness Detection for Simple and Advanced Signatures
text: Learn how to create identity verification configurations that use the Docusign ID Verification with Liveness verification method.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations﻿ for Liveness Detection for Simple and Advanced Signatures
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations﻿ for Liveness Detection for Simple and Advanced Signatures
text:
Ensure to acknowledge the information from this section before adding your configuration.

- Permissions and subscriptions: To enable Liveness Detection for ID Verification, you must have full account administrator or delegated permissions. You must also have subscribed to the “ID Verification” or “ID Verification Premier” packages.
- Verification of Japanese IDs: Japanese customers, note that you can set up this configuration to store the back of IDs, including Japan's National Identity Card (My Number Card). If you implement this setup, ID Evidence will store the 12-digit individual number, which is personally identifiable information (PII). Instead, you may use the Docusign ID Verification with Japanese IDs configuration. This ID Verification method respects data privacy by only allowing storage of the front of Japanese Individual Number Cards.

As an administrator, you are in charge of adding an ID Verification configuration that supports Liveness Detection for simple and advanced signatures.

Liveness Detection for ID Verification uses artificial intelligence (AI) and biometrics to authenticate ID documents and confirm the owner’s identity. It reduces the risk of fraud and improves the verification success rate by guiding recipients throughout the process.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity Verification.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration screen opens.
3. Select the ID Verification with Liveness method and select Next.

   The Add ID Verification with Liveness page opens.
4. Enter a name for the configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   The system does not translate the name and description. Senders see the text in its original language.
5. Select the maximum number of verification attempts.

   This number represents how many times recipients can attempt to verify before the envelope is locked. By default, ID Verification locks the envelope when recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their identity. Recipients must contact the sender to unlock the envelope.
6. Select the level of variation for name acceptance standards.

   Use this setting to match the recipient's name with the name in the ID. You can choose one of the following variations:
   - Maximum variation: Accepts additional variations of middle names, suffixes, equivalent names and tolerates minor typing mistakes. The Maximum variation option is the default selection. It is not compatible with the IDV - DS EU Advanced signature type.
   - Moderate variation: Authorizes additional variations of middle names and suffixes.
   - Minimal variation: Authorizes minor variations of first and last names only.

   Note: For more information about this setting, see the  [ID Name Matching](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=rhb1657542911402.html&_LANG=enus)  page.
7. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.

   - Every access: recipients must identify every time they access an envelope.
   - Every access until role is completed: recipients must identify until they sign the envelope’s documents.
   - Once: recipients must only identify once.
8. (Optional) Uncheck the Accept all recommended ID types, issuing countries, and regions option and select Manage to choose the supported countries and IDs for your configuration.
9. (Optional) Select the additional data that you want ID Evidence to store for each ID verification.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations﻿ for Liveness Detection for Simple and Advanced Signatures
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations﻿ for Liveness Detection for Simple and Advanced Signatures
text:
regions option and select Manage to choose the supported countries and IDs for your configuration.
9. (Optional) Select the additional data that you want ID Evidence to store for each ID verification.

   Attention: If your configuration authorizes the use of Japanese Individual Number Cards and allows storage of data on the back of IDs, ID Evidence will store the 12-digit individual number showing on the back of Japanese Individual Number Cards. This data is personally identifiable information (PII) and considered sensitive. To avoid this situation, either unselect storage of the back of IDs or remove the Japanese Individual Number Card from the list of Accepted IDs for your configuration. Alternatively, use the Docusign ID Verification with Japanese IDs configuration. This ID Verification method respects data privacy by only allowing storage of the front of Japanese Individual Number Cards.
10. Choose your preferences for the Identity Wallet:

    - Select Recipients can create or update a Docusign Identity Wallet to allow recipients to create or update their Identity Wallet.
    - Select Recipients can use their Docusign Identity Wallet to allow recipients to identify using their identity wallet.
11. Select Add.

The configuration displays in the configurations list and is available for senders.

title: Search for Users
text:
Learn how account administrators quickly locate users by name or email address from the Users page. Filters help refine the search by limiting it to users by permission profile, user group, or status, such as active, pending, and closed.

As an account administrator, you can search for users by name or by email address from the Users page. You can use filters to refine your search and limit it to users by permission profile, user group, or status, such as active, pending, and closed.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Enter any portion of a name or email address in the Search box.

   The matching users appear in the dropdown list.
3. Select the user that you want to view, or select Enter at the end of the dropdown list to view all matching users.
4. (Optional) Select Filter to restrict your search to users by permission profile, group, and status. Select the filter combination that you want to use, and select Apply.

   You can combine the filters to narrow your results, but you must have at least one option selected for Status. By default, Active and Pending are selected.

The users that meet your search selections are displayed.

title: Delete Permission Profiles
text:
Learn how account administrators delete permission profiles when they are not
needed.

When a permission profile is no longer needed, you
can remove it.

1. From the Accounts view, select Permission
   Profiles.
2. Find the permission profile that you want to delete.

   Note: You cannot delete
   the DS Admin, DS Sender, and DS Viewer permission profiles.
3. Select Actions > Delete.
4. Confirm that you want to delete the permission profile from the account.

The permission profile is removed from the list.

title: Upgrade from an eSignature Plan to an IAM Plan
heading_path: Upgrade from an eSignature Plan to an IAM Plan
text:
Learn how to upgrade your eSignature account to an IAM plan.

As an administrator of an existing eSignature account, you can upgrade your account to an IAM
plan.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   The Plan and billing page opens.![The screenshot shows the Plan and billing page for an eSignature Standard account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/esign-plan-and-billing-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4OTUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9._pvZCVR_hcoP6CL9qK5lTsjjfCoq1JeMrdPXRpZWBSc&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Plan and billing page for an eSignature Standard account.")

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Your Subscription  > Upgrade Plan.

   The Upgrade your plan window opens.
3. Select View All Plans.

   The Upgrade your plan page opens.
4. Select the IAM tab.
5. (Optional) View monthly plans by selecting the Monthly
   toggle.
6. Choose an IAM plan and select Buy Now.

   The Add billing details page opens.
7. (Optional) You can make changes to your billing options and user licenses for
   the plan. Select from the dropdown menus on the checkout page. Checkout will
   automatically update totals.
8. Select your payment method.
9. Select Purchase.

   The confirmation page opens.

You have upgraded to an IAM plan. An order confirmation is
sent to your email.

title: User Invitation and Activation Process
text:
Read about how administrators initiate the user invitation process and the steps users
must complete to activate their membership and join an account.

Adding a user to an account requires actions from an administrator and the new user. As an
administrator, you can add a user and send an invitation to their email address. To
complete the process, the user must complete the activation process.

Optionally, you can include an access code as part of the activation process. The activation
process also has an option for the user to decline the invitation.

The invitation and activation process includes these steps:

1. Log in to your Docusign account and open the Users
   page.
2. Create a user by adding their email address, profile information, and optional access code.
   An invitation is sent to the email address of the new user. ![The screenshot shows the optional access code field when you are creating a user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Add-user.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.r6ECj0fRrkG6cePO_hjpeEWmSaOd9-ApxWii44BFQUM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the optional access code field when you are creating a user.")
3. The user receives an activation email and selects the Activate button.
   ![The screenshot shows an example of the user activation email.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/User-activation-email.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.r6ECj0fRrkG6cePO_hjpeEWmSaOd9-ApxWii44BFQUM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the user activation email.")

title: User Invitation and Activation Process
text:
aWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.r6ECj0fRrkG6cePO_hjpeEWmSaOd9-ApxWii44BFQUM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the user activation email.")

   The Create Account or Decline Invitation page opens. ![The screenshot shows the Create Account or Decline Invitation page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/User-accept.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.r6ECj0fRrkG6cePO_hjpeEWmSaOd9-ApxWii44BFQUM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Create Account or Decline Invitation page.")
4. If the user selects Create Account and the administrator has required
   an access code, the user views the Access Code page. If an access code is not
   required, the user is redirected to the Create Your Password page. If the user
   declines the invitation, the invite is invalidated and a message page informs the
   user that the invitation is no longer valid. ![The screenshot shows an example of the Access Code page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/user-access-code.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.r6ECj0fRrkG6cePO_hjpeEWmSaOd9-ApxWii44BFQUM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the Access Code page.")
5. If the user enters the access code correctly, they are redirected to the Create Your Password
   page. If the user does not enter the access code correctly, the invitation is
   invalidated. If you want to re-invite the user, you can resend an invitation on the
   Users page. ![The screenshot shows an example of the Create Your Password page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/User-password.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.r6ECj0fRrkG6cePO_hjpeEWmSaOd9-ApxWii44BFQUM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the Create Your Password page.")
6. After the user sets a password, the user is activated and added to the account. The user is
   logged in to the account and views the Docusign account Home page.

title: Enable Stamps to be Used for Accounts
heading_path: Enable Stamps to be Used for Accounts
text:
Learn how administrators enable stamps to be used by users and groups. Users of accounts
that have stamps enabled can assign stamp fields to documents. Users that open a document that
contains a stamp field can upload or create one.

To enable stamps to be used by users and groups of a Docusign account, an
administrator must enable the Enable signature stamp field check box in
the Sending Settings page.

You have the option to either create a stamp or upload a stamp.

title: Integrations
text:
Read about how administrators manage the Docusign Connect
configurations and API integration keys for their accounts from the Docusign
eSignature account administration web application.

Use the Integrations section of the Docusign eSignature account
administration web application to manage the Connect configurations and API integration keys
for your account.

title: Advanced Configuration for Signing Brands
text:
Read about the advanced configuration options available to administrators that want to
customize the signing experience. Administrators can customize destination URLs, header and
footer links, and signing brand languages.

In addition to the logo and color customization you can apply to a [signing brand](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=lfr1583277366660.html&_LANG=enus "Branding offers account administrators a way to customize the Docusign experience for both senders and recipients."), you can use additional advanced
configuration options to modify the signing experience.

title: Connect an Application
text:
Learn how administrators connect applications to Docusign for use with Agreement Rules.

As an account administrator, you can connect applications to Docusign for use with Agreement Rules.

1. From the Accounts view, select Connections in the AGREEMENT ACTIONS section.
2. Select CONNECT NEW APP.
3. Select the application that you want to connect to Docusign.

   ![The screenshot shows the apps that you can connect to.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/The-screenshot-shows-the-apps-you-can-connect-to.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU5MDEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.tSdh3M_7KV1_817yV-xehATcVYrQfk0MQwW1OV1jAYA&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the apps that you can connect to.")
4. Enter a nickname for your account and select SAVE.

   Note: Consider using a service account and not a personal account to log into your application. A service account allows multiple administrators to manage service account credentials.
5. Log in to the application that you are connecting, and select Grant Access.

   The application is added to your Active Apps list.

   Note: Docusign *does not* store credential information. This process uses standard Open Authentication (OAuth). Docusign only seeks permissions to create files and folders when processing a rule.

The application you selected is displayed in the Active Apps list.

title: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
text:
Read about the Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan read-only configuration. Learn about its configuration and the parameters that you can edit in your customized configuration.

Docusign ID Verification with IC‑chip Reading for Japan is a read-only configuration that helps your organization meet the eKYC (Know Your Customer) and AML (Anti-Money Laundering) regulations in Japan. It complies with the planned updates to the identity verification requirements in the Act on Prevention of Transfer of Criminal Proceeds and the Mobile Phone Improper Use Prevention Act.

This configuration allows recipients to verify their identity in two ways:

- The Public Certification Service for Individuals.
- The biometric and IC-chip reading check

Administrators can use the Docusign ID Verification with IC‑chip Reading for Japan read-only configuration for their account. They can also choose to copy and customize it to meet their specific needs.

title: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
text:
Recipients initiate one of the following verification paths depending on the ID document that they select to verify their identity:

- The Public Certification Service for Individuals starts when recipients select Individual number card (JPKI) to verify their identity.
- The Biometric and IC-chip reading check starts when recipients select any other supported ID document

While both paths deliver a high level of trust, the Public Certification Service for Individuals is the most robust. The biometric and IC-chip reading check method provides a fallback option when recipients cannot meet the requirements of the Public Certification Service for Individuals.

The Public Certification Service for Individuals (KA method as of December, 2025) and the biometric and IC-chip reading check (HE method as of December, 2025) both comply with the Act on the Prevention of Transfer of Criminal Proceeds.

For more information about each verification path, read the [Verification Using Docusign ID Verification with IC‑chip Reading for Japan](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=con8ce1419e-2dda-46cc-952d-9bb07dcbc87e.html&_LANG=enus) page.

title: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
text:
Before you can use the Docusign ID Verification with IC‑chip Reading for Japan read-only configuration, ensure that your organization meets the following requirements:

- Your eSignature account is in the Japan data region (JP1)
- Your organization has an active ID Verification, ID Verification Premier subscription or appropriate IAM (Intelligent Agreement Management) plan, such as IAM for CX
- Your administrator activated the Docusign ID Verification with IC‑chip Reading for Japan read-only configuration. For more information about activating a configuration, read the [Activate Identity Verification Configurations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ett1690403409655.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how account administrators activate identity verification configurations.") page.

title: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
text: For more information about the limitations for ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan configuration, read the [Verification Using Docusign ID Verification with IC‑chip Reading for Japan](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=con8ce1419e-2dda-46cc-952d-9bb07dcbc87e.html&_LANG=enus) page.

title: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
text:
The Docusign ID Verification with IC‑chip Reading for Japan configuration is read-only. You cannot edit its configuration parameters. However, you can create a copy of the read-only configuration and customize it to meet your requirements. For more information about creating a customized configuration, read the [Customize your Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=tsk92d2cf56-3d67-462e-b1bc-73fe3fbde25e.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to copy and edit the read-only Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan configuration to meet your specific requirements.") page.

The following list enumerates the parameters that you can edit when creating a customized configuration. For more information about the ID Verification configuration parameters, read the [Add Identity Verification Configurations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=dnh1583277456343.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how account administrators add configurations for identity verification.") page:

- Configuration name and description: Name and description of your customized configuration as they appear to senders when they select an identity verification for a recipient. For more information, read the [To Add ID Verification for a Recipient](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=whh1578456529441.html&_LANG=enus) page.
- Maximum verification attempts: The maximum number of times a recipient can try to verify their identity. When recipients exceed this limit, their envelope is locked.

  Note: The Japan Public Key Infrastructure imposes a limit of 5 unsuccessful password entry attempts for Individual Number Card holders. This limit applies to the Public Certification Service for Individuals flows because they use the digital signature certificate in the Individual number card (JPKI) ID document.
- Name variation level: By default, ID Verification only allows the minimal variation level. For more information about the recommended configuration parameters and name matching examples, read the [Japanese Name-Matching](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=ewf1742481620817.html&_LANG=enus) page.
- Recipients must verify: ID Verification requests that recipients verify their identity only once to access the envelope.
- Accepted IDs: Read the [Available ID documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=con136df38a-18a8-4c57-9d7c-69d9e8e9d9cd.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=available-id-documents) section.
- Stored data: By default, ID Evidence stores a specific set of data every time a recipient verifies their identity using the Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan configuration. Administrators can change which data ID Evidence stores to meet their organization's needs.

title: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
text:
Administrators can select the supported IDs from the following list:

- Individual number card (JPKI). The Individual number card (JPKI) uses the Public Certification Service for Individuals that uses the Japanese Public Key Infrastructure (JPKI) to verify recipients’ identity. It is the most robust identity verification. For more information about this ID verification flow, read the [Verification Using Docusign ID Verification with IC‑chip Reading for Japan](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=con8ce1419e-2dda-46cc-952d-9bb07dcbc87e.html&_LANG=enus) page.

  Important: if you select the Individual number card (JPKI) ID document, Docusign recommends that you choose a biometric ID verification as a fallback option.
- Individual number card. When this ID is selected, ID Verification uses the Individual Number Card (also known as My Number Card) to verify recipients’ identity. Unlike the Individual number card (JPKI), this verification flow does not require the password. Instead, ID Verification uses the biometric and IC-chip reading check and requests that the recipient enter card information from the front of the card. Next, the trusted partner takes a selfie of the recipient.
- Driver’s license. This ID document relies on the biometric and IC-chip reading check. Recipients who use this ID document for identification must provide two sets of 4-digit PINs for IC chip reading.

  Note: Names or addresses that include old Kanji characters (Gaiji) in a Driver’s License may result in verification failure.
- Residence card and special permanent residence certificate. Both identity documents rely on the biometric and IC-chip reading check. Recipients who use these ID documents to identify must provide additional information from the card.

  Note: Residence cards and special permanent residence certificates with an updated address will fail the verification because they require manual inspection to meet the identity verification requirement. This requirement complies with the Act on the Prevention of Transfer of Criminal Proceeds.

title: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
text:
Read about Docusign ID Verification for EU Qualified configurations and how administrators can customize it to meet their organization’s needs.

The Docusign ID Verification for EU Qualified configuration allows you to verify recipient identities in compliance with the eIDAS Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) regulation. QES is the most reliable signature type. It is legally equivalent to a handwritten signature within the European Union (EU).

When you select Docusign ID Verification for EU Qualified, Docusign automatically sets the signature type to IDV - DS EU Qualified. This signature type ensures that all envelopes using this configuration generate valid qualified electronic signatures. You cannot edit this setting.

Note: The Docusign ID Verification for EU Qualified configuration is available only for account plans that include ID Verification Premier. For more information on using ID Verification Premier for EU Qualified Signatures, read the [Verification of Government-Issued IDs for European Qualified Signatures](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=vke1672930715877.html&_LANG=enus) help page .

title: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
text:
The “Basic settings” section includes the following parameters:

- Name and description
- Maximum verification attempts
- Agreement protection

title: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
text: The name and description you choose for your configuration reflect the information that senders see when selecting an ID verification method. You cannot localize this information.

title: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
text: The Maximum verification attempts parameter sets the limit for how many times a recipient can attempt verification. If recipients reach this limit without success, the envelope locks automatically.

title: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
text:
The Agreement protection section allows you to secure access to already signed agreements. If enabled, the Recipient always verifies parameter requires recipients to authenticate to access agreements they have already signed. They can authenticate through SMS or by using the Docusign Identity Wallet.

The Recipient always verifies parameter also applies when recipients have verified their identity but haven’t signed the agreement yet. If they access the document within 24 hours after verifying their identity, the agreement protection is enforced.

SMS authentication remains active for 1 year after formal identity document verification. After one year, recipients can no longer use SMS to authenticate. They must use their Identity Wallet or verify their identity again using an ID document.

Note: Docusign uses the phone number that recipients have provided during the identity verification process to send the SMS code. If recipients change their phone number after identity verification, they must verify their identity again to update it.

title: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
text: The Stored data from IDs section allows you to choose which ID document information to store in ID Evidence. By default, ID Verification selects only information from the transaction, such as the status of the identity verification process. Administrators can choose additional data if your organization requires it.

title: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration
text:
The Identity Wallet allows recipients to save their verification results for future use. It spares recipients the need to verify their identity for every new envelope.

Administrators can decide whether recipients can create or update an identity wallet to save their identity verification results. They can also choose whether recipients can use their identity wallet to verify their identity.

Note: ID Verification cannot store data when recipients verify their identity using their wallet.

title: AI Tools in the Signing Experience
heading_path: AI Tools in the Signing Experience
text:
The AI-Assisted Summary and Extraction feature provides a concise overview of agreements and key details. Account administrators on Direct plans can disable this feature in account adminstration settings.

When users are signing documents, they can take advantage of the AI-Assisted Summary and Extraction feature. It provides a short summary of the agreement and displays the key agreement details.

The AI-Assisted Summary and Extraction feature is enabled by default for eligible customers and agreements to offer to their signers. Account administrators on Direct plans can disable this feature for users through the account administration settings.

Note:

- AI-assisted features might not be available on your account.
- AI-assisted features are currently only available in North America.

For more information about using the AI-Assisted Summary and Extraction feature, see the [View Key Agreement Information with the Docusign Signing AI Summary](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=dvd1751051359819.html&_LANG=enus)﻿ topic.

title: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
text:
Read about the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration. This ID Verification configuration helps organizations to comply with the 23 NYCRR Part 500 cybersecurity regulation for U.S.-based recipients.

ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance is an ID Verification configuration that helps organizations operating in New York, United States (U.S.), comply with the 23 NYCRR Part 500 cybersecurity regulation. The New York State Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) issued this regulation. It requires covered entities in the Financial Services sector to implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) and conduct risk assessments to protect nonpublic information.

The ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration combines a live risk assessment with a two-factor authentication sequence in a single recipient experience. Docusign works with a trusted partner (Socure) to perform recipients’ risk assessment.

If the assessment is successful, recipients must comply with Knowledge-Based Authentication to verify their identity. Eventually, if ID Verification confirms the recipients’ identity, they proceed to Phone Authentication and receive a one-time passcode (OTP) to access the envelope. This step sequence satisfies the MFA requirement of 23 NYCRR Part 500.

Note: Docusign recommends that organizations consult with their legal counsel to determine if ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance helps them meet their compliance requirements under 23 NYCRR Part 500.

title: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
text:
ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance can help organizations ensure security and regulatory compliance in the following situations:

- Banking and lending: ID Verification checks borrower identities before signing high-value loan agreements or opening retail accounts.
- Insurance: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance authenticates policyholders and claimants for sensitive digital transactions.
- Wealth management: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance secures account transfers and high-risk investment authorizations with an audit-ready MFA trail.
- Fintech: ID Verification reduces fraud during digital onboarding while maintaining compliance with New York's cybersecurity framework.

title: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
text:
The ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration is available only for account plans that include ID Verification, ID Verification Premier, or IAM for CX. It is only available to Docusign accounts hosted in the U.S.-based datacenters. Recipients applying for this ID Verification configuration must be U.S. residents or U.S. citizens with a valid SSN (Social Security number) and a U.S. address.

ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance is a read-only configuration. You cannot edit its parameters.

title: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration
text:
Identity Verification provides a read-only configuration for ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance. You cannot edit its parameters. This configuration includes the following parameters:

- Name and description: The name and description display to senders when they select an identity verification method for their envelope recipients.
- Maximum verification attempts: Recipients can attempt up to 4 unsuccessful verifications. Beyond this limit, the envelope locks automatically.
- Name Variation level: ID Verification accepts maximum variation for name matching, as explained in the [ID Name Matching](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=rhb1657542911402.html&_LANG=enus) help page.
- Recipient must verify: ID Verification requires recipients to verify their identity every time they access the envelope.
- Workflow: Recipients must comply with strict ID Verification methods to satisfy the MFA requirement of 23 NYCRR Part 500. For more information, read the [ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=con427cc6d7-6044-4e8b-aaff-a2a09cbf69ae.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=con427cc6d7-6044-4e8b-aaff-a2a09cbf69ae__id-verification-for-nycrr500-compliance-workflow) section.
- Data to store: ID Evidence stores all the authentication events and risk signals. This record helps organizations maintain the documentation they need for regulatory reporting and audit purposes under 23 NYCRR Part 500.

title: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance workflow
text:
The workflow for verifying recipients' compliance with the 23 NYCRR Part 500 cybersecurity regulation includes the following steps:

1. Risk assessment: ID Verification asks recipients to submit a set of information that the trusted partner (Socure) analyzes to determine the appropriate risk level. For example, an email domain listed as high risk can increase the risk score. Docusign administrators cannot change the risk assessment parameters. If their risk score is satisfactory, recipients can proceed with Knowledge-Based Authentication. For more information about the reasons for risk assessment failure, read the [Rejection criteria](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=con427cc6d7-6044-4e8b-aaff-a2a09cbf69ae.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=con427cc6d7-6044-4e8b-aaff-a2a09cbf69ae__rejection-criteria) section.
2. Knowledge-Based Authentication (KBA): ID Verification asks the recipients to answer a series of questions based on their public records, such as their current home address. If the recipients’ answers are correct, ID Verification approves their identity and allows access to the Phone Authentication step.
3. Phone Authentication: ID Verification sends a one-time passcode (OTP) through SMS or phone call to recipients. This step confirms that the recipient possesses a verified device. If the submitted code is correct, recipients can access the envelope.

This three-step sequence satisfies the MFA requirement of 23 NYCRR Part 500.

title: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
text:
The Reject Recipients section lists the triggers that automatically block recipients during the risk assessment process. Administrators cannot change those parameters.

- Almost certain probability of fraud: ID Verification blocks recipients when the trusted partner detects a high probability of fraud based on data analysis.
- Date of birth indicates COPPA review: ID Verification blocks recipients when the provided date of birth indicates they are under 13 years old. This trigger requires a review under the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA).
- Email or IP address in a sanctioned country: ID Verification blocks recipients when their email or IP address originates from a country listed in the OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) database.
- Social Security Number has been reported as deceased: ID Verification blocks recipients when the Social Security Administration reports the provided Social Security Number as deceased.
- Global Watchlist Screening: ID Verification blocks recipients when the trusted partner finds that recipients display on the AML (Anti-Money Laundering) or the OFAC list. However, recipients displaying on the PEP (Politically Exposed Persons) or Adverse Media lists can still complete the verification process.

title: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
heading_path: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration
text:
ID Evidence captures all relevant information occurring during ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance. This data provides a unified, tamper-evident record of the verification process.

ID Evidence records the following risk assessment metadata:

- Risk level: ID Verification can assign recipients a Low, Medium, High, or Reject profile.
- Personal Information: ID Evidence captures recipients’ first and last names, date of birth, and gender.
- Risk-assessment details: ID Evidence shows the data that recipients submitted for risk assessment, and that the trusted partner flagged as High risk or Neutral. For example, if a recipient submits an invalid or nonexistent address, the trusted partner flags this information as high risk.

When recipients proceed with Knowledge-Based Authentication, ID Evidence captures the following information:

- Verification status and timestamps: ID Evidence indicates if the recipient passed the authentication and when the verification occurred.
- Type of ID: ID Evidence records the type of ID document that recipients submitted and the country or region they selected.
- Quiz results: ID Evidence stores the number of questions asked to recipients and the number of correct answers.
- Identity data: ID Evidence shows the date of birth and address that the recipient submitted.
- Envelope ID: ID Evidence returns the ID of the envelope that the recipient is trying to access.

ID Evidence also stores the verification status of the Phone Authentication process.

title: User Password Reset with the AI Agent
heading_path: User Password Reset with the AI Agent
text:
Read about how account administrators on digital plans can use the Docusign AI agent to reset user passwords, resend invitations, and reactivate users.

Account administrators can reset passwords for their users by using the Docusign AI agent. In account administration, you can open the AI agent and select one of the quick actions or type requests in natural language, such as "Reset password for Lisa Chin." The AI agent locates the user, confirms the name and email address, and sends the password reset email. The AI agent handles common edge cases, including misspelled names or email addresses, multiple matching users, and pending or closed users.

The AI agent also includes guided steps to resend invitations or reactivate users. If a user is managed through SSO, you are instructed to contact your IT department to make any changes.

This feature is currently available only to account administrators on digital plans, either paid or trial, in the US region and the English locale. End users cannot access this AI agent to reset their password.

title: Responsive Signing Optimize View Setting for Administrators
heading_path: Responsive Signing Optimize View Setting for Administrators
What is the difference between AI-assisted mode and Basic mode for the mobile responsive Optimize View setting?
text:
When using mobile responsive signing with Optimize View for User's Device selected as the default view, Docusign automatically determines whether a recipient should open a document in the mobile-friendly (HTML) view or the desktop (PDF) view on a mobile device. This decision is made to ensure the best possible reading experience and is powered by one of two modes: AI-assisted mode (default for US and EU accounts) or Basic mode (non-AI, default for Canada, Japan, Australia, and USFed accounts).

The key difference between these modes is how the decision is made. AI-assisted mode evaluates the actual mobile rendering (the HTML output) to determine readability and formatting quality, providing a more accurate and user-friendly experience. In contrast, Basic mode analyzes only the original document's structure and complexity, such as layout, tables, and formatting, to predict whether it will render well on mobile, without evaluating the final HTML output. In other words, AI-assisted mode makes decisions based on the final user experience, while Basic mode relies only on characteristics of the source document.

For more information on mobile responsive signing, see the [Responsive Signing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=cbv1578456473989.html&_LANG=enus) topic.

title: Responsive Signing Optimize View Setting for Administrators
heading_path: Responsive Signing Optimize View Setting for Administrators
Is responsive signing AI-assisted? What options are available to opt out?
text: Responsive signing is AI-assisted in all regions except for Canada, Japan, Australia, and USFed accounts. To disable AI-assisted mode and switch to the Basic mode of responsive signing, administrators can disable Enable AI tools in signing for sent agreements setting on the Signing Settings page.

title: Signature Styles
text:
Read about the signature styles that signers can select.

Select the signature styles available for signers to choose from in the Adopt Your Signature
dialog box. If no styles are selected, Arial Unicode is used. The Arial Unicode signature
style cannot be removed from the list.

For language-speciﬁc styles, select the language tab and select the signature styles. The
language-specific styles are presented to a signer who is signing in that language.

![The screenshot shows a sample of the signature styles to choose from.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/zz.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU0MTQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.MdF4U8kSjJ3PYUkH_NgyExx-uDNCJndQyB_iQ_toaT8&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows a sample of the signature styles to choose from.")

title: Agreement Rules Modification
text:
Read about the actions that account administrators can take on agreement rules. From the
RULES page, administrators can edit, disable, and delete
agreement rules.

Administrators can edit existing rules from the RULES page of the
Agreement Rules area. From the Actions
menu, administrators can modify the conditions and actions associated with the rule,
disable the rule, or delete the rule. Disabled and deleted rules no longer affect
completed envelopes.

![The screenshot shows the Agreement Rules page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/agreerules.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4ODIsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.KdUwvmGIWbwZkY-KgAahE71BBY3TbEwAfE8Ys3auLoc&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Agreement Rules page.")

title: Document Custom Fields
text:
Read about how administrators and users create custom fields for documents to collect
information specific to their business or organization. Custom fields can be saved for reuse in
any document to help speed document preparation.

Docusign users and administrators can create custom versions of standard
fields and save them for reuse on future documents. Define any combination of field
properties, such as font type or size, or a validation setting. Document custom fields help
speed up document preparation by allowing you to save fields customized for repeated
use.

The custom fields created by users display in the Fields palette for adding recipient fields
to documents. Custom fields are also listed in their user preferences.

Account administrators can view and manage the custom fields saved by all account users, as
well as add new custom fields for themselves or to share with all users.

Note: Changes to Document Custom Fields are captured in your account audit
log.

Note: Some advanced features and options are
supported only in certain Docusign plans. Your account plan might not
support some options discussed in this help topic. For more information about which options
are available for your account, check your account plan or contact Docusign
Support.

title: Add Custom Logos and Specify Brand Colors
heading_path: Add Custom Logos and Specify Brand Colors
text:
Learn how administrators customize the sending experience with a custom logo and brand
color scheme.

As an administrator, you can customize the sending experience
with a custom logo and color scheme that is applied to envelopes sent from your
account.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Brands.
2. Select the Sending tab, and select Edit Your
   Theme.
3. In the sending or email logo section, select the "x" to remove
   the existing logo.
4. Select Upload Logo and select your logo file. The logo should
   meet the following requirements:

   Maximum file size: 300 KB

   Dimensions (recommended): 296x76 pixels

   File formats: JPG, GIF, PNG
5. To specify colors:
   1. Select the color box of the Banner or Button color that you want to edit. The color
      controls open.

      ![The screenshot shows the color picker.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/sending-color-picker.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNDgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.BMmi1S_qrmYbI2eMpoh0vyA8GK7HpFZ--3a0gqenunU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the color picker.")
   2. Select a color using the color picker, use the eye dropper to match a color value in your custom logo or other image, or enter the hexadecimal value for the color.
6. Use the Preview views to check what account members will see for
   the Home and Manage pages. Use the Email preview to view what your recipients will see
   when they receive a Docusign envelope.
7. To finish and apply your brand, select Apply Brand.

   Your changes are summarized on the Branding page and all account members will see your
   custom sending brand when they log in to their account.
   ![The screenshot shows the Branding page summary.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/sending-brand-complete.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNDgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.BMmi1S_qrmYbI2eMpoh0vyA8GK7HpFZ--3a0gqenunU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Branding page summary.")

Your customized brand is available to account members
when they log into their account.

title: Shared Access Permission Access Levels
text:
View the levels of access administrators can grant through the Shared
Access permission.

The following table describes the level of access provided by the Shared
Access permission in combination with each of the Users
and Groups access levels.

| Administrative area | Page | Level of access: View Only | Level of access: Edit Groups Only | Level of access: Edit Users & Groups | Level of access: Edit Users, Groups, & Administrators |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N/A | Overview | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Users | View | View | Edit[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ctp1661539662041.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_1) | Edit[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ctp1661539662041.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_2) |
| Users and Groups | Permission Profiles | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Groups | View | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Users and Groups | Signing Groups | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Audit Logs | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Bulk Actions | View | View | View | View |
| Auditing | Validations | None | None | None | None |

[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ctp1661539662041.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_1) Edit access does not include the ability to edit users with "Allow All"
administrative permissions enabled, such as those with the DS
Admin permission profile.

[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ctp1661539662041.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_2) Edit access does not include access to Shared Access or
Shared Envelopes unless the
Envelope Sharing permission is also
selected, and does not include access to Transfer
Envelopes or Transfer
Templates unless the Transfer
Envelopes permission is also
selected.

title: Review Seat Requests From Users
text:
Learn how to review and confirm or deny new seat requests.

When a user requests to join an account through the Account Discovery feature, you
receive an email to review and confirm or deny the request.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Users.

   Note: For some plans, the Users page is
   titled Seats & Users.

   ![The screenshot shows the Users page with new requests to join the account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/seatmgmt2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.N8Vuny6fjnDPglglmFcBPWMOkFBzFPbEqoaKyC0lxp0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Users page with new requests to join the account.")
2. In the User Requests section, review any requests awaiting
   approval.
3. Select Confirm or Deny to accept
   or decline seat requests.

   This action processes the request and removes the request from the list.

   Note: Depending on your plan, you can purchase more seats if
   needed.

New users are added to the Users list. Refresh the page to
view the updated list of users. Approved users are assigned a seat on the account and
are assigned the DS Sender permission.

title: Reports and Envelope Custom Fields
text:
Read about how account administrators include custom field information for accounts in
envelope reports.

As an account administrator, you can use reports to create a custom envelope report that includes
envelope custom field information. When you add the envelope custom field column to a report,
all fields defined on your account will be listed in the envelope report, including those that
are not shown to envelope creators.

![Reports and Envelope Custom Fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/envelope_reportcf.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU2MjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.5CeccgGyvp2Ptsd8VjHJcwFqeyYq92pECN_89J_8j-c&_LANG=enus "Reports and Envelope Custom Fields.")

For information on how to create a custom report, see [View and Modify Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ekf1578456440590.html&bundleId=cnu1642812012214&_LANG=enus).

title: Groups
text:
Read about how account administrators use groups to organize users into functional
units for template sharing and other functions.

Note:
Docusign CLM
enabled accounts create, edit, and delete groups for the account through Docusign organization settings. Contact your Docusign
administrator to manage groups at the organization level. For more information, see [Managing Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=fza1583359190504.html&bundleId=rrf1583359212854&_LANG=enus).

As a Docusign account administrator, use the Groups page to manage the
groups on your account.

Groups are used to organize users into functional units for template sharing to limit user access to templates. Additionally, if multiple account brands are enabled, groups are used to set which brands can be used by group members.

There are two default groups for every account: Everyone and Administrators. These groups cannot be edited or deleted from the account. The Everyone group contains all active account users. The Administrators group has all active users that have the All administration capabilities option enabled in their associated [permission profile](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously."). If your account includes the ability to define delegated administration permission profiles, users who are assigned to these sets are not included in the Administrators group.

title: Sample Default Legal Disclosure
text:
Read the default legal disclosure that is available to administrators who want to
customize it for their business.

The following default legal disclosure is available to administrators who want to customize it
for their business.

**IMPORTANT:** If you choose to create a custom disclosure, you will not be able
to change back to the default disclosure once you save the custom disclosure.
Additionally, Docusign will not automatically translate your
custom disclosure, and you must create a disclosure for each language used by your
signers.

**ELECTRONIC RECORD AND SIGNATURE DISCLOSURE**

From time to time, [[CompanyName]] (we, us or Company) may be required by law to
provide to you certain written notices or disclosures. Described below are the terms
and conditions for providing to you such notices and disclosures electronically
through the Docusign system. Please read the information below
carefully and thoroughly, and if you can access this information electronically to
your satisfaction and agree to this Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure
(ERSD), please confirm your agreement by selecting the check-box next to ‘I agree to
use electronic records and signatures’ before clicking ‘CONTINUE’ within the Docusign system.

**Getting paper copies**

At any time, you may request from us a paper copy of any record provided or made
available electronically to you by us. You will have the ability to download and
print documents we send to you through the Docusign system during
and immediately after the signing session and, if you elect to create a Docusign account, you may access the documents for a limited
period of time (usually 30 days) after such documents are first sent to you. After
such time, if you wish for us to send you paper copies of any such documents from
our office to you, you will be charged a $[[CopyCostPerPage]] per-page fee. You may
request delivery of such paper copies from us by following the procedure described
below.

**Withdrawing your consent**

If you decide to receive notices and disclosures from us electronically, you may at
any time change your mind and tell us that thereafter you want to receive required
notices and disclosures only in paper format. How you must inform us of your
decision to receive future notices and disclosure in paper format and withdraw your
consent to receive notices and disclosures electronically is described below.

**Consequences of changing your mind**

If you elect to receive required notices and disclosures only in paper format, it
will slow the speed at which we can complete certain steps in transactions with you
and delivering services to you because we will need first to send the required
notices or disclosures to you in paper format, and then wait until we receive back
from you your acknowledgment of your receipt of such paper notices or disclosures.
Further, you will no longer be able to use the Docusign system to
receive required notices and consents electronically from us or to sign
electronically documents from us.

**All notices and disclosures will be sent to you electronically**

title: Sample Default Legal Disclosure
text:
use the Docusign system to
receive required notices and consents electronically from us or to sign
electronically documents from us.

**All notices and disclosures will be sent to you electronically**

Unless you tell us otherwise in accordance with the procedures described herein, we
will provide electronically to you through the Docusign system all
required notices, disclosures, authorizations, acknowledgements, and other documents
that are required to be provided or made available to you during the course of our
relationship with you. To reduce the chance of you inadvertently not receiving any
notice or disclosure, we prefer to provide all of the required notices and
disclosures to you by the same method and to the same address that you have given
us. Thus, you can receive all the disclosures and notices electronically or in paper
format through the paper mail delivery system. If you do not agree with this
process, please let us know as described below. Please also see the paragraph
immediately above that describes the consequences of your electing not to receive
delivery of the notices and disclosures electronically from us.

**How to contact [[CompanyName]]:**

You may contact us to let us know of your changes as to how we may contact you
electronically, to request paper copies of certain information from us, and to
withdraw your prior consent to receive notices and disclosures electronically as
follows:

[[WithdrawOptions]]

**To advise [[CompanyName]] of your new email address**

To let us know of a change in your email address where we should send notices and
disclosures electronically to you, you must send an email message to us at
[[ChangeEmail]] and in the body of such request you must state: your previous email
address, your new email address.[[ChangeEmailOther]]

If you created a Docusign account, you may update it with your new
email address through your account preferences.

**To request paper copies from [[CompanyName]]**

To request delivery from us of paper copies of the notices and disclosures previously
provided by us to you electronically, you must send us an email to
[[CopyRequestEmail]] and in the body of such request you must state your email
address, full name, mailing address, and telephone number.
[[CopyFeeCollectionMethod]]

**To withdraw your consent with [[CompanyName]]**

To inform us that you no longer wish to receive future notices and disclosures in
electronic format you may:

1. decline to sign a document from within your signing session, and on the
   subsequent page, select the check-box indicating you wish to withdraw your
   consent, or you may;
2. send us an email to [[WithdrawEmail]] and in the body of such request you must
   state your email, full name, mailing address, and telephone number.
   [[WithdrawOther]]. [[WithdrawConsequences]].

**Required hardware and software**

The minimum system requirements for using the Docusign system may
change over time. The current system requirements are found here: [Signer Guide Signing System Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/guides/signer-guide-signing-system-requirements)

**Acknowledging your access and consent to receive and sign documents
electronically**

To confirm to us that you can access this information electronically, which will be
similar to other electronic notices and disclosures that we will provide to you,
please confirm that you have read this ERSD, and (i) that you are able to print on
paper or electronically save this ERSD for your future reference and access; or (ii)
that you are able to email this ERSD to an email address where you will be able to
print on paper or save it for your future reference and access. Further, if you
consent to receiving notices and disclosures exclusively in electronic format as
described herein, then select the check-box next to ‘I agree to use electronic
records and signatures’ before clicking ‘CONTINUE’ within the Docusign system.

By selecting the check-box next to ‘I agree to use electronic records and

title: Transfer Templates from Your Account
text:
Learn how administrators transfer ownership of templates created by users on an account
to other users.

As an administrator, you have access to all templates created by the users on your account. You can transfer ownership of templates from the Templates page of your Docusign account.

1. From your Docusign account, select the
   Templates tab in the global header.
2. Select All Templates and locate the templates you want to
   transfer by filtering, searching, or scanning the list.
3. To transfer a single template:
   1. Select the action menu for the template and select Transfer
      Ownership.
   2. Select the new owner for the template and select
      Transfer.

      The transfer is initiated. The template may remain in the original owner's account
      for a brief time until the transfer is complete. Refresh your browser for an updated
      view of the transferred item.
4. To transfer multiple templates:
   1. Select the check box for each template that you want to transfer.
   2. Select More > Transfer Ownership.
   3. Select the new owner for the templates and select
      Transfer.

      The transfer is initiated. The templates may remain in the original owner's
      account for a brief time until the transfer is complete. Refresh your browser for an
      updated view of the transferred items.

title: Users and Groups Management
text:
Read about how administrators manage individual users, groups of users, and permission profiles for an account through the Users and Groups section.

As an account administrator, you can manage individual users, groups of users, and permission profiles in the Users and Groups section.

Note: Docusign CLM enabled accounts manage users and groups for the account through your Docusign organization settings. Contact your Docusign organization administrator to manage users and groups at the organization level. For more information, see [Managing Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=fza1583359190504.html&bundleId=rrf1583359212854&_LANG=enus) and [Managing Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=jni1583359185567.html&bundleId=rrf1583359212854&_LANG=enus).

title: Share Access with Other Users
text:
Learn how administrators manage shared access to envelopes among users.

As an administrator, you can allow users of an account
to share access to envelopes with other users on the account.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Users.
2. Locate the user that you want to manage sharing settings for, and select Actions > Manage Shared Access.
3. Select Share Access with Others.
4. Select one of the following permissions, and select
   Next:

   - Allow to manage on behalf of [User Name]: Allows
     the selected user to view envelopes, view history, download files, and
     move envelopes into folders.
   - Allow to edit on behalf of [User Name]: In
     addition to the manage permissions, allows the selected user to correct,
     resend, and void the user's envelopes.
   - Allow to send on behalf of [User Name]: In
     addition to the manage and edit permissions, allows the selected user to
     send envelopes, edit drafts, resend, void, and correct in addition to
     managing on the user's behalf.

   Note: Depending on your account plan, you might not have
   access to all permissions.

   ![The screenshot shows Select Permissions for Shared Access.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Share_Access_with_Other_Users_1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUyMTEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Rlami54MP26pStbOjUXJG_ApgTfQvs5cAJHsEzbYycE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows Select Permissions for Shared Access.")

   Users that do not have sending permissions can only be granted
   access to manage another user’s envelopes. Inactive users or users pending
   activation cannot be granted access and cannot have their envelopes accessed
   by other users.

   The send permission allows others to send and manage envelopes on
   behalf of the user, whereas the manage permission allows others to manage
   envelopes on behalf of the user but not send.
5. Select the start and finish dates to control the duration of the shared
   access.

   The start date can be the current date or any future date. An end date is
   optional and can be any date equal to or after the start date. If an end date is
   specified, the access will expire on that date without any manual action needed.
   If you do not specify an end date, the access continues until someone manually
   removes it. ![The screenshot shows Set Duration for Shared Access.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Share_Access_with_Other_Users_2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUyMTEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Rlami54MP26pStbOjUXJG_ApgTfQvs5cAJHsEzbYycE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows Set Duration for Shared Access.")
6. Select Next.
7. Select the users for the shared access permission.

   To locate users more easily, use the Search option. You can select up
   to 100 users to share access to a user's envelopes.

   If users are not available for selection, those users have shared access. To
   update their permission, remove their current access.

   You can view which users have shared envelopes access to this user. This is
   helpful if you want to create similar access using the shared access
   feature.

   ![The screenshot shows Select Users for Shared Access.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Share_Access_with_Other_Users_3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUyMTEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Rlami54MP26pStbOjUXJG_ApgTfQvs5cAJHsEzbYycE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows Select Users for Shared Access.")
8. Select Save.

The users you selected have shared access to the envelopes of the
current user.

title: Delete Signing Brands
text:
Learn how administrators delete signing brands from accounts when the brand is not
needed.

As an administrator, you can delete a signing brand from an
account when the brand is no longer needed.

1. From the Accounts view, select Brands to
   view the Signing tab.
2. Locate the brand that you want to delete.
3. Select the menu icon and select Delete.

The selected brand is permanently removed from your
account.

title: Agreement Rules Logs
text:
Read about the logs used by account administrators to track the actions triggered by
agreement rules. The logs show the conditions specified for the rule and the actions that
resulted from its use. Logs are an important tool for support teams when
troubleshooting.

The Agreement Rules LOGS page tracks the actions that are triggered
by the rules. The logs contain information about the conditions set for the rules and
actions that were taken.

A log is created each time an agreement rule results in an action whether it is
successful or not. If an envelope triggers multiple rules, there is a log for each
action that results from the evaluation of those rules. Similarly, if a rule has
multiple actions, there will be multiple log entries.

You can view details about a rule, such as the envelope ID and request ID, from the
View details option in the Actions
menu. These details help support teams troubleshoot issues with actions.

![The screenshot shows an example of the agreement rules log details.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/aamanage3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.9JlWta9amNQX6LYj5A-HGPl3uWIwRC-nlYgz4U5TT-k&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the agreement rules log details.")

title: Recipient Identification Using Bank Logins
text:
Read about how recipients can use their bank logins to verify their
identity.

In addition to passports and driver licenses, recipients can use their bank logins to
verify their identity for access to envelopes. For example, recipients who need to
identify themselves can choose between a bank login, a driver license, a passport, or
KBA.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations
text: Learn how account administrators add configurations for identity verification.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations
text:
Japanese customers, note that you can set up this configuration to store the back of IDs, including Japan's National Identity Card (My Number Card). If you implement this setup, ID Evidence will store the 12-digit individual number, which is personally identifiable information (PII). Instead, you may use the Docusign ID Verification with Japanese IDs configuration. This ID Verification method respects data privacy by only allowing storage of the front of Japanese Individual Number Cards.

As an account administrator, you can add a configuration to accounts to verify the identity of recipients. By default, each account must have at least one configuration for identity verification. You can add configurations as needed.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity Verification.

   The Identity Verification screen opens.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration screen opens.
3. Choose an Identity Verification method and select Next.

   Note: The default verification method is ID Verification. To add other methods, contact Docusign customer support.

   The Add ID Verification page opens.![The screenshot shows the ID Verification configuration screen](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/idverification.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU3MDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.ebFOZuGw5M5xc2Ph3BFIUx-O8FNBwwamTnvq5EZxsms&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the ID Verification configuration screen")
4. Enter a name for your configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   Note: The name and description are not translated. Senders see the text in the language it is written.
5. Select the maximum number of verification attempts. This number represents how many times recipients can attempt to verify before the envelope is locked. If recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their identity, by default, the envelope is locked and users must contact the sender to unlock it.
6. Select one of the levels of variation for name acceptance standards when matching the name of the recipient with the name in the ID:

   - Maximum variation: Allows additional variations of middle names, suffixes, equivalent names and tolerates minor typing mistakes. This is the default selection. The Maximum variation option is not compatible with the IDV - DS EU Advanced signature type.
   - Moderate variation: Allows additional variations of middle names and suffixes.
   - Minimal variation: Allows minor variations of first and last names only.

   Note: For more information about this setting, see [ID Name Matching](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=rhb1657542911402.html&_LANG=enus).
7. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations
text:
Name Matching](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=rhb1657542911402.html&_LANG=enus).
7. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.

   If you select Every access or Every access until role is completed, you can turn on Allow SMS authentication after one successful Photo ID or Passport verification.
