The recipient provides a mobile phone number to complete this type of authentication. This option is required if you want to use manual review and the IDV - DS EU Advanced signature type in the same configuration.
8. (Optional) In the Accepted IDs section, uncheck the Accept all recommended ID types, issuing countries and regions and select Manage if you want to customize the list of countries and regions from which IDs are accepted. Select Save in the Manage accepted IDs screen to update your list.
9. (Optional) In the Stored data from IDs section, select the additional data that you want ID Evidence to store for each type of ID verification and transaction. Depending on your configuration, types of identity verification may include Passport and Photo IDs, Electronic ID, and Knowledge-Based Authentication (KBA). By default, only non-personally identifiable information (non-PII) is stored. A copy of the ID itself is not stored. For more information about how and where data is stored, see the  [ID Evidence](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=mrc1578885288021.html&_LANG=enus)  page.

   Attention: If your configuration authorizes the use of Japanese Individual Number Cards and allows storage of data on the back of IDs, ID Evidence will store the 12-digit individual number showing on the back of Japanese Individual Number Cards. This data is personally identifiable information (PII) and considered sensitive. To avoid this situation, either unselect storage of the back of IDs or remove the Japanese Individual Number Card from the list of Accepted IDs for your configuration. Alternatively, use the Docusign ID Verification with Japanese IDs configuration. This ID Verification method respects data privacy by only allowing storage of the front of Japanese Individual Number Cards.
10. In the Other settings section, select the action to take when recipients are unable to verify their identity after the maximum automatic verification attempts.

    Note: You can choose what happens when the maximum number of verification attempts are reached. The setting Manually review the ID document allows senders to examine the ID document and either accept or reject it instead of locking the envelope. In most cases, the sender completes the manual ID review. An administrator can also navigate to the envelope and select Review ID to complete the review if necessary.
11. Select how recipients submit their IDs, either by photo capture or file upload or by photo capture only.
12. Select Add.

The configuration appears in the configurations list and is available for senders.

title: Update Your Account Name and Address
heading_path: Update Your Account Name and Address
text:
Learn how administrators change the name of an account and the address associated with
it.

The account name is a global value for every user on your Docusign
account. The account name displays in notification emails to recipients and in the account
drop down menu. This value can only be changed by an account administrator.

1. From the Accounts view, select Account
   Profile. ![The screenshot shows Account Profile in the Account view.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Account-Profile-menu-item.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.rHQM6jTKpDUFANiVkRUJApZkbvmi2QPiA6tl4WatguU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows Account Profile in the Account view.")
2. To edit your account name:
   1. Select Edit next to your current account name.

      The Change Account Name widow opens.
   2. In the Change Account Name window, enter the new value, and
      select Change Name.![The screenshot shows the Change Account Name window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Edit-Account-Name-window.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.rHQM6jTKpDUFANiVkRUJApZkbvmi2QPiA6tl4WatguU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Change Account Name window.")
3. To edit your account address:
   1. Scroll down to the Account Address section at the end of the Account Profile
      page.
   2. Change the values for the address fields as needed.

      All fields with a red asterisk (\*) must be completed.

      ![The screenshot shows the Account Address fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Edit-Account-Address.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.rHQM6jTKpDUFANiVkRUJApZkbvmi2QPiA6tl4WatguU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Account Address fields.")
   3. Select Save.

The changes are saved.

title: Configure Signature Adoption Options
text:
Learn how administrators configure the format for signature adoption.

As an administrator, you can configure the format for
signature adoption.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signing
   Settings.
2. In the Signature section, select the Signature Adoption
   Configuration link.
3. Set the signature adoptions options described in the following topics.
4. Select Save to save your changes.

title: Resend Activation Emails to Pending New Users
heading_path: Resend Activation Emails to Pending New Users
text:
Learn how account administrators resend activation emails to users with a status of
Pending.

As an administrator, you can resend activation emails to users
with a status of Pending.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Use the filter to locate users with a status of Pending.
3. Select Actions > Resend Invitation.
4. (Optional) For additional security, enter an access code. Provide the code to the user,
   as it is required for account activation.

The user receives an email with activation instructions for the
account.

title: Set Default Email Notification Preferences for New Users
heading_path: Set Default Email Notification Preferences for New Users
text:
Learn how administrators set default notification preferences for new users, including
signers and senders.

As an account administrator, you can set default notification
preferences for new users.

1. From the Accounts view, select Email
   Preferences.
2. Select the User tab.
3. Review the list of email preferences and make changes as needed.
   1. For Signers. This list presents the notifications that your
      account members will receive when they are the recipient of an envelope. This affects
      the Recipient Notifications list in the user's My Preferences
      > Notifications settings.
   2. For Senders. This list presents the notifications that your
      account members will receive when they are the sender of an envelope. This affects the
      Sending Notifications list in the user's My Preferences >
      Notifications settings.
4. Select Save.

Your default notification preferences are saved. Users added to
your account inherit the default email notification preferences. Users can change these
notification preferences at any time using their My Preferences
settings.

title: Signature Adoption Configuration
text:
Read the signing options administrators can specify for an account through the Signature
Adoption Configuration view. For example, administrators can specify signing modes, limit
signature styles, and specify a minimum pixel size for drawn signatures.

The Signature Adoption Configuration view is used to set the signing options for the account.
From this view, account administrators can specify which signing modes are available, limit
signature styles for adopting a signature, lock recipient name for signing, and set the
minimum pixels required to adopt a drawn signature.

Note: Your account may be configured to provide only a subset of the three possible
signing methods (select style, draw, or upload image). In that case, only the methods
supported by your account can be configured in the signature adoption configuration view.

title: Add and Copy Permission Profiles
text:
Learn how account administrators add permission files. To save time, administrators can
create a new permission profile by copying and modifying the settings from an existing profile.

As an administrator, you can add permission profiles or
create new ones from existing profiles.

1. From the Accounts view, select Permission
   Profiles.
2. Select Add Permission Profile.

   Tip: You can select Actions > Copy to create a new permission profile that is a copy of an existing set. The
   copied permission profile contains the same settings as the original permission profile.
   You can modify the name and settings as needed using the following steps.
3. Enter a name for the permission profile.
4. Adjust the settings as needed. See the [Permission
   Profile Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mfo1583277360424.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the administrator and user permissions that administrators use to define the actions available to account users that are assigned to the Permission Profile.") topic for information about each setting.
5. To finish and add the permission profile, select Add.

The permission profile is added to the account.

title: Document Sources
text:
Read about how administrators specify the sources, such as Dropbox or Google Drive, that account members can use when they add a document to an envelope or template.

As an account administrator, you can select the cloud storage providers that can be used when account members add documents to an envelope or template. The sources include Box, Dropbox, Google Drive, and OneDrive.

Account members view the selected document sources in the Upload menu when they add documents to their envelopes and templates.

Important:

This setting only changes the user interface behavior for showing an upload platform in the dropdown list when a user uploads a document in an envelope or a template. Turning off one of the options, such as Google Drive, does not prevent users from uploading a document from that document source by accessing information through the API. A malicious user or attacker accessing the API can bypass this setting and upload documents even if a specific document source is excluded. Be aware of any suspicious files being uploaded if you have turned off any of these settings.

title: Templates
text:
Read about how administrators configure the way templates are used, such as whether they are required for an envelope or can be uploaded by users.

These settings configure template usage for the account.

- Require a template for all envelopes sent from this account: This setting is available for all accounts except for Simplified Sending accounts. When selected, users will not be able to send envelopes without selecting a template. The Send an Envelope option will not appear in the sending experience. The user can still send an envelope without a template through the API.
- Enable template upload: This setting is only available for Simplified Sending accounts. When selected, users will be able to upload templates.
- Enable Agreement Template Builder: This setting is currently available as part of a limited release for customers who have purchased the Document Generation add-on for eSignature. When selected, the Agreement Template Builder is part of the template creation flow. The Agreement Template Builder allows users to edit template documents directly in Docusign eSignature. For more information, see the [Agreement Template Builder for Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=jct1697745471160.html&_LANG=enus) topic. This setting is enabled by default.

title: Document Retention Permission Access Levels
text:
View the level of access administrators can grant through the Document
Retention permission in combination with the Users and
Groups access levels.

The following table describes the level of access provided by the Document
Retention permission in combination with each of the
Users and Groups access levels.

| Administrative area | Page | Level of access: View Only | Level of access: Edit Groups Only | Level of access: Edit Users & Groups | Level of access: Edit Users, Groups, & Administrators |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N/A | Overview | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Users | View | View | Edit[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=eiv1661542707163.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_1) | Edit[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=eiv1661542707163.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_2) |
| Users and Groups | Permission Profiles | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Groups | View | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Users and Groups | Signing Groups | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Signing Settings | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Sending Settings | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Identity Verification | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Email Preferences | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Email Archive Configuration | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Custody Transfer | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Document Retention | Edit | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Signing and Sending | Legal Disclosure | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Reminders and Expirations | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Comments | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Document Custom Fields | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Envelope Custom Fields | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Audit Logs | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Bulk Actions | View | View | View | View |
| Auditing | Validations | None | None | None | None |

[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=eiv1661542707163.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_1) Edit access does not include the ability to edit users with "Allow All"
administrative permissions enabled, such as those with the DS
Admin permission profile.

[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=eiv1661542707163.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_2) Edit access does not include access to Shared Access or
Shared Envelopes unless the
Envelope Sharing permission is also
selected, and does not include access to Transfer
Envelopes or Transfer
Templates unless the Transfer
Envelopes permission is also
selected.

title: Signature Pads
text:
Read about the additional hardware and software that is required to use signature pads
for signing. The signing process is different with various signature pads.

The Docusign Signature Pad option extends signing functionality to signature
pads for Docusign customers. The Docusign Signature
Pad option supports Topaz Systems, Inc™ and ePadLink™ ePad- ink™ signature pad
devices.

title: Signature Pads
text:
To use signature pads, customers must meet certain hardware and software requirements. These
requirements are in addition to the standard [Docusign minimum system requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Subscription-Service-Specifications?language=en_US).
The additional requirements include:

- A Topaz Systems, Inc or ePadLink ePad-ink with IntegriSign™ signature pad
  device compatible with the computer system used for signing. Refer to the
  signature pad manufacturer’s documentation for any additional
  requirements.
- The Signature Pad Allowed option is enabled and the appropriate
  signature pad type is selected by Docusign for your
  account. For more information about selecting your signature pad type, see
  [Options for Envelope Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=tfs1583277354215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the settings that administrators use to define the actions required for recipients of envelopes. For example, administrators can specify whether a reason is required when users decline to sign and specify the types of devices that can be used for signing.").
- Supported browsers for the Topaz device:

  - Google Chrome
  - Firefox
  - Microsoft Edge

    Topaz SigPlusExtLite extension is required for Chrome, Firefox, and Microsoft Edge. See
    the Topaz Systems [Topaz SigPlusExtLite User Installation
    Guide](https://www.topazsystems.com/software/sigplusextlite_userinstall.pdf) for details.
- Supported browser for the ePadLink device:

  - Google Chrome
  - Firefox
  - Microsoft Edge

title: Signature Pads
heading_path: Example of signing with ePadLink ePad-ink with IntegriSign™
text:
This example shows signing on a specific ePadLink ePad-ink signature pad device. Each
signature pad device can be different.

1. The signer selects a Sign or Initial
   field.

   The Adopt Your Signature window opens.

   ![Select Start Capture.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Start-Capture.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU0MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.bsbAUwcdqYeJDBhmUW6Q42I68edtz1BCkfn04hcR0io&_LANG=enus "Select Start Capture.")
2. The signer selects Start Capture.
3. The signer uses the signature pad device to draw the signature or initials, and selects
   OK.

   ![Draw the signature on the signature pad.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Use-the-signature-pad-device-to-draw-the-signature.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU0MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.bsbAUwcdqYeJDBhmUW6Q42I68edtz1BCkfn04hcR0io&_LANG=enus "Draw the signature on the signature pad.")
4. The signer selects all remaining signature or initial fields, and selects
   Finish to finish signing.

title: Bulk Add New Users
text:
Read about how organization administrators can bulk add new users to one or more
accounts.

Note: This guide is for Docusign
organization administrators who oversee multiple accounts. For administrators of
individual accounts, see the [Add and Update Users in
Bulk](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mxm1583277431469.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators add or update multiple users simultaneously through the Bulk Add and Bulk Update options. To perform bulk actions, administrators upload comma-separated value (CSV) files that contain updates or new user information.") topic.

Docusign organization administrators with the Administrators and Users
Administrators permission profiles can bulk add new users to one or more accounts by
uploading a comma-separated value (CSV) file. The format of the CSV must match the [sample file](https://admin.docusign.com/static-resources/organization-user-import.csv). For more information about the CSV format, see
[Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wec1593211169952.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can use a CSV file to add multiple users.").

You can add up to 2,000 users to an account and include up to 50 accounts per
imported CSV. The maximum number of users per import is 8,000.

Note: Only one type of import or export can be in
progress at a time.

title: Specify Post-Signing Destinations
text:
Learn how administrators can configure a destination URL that users see when they finish
signing. A URL can be configured for a variety of situations, such as Finish
Later, Decline, and Session
Timeout.

Note: These features are temporarily
disabled for account plans purchased on the web after November 20, 2024. For more
information, contact Docusign Support.

As an administrator, you can
configure a destination URL that users see when they finish signing. A URL can be configured
for a variety of situations, such as Finish Later,
Decline, and Session Timeout.

1. From the Accounts view, select Brands to
   view the Signing tab.
2. Locate the signing brand that you want to configure, and select the action menu and
   select Edit.
3. In the Advanced Configuration section, select Destination URLs.
4. In the Configure Destinations dialog box, add a URL for each action you want to
   configure.

   ![The screenshot shows the Configure Destinations window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/configuredesinationsforsigningbrand.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.qzxTbt9L2-9usGPv6UNrmeZqnn-IMHWV2yCtFS716Bw&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Configure Destinations window.")
5. (Optional) Select the Insert Merge Field menu and select a field
   to add to the destination URL.![The screenshot shows the Insert Merge Field dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Merge-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.qzxTbt9L2-9usGPv6UNrmeZqnn-IMHWV2yCtFS716Bw&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Insert Merge Field dropdown.")
6. (Optional) To reset any destination to the Docusign default, simply delete your custom entry in the field.
7. To save your changes, select Save.

title: Activate Identity Verification Configurations﻿
text:
Learn how account administrators activate identity verification
configurations.

You can reactivate a previously deactivated Identity
Verification configuration to make it usable again.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. Search the configuration you want to activate and select
   Activate in the Actions
   column.
3. Select Activate in the confirmation message.

The configuration is now activated and can be used
again.

title: Identity Verification Configurations
text:
Read about how account administrators configure identity verification for senders to use
to verify the identity of recipients. Administrators can specify verification service providers,
the countries and regions from which IDs are accepted, and the signature type associated with
each configuration.

Administrators can create and manage identity verification configurations that senders use to
verify the identity of recipients. Administrators can specify the verification service
provider, the countries and regions from which IDs are accepted, and the signature type
associated with each configuration. They can also determine which, if any, ID evidence data is
stored by Docusign.

Note: For more information for senders and recipients
using ID verification, see [ID
Verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=zfu1578456528197.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Try an Add-On for Free
text:
Learn how to enable a free trial of an add-on to your subscription.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Go to the add-on section, and select Learn More.

   A window opens with trial add-on information.![The screenshot shows the Try Identity Verification window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/addonstrial.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NTksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.-tILA48YriVZb7hrf3sd_3e3gg9jqCb3zx_C5Sr9-Dc&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Try Identity Verification window.")
3. Select Start Free Trial.

   The window confirms that the add-on is enabled.
4. Select Get Started.

The add-on is enabled in your account. Usage information displays
in the add-on section on the Plan and Billing page.

Note: Enabling
the add-on service can take up to one minute. If you do not see your add-on enabled,
refresh your browser.

title: Transfer of Envelopes and Templates to Account Users
heading_path: Transfer of Envelopes and Templates to Account Users
text:
Read about the ways account administrators can transfer envelopes and templates from one
account member to another.

Transferring envelopes and templates is useful when you close a user, or a user's role in
your company changes and you want to assign responsibility for their Docusign items to another account member.

You may have someone who creates all of your account templates. Once a template has been created, you may want to transfer ownership to someone else on your account for ongoing maintenance. Anyone with whom a template is shared can edit or delete it. Administrators can edit or delete all account templates, regardless of ownership.

Administrators can transfer envelopes and templates between users from either the Users page or the Custody Transfer page. They can also transfer items directly from within their Docusign account, using the Shared Envelopes interface to transfer envelopes, and the Templates page to transfer or delete templates.

Note: For more information about deleting templates
from within your Docusign account, see [Delete Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=prv1578456606781.html&bundleId=xry1643227563338&_LANG=enus).

title: Turn Off KBA in a New Identity Verification Configuration
heading_path: Turn Off KBA in a New Identity Verification Configuration
text:
Learn how account administrators can turn off KBA when creating a new
configuration.

When you are creating a Identity Verification configuration, you can turn off KBA if you want
recipients to identify themselves using methods other than KBA.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.

   The Identity Verification screen opens.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration screen opens.
3. Choose the ID Verification method and select Next.

   The Add ID Verification screen opens.
4. Enter a name for your configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   Note: The name and description are not translated. Senders
   see the text in the language it is written.
5. Select the maximum number of verification attempts.

   This number represents how many times recipients can attempt to verify before
   the envelope is locked. If recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their
   identity, by default, the envelope is locked and users must contact the sender
   to unlock it.
6. Select one of the levels of variation for name acceptance standards when
   matching the name of the recipient with the name in the ID:

   - Maximum variation: Allows additional variations
     of middle names, suffixes, equivalent names and tolerates minor typing
     mistakes. This is the default selection. The Maximum variation option is
     not compatible with the IDV - DS EU Advanced signature type.
   - Moderate variation: Allows additional variations
     of middle names and suffixes.
   - Minimal variation: Allows minor variations of
     first and last names only.

   Note: For more information about this setting, see [ID
   Name Matching](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=rhb1657542911402.html&_LANG=enus).
7. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.

   If you select Every access or Every access
   until role is completed, you can turn on Allow SMS
   authentication after one successful verification. The recipient
   provides a mobile phone number to complete this type of authentication.
8. In the Accepted IDs section, uncheck the
   Accept all recommended ID types, issuing countries and
   regions option and select Manage.

   The Manage accepted IDs screen opens.
9. Type “United States” in the search field.

   The Manage Accepted IDs screen displays the
   "United States" entry.
10. Uncheck the KNOWLEDGE-BASED AUTHENTICATION column.
11. Select Save.

    The Add ID Verification screen opens.
12. (Optional) In the Stored data from IDs section, change
    the data default selection.
13. Select Add.

ID Verification saves your new configuration.

title: Assign Groups to Stamps
text:
Learn how administrators assign groups to a stamp.

As an administrator, you can assign groups to stamps.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Stamps.
2. Select Actions > Users and Groups.
3. Select the Groups tab.
4. Select Assign Groups.
5. Select the groups that you would like to assign access to this stamp.
6. Select Assign.

title: Edit Stamps
text:
Learn how administrators edit stamps.

As an administrator, you can edit stamps associated with your
account.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Stamps.
2. Choose the stamp row and select Actions > Edit.
3. Make any changes to the stamp name, height, and resize setting.
4. Select Save.

title: Docusign Account Settings Overview Page
text:
Read about how the Docusign account settings Overview page helps
account administrators find information to help them manage accounts. From the Overview page,
administrators can find users, access account settings, and links to information.

The Overview page helps account administrators to find what they need and stay up to date.
You can search for users and account settings to quickly locate information. The Overview page
also includes links to information resources, such as Docusign University
and the Support Center.

Note: Delegated administrators only see content
available to them based on their permission profile. For example, a user with the Account
Settings delegated permission will not see the Find a User search box and will only be able to
access content related to account settings. For more information, see [Delegated Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=gih1583277347533.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators assign limited administrator capabilities through the Delegated Administration functionality.").

title: Document Retention
text:
Read about the document retention policy and how account administrators can change it to
fit their business needs.

With a document retention policy, you can set the number of days to retain completed,
declined, and voided documents. Setting a document retention period is optional. By default,
Docusign securely retains all customer documents on the service for as
long as the account is active.

When an account is closed, Docusign may retain all sent, signed, and voided envelopes for a period of approximately two years from the date of account closure to meet legal, compliance, auditing, or security obligations. This retention period may be further extended if legally required.

If you choose to set a retention period, any affected envelopes are placed in a [purge queue](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=rlk1578456343945.html&_LANG=enus) prior to actual purging. After expiration of the purge queue period, the Docusign system deletes the
documents in the envelopes.

The maximum retention period that you can set is 9,999 days.

Note: Users can read [Purge Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=edo1578456348066.html&bundleId=oeq1643226594604&_LANG=enus) for information on purging.

Note: For more information, read the [Best Practices for Docusign Document Retention and Purging](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/km-guide-best-practices-for-docusign-document-retention-and-purging)
whitepaper.

title: Provide Feedback in Docusign Community
text:
Read about the ways that you can provide feedback to Docusign to help
us meet your needs.

Docusign is committed to your success. Connect with the
[Docusign
Community forum](https://community.docusign.com/ideas) to ask questions, provide feedback, and share best practices with other community members.

title: Assignment of Users and Groups to Stamps
heading_path: Assignment of Users and Groups to Stamps
text:
Read about how administrators assign users or groups to stamps.

After you create one or more stamps, you can assign users or groups to them. When users or
groups of users receive a document with a stamp field, their assigned stamps are available for
them to use.

title: Envelope Delivery
text:
Read about delivery options administrators can specify for the delivery of envelopes,
including whether completed documents are attached to the completed email sent to senders and
signers.

These settings are used to set the envelope delivery options for recipients.

- Attach documents to completion email: When selected all
  the completed documents are included in the completed email sent to senders and signers as
  PDF file attachments. To include the Certificate of Completion along with the documents, you
  must also select the setting Attach certificate of completion to
  envelope.

  Note: If sending or
  requesting sensitive information such as PII data during the signing process, we recommend
  that you opt to not attach signed documents to the completion emails that get sent out to
  all recipients. We recommend that you consult with your legal and privacy team to
  determine the appropriate level of security for the account.
- Attach certificate of completion to envelope: When
  selected, when an envelope is downloaded as a Combined PDF, the Certificate of Completion is
  included at the end of the file. (The Certificate is always included with the Separate PDF
  download option.) If the setting Attach documents to completion email
  is also selected, the Certificate is included as a separate attachment to the completed
  email that is sent to senders and signers.
- Suppress emails to embedded signers: When selected, an
  email completion notice is not sent to embedded signers when envelope signing is completed.
  This option is only used with the embedded signing function and cannot be selected if the
  Send completion emails to embedded signers option is selected.
- Send completion emails to embedded signers: When
  selected, an email completion notice is sent to embedded signers when envelope signing is
  completed. This option is only used with the embedded signing function.
- Allow account users to decide if they should send a link or attach a
  PDF: When this option is selected, the Deliver self-signed email
  notifications by option can be set in user [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") and will override the account setting. It also allows Docusign mobile application users
  to set the email option used when sending from the application.
- Deliver self-signed email notifications by: This option
  selects the default account setting for how self-signed documents (documents sent using the
  Sign a Document option) are presented to the email recipients. This setting also affects
  self-signed documents sent from Docusign mobile applications. There are two possible
  selections:
  - Link: A secure link to the self-signed documents is included in
    the email. Email recipients select the link to navigate to the completed documents,
    where they can view and download the documents.
  - PDF: A PDF of the completed documents is attached to the
    email. Email recipients can view and download the attached documents.

title: Connect
text:
Get started with Docusign Connect.

Docusign Connect sends real‐time updates as event messages or webhooks to other applications. Administrators can choose which events to follow and use Connect messages to start downstream processes. Connect is available at the account level and the organization level. If you have Organization-Level Connect, you can create configurations that track activity across multiple accounts.

For more information about Connect, see the following topics:

- [Create a Connect Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=xwi1583277389681.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Configure Connect for eOriginal](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=vxi1583277394106.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Create a Recipient Connect Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=vtj1621962575657.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Webhooks](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/webhooks/)

Connect is available at the account level and the organization level. If you have Organization-Level Connect, you can create configurations that track activity across multiple accounts.

title: Connect
text:
- Connect sends updates about sent envelopes.
- [Recipient Connect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=gxb1621981308410.html&_LANG=enus) sends updates when a user on your account is a recipient and a specific event occurs.

You can also use one of the prebuilt integrations. See the following topics for details:

- [Connect for eOriginal](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=tsn1583277394951.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Docusign Connect for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=voz1581360675739.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Connect for Box (legacy)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=ysr1583277392059.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Connect for OneDrive (legacy)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=oic1583277393502.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Set Draft Envelope Retention Periods
text:
Learn how account administrators modify the retention policy for draft envelopes.

You can modify the draft envelope retention policy to specify the number of days that draft
envelopes are retained. After the number of days has passed, the drafts are deleted
from the system without further notification.

1. From the Accounts view, select Document
   Retention.
2. Select the Envelope Retention Policy tab.
3. Select Set the draft envelope retention policy for this
   account.
4. In the Keep for this many days field, enter the number
   of days between 30 and 120 to keep draft envelopes before sending them to the
   purge queue. The default value is 30 days.
5. Select Save.

title: Delegated Permissions
text:
Read about how account administrators assign limited administrator capabilities through
the Delegated Administration functionality.

As a Docusign account administrator, the Permission Profiles page lets you
manage the user permission profiles for your account. The Delegated Administration
functionality allows administrators to create Permission Profiles that have limited
administrator capabilities in addition to the standard user permissions.

Note: For a more in-depth discussion of use cases,
review the [Best Practices for eSignature Delegated Administration](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/km-guide-best-practices-for-esignature-delegated-administration/) white paper.

Note: If your account does not have Delegated
Administration enabled, the Permission Profiles dialog box only shows User Permissions. Refer
to [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") for more information on User
Permissions.

Delegated Administration is available only for certain plans. If you do not
see the functionality described in this guide, contact Docusign Support to
determine if it can be enabled for your account.

title: Manage Envelope Sharing Among Users
text:
Learn how administrators manage envelope sharing among users. Administrators can allow
specific users to access envelopes that belong to other users on an account and allow users on
an account to access a specific user's envelopes.

As an administrator, you can allow users of an account to
share envelopes with other users on the account. Specifically, you can allow a selected user
to access the envelopes of other users, or you can allow other users to access the selected
user's envelopes.

Note: The options on the Actions menu for
Share envelope with a user and Share user’s
envelopes are now on the Manage Shared Access page. Shared Envelopes is now in
maintenance mode and will no longer be updated.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Locate the user that you want to manage sharing settings for, and select the
   Actions menu.

   Note: If the Manage Shared Access option displays in the Actions
   menu, select Manage Shared Access and review the information
   message at the top of the page. You can select either Share envelopes with
   user or Share user's envelopes from here.
3. If you have Shared envelopes only, select one of the following options from the
   Actions menu:

   - **Share envelopes with user**: This option grants the selected user access to the
     envelopes of other users.
     1. Select Add Users on the Envelopes shared with
        [user name] page.
     2. Select the users whose envelopes you want to share with the selected user.
   - **Share user's envelopes**: This option grants access to the selected user's
     envelopes to other users.
     1. Select Add Users on the Envelopes shared by
        [user name] page.
     2. Select the users who can access the envelopes that belong to the selected user.

   Note: To locate users more easily, use the
   Search and Filter options. You can
   select up to 100 users at a time. To select all users that are listed in the search
   results (up to 100), use the select all option at the top of the list.
4. Select Assign.

The users you selected are displayed.

title: Account Administrator Notifications
text:
Read about how account administrator notifications provide important information to
administrators to help them stay current with product updates and account-related information.

You can view the account administrator notifications. These notifications provide
just-in-time communications on important account health information, product release
notifications, and Docusign news and events.

Notifications remain in the list for 90 days, and then they are removed. You can select
individual notifications to view their details. You can filter the notifications by selecting
a type, such as Account Health, Product Update, or Training and Learning. You also can filter
the notifications by selecting a date range, such as the last 24 hours, 7 days, 30 days, or 3
months.

![The screenshot shows the Overview page in the Accounts view.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/accountadmin01.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4NjksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.m38AnO7wLMhn5M_vcfxupPO2OfehfKnl19DohAQ7KjM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Overview page in the Accounts view.")

title: Transfer of Envelopes
text:
Read about how administrators transfer sent envelopes to other users on the
account.

As an administrator, you can transfer your own sent envelopes from within your Docusign account view, and you can transfer envelopes belonging to other account members. To transfer
another user's envelopes, their envelopes must first be shared with you. See [Manage Shared Access to Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=imk1656607611659.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators manage settings for shared access to envelopes among users.") and [Envelope Sharing Between Account Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=uzg1583277438725.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators permit users to see and take action on envelopes that belong to other users on the account.") for details on how to share envelopes.

title: Claim a Domain
text:
Learn how to prove ownership of a domain as part of claiming the domain for an
organization.

1. From the Organization view, select Domains.
2. Select Add Domain.
3. Enter the domain name, for example, fontara.com.

   If another organization has claimed this domain, an error message displays.
   ![The screenshot shows the Add Domain page and the Domain Name field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/adddomainfont.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU5NDgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.AHierVRQMTarmQm8yLmVAU2fi1THmHBG-T-WP6e19nI&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Add Domain page and the Domain Name field.")
4. Select Claim.

   The Verify Domain information page opens.
   ![add-domain-font2](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/adddomainfont2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU5NDgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.AHierVRQMTarmQm8yLmVAU2fi1THmHBG-T-WP6e19nI&_LANG=enus "add-domain-font2")
5. Copy either the CNAME record or TXT record to your domain name server (DNS). You might
   need to work with your organization's IT administrator to add this to your DNS.
6. Outside of Docusign Admin, update your domain’s DNS entry with either a CNAME record or
   a TXT record:

   **To create a CNAME record in your DNS (recommended)**

   1. Navigate to your domain's DNS record management page.
   2. Add a new CNAME record.

      Note: This step is not to modify your
      corporate domain’s apex. This step is to add a subdomain using a CNAME resource
      record pointing to verifydomain.docusign.net. Most DNS vendors ask for the value
      below.
   3. **Host Name/Alias:** Input your subdomain value using the 32-digit GUID. Example:
      2cacbbaa-e1b9-4b82-a86e-68d539d865da

      Note: If you are using the
      Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN), add the domain at the end of the GUID. Example:
      2cacbbaa-e1b9-4b82-a86e-68d539d865da.**fontara.com**
   4. **Destination Data/Target/Alias To/Canonical Name:** Input where traffic should
      be directed. Example: verifydomain.docusign.net.

      Note: The
      trailing "." at the end of the domain is required.
   5. **TTL:** Default or 1 hour / 3600 seconds

   **To create a TXT record in your DNS**

   1. Navigate to your domain's DNS record management page.
   2. Add a new TXT record.
   3. **Name:** @ or \*
   4. **Text:** TXT token from step 5 - Example:
      docusign=2cacbbaa-e1b9-4b82-a86e-68d539d865da
   5. **TTL:** Default or 1 hour / 3600 seconds

   Note: The process of
   updating DNS entries varies by vendor. You might need to coordinate with your network
   administrator to make this change. It may take up to 72 hours for DNS changes to
   propagate. Coordinating ahead of time will ensure timely deployment of Single Sign-On.

   You can confirm that your changes are active with the steps
   outlined in [Additional Information for Claiming Domains](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=uqb1583359141519.html&_LANG=enus "Read about additional information for organization administrators who are claiming domains.").
7. After the DNS changes are active, return to your organization, and select
   Domains.
8. In the same row for the domain, select Actions > Verify DNS.

   Docusign checks if the generated tokens are part of the DNS record.
   If successful, the domain status changes to "Active".

Your domain ownership is proven.

title: Edit the Default Legal Disclosure
text:
Learn how to modify the default legal disclosure. Administrators can edit the legal
disclosure contents to include their company information.

As an account administrator, you can edit the legal disclosure
contents to include your company information.

1. From the Accounts view, select Legal
   Disclosure.
2. In the Configure a Disclosure section, edit your Account Name as
   needed. This is the company information shown to recipients in the disclosure.

   Optionally, you can select to have your account name, as shown in the [Account Profile](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=lva1583277327185.html&_LANG=enus "Read about administrators can update and maintain an account name and address of record in the Account Profile."), automatically updated to be the
   same as the account name you just entered.
3. Update the text fields in the disclosure as needed. Review the question above each
   field and enter or select the appropriate information for the field.

   The maximum number of characters for the text fields are:
   - Email address your customer should use if they want to request paper copies of their
     documents? = 100 characters.
   - If your customer requires paper copies how will you collect those fees? = 255
     characters.
   - Customer consent withdrawal Email contact = 100 characters.
   - Customer consent withdrawal Phone contact = 20 characters.
   - Customer consent withdrawal Mail contact Address line 1 = 100 characters.
   - Customer consent withdrawal Mail contact Address line 2 = 100 characters.
   - Customer consent withdrawal Mail contact City = 50 characters.
   - Customer consent withdrawal Mail contact Postal Code = 20 characters.
   - If your customer decides to withdraw their consent, what other information will you
     need? = 255 characters.
   - Are there any special consequences of your customer withdrawing their consent to
     receive electronic records from you? = 255 characters.
   - If your customer needs to change their email address, where should they send the
     change request? = 100 characters.
   - Enter any other information you require from your customer if they change their
     email address. = 255 characters.
4. Select Save.

title: Use a Brand Company Name in the Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure
heading_path: Use a Brand Company Name in the Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure
text:
Learn how account administrators customize the legal disclosure with a company name. The
company name is used in email notifications and in the Signing experience.

As an administrator, you can customize the legal disclosure
with a company name to be used in email notifications and in the signing
experience.

1. From the Accounts view, select Legal
   Disclosure.
2. With your ERSD enabled, select the option Use brand company
   name.

   ![Use the brand company name in the ERSD.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Enable-Brand-Company-Name-in-ERSD.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1NzAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.AdD6lAZ9x-jxU6fYHUePfCSJ2OGUDhdFc4iIkQqp_Bw&_LANG=enus "Use the brand company name in the ERSD.")
3. Select Save.

   Your settings are saved. Whenever an envelope is sent from the account that uses a
   signing brand with a specified Company Name, the value is used in email notifications and
   in the Signing experience. If the signing brand does not specify a Company Name, the
   Account Name is used instead.

title: Add Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text: Learn how to add Risk-Based Verification configurations to an account.

title: Add Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text:
You must fulfill the following prerequisites to enable risk-based identity verification:

- You must have full account administrator or delegated permissions
- You activate Risk-Based Verification for Docusign accounts hosted
  in the United States

As an administrator, you are in charge of adding a Risk-Based Verification configuration
to your account. Risk-Based Verification automatically assigns the appropriate
identity verification relevant for each recipient based on a risk assessment.

Docusign delegates the assessment of each participant's risk score to
the Socure trusted partner. You cannot change the criteria the trusted partner
considers when assessing the risk score. However, you can
choose the identity verification method for each risk profile. You can also
select the signals to reject recipients from a transaction due to their strong
correlation with fraudulent activity. For more information about the
risk-based identity verification settings, read the [Risk-Based Verification Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=njf1750155136303.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Risk-Based Verification (RBV) and how you can configure it.") page.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity Verification.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration screen opens.
3. Select Risk-Based Verification.

   The Edit risk-based verification page opens.
4. Enter a name for the configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   The system does not translate the name and description. Senders see the text in
   its original language.

   The Risk assessment section shows recipients' data
   that the trusted partner analyzes to determine the risk levels. You cannot
   customize the assessment criteria. Select Learn more in
   the Risk assessment section for more information about
   those criteria.
5. Select the identity verification method that you want to
   associate with each risk profile in the Risk levels and identity
   verification methods section.

   For more information about risk profiles, select
   Learn more in the Risk levels and
   identity verification methods section or read the [Risk-Based Verification Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=njf1750155136303.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Risk-Based Verification (RBV) and how you can configure it.") page.
6. Select the reasons for rejecting recipients from the identity verification flow
   in the Reject Recipients section.

   For more information, read the [Risk-Based Verification Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=njf1750155136303.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Risk-Based Verification (RBV) and how you can configure it.") page.
7. Select the data to store in ID Evidence for each recipient applying to Risk-Based Verification.
8. Select Add

   The configuration displays in the configurations list and is available
   for senders.

title: Delete Custody Transfer Rules
text:
Learn how account administrators delete custody transfer rules when they are not
needed.

You can delete custody transfer rules when they are not
needed.

1. From the Accounts view, select Custody
   Transfer.
2. Locate the rule that you want to delete.
3. Select Actions > Delete.
4. Select Delete to confirm the change.

The rule is deleted.

title: User List Exports
text:
Read about the type of user information that you can export.

Note: This guide is for Docusign
organization administrators who oversee multiple accounts. For administrators of
individual accounts, see the [User Bulk Actions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mxm1583277431469.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators add or update multiple users simultaneously through the Bulk Add and Bulk Update options. To perform bulk actions, administrators upload comma-separated value (CSV) files that contain updates or new user information.") topic.

Docusign organization administrators with the Administrators and Users
Administrators permission profiles can export a list of users across all accounts in the
organization as a comma-separated value (CSV) file. Exports include user details such as
full name, email address, and permission profile.

Export types:

- Users and Memberships: All users and their memberships across
  all accounts in the organization.
- Domain Users: All domain users, their profile details, default
  account, login policy, which login feature they have enabled, and last login
  timestamp.
- External Domain Users: All domain users memberships in
  accounts external to the organization.

Note: Organizations without
a claimed domain will see only the Users and Memberships export option.

title: Delete Groups
text:
Learn how administrators delete groups from an account when they are no longer
needed.

As an administrator, you can delete groups from accounts when
the groups are not needed.

1. From the Accounts view, select Groups.
2. Find the group that you want to delete.

   Note: You cannot delete
   the Everyone and Administrators groups.
3. Select Actions > Delete.
4. Confirm that you want to delete the group from the account.

The group is removed from the group list.

title: Reset the Default Docusign Sending Brand
heading_path: Reset the Default Docusign Sending Brand
text:
Learn how administrators reset a sending brand to the default Docusign sending brand.
This procedure discards all sending brand customizations.

You can revert the sending brand for your account to the default Docusign
sending brand at any time. When you revert to the default brand, any sending brand
customizations you made are discarded.

1. From the Accounts view, select Brands, and
   select the Sending tab.

   Your current sending brand configuration is displayed.
2. Select Edit Your Theme to open the brand customization view.

   ![The screenshot shows the Revert to Default button in the Customize a Sending Brand window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/sending-brand-reset.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNTUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.qDkROJ1o8skwq7AYa9ZcoaYrOoCElMEuOKANRtgVIbU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Revert to Default button in the Customize a Sending Brand window.")
3. Select Revert to Default, and select
   Reset to confirm.

The sending brand for your account is reset to the
Docusign default branding.

title: View Users Assigned to Permission Profiles
heading_path: View Users Assigned to Permission Profiles
text:
Learn how administrators view the users that are assigned to permission profiles.

As an administrator, you can view the users that are
assigned a specific permission profile.

1. From the Accounts view, select Permission
   Profiles.
2. Find the permission profile that you want to view.
3. Select Actions > View Users for the permission profile.

A list of users assigned with the selected permission profile is shown.

title: Delete Signing Groups
text:
Learn how account administrators delete signing groups when they are no longer needed.

Deleting a signing group prevents it from being used in the future. It does not affect any in progress envelopes sent to the group. In progress envelopes will still be accessible to group members even if the group is deleted. To remove access to these members, [void](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=vks1578456426150.html&_LANG=enus) the envelope.

If a signing group was used in a template, it is replaced with an empty recipient.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signing Groups.
2. Locate the signing group that you want to delete by searching or viewing the list.
3. Select Actions > Delete.
4. Confirm that you want to delete the group from the account.

   The group is removed from the Signing Groups list.

title: Minimum Pixel Size
text:
Read about the importance of administrators specifying a minimum pixel size for
signatures.

To ensure that drawn signatures and initials are visible on completed documents, set a
reasonable minimum pixel size. This minimum is applied to drawn signatures only, and does not
apply to uploaded images.

title: Add Email Archive Addresses
text:
Learn how account administrators add addresses to the email archive.

As an account administrator, you can add addresses to
the email archive.

Remember: An account can
only have a maximum of five active or pending email archive addresses at one time.

1. From the Accounts view, select Email Archive
   Configuration.
2. Select Add Email.
3. In the Email field, type the email address and select
   Add.

   Docusign sends a validation email to the email address with a link
   to activate the email address. If the link is not selected, the email address remains in a
   pending status and email copies are not sent to the email address.

   Tip: If the validation email
   is deleted or lost, you can close the pending email address and then reactivate it to
   send a new validation email.

The following events will trigger a Docusign email notification which will also be sent to the archive address:

- In Session Recipient Envelope Signed
- Failed Envelope Vaulting
- Recipient Envelope Complete
- Recipient Envelope Complete In Person
- Envelope Activation Failed
- Envelope Activation (this includes reminder and expiration emails)
- Certified Delivery Notification
- Reassign Signer
- In Session Recipient Envelope Viewed
- Envelope Corrected
- Fax Received
- Envelope Voided
- Change Signer
- Voided Fax Received
- Recipient Add Access Code
- Fax Received Wet Sign
- Carbon Copy Notification
- Envelope Declined
- Purge Envelopes
- Document Markup Activation
- Agent Notification

title: Access Account Administration Settings from Your Docusign Account
heading_path: Access Account Administration Settings from Your Docusign Account
text:
Learn how to access account administration settings from your Docusign account.

If you are an account administrator, you can use the Admin tab to navigate to your account administration settings.

1. Log in to your Docusign account as an administrator.

   Your account opens. The top navigation bar includes the Admin tab. ![The screenshot shows the Admin tab in the top navigation bar.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/access-acct-admin1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.a_S6cXnJvnsw5xhV46S8h9lXjH6bP3dbZB9jG5w3sBQ&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Admin tab in the top navigation bar.")
2. Select the Admin tab in the top navigation bar.

   The Overview page opens in the Accounts view. You can return to your Docusign account by selecting Home on the top navigation bar.![The screenshot shows the Overview page in the Accounts view when you have an organization and one or more linked accounts.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/access-acct-admin2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.a_S6cXnJvnsw5xhV46S8h9lXjH6bP3dbZB9jG5w3sBQ&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Overview page in the Accounts view when you have an organization and one or more linked accounts.")

   If you have an organization and one or more linked accounts, you can use the left tabs to navigate to the organization or the other accounts.

   If you have only one account and no organization, the navigation bar on the far left does not appear. In this case, you only have access to one account in your Accounts view. ![The screenshot shows the Overview page in the Accounts view when you have one account and no organization. The navigation bar on the far left does not appear.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/access-acct-admin3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.a_S6cXnJvnsw5xhV46S8h9lXjH6bP3dbZB9jG5w3sBQ&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Overview page in the Accounts view when you have one account and no organization. The navigation bar on the far left does not appear.")

title: Add Capacity for an IAM Plan
heading_path: Add Capacity for an IAM Plan
text:
Learn how to add feature capacity to your IAM plan.

As an administrator of an existing IAM plan, you can add feature capacity to your account.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

   The Subscription and Billing page
   opens.
2. In the Feature and extension usage section, review
   available features, feature use status, and quantity of feature remaining.![The screenshot shows the Feature and extension usage section on the Subscription and Billing page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/IAM-feature-and-extension-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MDUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.A3ldasmvoX7JaH1Jx9gNkjBFzsx0TMOQcFuGVClB64Y&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Feature and extension usage section on the Subscription and Billing page.")
3. Select Add [feature name] or Upgrade
   Plan in the Actions column for the feature that you want to add
   capacity.

   The feature purchase window opens.
4. Choose the quantity of the feature that you want to purchase.
5. Select Purchase.

   The confirmation window opens.
6. Select Done.

Additional feature capacity is purchased and added to your
account.

title: Copy Identity Verification Configurations
text:
Learn how account administrators can copy configurations for identity
verification.

You can add a configuration to your Docusign account by copying an existing configuration.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. Search the configuration you want to copy and select Create a
   copy in the Actions column:
3. Make any changes to the configuration as needed.
4. Select Add to save the new configuration.

The new configuration is created and is now active.

title: Edit or View Signing Groups
text:
Learn how administrators change the name of a signing group and add or delete members.

Changes to a signing group do not affect envelopes that have already been sent and are still in progress, only future envelopes sent using the group. In-progress envelopes are accessible to all group members included at the time of sending. You can edit a group to change the name or modify the list of group members.

- **If you remove a group member**, the sender must [void](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=vks1578456426150.html&_LANG=enus) any in progress envelopes for the group in order to remove access to the member.
- **If you add a group member,** the sender must [resend](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=hqt1578456337100.html&bundleId=oeq1643226594604&_LANG=enus) any in progress envelopes for the group in order to grant access to the new member.

If group members have changed their email address, you'll need to edit the signing group to remove the old instance and then add in the new one.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signing Groups.

   You can view the list of signing groups, the number of users in each signing group, and the signing group ID. The signing group ID is a unique identifier for the group. This identifier is useful for API developers using the Docusign API.
2. Locate the signing group that you want to edit or view by searching or viewing the list.
3. Select ACTIONS > Edit.
4. When editing a signing group, you can:

   - Change the signing group name.
   - Assign users to the signing group.
   - Remove users from the signing group.
5. Select Save to save your changes.

title: Administrator Permissions
text:
Read about how account administrators assign limited levels of access to account users
through permission profiles.

These determine the administrative actions available for all account users assigned to the
permission profile.

By selecting Allow Some, you can further tailor a permission profile
to meet the needs of your users. Provide users with the level of access required for their
roles while limiting access to other administrative tools.

![Delegated Permissions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Delegated-Permissions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUyNjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.7dwu4VyLxbg37mSnKPAcuztpX5dpEvSF83emQcVBS0Y&_LANG=enus "Delegated Permissions.")

- Allow All: When selected, users with this permission have access to
  the Administration view for the account and can manage account settings, users and groups,
  signing and sending settings, the document retention policy, and integrations. The default
  permission profile DS Admin uses this option. Default permission profiles cannot be
  modified.
- Allow Some: These alternative administrator options grant limited
  administrative rights. Delegated administrators are restricted to the categories selected
  and limit a user’s access to certain administrator functions. The options are as follows:
  - Users and Groups: When selected, also need to select the level
    of access to grant users with this permission. At least the View
    Only level of access is required to enable Shared
    Access or Envelope Transfer.
    - Users with View Only access can view all users,
      administrators, and groups in the account. They won't be able to make any changes to
      these users or groups.
    - Users with Edit Groups Only access can view all users,
      administrators, and groups in the account. They can manage groups but not users.
      They can add, rename, and delete groups, add users to a group, and remove users from
      a group.
    - Users with Edit Users and Groups access can view all users,
      administrators, and groups in the account. They can add, edit, or close
      non-administrator users. They can perform bulk user actions to add or update
      non-administrator users, and can download users. They can also add, rename, and
      delete groups, add users to a group, and remove users from a group.
    - Users with Edit Users, Groups, and Administrators access
      can view all users, administrators, and groups in the account and can add, edit, or
      close any user. They can perform bulk user actions to add or update users, and can
      download users. They can also add, rename, and delete groups, add users to a group,
      and remove users from a group.

title: Administrator Permissions
text:
user. They can perform bulk user actions to add or update users, and can
      download users. They can also add, rename, and delete groups, add users to a group,
      and remove users from a group.

      Note: In an eSignature account
      linked to a CLM account where the delegated account administrator is granted
      permission for Edit Users, Groups, and Administrators, the
      delegated account administrator can't edit users and groups through the Docusign
      account administration user interface because the account is linked to CLM.
      Delegated account administrators with this permission are able to edit users, user
      permission profiles, and groups using the Docusign Admin API. They can also bulk
      add users through the API. For more information about CLM administrators, see
      [Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM
      and CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=cjc1583426236868.html&_LANG=enus).
  - Shared Access: When selected, users with this permission can
    set and modify user envelope sharing settings. Because users with this permission can
    change information for other users, selecting Shared Access also
    automatically selects the Users and Groups setting with
    View Only access.
  - Envelope Transfer: When selected, users with this permission
    can transfer envelope and template custody from one user on the account to another.
    Because users with this permission can change information for other users, selecting
    Envelope Transfer also automatically selects the
    Users and Groups setting with View Only
    access.
  - Document Retention: When selected, users with this permission
    can enable, disable, or modify the document retention policy for the account. Users with
    this permission profile can also use the targeted purge feature to add individual
    envelopes to the purge queue.
  - Account Settings: When selected, users with this permission can
    manage account settings, signing and sending settings, and integrations (excluding
    Connect configuration). Users with this permission profile cannot manage users, groups,
    or security settings.
  - Reporting: When selected, users with this permission can access
    the reporting dashboard for account-wide data. From there, they can run, customize,
    schedule, and download and print reports for the account.
  - Security: When selected, users with this permission can manage
    all items within the Security Settings area. This includes items, such as account login
    requirements and password security settings.
  - Signing Groups: When selected, users with this permission can
    create, edit, and delete signing groups.
  - Connect: When selected, users with this permission can manage
    all of the Connect configurations and settings in the UI and in the API. Delegated
    administrators for Connect require the ability to send envelopes in their permission
    profile so that they can send out the envelope event notification Connect webhooks.
  - Stamps: When selected, users with this permission can upload
    stamps and assign users to the stamps.

**Example: Select multiple permissions to tailor access**

You can select multiple permissions to create any combination of access you want. For
example, you might create a permission profile that includes permissions for Shared
Access, Signing Groups, and
Stamps. Your delegated administrator with this permission profile can
add, change, or delete items in these areas.

title: Administrator Permissions
text:
a permission profile that includes permissions for Shared
Access, Signing Groups, and
Stamps. Your delegated administrator with this permission profile can
add, change, or delete items in these areas.

In this example, when you select Shared Access and
Stamps, the Users and Groups setting changes
to the minimum access level of View Only. If you want your delegated
administrator to be able to edit groups, you can select Edit Groups
Only. Your delegated administrator with this permission profile can view users
and can edit groups. They cannot edit user information. Because you did not select
Account Settings or Security Settings, this
delegated administrator cannot view or change any settings in these areas.

**Levels of access**

Permission settings might not allow delegated users to access to all of the page settings
within an administrative area. For those page settings that delegated administrators can
access, administrators can either view or edit page settings. If the permission setting does
not allow a delegated administrator to view or edit page settings, the page settings are not
visible in the administrative console.

Levels of access for administrative areas are designated as either
Edit, View, or
None:

Edit
:   Users can view and make changes to page settings for an administrative area.

View
:   Users can view but not alter the page settings for an administrative area. Users may
    also be able to download information that is visible to them.

None
:   Users do not have the ability to either view or edit settings for the administrative
    area.

title: eSignature Value Calculator
text:
Read about how administrators estimate the value of an eSignature implementation with
the eSignature Value Calculator. The calculator uses benchmark data to derive a value per
envelope to help administrators optimize their use of Docusign eSignature.

The Docusign eSignature Value Calculator estimates the current and potential
value of your Docusign implementation. Docusign
regularly partners with customers of all sizes across multiple industries and use cases
to gather usage data and develop benchmarks. This benchmark data represents aggregated
values for a selected industry and use case. You can modify the estimate by selecting an
estimation approach to best match your company's current or future use of Docusign.

The Value Calculator displays in the Account section in your Docusign account
settings. Contact Docusign Support if you do not see the Value
Calculator option.

Note: All estimates are based on aggregated customer data. They do not indicate or guarantee
past or future benefits. All currency data is provided in United States Dollars
(USD).

![The screenshot shows the value calculator benchmark data section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/dfa.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.X_JlL_-5_QM0bJbCPFT1wxEsVa9lgkYbu2ucFcVIwsg&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the value calculator benchmark data section.")

Docusign provides three types of benchmark data:

- Hard Cost Savings per Document: Each document sent with Docusign reduces the operational costs of printing, distributing,
  and storing hard copies. This is an estimate of the operational costs eliminated
  when using Docusign.
- Productivity Improvements per Document: Docusign
  increases efficiency in a variety of areas. This efficiency translates to improved
  productivity for your company. This is the estimated time saved by expediting the
  creation, processing, and storage of documents. The dollar value displayed
  represents the average value at that time.
- Revenue Gains per Document: When using Docusign in a
  customer facing role, the reduction of time to completion and the increase in close
  rates translates to increased revenue. This value represents the potential revenue
  gained by using Docusign with your Customers.

  Note: Revenue gains per document are only
  available for customer-facing, revenue generating use cases.

Once you've selected your industry and use case, Docusign can estimate your value per
envelope. This estimate is a combination of the benchmark data that shows the average
value of each document sent with Docusign.

You can adjust the number of documents sent per month to estimate the value Docusign can
bring to your company.

![The screenshot shows the estimated total value section in the value calculator.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/afds.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.X_JlL_-5_QM0bJbCPFT1wxEsVa9lgkYbu2ucFcVIwsg&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the estimated total value section in the value calculator.")

title: Automate Common Post-Signature Tasks With Agreement Actions
