heading_path: Automate Common Post-Signature Tasks With Agreement Actions
text:
Read about how account administrators use Agreement Actions to create and manage business rules to determine what happens to completed envelopes. The rules can trigger actions, such as archiving to a cloud storage application, starting a Docusign CLM workflow, and exporting data to a spreadsheet.

Note: Agreement Actions is not included with all account plans. If you do not see Agreement Actions, it may not be enabled for your account. Contact Docusign Support for assistance.

With Agreement Actions, account administrators can automate common post-signature tasks when an envelope is completed. You can create agreement rules based on document custom fields, template type, or envelope data.

The rules can trigger actions, such as archiving to a cloud storage application, starting a Docusign CLM workflow, and exporting data to a spreadsheet.

Note: For more information about CLM workflows, see [Agreement Actions in CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=trr1605121275463.html&_LANG=enus).

Before automating post-signature tasks in an external application, you must connect your application to Docusign. Connected applications for agreement actions are managed in Docusign App Center. The Docusign App Center provides administrators with a single, secure place to install, approve, and manage all connected apps, now referred to as connections of an installed app. Using the Docusign App Center simplifies setup, strengthens governance, and ensures consistency across Docusign products like eSignature, Workflow Builder, and Agreement Manager. For more information on creating connections and managing connected apps, see the [Use Docusign App Center to Manage App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=con5c7c664f-0f3a-482b-89d9-0766a9544b4c.html&_LANG=enus) topic. Agreement action rules are managed in account administration as described in the [Create and Manage Agreement Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=btt1594673504460.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators create agreement rules to determine actions for completed envelopes. Actions include Archive to, Start workflow, and Export to spreadsheet.") topic.

title: Download a Sample CSV File for the Add Users Bulk Action
heading_path: Download a Sample CSV File for the Add Users Bulk Action
text:
Learn how administrators download a sample comma-separated value (CSV) file they can use
to upload users in bulk. The pre-formatted sample CSV file can save administrators time by
eliminating the need to create and format a file. Administrators populate the sample file
with the new user content and upload it to the Docusign
account.

To save time preparing the comma-separated value (CSV)
file needed to upload users in bulk, you can download a sample file. The sample CSV file
is pre-formatted for the bulk add operation and ready to be populated with your new user
information.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Users.
2. Select Bulk Add.
3. Select Download Template in the Add
   Users dialog box.

The sample file is downloaded and available for you to
populate with your new user information.

title: Assign Brands to Groups
text:
Read about how administrators assign brands to groups for group members to use when they
send envelopes or create templates.

If your account includes multiple signing brands, you can use the groups functionality to
manage which brands are available to your Docusign account members.

For the custom groups you define for your account, you can assign brands to specify the ones
that group members can use. Group members can use the available brands when they send
envelopes or create templates.

Note: You cannot assign brands to the two default groups: Administrators
and Everyone. Account administrators have access to all brands configured for the account,
regardless of the groups they are assigned to.

You can simplify and control brand usage in your account by limiting brand access by groups.
In most cases, each user likely needs to use only a single brand for their Docusign envelopes.
But you may have defined multiple brands to support various divisions or teams within your
company. By assigning brands to groups, and assigning users to those groups, you can control
the brands used to send envelopes and create templates.

**Example scenarios for users and groups**

Here are the possible scenarios for users and groups, and how it affects the brands
available:

- **User is not assigned to any groups**. The user uses the default signing brand for all
  envelopes he sends and any templates he creates.
- **User is assigned to a group that does not have any assigned brands**. The user uses
  the default signing brand for all envelopes he sends and any templates he creates.
- **User is assigned to multiple groups, which all have assigned brands**. The user has
  access to all of the brands assigned to all of the groups to which he belongs. He can select
  which brand to use for each envelope he sends or template he creates.
- **User is an administrator on the account**. Account administrators have access to all
  brands configured for the account, regardless of the groups they are assigned to.

title: Buy an Add-On
text:
Learn how to purchase an add-on to your subscription.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Buy for the add-on.

   A window to purchase the add-on for your subscription opens. ![The screenshot shows the Add SMS delivery to your subscription window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/addons3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.zEUOq6leQtBQixYBRXCgbbjoLAKmcSwbc3o3Y8Kj8Oc&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Add SMS delivery to your subscription window.")
3. Enter an amount to purchase using your payment information, and select
   Purchase.
4. Select Done on the purchase confirmation.

The add-on is enabled in your account. Usage information displays
in the add-on section on the Plan and Billing page.

Note: Enabling
the add-on service can take up to one minute. If you do not see your add-on enabled,
refresh your browser.

title: Add-Ons to Your Subscription
text:
Read about how administrators can trial and buy add-ons to subscriptions.

Note: Free trial and purchased add-ons are currently only available to some
customers in a limited availability release.

Note: This feature is available only for Standard or Business Pro
eSignature plans that were purchased online.

Add-ons provide additional features that you can add to your account subscription.
Currently available add-ons include SMS Delivery and Identity
Verification.

Purchases of add-ons or increased amounts in an add-on are processed immediately.
Refunds are applied as credits in your next billing cycle. To remove a purchased add-on,
contact Docusign Support.

On the Plan and billing page, you can view the following information for
add-ons in your account:

- New add-ons available for a trial include a Learn More
  link.
- Add-ons that are in a trial mode include a Trial label next
  to the add-on name. Trials include a limited amount of free usage before a
  purchase is required.
- Add-ons that have been purchased show subscription amounts and costs
  for the add-on.
- Each add-on shows an amount for usage.

Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

![The screenshot shows the add-ons in the Plan and billing page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/addons.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.sntNvwBR58xiKu_YDZrwi45WhZ_G19rJPQhTLy_ECFY&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the add-ons in the Plan and billing page.")

title: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing for Applications
text:
Read about using Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) in custom
applications.

Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) is an HTTP-header-based mechanism that allows
web browsers to request resources from other origins. Enabling CORS allows applications
running in a browser to directly access the Docusign eSignature REST
API without requiring a backend server.

As a developer, you can configure your application to use CORS. In the Apps and
Keys page, you can specify allowed origin URLs from which your app can make CORS
requests. In the CORS page, administrators can enable or disable CORS permissions for
individual applications. Administrators can also set CORS to be enabled or disabled by
default for all new applications.

For information on using CORS, see the following topics in the Developer
Center:

- [Single-page applications and CORS](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/single-page-applications-cors/)
- [Enable or disable CORS for
  apps](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/single-page-applications-cors/enable-disable-cors/)
- [CORS configuration](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#cors-configuration)

Read more about CORS in these developer blog articles:

- [CORS is here!](https://www.docusign.com/blog/developers/cors-here)
- [OAuth for CORS applications](https://www.docusign.com/blog/developers/oauth-cors-applications)

For general information on CORS, see [Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS).

For more information about developing Docusign integrations, see [Build a Docusign integration](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/build-integration/).

title: Customize Header and Footer Links
text:
Learn how administrators customize the links that recipients see in the documents. The
customized links replace the default Docusign links.

Note: These features are temporarily disabled for account plans
purchased on the web after November 20, 2024. For more information, contact Docusign Support.

You can customize the links that display when a recipient opens documents to view and sign.
The links can point to web pages or mailto: email addresses. If you do not customize these
links, or if you delete previously saved customizations, the links use the default Docusign links.![The screenshot shows the Close button and the My Document menu with the default links.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/examplehidingsigningelements.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNzEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.N8QjpJlgwSzqXCLU6ZvodOlYYd7gA8idajZUGnLFMNU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Close button and the My Document menu with the default links.")

1. From the Accounts view, select Brands to
   view the Signing tab.
2. Locate the signing brand that you want to configure, and select the action menu and
   select Edit.
3. In the Advanced Configuration section, select Header and Footer
   Links.
4. (Optional) To hide the following elements in a signing brand, select the
   Hide Button toggle for the link or button:

   - About Docusign link in the Actions
     menu
   - Close button shown on a completed document
   - Help & Support link shown in the
     Actions menu
   ![The screenshot shows the links that you can customize for a Signing brand.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/The-screenshot-shows-the-links-you-can-customize-for-a-Signing-brand.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNzEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.N8QjpJlgwSzqXCLU6ZvodOlYYd7gA8idajZUGnLFMNU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the links that you can customize for a Signing brand.")
5. To customize the Help & Support link, enter your text and link into the
   Help & Support field.
6. In the Customize Links page, you can add up to three links to the footer by specifying
   text and URLs for Footer Link 1, 2, and 3.
7. (Optional) To hide a footer link, select the Hide Link
   toggle.

   If the footer link is blank, with no text or URL, it will not display to your users.
   Using the Hide link option is useful if you have completed the link information, but you
   want to hide it without deleting the information.
8. Select Save.

Your link changes are saved.

title: What the Signer Sees When Using Stamps
heading_path: What the Signer Sees When Using Stamps
text:
Read about how users interact with stamps.

**Opening the envelope**

When a recipient opens an envelope that contains a stamp, they see the stamp field in the
location where it was placed.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/stampsigningfield.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwODAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.B_MPWDRlZw2H4WV3DiZs1v7dZUtRwa4ySvU1kY0sU0o&_LANG=enus)

**Clicking on the stamp**

Clicking on the stamp field displays a dialog box where the recipient can either choose an existing stamp or assign a new one.

**Selecting a stamp**

The recipient can select a stamp and select DONE to apply that stamp
to the document.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/stampssigningselect.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwODAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.B_MPWDRlZw2H4WV3DiZs1v7dZUtRwa4ySvU1kY0sU0o&_LANG=enus)

The stamp is applied to the document.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/stampstimedatesigning.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwODAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.B_MPWDRlZw2H4WV3DiZs1v7dZUtRwa4ySvU1kY0sU0o&_LANG=enus)

**Stamp options**

The recipient can modify the stamp before completing the envelope through several
options.

- **Rotate** - Rotate the stamp.
- **Custom Date** - Change the date on the stamp (only for **Name + Date**
  format stamps). The date must be in 'YY.MM.DD format.
  ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/stampschangedate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwODAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.B_MPWDRlZw2H4WV3DiZs1v7dZUtRwa4ySvU1kY0sU0o&_LANG=enus)
- **Create New** - Replace the stamp with a new one.
- **Choose From Existing** - Replace the stamp with an existing stamp.
- **Remove** - Remove the selected stamp.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/stampssigningoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwODAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.B_MPWDRlZw2H4WV3DiZs1v7dZUtRwa4ySvU1kY0sU0o&_LANG=enus)

If the recipient selects either the **Create New** or **Add New** option in the
**Existing Stamps** dialog box, a new dialog box displays to generate a new stamp.

title: Manage Shared Access to Envelopes
text:
Read about how administrators manage settings for shared access to envelopes among
users.

Shared access offers users the ability to send and manage envelopes on behalf of
another user on the account. Administrators can select the Manage Shared
Access option to manage which users have access to the envelopes of a
specific user.

While the existing shared envelopes options allows managing envelopes on behalf of
other users, shared access also allows users to share draft envelopes, move the shared
envelopes, create custom folders, and view which users have access to the envelopes.

You can also view the active or inactive status of the access you have granted, the permissions applied for each user, and the start and end dates of access for each user.

Note: The options on the Actions menu for Share envelope with a user
and Share user’s envelopes are now on the Manage Shared Access page. Shared envelopes is
now in maintenance mode and will no longer be updated. Contact your account
representative for more information.

For auditing which users have access to other users' envelopes, you can monitor events in the
Audit Logs page. The Audit Logs page shows a log entry for every time the shared access
settings are changed in an account. For information about shared access events in audit
logs, see [Shared Access Events in Audit Logs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wjq1669418756360.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators view changes to shared access settings for users in an account."). For more information about audit logs,
see [Audit Logs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=vit1583277397519.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the types of changes to accounts that are captured in the audit logs. Account administrators can view changes to account settings, billing information, plan type, users, and more. They can filter the information by date range and download the selected information to a comma-separated value (CSV) file for further processing.").

![Manage Shared Access to Envelopes](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Manage_Shared_Access_to_Envelopes.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUyMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.cpvTGRAa2oja9x6E0wZNUyoz0gNAIR8bubotJHIeztg&_LANG=enus "Manage Shared Access to Envelopes")

title: Manage Shared Access to Envelopes
text:
- Sharing with groups: Shared access can be granted by administrators
  from one user to another, but not from a user to a group.
- Sharing with users outside of account: Shared access can only be
  granted to users on the same account, similar to shared envelopes and
  delegated signing.
- Learn about the changes to the sending and envelope management
  experience in [Shared Access to Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=rdu1656546423934.html&_LANG=enus).
- If you need to share access to envelopes of a closed user, you can transfer the
  envelopes to yourself or to another active user and then access the envelopes.
  For more information, see [Transfer Envelopes and Templates Between Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=xyt1583277373629.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how administrators transfer envelopes and templates from one user to another from the Users page.").

title: eNote Credentials
text:
Read how account administrators configure their accounts with eOriginal credentials so
that eNotes can be sent.

To configure your account to create and send eNotes, you must add your eOriginal credentials.
With these credentials in place, eNotes sent through your account are tamper sealed and
vaulted in your eOriginal vault.

To send eNotes, you must also create a Connect to eOriginal configuration. See [Connect for eOriginal](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=tsn1583277394951.html&_LANG=enus) for more information.

eNotes are sent for electronic signature using the Docusign REST API. For details about
sending eNotes and eMortgage integration, see the eMortgage Implementation guide.

eNote credentials are managed from the eNote Credentials page in the
account administration settings. From this page, you can add, edit, and delete eNote
credentials.

title: Direct Access to Account Administration Settings
heading_path: Direct Access to Account Administration Settings
text:
Read how administrators can access account administration settings.

You can directly access account administration settings by going to the administrator login
page and entering your credentials at: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin>

To access the Demo environment, use this URL instead: <https://apps-d.docusign.com/admin>

You can directly access areas by using the following links:

Note: If you are not logged in, you will be prompted
to enter your login information. Users with SSO must log in before accessing these links. All
administrators should be able to access the links once logged in.

- Account Profile: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=accountProfile>
- Legal Disclosure: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=legalDisclosure>
- Security Settings: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=securityInfo>
- Brands: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=brands>
- Connect: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=connect>
- Plan and Billing: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=billing>
- Users: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=users>
- Permission Profiles: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=roles>
- Groups: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=groups>
- Sending Settings: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=sending>
- Reminders and Expiration: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=reminders>
- Document Custom Fields: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=customFields>
- Envelope Custom Fields: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=documentLabels>
- Regional Settings: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=regionalSettings>
- Apps and Keys: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=apiIntegratorKey>
- Audit Logs: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=auditLog>
- Payments: <https://apps.docusign.com/admin/authenticate?goTo=payments>

Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

title: Turn Off Account Discovery
text:
Learn how to turn off account discovery so that your eSignature account is not found by
new Free or Trial users in a company’s domain.

As the account administrator, you can turn off account discovery in Security Settings by
disallowing your eSignature account to be searchable. When the setting is turned
off, no Free or Trial users in a company’s domain can view the Account Discovery
screen, and administrators will not receive any account discovery email requests to
join specific accounts from users.

Note: If you are an account administrator for multiple Docusign accounts, make sure that you switch to the appropriate
account before changing any security settings.

1. From the Accounts view, select Security
   Settings.
2. Scroll down to the Account Discovery section at the end of the page.
3. Uncheck Make my account searchable.

Your eSignature account is not searchable for account
discovery.

title: Reactivate Users
text:
Learn how account administrators reactivate closed users. If you are an administrator with delegated administration rights for Users and Groups only, you can close or reactivate only non-administrative users. You cannot manage users with permission profiles that include any level of administrative rights.

As an administrator, you can reactivate closed users. If you are an administrator with delegated administration permissions for Users and Groups only, you can close or reactivate only non-administrative users. You cannot manage users with permission profiles that include any level of administrative rights.

Note: An email address can belong to only one active or pending user. When you attempt to reactivate a user, the system checks to see if the email address is in use. If it is active, you cannot reactivate the user unless you close the user with the active email address.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Locate the closed user whose account you want to reactivate.

   To search for closed users, select the Status filter, and select the  Closed status. Clear the Active and Pending statuses, and select Apply.
3. Select Actions > Reactivate for the closed user that you want to reactivate.

   The system checks to see if the email address is in active use for another user. If the email address is in use, you can view the user profile, but you cannot reactivate the user unless you first close the active user.
4. Review the user profile information and update it as necessary. Select Next.
5. (Optional) Enter an access code for additional security, and provide the code to the user. The user must have the code to reactivate the account.
6. Select a permission profile for the user and optionally assign groups.
7. Select Reactive User.

   The user receives an email with activation instructions for the account.

title: Assign Users to Stamps
text:
Learn how administrators assign individual users to a stamp.

As an administrator, you can assign individual users to
stamps.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Stamps.
2. Select Actions > Users and Groups.
3. Select Assign User.
4. Select the checkboxes next to the users that you would like to assign access to this
   stamp.
5. Select Assign.

title: Delegation
text:
Read about how administrators can allow recipients to delegate other users to sign on
their behalf.

Delegated signing provides the ability to delegate another user to sign envelopes on behalf of
the recipient. Delegates can be set up for both a defined or an indefinite period of
time, and can also be scheduled in advance.

If you select Allow recipients to assign delegated signer, the
recipients can choose someone to sign envelopes on their behalf. You must also select
Allow recipients to change signing responsibility setting in
Recipient options for an envelope with a signer that has an active delegate to be sent
to the delegate.

For more information about using delegated signing, see [Delegated Signing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=bbk1637048103951.html&bundleId=jux1643235969954&_LANG=enus).

title: User Management
text:
Read about how account administrators manage the users in an account through the Users page. You can add, edit, close, reset passwords, and modify sharing for your users.

Note: Docusign CLM enabled accounts add, update, and close users for the account through your Docusign organization settings. Contact your Docusign organization administrator to manage users at the organization level. For more information, see the [User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=jni1583359185567.html&_LANG=enus) topic in Docusign Organization Administration.

As a Docusign account administrator, the Users page lets you manage the users on your account. You can add users on your account in accordance with your plan details. You can view how many active users you have on the [Plan and Billing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=sko1583277326930.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators review and modify subscription and billing information for accounts.") page. Users must activate their account, at which point they become billable users on the account.

Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

For users in the Docusign system, an email address can belong to only one active or pending user. When you add users, the system checks if the address is already in use before creating the new user. If there are one or more closed users only (no active or pending users), you have the option of reactivating one of the closed users or adding a new user to your account. You can view closed users by using the filter menu on the Users page.

If your company recycles email addresses and reuses them for different employees, when you run into the reactivate or add new user option, you should choose to add a new user. This ensures that the new employee does not get access to any envelopes that belonged to the former account member.

Note: If you are an administrator with delegated administration rights for Users and Groups only, you can perform the actions described in this guide for non-administrative users only. You cannot manage any user with a permission profile that includes any level of administrative rights.

title: PDF/A Conversion and Compliance
text:
Read about how administrators can enable PDF/A conversion and compliance for documents
in the account.

PDF/A is an ISO-standardized version of PDF for archiving documents. PDF/A requirements do not
allow the documents to include JavaScript, audio, video, and external fonts. Encryption
is also not allowed. Under the ISO-standard, the PDF/A documents are converted into an
ISO-standard format intended for long-term preservation of the document’s
content.

Administrators can control the conversion of documents to the PDF/A format at the
account level. Administrators can turn on the Enable PDF/A Conversion and
Compliance setting on the Sending Settings page in the account
administration settings. When this setting is enabled by the account administrator, all
new envelope documents uploaded in the account go through PDF/A conversion and
compliance checking against the PDF/A ISO-standard. The documents are available in PDF/A
format when downloaded.

PDF/A conversion includes a validation and repair process. Under this process, the
PDF document is inspected to check if it meets the requirements of PDF/A. If it does
not, it is often possible to repair the document to meet the requirements. For example,
if a PDF document has references to external fonts, the PDF document can be repaired by
embedding the font.

PDF/A “compliance” means that the document is saved in a format that meets the PDF/A
ISO-standard. Specifically, a PDF/A-conforming document contains metadata that
identifies the PDF/A level and the subvariant, such as PDF/A-1a or PDF/A-2u. PDF
documents extracted from a downloaded envelope archive (.zip file) are PDF/A
compliant.

Important:
Docusign disclaims any express or implied warranties that a
converted PDF/A document meets a customer's specific needs.

Only the documents uploaded in the envelopes after the PDF/A setting is enabled go
through PDF/A conversion and compliance. If the setting is turned off, the documents
uploaded in the envelopes do not go through PDF/A conversion and compliance.

For more information about PDF/A standards, see [PDF/A in a Nutshell](https://www.pdfa.org/wp-content/uploads/2013/05/PDFA_in_a_Nutshell_211.pdf).

title: PDF/A Conversion and Compliance
text:
- Downloading combined documents is not recommended. Instead, to preserve the PDF/A conversion
  of individual documents in the envelope, you can download an envelope archive
  (.zip file) or a Portfolio PDF. The envelope archive (.zip file) keeps the
  individual documents intact, whereas a combined document download loses document
  metadata. Currently, using a Portfolio PDF is available only through the API.
  The Portfolio PDF is a container file that contains any embedded files, which
  are kept intact and can be extracted.
- Supported conformance levels include:
  - PDF/A-1b, PDF/A-1a
  - PDF/A-2b, PDF/A-2a, PDF/A-2u.
- PDF/A-3b, PDF/A-3a, PDF/A-3u, PDF/A-4e, and PDF/A-4f are not currently
  supported.
- Documents saved as PDF/A might contain additional metadata and become larger in file size
  since external objects are embedded in the PDF. The appearance of the document
  is not affected.
- During Document Upload, there might be a minor increase in the latency due to
  additional operations for conversion and repairs for PDF/A.

title: Signing Groups Administration
text:
Read about how administrators can set up and manage Signing Groups and alternate emails
for the members of the group. Any member of the Signing Group can open and act on the document
fields addressed to the group.

Note: Signing Groups are incompatible with the Docusign mobile app. Users must sign in at [www.docusign.com](https://www.docusign.com/) to use the
feature. If you do not see Signing Groups, the feature might not be enabled for your account.
Contact Docusign Support to enable this feature.

The Signing Groups option allows account administrators to create and edit Signing Groups. A
Signing Group is used to send documents to a group of people, where any member of that group
can open and act on the document fields addressed to the group, even though signature and
other fields are not specifically assigned to a person.

title: Cancel Your IAM Subscription
text:
Learn how to cancel your IAM subscription, and switch to a free account.

As an administrator of an existing IAM plan, you can cancel your subscription.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

   The Subscription and billing page opens.![The screenshot shows the Subscription and billing page for an IAM Professional account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/IAM-Sub-and-billing-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.UHKbCyQTJ_j3vBnZK8oQT-2Cck84eKXUVyWLMZab9Aw&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Subscription and billing page for an IAM Professional account.")
2. Select Manage Your Subscription > Cancel Plan.

   The survey page opens.
3. Choose a reason for cancellation, and select Next.

   The plan information page opens.
4. Review feature information, and select Next.

   The offer page opens.
5. Review the discount offer. To continue to cancel your subscription, select
   Next.

   The confirm cancellation page opens.
6. Review cancellation and refund information, and select
   Confirm.

Your subscription is canceled on the date shown. Your account is
automatically switched to a free account.

title: Bulk Actions Log
text:
Read about how account administrators use the Bulk Actions Log
to track the progress and results of bulk actions, such as an upload of multiple users and
the transfer of envelopes for an account. Administrators can export the log information as a
comma-separated value (CSV) file.

The Bulk Actions Log provides an easy way for administrators to
track different bulk actions, such as uploading multiple users and transferring
envelopes for their accounts.

As an accoun tadministrator, you can view details about each bulk action job and its
progress. The log displays the type of action, its status, the name of the administrator
who initiated the action, and the date of the action.

From the Actions menu, you can view more details about each action, such as error
messages, and export the log information as a comma-separated value (CSV) file.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/bulkactionslog.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Qvqw1Aq1qPS4vnfmKIbqVVip43BQc1M3JgcLPaD7X4A&_LANG=enus)

title: View Subscription and Usage
text:
Learn how to view your subscriptions, renewal periods, and the status of your product
usage.

As an account administrator, you can view subscription and usage information for
products and add-ons. Usage information includes number of envelopes sent, seats
allocated, and add-on usage. You can also view renewal period information below the
subscription ID.

Note: This feature is available only for eSignature customers who have
purchased plans from a Docusign sales representative.

1. In the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Subscription.
3. In Product Usage, view the amounts used and the total
   amounts purchased for each product.![The screenshot shows the Product Usage section of the Subscription and usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Sub-and-usage-SSVC.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.c71i6L28XGGyviycVO8SbmbRUP-9h74Idfz-t8slyBI&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Product Usage section of the Subscription and usage page.")
4. Select Purchased Products to view the quantity of
   products and add-ons purchased in your subscription.![The screenshot shows the Purchase Products tab on the Subscription and usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Sub-and-usage-tab-SSVC.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.c71i6L28XGGyviycVO8SbmbRUP-9h74Idfz-t8slyBI&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Purchase Products tab on the Subscription and usage page.")
5. (Optional) Depending on your account type and renewal period, select
   Manage Renewal or Contact
   Sales to increase your amounts.

   Renewal period information displays below the subscription
   ID.

title: Identity Verification for FINTRAC Compliance
text:
Read about how adminstrators can use the Docusign ID Verification for FINTRAC compliance
configuration to maintain compliance requirements for financial institutions in
Canada.

The [Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada](https://fintrac-canafe.canada.ca/intro-eng)
(FINTRAC) is the financial intelligence unit and anti-money laundering and
anti-terrorist financing supervisor in Canada.

FINTRAC regulations and compliance apply to organizations in Canada that are
part of any type of financial transaction. Examples include banks, credit unions, and
insurance companies. For a complete list of institution types, see the [Who is this guidance for](https://fintrac-canafe.canada.ca/guidance-directives/client-clientele/Guide11/11-eng) article on the
FINTRAC website.

If you are performing identity verifications as one of the applicable institution
types in Canada, you can use the FINTRAC Compliance configuration to outline the FINTRAC
requirements. The Docusign FINTRAC configuration using Identify
Liveness includes all of the mandatory fields required for ID Evidence to comply with
FINTRAC requirements. By using the FINTRAC configuration, your identity verifications
should meet the FINTRAC compliance requirements.

title: Identity Verification for FINTRAC Compliance
heading_path: Your Responsibility for Retaining FINTRAC Information
text:
FINTRAC requires that the financial institution or organization retains the
information relating to the identity verification checks for a minimum of five
years. You are responsible for storing the information locally within your company’s
control. Docusign is not responsible for assuring you have complied with this
retention requirement. You can save the related data by downloading a PDF or using
the API to collect the data and store it outside of Docusign for the required time
period. FINTRAC requires that you keep records so that they can be provided to
FINTRAC within 30 days of a request. For more information, see the [What are my responsibilities when maintaining
records?](https://fintrac-canafe.canada.ca/guidance-directives/recordkeeping-document/record/fin-eng#s2) article on the FINTRAC website.

title: CSV File Format for User Additions
heading_path: CSV File Format for User Additions
text:
Read about the format required for the comma-separated value (CSV) file that
administrators use to upload new users in a bulk action.

As an administrator, you can add multiple users to your account in a bulk action by uploading
a CSV file that contains the user information. To save time, you can download a sample
CSV file from the bulk actions in your Docusign account. It has the
required format so that you can populate it with the user information.

The CSV upload file is made up of a header row with the column headers and a row of user
data for each user that you want to add to your account.

Adding and updating users must be done in separate bulk actions. Your CSV can contain
both new and existing users, but when you do a bulk add users, any existing users will
be skipped and not updated. You must run a separate bulk update users action to update
existing users.

Note: The maximum number of users in a CSV is 2,000. To add
more users, split them into multiple CSV files.

title: CSV File Format for User Additions
heading_path: CSV File Format for User Additions
The header row for add users
text:
The first line of the file is the header row for the file and defines each of the columns. The header values are not required to be in the order listed and are not case-sensitive, but the text must match listed values.

\*Required columns: Your Add Users CSV file must contain these columns: FirstName,
LastName, UserEmail, and eSignPermissionProfile. The rest of the header values are
optional. Do not add additional columns to the CSV, as this will cause the import to
fail.

The acceptable column header values for an Add Users CSV file are:

| Header Row Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| FirstName | The user's first name. Required column. |
| LastName | The user's last name. Required column. |
| UserEmail | The user's complete email address. Required column. |
| eSignPermissionProfile | The user's permission profile on the Docusign account. The value must match an existing permission profile on the account. This value is not case-sensitive. For example, for the default permission profile "DS Sender", you could enter "ds sender", "DS SENDER", or "DS Sender". Required column. |
| JobTitle | The user's job title. |
| CompanyName | The user's company name. |
| Group | The user's assigned groups. The Group values must match existing Group names for the account. To add users to more than one group, you can add Group columns to the file.  Notes:   - You can add groups to a user, but you cannot remove groups   from a user through the bulk actions option. - You do not need to add users to the Everyone group, because   all new users are automatically added to that group. |
| AddressLine1 | The user's address - first line. |
| AddressLine2 | The user's address - second line. |
| City | The user's city name. |
| StateRegionProvince | The user's regional location. |
| PostalCode | The user's postal code. |
| Phone | The user's phone number. |
| Language | The user's display language for their Docusign account. See the [list of language codes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wat1583277427901.html&_LANG=enus "See the values for the language codes that administrators use in the CSV upload file. Administrators specify the default language for users during bulk add and bulk update operations."). |

The access code option (adding an access code for authentication during user activation) cannot be used with this bulk action.

title: CSV File Format for User Additions
heading_path: CSV File Format for User Additions
text:
In the lines of the file below the header row, add the user information with commas used as the delimiter (separator) between each value.

If you are using Microsoft® Excel® to create your file, you can enter the header values
(UserName, UserEmail, and so on) in different columns on the first line, enter the
user information on subsequent lines, and save the file as a CSV file. You do not
need to add commas. Excel will automatically do this when you save the file.

title: CSV File Format for User Additions
heading_path: CSV File Format for User Additions
text: ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/uploadexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxODEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.urDvquEVnlJvpRmZkbCbx-RJmPTibWZ2YKd--j6RPg8&_LANG=enus)

title: Envelope Correct
text:
Read how administrators use the Envelope Correct setting to specify whether senders can
add documents to envelopes that are in process.

This setting controls whether senders can add documents when correcting an in-process
envelope.

- Allow adding documents after one or more recipients have completed
  their action: When selected, this option allows senders to correct an
  in-process envelope and add documents at any time during the transaction. If this option is
  not selected, senders can only add documents to an envelope when no recipients have
  completed their action.

title: Add Users to Accounts
text:
Learn how administrators add users to accounts and reactivate closed users.

As an administrator, you can add users to accounts and
reactivate closed users.

Note: For accounts on IAM plans, the Users
page includes an additional column titled License that shows the license type assigned to
the user. This is only for customers on IAM plans. Other customers on eSignature plans do
not have this column and do not have licenses.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Select Add User.

   ![The screenshot shows the email address field for adding a new user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/adduser.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMzQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.EyFwNLMl5bej5ADNy-Ytev6fEDliRyobRxxI2vKeXz4&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the email address field for adding a new user.")
3. Enter the email address for the new user, and select Next.
4. If the email address is already part of your account:

   - Active or Pending user: If the email address belongs to a
     user whose status is either an active or pending, the Existing
     User dialog box displays. You can select View
     Profile and review and edit the user.
   - Closed user: If the email address belongs to one or more
     closed users only, you have the option to reactivate a closed user or add a new user.
     If you reactivate a closed membership, the user gains access to all associated
     envelopes. If you are recycling an email address to add a new user to your account,
     select Add New User.
5. Enter the profile information for the user, such as user name and default language, and
   select Next.

   After activation, the user can change these fields by using the My
   Preferences option.
   ![The screenshot shows adding the user profile information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/addluser2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMzQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.EyFwNLMl5bej5ADNy-Ytev6fEDliRyobRxxI2vKeXz4&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows adding the user profile information.")
6. (Optional) Enter an access code, and select Next. If you add a
   code, you must provide the code to the user for them to activate their account.
7. In the permission profile and groups step:
   1. Select a permission profile to assign to the new user. See the [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") topic for more information.
   2. Select a license type to assign to the new user. If your account is on an IAM plan,
      you must assign a license or select the Unmanaged Participant license.
   3. (Optional) Select Assign Groups to select groups to assign
      to the new user.

   ![The screenshot shows adding the license, permission, and groups for the new user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/addluser3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMzQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.EyFwNLMl5bej5ADNy-Ytev6fEDliRyobRxxI2vKeXz4&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows adding the license, permission, and groups for the new user.")
8. Select Add User.

   The user is added to the account with a status of Pending.

The user you added receives an activation email. After the user
completes the steps in the activation email, the membership status changes to
Active.

title: Signing and Sending
text:
Learn how account administrators manage the default behavior and options for all
documents sent from an account through the Signing and Sending
section.

In the Signing and Sending section of your Docusign account settings, you
can manage the default behavior and various options for all documents sent from your account.

title: Domains
text:
Read about how administrators claim a domain for an organization and manage all users
for that domain.

Claiming domains requires that you have already created your organization as described in [Create an
Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=nkj1583359134234.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an organization and begin to manage multiple accounts."). To claim a domain, you must have access to Docusign Admin and have the Administrator permission
profile.

Note: Claiming a domain is also part of the
authorization process for connected applications. For more information, see [Connected
Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ynk1583359206013.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators manage the applications that can access your Docusign organization. Applications are authorized for all domain users and access is limited by the permissions you specify.").

As a Docusign administrator, you can claim domains for use with Docusign through the Domains page of your organization. When you claim
and verify an email domain for your organization, you can manage all users for that
domain across all accounts linked to the organization.

You can restrict users from creating personal Docusign accounts using an email address
from a claimed domain. You can also grant administrative consent for connected
applications on behalf of domain users.

Important: A domain can only be claimed by one Docusign organization. If one organization has claimed and verified a
domain, another organization cannot claim it. An organization can claim the same domain
in both the demo and production environments.

To start, you'll initiate a claim for your organization from the Domains page in Docusign Admin. Docusign generates a special token
that you add to the DNS (Domain Name System) for the domain. Once Docusign verifies this token in the DNS, the domain is registered to
the organization.

Note: You can choose to add a CNAME record or a TXT record to your
domain’s DNS. Docusign recommends using CNAME record when claiming a
domain.

Note: All users in the organization might have access to the
organization's reserved domain and identity provider information.

title: Find Templates Where a Removed User Is a Recipient or Signer
heading_path: Find Templates Where a Removed User Is a Recipient or Signer
text:
Learn how to find templates that include a removed user.

You can use filters on the Templates page to locate the templates that have a
removed user as a recipient or signer. If a template includes a removed user as a
recipient or a signer, identify the templates that require updating and notify the
template owner to make the changes. For more information about templates, see [Working with Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=dqj1578456412286.html&_LANG=enus).

For information on using a report to find a removed user, see [Use the Template Report to Find Templates Where a Removed User Is a Recipient or Signer](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pel1679345889295.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the template report to find templates that include a removed user.").

1. Log in to your Docusign account, and select
   Templates on the global header navigation menu.
2. In the top right of the Templates page, select
   Filters.
3. Select Recipient Name & Email in the Select Field
   dropdown.
4. Enter the email address of the removed user in the search field.
5. Select Apply.

The results show the templates in the account that include
the removed user.

title: Bulk Update Users
text:
Read about how organization administrators can bulk update existing users in one or more
accounts.

Note: This guide is for Docusign
organization administrators who oversee multiple accounts. For administrators of
individual accounts, see the [User Bulk Actions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mxm1583277431469.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators add or update multiple users simultaneously through the Bulk Add and Bulk Update options. To perform bulk actions, administrators upload comma-separated value (CSV) files that contain updates or new user information.") topic.

Docusign organization administrators with the Administrators and Users
Administrators permission profiles can bulk update existing users across one or more
accounts by uploading a comma-separated value (CSV) file. For more information about the
CSV format, see [Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jxh1593210657819.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can use a CSV file to update multiple users.").

You can update up to 2,000 users on an account and include up to 50 accounts per imported
CSV. The maximum number of updated users per import is 8,000. Only one type of import or
export can be in progress at a time.

Note:
Docusign organization
administrators with a claimed domain can also update email addresses for users on their
domain. For more information, see [Update User Email Addresses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=sle1593057084011.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can bulk update user email addresses using a CSV file.").

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication
text:
Learn how to create identity verification configurations that use the Docusign ID Verification for Phone Authentication verification
method

As an account administrator, you can add an identity verification
configuration that uses an automated voice phone call or a Short Message Service
(SMS) text message. Recipients receive a code by SMS or answer a phone call and
receive a verbal authentication code. They enter the code into the platform to open
an envelope.

You can define your identity verification configuration to use either SMS
or phone, SMS only, or phone call only.

For more information about phone authentication for recipient identity verification,
see the [How Phone Authentication Works](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=kvc1578456523785.html&_LANG=enus) topic.

Note:
Docusign Workflow Builder does not support Legacy SMS or
Legacy Phone Authentication. If you are using phone authentication with Workflow Builder, you
can copy and edit the default Docusign configuration that is
provided. Alternatively, you can create a new identity verification configuration
for phone authentication.

If you want to use phone authentication and you do not
have access to add the verification method, contact Docusign
Support.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration page opens.
3. Choose Phone Authentication and select
   **Next**.

   The Add Phone Authentication page
   opens.
4. Enter a name for the configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   Note: The name and description are not translated. Senders
   see the text in the language that it is written.
5. Select the maximum number of verification attempts. This number represents how
   many times recipients can attempt to verify before the envelope is locked. If
   recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their identity, by default, the
   envelope is locked and users must contact the sender to unlock it.
6. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.
   - Every access: recipients must identify every time
     they access an envelope.
   - Every access until role is completed: recipients
     must identify until they sign the envelope’s documents.
   - Once: recipients must only identify
     once.

   Note: Docusign has set up an early
   access program to test Phone Authentication persistence across all envelopes
   sent by the same sender within an account. This feature is available to
   selected accounts only and will be available to all customers in a future
   release. Enabling Phone Authentication persistence requires that the
   verification frequency settings in your Phone Authentication configuration
   align with the account-level authentication settings. The following table
   describes the required settings for both the Phone Authentication
   configuration and the account-level authentication settings. Only customers
   in the early access program must comply with those settings. For more
   information about account-level authentication settings, read the [Authentication Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=muh1583277327950.1.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators configure account authentication requirements for recipients.") help page.

   Table 1. Required settings for Phone Authentication

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication
text:
cId=muh1583277327950.1.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators configure account authentication requirements for recipients.") help page.

   Table 1. Required settings for Phone Authentication

   | If you select this Phone Authentication configuration… | Ensure you configure your account-level authentication setting as follows… |
   | --- | --- |
   | Every access | - Select Every time a recipient   accesses an envelope - Uncheck Completed envelopes are   accessible without additional   authentication if available |
   | Every access until role is completed | - Select Every time a recipient   accesses an envelope - Select Completed envelopes are   accessible without additional   authentication |
   | Once | - Select The first time a recipient   accesses an envelope per device - (Optional) Select Recipients can skip   authentication when accessing subsequent envelopes   from the same sender when using the same browser,   on a device, for the following   duration: |
7. (Optional) Select Mask phone number if you want to hide
   part of the phone number. This defines how the phone number entered by the
   sender is displayed to the recipient.
8. Select the method for providing the code to the recipient:

   - SMS or Phone Call: The recipient can choose
     either SMS or phone call.
   - SMS only: The recipient can only receive the code
     by SMS.
   - Phone Call only: The recipient can only receive
     the code by phone call.
9. Select Add.

The configuration displays in the configurations list and
is available for senders.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication
text:
Learn how to create identity verification configurations that use the Docusign ID Verification for Phone Authentication verification
method

As an account administrator, you can add an identity verification
configuration that uses an automated voice phone call or a Short Message Service
(SMS) text message. Recipients receive a code by SMS or answer a phone call and
receive a verbal authentication code. They enter the code into the platform to open
an envelope.

You can define your identity verification configuration to use either SMS
or phone, SMS only, or phone call only.

For more information about phone authentication for recipient identity verification,
see the [How Phone Authentication Works](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=kvc1578456523785.html&_LANG=enus) topic.

Note:
Docusign Workflow Builder does not support Legacy SMS or
Legacy Phone Authentication. If you are using phone authentication with Workflow Builder, you
can copy and edit the default Docusign configuration that is
provided. Alternatively, you can create a new identity verification configuration
for phone authentication.

If you want to use phone authentication and you do not
have access to add the verification method, contact Docusign
Support.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration page opens.
3. Choose Phone Authentication and select
   **Next**.

   The Add Phone Authentication page
   opens.
4. Enter a name for the configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   Note: The name and description are not translated. Senders
   see the text in the language that it is written.
5. Select the maximum number of verification attempts. This number represents how
   many times recipients can attempt to verify before the envelope is locked. If
   recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their identity, by default, the
   envelope is locked and users must contact the sender to unlock it.
6. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.
   - Every access: recipients must identify every time
     they access an envelope.
   - Every access until role is completed: recipients
     must identify until they sign the envelope’s documents.
   - Once: recipients must only identify
     once.

   Note: Docusign has set up an early
   access program to test Phone Authentication persistence across all envelopes
   sent by the same sender within an account. This feature is available to
   selected accounts only and will be available to all customers in a future
   release. Enabling Phone Authentication persistence requires that the
   verification frequency settings in your Phone Authentication configuration
   align with the account-level authentication settings. The following table
   describes the required settings for both the Phone Authentication
   configuration and the account-level authentication settings. Only customers
   in the early access program must comply with those settings. For more
   information about account-level authentication settings, read the
   [Authentication Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=muh1583277327950.1.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators configure account authentication requirements for recipients.") help page.

   Table 1. Required settings for Phone Authentication

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication
text:
cId=muh1583277327950.1.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators configure account authentication requirements for recipients.") help page.

   Table 1. Required settings for Phone Authentication

   | If you select this Phone Authentication configuration… | Ensure you configure your account-level authentication setting as follows… |
   | --- | --- |
   | Every access | - Select Every time a recipient   accesses an envelope - Uncheck Completed envelopes are   accessible without additional   authentication if available |
   | Every access until role is completed | - Select Every time a recipient   accesses an envelope - Select Completed envelopes are   accessible without additional   authentication |
   | Once | - Select The first time a recipient   accesses an envelope per device - (Optional) Select Recipients can skip   authentication when accessing subsequent envelopes   from the same sender when using the same browser,   on a device, for the following   duration: |
7. (Optional) Select Mask phone number if you want to hide
   part of the phone number. This defines how the phone number entered by the
   sender is displayed to the recipient.
8. Select the method for providing the code to the recipient:

   - SMS or Phone Call: The recipient can choose
     either SMS or phone call.
   - SMS only: The recipient can only receive the code
     by SMS.
   - Phone Call only: The recipient can only receive
     the code by phone call.
9. Select Add.

The configuration displays in the configurations list and
is available for senders.

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
text:
Read about how organization administrators can use a CSV file to update multiple
users.

Your CSV import file is made up of a header row with the column headers and a row of account
and user data for each user that you want to update. Only existing users will be
updated. To add new users, see the [Bulk Add New Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=emu1593207862708.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can bulk add new users to one or more accounts.")
topic.

Tip: To start, [create a user list](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=haf1593211353978.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the type of user information that you can export.") from accounts within your organization. The user data in this list can be used to
populate your bulk update CSV file.

To ensure your CSV is properly formatted, download the CSV template from the Update Users
dialog box to create your new CSV file.

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
The header row for update users
text:
The first line of the file is the header row, which defines each of the columns. The header
values are not required to be in the order listed and are not case-sensitive, but
the text must match the listed values.

Important: Your CSV file must contain these required
columns: AccountID, APIUserName, and UserEmail. The rest of the header values are
optional.

Note: For the user's name, you can use either the FirstName
and LastName columns together, or the UserName column. Your spreadsheet should only
contain one of these. If both the FirstName and LastName columns and the UserName
column are present in your CSV, the values entered in the UserName column take
precedence.

Note: All of the required columns must
remain unchanged.

The acceptable column header values for an Update Users CSV file are:

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
The header row for update users
text:
in your CSV, the values entered in the UserName column take
precedence.

Note: All of the required columns must
remain unchanged.

The acceptable column header values for an Update Users CSV file are:

| Header Row Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| AccountID | The 32 character API Account ID of the user's account in your organization. **Required column**. |
| AccountName | The name of the account. |
| APIUserName | The unique user ID. **Required column**. |
| FirstName | The user's first name. |
| LastName | The user's last name. |
| UserName | The user's full name. |
| UserEmail | The user's complete email address. To update the user's email address, use the UpdatedUserEmail column. **Required column**. |
| eSignPermissionProfile | The user's permission profile for the eSignature product. This value must match an existing permission profile for the account. This value is not case-sensitive. |
| EnableCLM | Grants the user access to the CLM product. If you grant a user access to CLM, you must also assign them a Permission profile for that product with the CLMPermissionProfile column.  - TRUE - The user has access to CLM. - FALSE - The user does not have access to CLM. |
| CLMPermissionProfile | The user's permission profile for the CLM product. This value must match an existing permission profile for the account. This value is not case-sensitive. If you assign a user a CLM permission Profile, you must also grant them access to the CLM product with the EnableCLM column. |
| Language | The user's display language for their Docusign account. See the [list of language codes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=rga1593054537902.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the code for the language the user views in the user interface."). |
| UserTitle | The user's job title. |
| CompanyName | The user's company name. |
| AddressLine1 | The user's address - first line. |
| AddressLine2 | The user's address - second line. |
| City | The user's city name. |
| StateRegionProvince | The user's regional location. |
| PostalCode | The user's postal code. |
| Phone | The user's phone number. |
| LoginPolicy | The user's login policy. Valid values include the following:   - None = The user is created with no policy assigned. - FedAuthRequired = The user must log in with an Identity   Provider. - FedAuthBypass = The user may log in with an Identity   Provider or their Docusign username and password.   For more information about login policies, see [Setting a User Login Policy](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zah1583359147774.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can set a user login policy. When SSO is enabled for an organization, all account users follow the policy settings specified for the domain."). |
| Group | The user's assigned groups. The Group values must match the existing Group names for the account. Additional Group columns can be added to the file to add users to more than one group. You do not need to add users to the Everyone group, since all new users are automatically added to that group. |
| UpdatedUserEmail | If updating domain user email addresses, use this column to enter the new email address. For more information, see [Update User Email Addresses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=sle1593057084011.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can bulk update user email addresses using a CSV file."). |

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
text:
In the lines of the file below the header row, add the user information with commas
used as the delimiter (separator) between each value.

If you are using Microsoft® Excel® to create your file, you can enter the header values
(FirstName, LastName, UserEmail, etc.) in different columns on the first line, enter
the user information on subsequent lines, and save the file as a CSV file. You do
not need to add commas. Excel will automatically do this when you save the file.

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Update Users
text: ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/ordadminbulkadd3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU5MDYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.baSh8YX5F7cRELA-1RXE6svDRkSig0DbGe9k9_Rn9dA&_LANG=enus)

title: Purging Envelopes
text:
Administrators can search for and purge all completed, declined, or voided envelopes on the account sent from or signed by a specific email address. Envelopes purged in this way are immediately moved into the purge queue.

Docusign account administrators can search for and purge all completed, declined, or voided envelopes on the account that have been sent from or signed by a specific email address. Envelopes purged in this way are immediately moved into the purge queue. For more information about the purge queue, see [Document Retention](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=frq1583277404325.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the document retention policy and how account administrators can change it to fit their business needs.").

Note: To be purged, envelopes must have a status of Completed, Declined, or Voided. In-process and Draft envelopes cannot be purged.

Along with deleting the documents, you can also choose to delete the envelope's metadata and redact all personally identifiable information from the envelope, certificate of completion, and history. The metadata includes any fields that were added to the documents and any API attachments that were added to the envelope from the system.

By default, Docusign securely retains all customer documents on the service for as long as the account is active. When an account is closed, Docusign may retain all sent, signed, and voided envelopes for a period of approximately two years from the date of account closure to meet legal, compliance, auditing, or security obligations. This retention period may be further extended if legally required.

title: Reactivate Email Archive Addresses
text:
Learn how administrators reactivate archived email addresses.

Closed email archive addresses can be reactivated.

Note: An account can only have a maximum of
five active or pending email archive addresses at one time.

1. From the Accounts view, select Email Archive
   Configuration.
2. In the [Email Archive Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=xjm1583277371373.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators can set up email addresses that automatically receive copies of Docusign signature requests and other email notifications for a sender’s account archiving and regulatory compliance purposes..") list, find the email address you want to reactivate.
3. In the Actions dropdown for the email address, select
   Reactivate.

   Docusign sends a validation email to the email address and the
   status and history for that address are updated. The link in the validation email must be
   selected to complete the reactivation. If the link is not selected, the email address
   remains in a pending status and email copies are not sent to the email address.

title: API Usage Center for eSignature Developers
heading_path: API Usage Center for eSignature Developers
text:
Read about the dashboard for developers to monitor the efficiency of their Docusign integrations.

The API Usage Center is a dashboard for monitoring the efficiency of apps that your
account uses to make API calls. You can:

- View the number of API calls over a period of time
- View efficiency scores for your apps
- Learn how to address issues that reduce efficiency
- View which apps approached or exceeded API limits
- Update the hourly call limit

For more information about API Usage Center, see the [API
Usage Center overview](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/api-usage-center/).

For more information about developing Docusign integrations, see [Build a Docusign integration](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/build-integration/).

**API Activity**

API Activity is a set of charts inside the API Usage Center that report on the
eSignature API activity. You can view information such as historical API usage,
successful and failed API calls, the distribution of API calls across all accounts API
connections, and the API limits.

See [API Activity by App](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/api-usage-center/#api_activity_by_app) for more information about viewing API
activity.

**API Efficiency**

The API Efficiency section inside the API Usage Center contains components that
assess your app’s efficiency and offer recommendations for how to improve it.

See [Efficiency score factors](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/api-usage-center/factors/) for a complete list of factors included in the
analysis.

**API Limit Management**

Each account has an hourly API call limit, which is the maximum number of calls
permitted from all apps associated with the account. If the account exceeds the limit,
calls will be blocked until the start of the next hour. If you already optimized
efficiency and your account continues to exceed the limit, you can increase it in the
API Usage Center. The limit management feature enables account administrators to change
the hourly limit without contacting support.

For more information about increasing your API rate limit on an account, see [API limit management](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/api-usage-center/limit-management/).

title: Manage Seats in Your Subscription
text:
Read about how administrators can change the number of seats and envelopes in their
subscriptions.

Administrators for accounts on Real Estate, Realtor, Standard, or Business Pro eSignature plans
can change the number of seats in their subscriptions. Reducing seats also reduces the
total number of envelopes for sending. Each seat includes 10 envelopes on a monthly plan
or 100 envelopes on an annual plan. The total number of envelopes can be shared among
all users.

Note: Make sure you have enough seats for your users. If you have more
users than seats, you can choose to close some users or increase your seats in your
subscription.

title: View and Export the Audit Logs
heading_path: View and Export the Audit Logs
text:
Learn how account administrators view and export the audit logs. The audit logs show
changes made to account settings, users, user settings, billing information, and plan
details.

1. From the Accounts view, select Audit Logs.

   By default, the Audit Logs page shows the account changes from
   the last seven days.
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/auditlogsexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.lFpE_hWgaYKawpzD_yE1P8VXz_OXtTSEIbbr7xLdWP0&_LANG=enus)
2. (Optional) You can choose from the following actions:

   - **Change the Date Range:** Use the dropdown to select a date range. You can
     select one of the predefined ranges or select **Custom** and enter your own
     range.
   - **Change the Log Sort Order:** You can change the sorting of the audit logs by
     selecting a column heading.
   - **Export to CSV:** Select **Export to CSV** to export the audit logs
     information to a comma-separated value (CSV) file. If the date range is changed, the
     number of rows exported to the CSV is the same as the events shown at the top of the
     page.

     Note:

     The exported CSV file contains the same information as the displayed audit logs,
     with some minor differences. The CSV file has two additional columns and one
     column header with a different name.

     - **Audit ID:** This column shows an ID number for each save action. Because
       multiple events can be changed in one save action it is easier to sort and group
       changes that were made with the same save action.
     - **Change Type:** This column describes the type of setting change.
     - **Field Name:** This column contains the same information as the
       **Field** column that is displayed in the audit logs.

title: Use Bulk Uploads to Update License Assignments for Users
