heading_path: Use Bulk Uploads to Update License Assignments for Users
text: Learn how account administrators can use bulk upload to update license assignments for users.

title: Use Bulk Uploads to Update License Assignments for Users
heading_path: Use Bulk Uploads to Update License Assignments for Users
text:
- You must be logged in as an administrator for the account. See the [Administrator Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=hvs1583277346278.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators assign limited levels of access to account users through permission profiles.") topic if you do not have access as an account administrator.
- Select Admin in the top navigation bar to view account administration. If you have multiple accounts, select Accounts from the left pane and choose the account you want to work in. For single accounts with no organization, the left pane containing Organization and Accounts does not display in your view.

For accounts on IAM plans, account administrators can adjust license assignments to meet expected license usage. An account administrator can follow these steps to change license assignments.

Downgrading user licenses to Free licenses removes access to functionality not supported by the license. Administrators are responsible for communicating these changes to their users and managing the license assignments for their organizational needs.

To assign a license for an individual user, you can edit the user and change the license type.

Note: Every IAM user must have a license assigned to them. Licenses cannot be removed from users who are on an IAM plan. To remove a paid license from a user, you can edit the user and change the license type to Free.

Note: Additional functionality beyond signing will be increasingly restricted for Free licenses in the future. Users may experience changes without prior notice.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Select Download Users to export a CSV of all users in your account.
3. Update the LicenseType column to reflect the correct license type for the user. Examples are Free license or a Full license, such as Full - Enterprise.

   See the [list of license types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bhs1740099648152.html&_LANG=enus "Read about license information shown in the Users page.") associated with your purchased plan.
4. Bulk import the updated CSV file to the account.

title: Remove Users and Groups from Stamps
heading_path: Remove Users and Groups from Stamps
text:
Learn how administrators remove users and groups from stamps.

As an administrator, you can remove users and groups from stamps. This procedure does not
affect documents that were stamped by the users that you remove.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Stamps.
2. Select Actions > Users and Groups.
3. Select either the Users or Groups tab
   depending on which type you are removing.
4. Select Remove to remove that user or group.

title: Connect Permission Access Levels
text:
View the level of access administrators can grant through the
Connect permission in combination with the Users and
Groups access levels.

The following table describes the level of access provided by the
Connect permission in combination with each of the
Users and Groups access levels.

| Administrative area | Page | Level of access: View Only | Level of access: Edit Groups Only | Level of access: Edit Users & Groups | Level of access: Edit Users, Groups, & Administrators |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N/A | Overview | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Users | View | View | Edit[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=kyl1661539199379.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_1) | Edit[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=kyl1661539199379.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_2) |
| Users and Groups | Permission Profiles | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Groups | View | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Users and Groups | Signing Groups | None | None | None | None |
| Integrations | Connect | Edit | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Integrations | Apps and Keys | None | None | None | None |
| Integrations | API Usage Center | None | None | None | None |
| Integrations | Payments | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Audit Logs | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Bulk Actions | View | View | View | View |
| Auditing | Validations | None | None | None | None |

[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=kyl1661539199379.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_1) Edit access does not include the ability to edit users with "Allow All"
administrative permissions enabled, such as those with the DS
Admin permission profile.

[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=kyl1661539199379.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_2) Edit access does not include access to Shared Access or
Shared Envelopes unless the
Envelope Sharing permission is also
selected, and does not include access to Transfer
Envelopes or Transfer
Templates unless the Transfer
Envelopes permission is also
selected.

title: Define Security Settings
text:
Learn how administrators define settings to control security for accounts.

As an administrator, you can control account
security.

1. From the Accounts view, select Security
   Settings.
2. Adjust your security settings as needed. The options are described in additional topics
   for each section of settings.
3. Select Save.

title: Brands
text:
Branding offers account administrators a way to customize the Docusign experience for both senders and recipients.

Note: These features are temporarily disabled for account plans purchased
on the web after November 20, 2024. For more information, contact Docusign
Support.

Branding offers a way to customize the Docusign experience for both senders
and recipients. With the branding controls in Docusign, as an account
administrator you can reinforce your brand presence and reassure signers that documents sent
to them through your Docusign account are coming from your organization. The
branding configurations you specify are global and apply to all users on your account and
every recipient experience for every envelope sent from your account.

You must be an eSignature administrator with All Administration Capabilities in order to
manage these settings. See [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") for details.

All branding changes are captured in the account audit log.

Note: Some advanced features and options are
supported only in certain Docusign plans. Your account plan might not
support some options discussed in this topic. For more information about which options are
available for your account, check your account plan or contact Docusign
Support.

title: View the History for Email Archive Addresses
heading_path: View the History for Email Archive Addresses
text:
Learn how administrators view the history for an address in the Email Archive, such as
when it was created and closed.

The history view shows information about the actions and status changes associated with the email address, such as when it was created, became active, and was closed. It does not show information about emails that were sent to the email address.

1. From the Accounts view, select Email Archive
   Configuration.
2. In the [Email Archive Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=xjm1583277371373.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators can set up email addresses that automatically receive copies of Docusign signature requests and other email notifications for a sender’s account archiving and regulatory compliance purposes..") list, find the email address you want to view.
3. In the Actions dropdown for the email address, select View
   History.

   The View History dialog box for the email address is shown.
4. When you are finished, select OK to close the dialog box.

title: Remove Signature Types
text: Learn how to remove signature types from the list available to senders.

title: Remove Signature Types
text:
Ensure you can access the account administration settings before
removing signature types.

As an account administrator, your organization requires that you remove existing signature
types from the list available to senders.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signature
   Types.
2. Select Edit.

   The Manage Signature Type List screen
   opens.
3. Locate a signature type you want to remove.

   Important: In some cases, it’s possible that Docusign has assigned you signature types. If you delete
   these signature types, you won’t be able to restore them unless you contact
   Docusign Support.
4. Select Remove.
5. Repeat steps 3 and 4 for the other signature types you want to remove.
6. Select Save.

A confirmation message shows on the Signature
Types screen. The list of signature types is now up to date.

title: Add eNote Credentials
text:
Learn how account administrators add eOriginal credentials to their accounts so that
eNotes can be sent.

To use eNotes, account administrators must add their
eOriginal credentials to their accounts.

1. From the Accounts view, under Integrations,
   select eNote Credentials.
2. In the eNote Credentials page, select Add eNote
   Configuration.
3. Enter the eOriginal credentials for your organization.

   **Tip:**
   Docusign recommends that you create an eOriginal Admin user specifically
   for Docusign API calls and use those credentials here.
4. Select Verify and Save to check and store your eOriginal
   credentials.

Your eOriginal credentials are stored as eNote credentials.

title: Edit or Delete eNote Credentials
text:
Learn how account administrators edit or delete eNote credentials when updates are
necessary or they are no longer needed.

If your eOriginal credentials change, you can edit the eNote credentials. If you no longer
need the eOriginal credentials, you can delete them.

1. From the Accounts view, under Integrations,
   select eNote Credentials.
2. Select the Actions menu for the eNote credentials.
   - To modify your eOriginal credentials, select Edit and make
     the necessary changes. Select Verify and Save.
   - To remove the eOriginal credentials, select Delete, and
     select Confirm.

Based on your selection, the eOriginal credentials are
either updated or deleted from your account.

title: Set Signing Brand Languages
text:
Learn how administrators specify a language for a signing brand.

You can select the languages available to your signers and assign a default language for your
signing brand. If a signer is using one of the selected languages, they view the
brand in their preferred language. If they are using another language or have not
selected a signing language, they view the brand in the default language.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Brands to view the Signing
   tab.
2. Select the menu icon and select Edit to edit a signing
   brand. ![The screenshot shows the Signing Brand menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Edit-menu-brands.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNzMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.dFoepT-DP_hWKw2iGM3uAO1w4acDp8wXM4Ag_OTbnuo&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Signing Brand menu.")
3. Select Select Brand Languages in the Advanced
   Configuration section.
4. Select the languages that you want to include in this brand.
5. Select a default language.

   ![The screenshot shows the Select Languages window for a brand.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Select-branding-languages.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNzMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.dFoepT-DP_hWKw2iGM3uAO1w4acDp8wXM4Ag_OTbnuo&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Select Languages window for a brand.")

   Note: If signers aren't using one of the
   selected languages, the default language is applied to the brand.
6. Select Save.

The signing brand languages are updated.

title: Signing Settings
text:
Read about how administrators use the Signing Settings to specify the default signing
behavior for documents sent from an account.

These settings are the default signing behavior for envelopes sent from your account. In most
cases, you can choose to enforce these settings for all envelopes or allow account senders to
modify the values for each envelope they send.

You must be an account administrator with All Administration Capabilities in order to manage
these settings. See [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") for details.

Note: The information in these topics covers all
signing settings available in Docusign. Some advanced features and options
are supported only in certain Docusign plans. Your account plan might not
support some options discussed in these topics. For more information about which options are
available for your account, check your account plan or contact Docusign
Support.

title: Set Password Rules
text:
Learn how administrators set rules for account passwords, such as minimum length, type
of characters allowed, and expiration time frames. Depending on the security requirements of
the organization, administrators can either select from existing options or customize the
options to suit their needs.

Updated password requirements take effect when users
create a new password or update an existing password. Users who are doing neither can
still log in with an existing password created with the previous requirements.

1. From the Accounts view, select Security
   Settings.
2. Scroll down to the Password Security section, and select Show
   Password Rules.

   The Password Rules page opens.
3. Select the password strength for the account.
4. (Optional) If you select Custom for the password strength, select the password
   requirements for the length and age of passwords.
5. (Optional) To require that passwords expire, select **Enable password
   expiration** and enter the number of days until new passwords
   expire.
6. Select **Save**.

The password rules for your account are saved.

title: Account Profile
text:
Read about administrators can update and maintain an account name and address of record
in the Account Profile.

The Account Profile allows you to update and maintain your account name and address of
record.

In addition to the account name, your account users can set a unique company name. This is a
unique value for their Docusign user ID. The company name displays on the
user's ID Card and is shown to other Docusign users, depending on their
Privacy Settings.

title: Account Profile
text:
If you work in a small business, you'll likely use the same value for both names. For
example, your company name and account name would both be "Express Tire & Automotive".

What if you work for a large, global corporation? The account is owned by a corporate
parent company, and there are some users who work for a subsidiary. In this case, your
account name might be "Hargrave International, Inc." and the company name is that of your
subsidiary, "Western Supplies, Inc."

Administrators are able to create a custom message that is displayed within the eSignature
sending experience. This message can be used to relay important announcements or other
information to your users.

You can also add contact information for an administrator on the account. This information
will help senders answer account questions by putting them in touch with a selected
administrator, an IT help desk, or another internal resource that supports Docusign for your
company.

title: Enable Comments on Envelopes
text:
Learn how account administrators specify whether envelopes can include comments from
users of an account. Comments help facilitate transactions by allowing users to resolve
questions about documents during the signing process. Administrators can also allow senders to
enable or disable comments by overriding the setting.

As an account administrator, you can enable users of an
account, both senders and recipients, to comment on envelopes. You can also allow senders to
override the settings to either allow or disable comments on envelopes.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Comments.
2. Select Enable comments in envelopes sent from this
   account.
3. (Optional) Select Allow senders to override to enable senders to
   enable and disable comments for an envelope through the Advanced
   Options.
4. (Optional) Clear Include comment text in email notifications when a comment
   is posted. This option is enabled by default and allows comments and replies
   to be included in email notifications for individual users.
5. Select Save.

Comments can be posted by senders and recipients of
envelopes, unless the option is selected to allow senders to override the setting. If a sender
overrides the setting to allow comments on an envelope, users cannot comment on it.

title: Reset User Passwords
text:
Learn how account administrators reset user passwords either for security reasons or when users forget them. Administrators request a reset, which sends an email to users with instructions and a link for changing the password.

You can initiate a password reset for users on your account. You can reset a user password if a user forgets it or if you want users to create a new password for security reasons. When you initiate a password reset, users receive a link and instructions for changing the password.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Locate the user whose password needs to be reset.
3. Select Actions > Reset Password.

The user receives an email with a link and instructions for changing the password.

title: Disable or Enable Custody Transfer Rules
heading_path: Disable or Enable Custody Transfer Rules
text:
Learn how to enable or disable custody transfer rules. As an administrator, you can
enable and disable the rules as needed.

Custody transfer rules can be disabled or enabled from the Custody Transfer rule list.

1. From the Accounts view, select Custody
   Transfer.
2. Locate the rule that you want to disable or enable.
3. Select Actions > Disable to disable the rule. If a rule is disabled, select
   Enable to enable the rule.

   The check mark in the Enabled column changes to show if the rule is disabled (no check
   mark) or enabled (check mark).

title: Add Groups
text:
Learn how administrators create groups to organize users into functional units.

As an account administrator, you can organize users into
groups.

1. From the Accounts view, select Groups.
2. Select Add Group.
3. Enter a name for the group.
4. Select Add.

   The group is created.
5. Select Assign Users.
6. Locate the account users that you want to add to the group by searching or viewing the
   list of all users.
7. Select the checkboxes next to the account users that you want to add to the
   Selected list.
8. Select Assign.

The users are added to the new group.

title: Prevent Access to Personal Docusign Accounts
heading_path: Prevent Access to Personal Docusign Accounts
text:
Read about how organization administrators can prevent their users from accessing
personal Docusign accounts at work.

As an IT organization, you may have the requirement that employees should not have access to
personal Docusign accounts at work. Typically, organizations use a
proxy server to block URLs of web services employees shouldn't access at work.

Unfortunately, this doesn’t work for SaaS services, especially when the same web service
is being used as an enterprise application within the organization. Blocking a URL may
mean that certain Docusign properties won't function properly or users
may not be able to access the application, even with a legitimate work account.

Docusign allows you to have your proxy server send a hint to Docusign using an HTTP header to indicate what a user can access from
this network. The solution is hard to circumvent but still allows access for legitimate,
professional use of the product.

You might need to work with your IT team to complete the following process:

1. Set up a proxy server to ensure that all outbound traffic to access Docusign is routed through this proxy.
2. Enable SSL interception at the proxy. Since the proxy will be intercepting SSL requests,
   configure your employees’ devices to trust your SSL proxy. This means deploying the
   Internal Root Certificate Authority used by the proxy and marking it as trusted.
   Failure to do so may cause some browsers to not load the page or display an error.
3. Enable the proxy to intercept each HTTP request and insert a special header. The
   content of the HTTP request does not need to be modified.

**X-Docusign-Allow-Organization Header**

The header to be injected will use this format:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | X-Docusign-Allow-Organization: <organization id> |
```

This allows users to log into Docusign with an email address on a domain claimed by the
organization.

Note: For more information about the domain claim process, see
[Domains](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jub1589318086105.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators claim a domain for an organization and manage all users for that domain.").

You might need to include a proxy policy to overwrite/replace/drop this header if an end-user
tries to specify this header themselves. This prevents a user from circumventing your
proxy rules.

If the user tries to log in with an email address that does not belong to the
organization, then they will see an error on the Docusign login page indicating that
their network administrator has blocked access to personal Docusign accounts.

**Example Configuration**

These sample values represent your organization ID in the production or demo
environments.

Note: To find your organization ID in Docusign
Admin, open the Features page and select  Organization Details.
The ID differs between production and demo. Make sure to locate both IDs if you intend
to allow users to access both environments.

Table 1.

| Environment | Example Organization ID |
| --- | --- |
| Demo | 28abc869-df2d-4abc-b64d-573111abcd06 |
| Production | 39zyx869-df2d-4zyx-b64d-573111wzyx06 |

The proxy would include both values for demo and production so that all environments are
covered.

For example, this is what header the proxy would include on all requests to Docusign.com
or Docusign.net:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | X-Docusign-Allow-Organization: 28abc869-df2d-4abc-b64d-573111abcd06,39zyx869-df2d-4zyx-b64d-573111wzyx06 |
```

title: Enable the Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure for Your Account
heading_path: Enable the Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure for Your Account
text:
Learn how to enable the optional Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure.
Administrators can use the disclosure to obtain consent from signers before they take action on
a document.

As an administrator, you can obtain consent from signers
before they sign by enabling the optional Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure.

1. From the Accounts view, select Legal
   Disclosure.
2. Select Yes, I elect to use an Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure
   with my new recipients.

   ![Enable the ERSD.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Enable-the-ERSD.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.lUzyg8umnVqa-PX2KBQJQpBDUPbc9xBXJadt_5Ab0SI&_LANG=enus "Enable the ERSD.")
3. Specify how often recipients who must sign or view your documents must accept the ERSD.
   This setting applies only to those recipients who hold Docusign accounts. Any recipients
   who are not account holders must accept the ERSD for every envelope they receive from your
   account, regardless of this frequency setting. Setting options:

   - **Once for this account**. A recipient has to accept the disclosure only for the
     first envelope they sign from your account. Future envelopes sent by anyone on the
     account will not offer the disclosure. If a recipient does not have an active Docusign account, future envelopes will include a prompt to the recipient to accept the
     disclosure again.
   - **First time viewing each envelope sent from this account**. A recipient has to
     accept the disclosure only the first time they open a specific envelope. They must
     accept the ERSD once for each envelope they receive from any sender on your
     account.
   - **First time viewing each time the recipient appears in the signing order**. A
     recipient must accept the ERSD the first time viewing each envelope they receive from
     any sender on your account. If the recipient is listed multiple times in the routing
     order for an envelope, they must accept the ERSD again for each subsequent time they
     are required to act on the envelope.
4. Optionally, select Enforce frequency setting for members of this
   account to require all members on the account to accept the ERSD based on
   the frequency setting.
5. Optionally, select if you want to Allow recipients to withdraw their
   consent.

   If this option is selected, your recipients have the option to withdraw their consent
   when they decline to sign documents. These recipients will be required to agree to the
   terms in the disclosure if they sign documents sent to them from your account in the
   future.
6. Select Save.

   The ERSD is enabled for your account and recipients will need to agree to it for all
   envelopes sent from the account, as you have specified in the setting details.

title: Permission Profile Options
text:
Read about the administrator and user permissions that administrators use to define the actions available to account users that are assigned to the Permission Profile.

These options set the behavior and actions available for all account users assigned to the Permission Profile.

Note: The information in this topic covers all the settings available in Docusign. Some advanced features and options are supported only in certain Docusign plans. Your account plan might not support some options discussed in this topic. For more information about which options are available for your account, check your account plan or contact Docusign Support.

Administrator Permissions

- None: When selected, users will have no access to administrator permissions.
- Allow All: When selected, users with this permission have access to the Administration view for the account and can manage account settings, users and groups, signing and sending settings, and integrations. This option is automatically selected for the DS Admin permission profile.
- Allow Some: When selected, you determine the level of administrator access to provide users. You can enable or disable individual administrative features. For more information, see [Delegated Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=gih1583277347533.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators assign limited administrator capabilities through the Delegated Administration functionality.").

User Permissions

title: Permission Profile Options
text:
75390&topicId=gih1583277347533.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators assign limited administrator capabilities through the Delegated Administration functionality.").

User Permissions

- Allow user to send envelopes: When selected, the user can send envelopes.
- Agreement Manager Permissions: By default, users can only access their own shared, received, sent, and uploaded agreements. For information about applying Agreement Manager permissions at the user level, see the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus) topic.
  - View all agreements in this account: When selected, this permission gives access to users to view and download all agreements in the account.
  - Organize and manage all agreements: When selected, this permission authorizes users or groups to view, edit, download, and delete all agreements in the account. This permission also grants access to manage agreement sets and attributes, including agreement types and fields. This is the highest level of access usually reserved for contact managers.
  - Upload up to 1,000 files at a time: When selected, this permission gives users access to bulk upload up to 1,000 files at a time, including from third-party cloud storage locations.
- Playbook Access: For information about applying Playbook permissions at the user level, see the [Set Playbook Permissions for the AI-Assisted Review Add-in](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=mapfd7284e6-b699-4527-8651-a133914111fd&topicId=tsk8b028ec5-3d65-45a6-aec6-6b3540565b75.html&_LANG=enus) topic.
  - None: Cannot use AI-Assisted Review features.
  - Can View Playbooks: When selected, this setting allows users to view and run playbooks and playbook rules. Users can use Ask, Modify, and Add capabilities.
  - Can Manage Playbooks: When selected, this setting allows users to create, view, edit, run, delete playbooks and playbook rules. Users can use Ask, Modify, and Add capabilities.
- Document Templates: This option sets the user’s document template usage and management rights. Document templates settings are for customers on IAM plans only. The options are:
  - None: This user cannot view, use, or manage document templates.
  - Use: This user can view and use document templates, but cannot edit them.
  - Manage: This user can create, edit, delete, replace, and use document templates.
- Templates: This option sets the user’s template usage and management rights. The options are:
  - None: This user cannot use, create, or share templates.
  - Use: This user can use templates created and shared by other users, but the user cannot create or share templates.
  - Create: This user can create and use templates, but the user cannot share templates.
  - Share: This user can use, create, and share templates.
- PowerForms: This option sets the PowerForms access for a user. The options are:
  - None: The user cannot use PowerForms.
  - Standard User: The user can send PowerForms.
  - Administrator: The user can create, manage, and download PowerForms. With this option, you can select a secondary setting named View envelopes and form data from other PowerForms senders. Shared access also required. If selected, the PowerForm administrator is granted access to form data and envelopes from other PowerForm senders.
- Allow signer attachments: When selected, the user can add signer attachment fields while sending documents. The [Sending Setting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bjh1583277352580.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use Sending Settings to set the default behavior for documents sent from an account.")  Enable signer attachment field option must be enabled to use this option.
- Allow recipient viewed notifications: When selected, an email is sent to a sender the first time a recipient opens an envelope.
- Allow sending to bulk list: When selected, the user can use the Bulk Send feature. The [Sending Setting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bjh1583277352580.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use Sending Settings to set the default behavior for documents sent from an account.") Enable bulk recipients must also be enabled to use the feature. See [Bulk Send for Multiple Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=wtn1643071711000&topicId=kjz1578456386508.html&_LANG=enus) for more information about this feature.
- Allow language selection: When selected, the user can set the language used in the standard email format for a recipient when creating an envelope.

title: Permission Profile Options
text:
ANG=enus) for more information about this feature.
- Allow language selection: When selected, the user can set the language used in the standard email format for a recipient when creating an envelope.

  Note: The account setting Enable custom email and language for each recipient must also be selected for this permission setting to be applied. See [Sending Setting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bjh1583277352580.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use Sending Settings to set the default behavior for documents sent from an account.")  for more information.
- Disable document upload: When selected, the user cannot add files to the envelopes they send or correct. This restriction is applied to web app users only. It does not affect mobile app users or those using the API.
- Allow SMS delivery to recipients: When selected, users with this permission profile can choose to send signing notifications by SMS in addition to standard email notifications.
- Hide Actions menu: When selected, the Other Actions menu is not included in the Add Fields view, where the sender tags documents with fields for recipients to complete. This option restricts senders from accessing and changing other aspects of the envelope, including uploaded documents, recipient messages, and advanced options.
- Workflows: This option sets the Workflows access for a user. The options are:
  - None: Users cannot use or create workflows.
  - Use: Users can use workflows created and published by others.
  - Create: Users can create and manage their own workflows.
- Web Forms: This option sets the Web Forms access for a user. The options are:
  - None: The user cannot use or create Web Forms.
  - Use: The user can use Web Forms created and published by others, but the user cannot create Web Forms.
  - Create: The user can access, build, manage, and use Web Forms.
- Address Book: With this permission setting, you can control each users' access to personal and shared contacts, and to the ability to share contacts with other account users. Users access and manage contacts through their Contacts list, located in My Preferences. Of the four settings, only Share allows a user to share their personal contacts. If a contact is shared, it is shared with all account users who have access to shared contacts.
  - Use Personal Only: The user can create and use only personal contacts in their Contacts list. Signing groups are included in the personal contacts for users with this setting.
  - Use Shared Only: This user can use only the contacts shared by other account users. Signing groups are included in the shared contacts for users with this setting.
  - Use Personal and Shared: This user can create and use personal contacts, and can use any contacts shared by other account users and any signing groups.
  - Share: This user can create personal contacts, use any shared contacts, and share their contacts with other account users.

  Deliver self-signed email notifications by: This option selects the setting for how self-signed documents (documents sent using the Sign a Document option) are presented to the email recipients. This setting also affects self-signed documents sent from Docusign mobile applications. There are two possible selections:

  - Link: A secure link to the self-signed documents is included in the email. Email recipients select the link to be taken to the completed documents, where they can view and download the documents.
  - PDF: A PDF of the completed documents is attached to the email. Email recipients can view and download the attached documents.

  Allow view and manage envelope rights through API: When selected, the user can:

title: Permission Profile Options
text:
F of the completed documents is attached to the email. Email recipients can view and download the attached documents.

  Allow view and manage envelope rights through API: When selected, the user can:

  - View, manage, and request the status of all envelopes for the account through the Docusign API.
  - Transfer envelope custody and set custody transfer rules if the user is also a Docusign organization administrator.
  - Send on behalf of other users through API if Allow send on behalf of other users through API is also enabled.

  Allow send on behalf of other users through API: When selected, the user can send envelopes for (on behalf of) other users through the Docusign API. The Allow view and manage envelope rights through API option must be enabled for the user to select this option.

  Note: ‘Send on behalf of’ only applies to applications which use Legacy Authentication. All new applications should use OAuth authentication and existing applications should be updated to use OAuth. For OAuth authentication, the ‘send on behalf of’ equivalent is to use the JSON Web Token (JWT) grant flow. For more information, see [JWT Grant Authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt/).
- Allow supplemental documents: When selected, senders can send documents as supplements. For the possible recipient actions (must view, must accept, must read), the [signing settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=lue1583277359885.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use the Signing Settings to specify the default signing behavior for documents sent from an account.") for supplemental documents determines whether they are required by default.

  To allow senders to override the default behavior, select the appropriate Allow senders to override these signing settings options. With an override option selected, senders can change the relevant recipient action setting for any supplements they send. The override options are:
  - Signers must view supplemental documents
  - Signers must accept supplemental documents
  - Signers must read supplemental documents

  See the [Send Supplemental Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=nfs1578456470024.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information about using this feature.

title: Create a Docusign Support Case from the Help Menu
heading_path: Create a Docusign Support Case from the Help Menu
text:
Learn how to create a support case from your Docusign account. Users
and administrators can submit a case from the Help menu.

You can create support cases directly from your Docusign
account. You can describe a question or an issue in the support form and attach
related files.

You must be a user or administrator in a Docusign
eSignature, IAM, or Docusign Admin account. This feature is not
available to users on free or trial plans or CLM accounts.

1. Log in to your Docusign account, and select the
   Help menu.

   The Help menu opens.![The screenshot shows the Help menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/support01.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4NDksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.r_moxb4QuZFalVS-6K3BjntHDo7bdQ-EmszlAOISYY0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Help menu.")
2. Select Contact Support.

   The Contact Support window opens.![The screenshot shows the Contact Support form.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/support02.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4NDksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.r_moxb4QuZFalVS-6K3BjntHDo7bdQ-EmszlAOISYY0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Contact Support form.")
3. Select the reason for the support case from the following options:

   - Ask a question
   - Report a problem
4. Enter a short subject to identify the issue or question.
5. Enter a detailed description of the issue or question.
6. Select your preferred contact method:

   - Email
   - Phone - Request a callback
7. (Optional) Select Upload file if you need to attach a
   file to the case. You can add up to five files. The total size of the files must
   be less than 25KB.
8. Select Submit.

A message confirms that your case was submitted. A Docusign support representative will contact you.

title: Add a VAT or GST Number to Accounts
heading_path: Add a VAT or GST Number to Accounts
text:
Learn how administrators in certain countries add a Value-added tax (VAT) or a Goods and
Services Tax (GST) number to their accounts to obtain tax exemptions for qualified business
purchases.

title: Add a VAT or GST Number to Accounts
heading_path: Add a VAT or GST Number to Accounts
text:
Log in to your Docusign account as an
administrator, and select the Admin tab.

As part of billing, taxes are automatically collected at the point of checkout for accounts in
certain countries. For application of the reverse charge mechanism where applicable,
add your VAT or GST number to the billing information of your account.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Update Payment and Tax Details in the
   Billing Details section.
3. Enter your VAT or GST number in the Tax information
   (optional) field.

   ![The screenshot shows the Update payment and tax details page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/VAT.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MTQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.qZ9nrLxW1txekil7Qzce2MIvmARV78_7z-TUFCz3xEo&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Update payment and tax details page.")
4. Select Update.

Your tax information is saved.

title: What Is a Docusign Account?
text:
Read about the concept of an account in Docusign and the role that administrators play in account management.

An account is a container created for a Docusign customer (either a company or an individual) that includes their envelopes, user memberships, templates, and other customer data. An account provides access to Docusign services based on a subscription plan and its associated features. Each account is uniquely identified by a GUID known as the Account ID. A customer may have one or more accounts, based on their needs.

The customer designates individuals to act as administrators of their account. Administrators can change settings within an account, manage user memberships and access controls, and accomplish other administrative tasks.

For customers with multiple accounts, these can be collectively managed using a Docusign organization. For more information about organizations, see the [Docusign Organization Administration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=oum1583359129731.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: View Your Account Subscription
text:
Learn how administrators can view account subscription information.

In Plan and Billing, your account subscription shows active and upcoming
renewals. Subscriptions also show the products in each subscription.

Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

Active renewal period subscriptions include a Manage Renewal button.
Upcoming renewals show information about the timeframe of the renewal period.

Note: For accounts that use automatic renewal, you can access the
Renewals page. The Renewals page
becomes available 60 days before the renewal date and up to 7 days before the renewal
window closes.

![The screenshot shows the Renewals page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/renewals1png.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MzMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.6F-c-cGbtAmPhUvhwqusrWmCvYj9Ag6wIRvwLE8-f_A&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Renewals page.")

title: Authentication Settings
text:
Read about how administrators configure account authentication requirements for
recipients.

These options set recipient authentication behavior for the account. These settings can be
very important, since your account might be charged for some authentication checks.

**Recipient Authentication Settings** This section determines if recipient authentication
is required, can be configured by the sender, or is disabled for the account. This
setting applies to Phone Authentication, SMS Authentication, and Knowledge-Based
ID checks. There are three possible selections:

- **Any recipient must authenticate on every envelope sent from this account**
  An authentication check is required and the sender must apply one of the
  authentication methods to each new envelope they send. This setting applies to
  Phone Authentication, SMS Authentication, and Knowledge-Based ID checks.
- **The sender can require a recipient must authenticate on any envelope sent
  from this account** Authentication is configurable, allowing the sender to
  select one of the authentication methods as needed. This setting applies to Phone
  Authentication, SMS Authentication, Knowledge-Based ID, and Access Code checks.
- **Disable recipient authentication for this account** The sender cannot apply
  Phone Authentication, SMS Authentication, or Knowledge-Based ID checks to access
  documents.

**Recipient Authentication Triggers** This section controls how often recipient
authentication is required for recipients. This setting applies to Phone Authentication,
SMS Authentication, Knowledge-Based ID, and Access Code checks. The available settings
are as follows:

- **The first time a recipient accesses an envelope per device** The
  recipient must pass the authentication check the first time that they access an
  envelope on a given device.

  With this setting, recipients are not asked to authenticate again when they access the envelope from the same browser, on the same device. If the recipient attempts to access the envelope from a different browser or a different device, the recipient must pass authentication again. Once authenticated, that recipient is not challenged again on the new device or browser.

  When using Access Codes with this setting, the recipient is not
  prompted to authenticate again after the first time, even if using a different
  device.

  - **Completed envelopes are accessible without additional authentication**
    This setting offers the option to reduce the authentication burden for
    completed envelopes. When selected, it allows authentication from signing to
    persist. The recipient does not need to re-authenticate when accessing a
    completed envelope, regardless of the device or browser they use.

    Note: To enable the Completed envelopes are
    accessible without additional authentication option,
    your Docusign representative must enable the plan item
    that skips authentication for completed envelopes.
- **Every time a recipient accesses an envelope** The recipient must pass the
  authentication check each time that they try to access the envelope. However, this
  option can be modified by setting a time for the Recipient Authentication Skip
  Option. Additionally, In-Person Signers who opted to have completed documents emailed are exempt from Access Code requirements.

**Recipient Authentication Skip Option**. This option determines if recipients that have recently passed authentication can skip authentication. This setting only applies to Phone Authentication, SMS Authentication, and Knowledge-Based ID checks. There are two possible settings:

title: Authentication Settings
text:
that have recently passed authentication can skip authentication. This setting only applies to Phone Authentication, SMS Authentication, and Knowledge-Based ID checks. There are two possible settings:

- **Recipients cannot skip authentication when accessing subsequent envelopes from the same sender** When this option is selected, the recipient is always required to pass authentication to access envelopes.
- **Recipients can skip authentication when accessing subsequent envelopes from the same sender when using the same browser, on a device, for the following duration:** When this option is selected, administrators can set the length of time that recipients are allowed to skip authentication checks when the recipient recently passed authentication. The amount of time can be set in minutes, hours, or days.

Note: Docusign has set up an early access program to
test Phone Authentication persistence across all envelopes sent by the same sender
within an account. This feature is available to selected accounts only and will be
available to all customers in a future release. Enabling Phone Authentication
persistence requires that the verification frequency settings in your Phone
Authentication configuration align with the account-level authentication settings. The
following table describes the required settings for both the account-level
authentication settings and the Phone Authentication configuration. Only customers in
the early access program must comply with those settings. For more information about
Phone Authentication configuration, read the [Add Identity Verification Configurations for Phone Authentication](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jwu1731890669598.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create identity verification configurations that use the Docusign ID Verification for Phone Authentication verification method")help page.

Table 1. Required settings for Phone Authentication

| If you select this account-level authentication setting… | Ensure you configure Phone Authentication as follows… |
| --- | --- |
| Every time a recipient accesses an envelope | Select Every access |
| - Every time a recipient accesses an   envelope - Completed envelopes are accessible without   additional authentication | Select Every access until role is completed |
| The first time a recipient accesses an envelope per device | Select Once |
| Recipients can skip authentication when accessing subsequent envelopes from the same sender when using the same browser, on a device, for the following duration: | Select Once |

**Login Requirements:** This option sets account and log in requirements for recipients. There are four possible selections:

- **Not Required to Login:** The recipient is not required to log on to the system. If the recipient has a Docusign account, they can sign their document from the email link without logging on.
- **Login Required if Signer Has an Account:** If the recipient has a Docusign account,
  they must log on to their Docusign account to open the document.
- **Account Required – Login Once Per Session:** The sender cannot send documents to anyone who does not have a Docusign account and the recipient must log on to their Docusign account to open the document.
- **Account Required – Login for Each New Document:** The sender cannot send documents to anyone who does not have a Docusign account and the recipient must log on to their Docusign account to open each document.
