title: Download User Lists and Permission Profiles
heading_path: Download User Lists and Permission Profiles
text:
Learn how administrators download either a full list or a selected list of users on an
account to a comma-separated value (CSV) file. Optionally, administrators can include
permissions and values.

As an administrator, you can download either a full list or a
selected list of users on an account to a comma-separated value (CSV) file. Optionally, you
can include permissions and values.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. (Optional) Use the Search and Filter
   controls to select the set of users to download. The download includes only those users
   that are displayed.
3. On the Users page, select Download Users.

   Note: If you get a message
   that your download contains too many users to process, use the
   Search or Filters controls to reduce the
   results to an acceptable number.
4. (Optional) Select Include user permissions in the
   Download Users dialog box to include the list of user permissions
   and values.
5. Select Download.

The selected data is downloaded in a comma-separated value (CSV)
file to your Downloads folder.

title: Add Custom Fields to Envelopes
text:
Learn how account administrators add custom fields to envelopes.

As an account administrator, you can add custom fields to
envelopes.

1. From the Accounts view, select Envelope Custom
   Fields.
2. Select Add Field.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/addenvelopecustomfield.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU2MTYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Wi72rOdgfPV9MYreNk3sGAbxJOT3WYI5omKot02wM1Y&_LANG=enus)
3. Type a name.
4. Select whether the field is shown and required:

   - **Show field to envelope creators:** When selected, the field is shown in the
     Envelope Custom Fields section when an envelope is created.

     When deselected, the
     field types will behave as follows:

     - **Text:** Envelope creators cannot enter text into the value of the Envelope
       Custom Field before sending, rendering the Envelope Custom Field blank. The
       envelope creator and any recipients are unable to see the field or its contents
       when sending, signing, or viewing in the Certificate of Completion.
     - **List:** Envelope creators can set the field value upon creation, but the
       value cannot be changed when an envelope is created or sent. The envelope creator
       and any recipients are unable to see the field or its contents when sending,
       signing, or viewing in the Certificate of Completion.
   - **Make field required for envelopes:** When selected, senders are required to
     enter or select information into the field before the envelope can be sent.
5. Select the Field Type:

   - **Text:** Provides a text fields where senders can enter information. The text can be a maximum of 100 characters.
   - **List:** Provides a dropdown where the senders can select a value. When this is
     selected, the List of Values field is active. Type the list values separated by a
     semi-colon. For example, for the list options North, South, East, and West, enter
     "North;South;East;West".

     The List of Values field can be a maximum of 2048
     characters, but each value can only be a maximum of 100 characters.
6. Select Add to save the envelope custom field.

title: User Bulk Actions
text:
Read about how administrators add or update multiple users simultaneously through the
Bulk Add and Bulk Update options. To perform
bulk actions, administrators upload comma-separated value (CSV) files that contain updates or
new user information.

As an administrator, you can add or update multiple users simultaneously through the
Bulk Add and Bulk Update options. To perform
bulk actions, you upload a comma-separated value (CSV) file that contains the user
information.

The following considerations apply to bulk actions:

- You can perform only one type of bulk action in each CSV file upload. Therefore, you must
  use separate bulk actions to add and update users. If your CSV file contains both new and
  existing users, existing users are skipped and not updated.
- A CSV file upload is limited to 2,000 users. To add or update more than 2,000 users,
  divide them between CSV files.
- When working with CSV files for bulk actions, only UTF-8 encoding is supported.
- If copying data from another CSV file, always use Paste Special > Values when adding the data to a CSV file.
- All Docusign account administrators, including delegated administrators,
  can use bulk actions to add or update users. If you are an account administrator with
  delegated administration rights for users and groups only, you can perform bulk actions only
  for non-administrative users. For example, you cannot add a new user with a permission
  profile that includes any level of account administrative rights, and you cannot assign
  administrative rights to a user.

  Note: In account settings, you can add
  groups to a user through the bulk actions option, but you cannot remove groups from a user
  through the bulk actions option.
- Only users with an active or pending account membership can be updated through bulk
  actions.
- Account administrators cannot use bulk actions to change user email addresses for
  existing Docusign memberships. To update a user email address, ask the
  user to follow the steps in [Change Your Email Address](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=sdb1578456444133.html&_LANG=enus).

  A Docusign administrator can use bulk actions to update user email addresses for domain
  users. For more information, see [Bulk Update Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jwk1593209395373.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can bulk update existing users in one or more accounts.").

title: Change the Order of Signature Types Available to Senders for Envelope Creation
heading_path: Change the Order of Signature Types Available to Senders for Envelope Creation
text: Learn how to change the order of signature types in the list available to senders.

title: Change the Order of Signature Types Available to Senders for Envelope Creation
heading_path: Change the Order of Signature Types Available to Senders for Envelope Creation
text:
Ensure you can access the account administration settings before
changing the order of signature types.

As an account administrator, your organization requires that you change the order of signature
types available to senders.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signature
   Types.
2. Select Edit.

   The Manage Signature Type List screen opens.
3. Select a signature type and move it to the position of your choice in the
   list.

   Moving a signature type to the top of the list designates it as the
   default choice for signature type selection. Default signature types apply to
   signers unless senders select a distinct signature type.
4. Select Save.

   A confirmation message shows on the Signature Types
   screen.

The list of signature types updates with your changes.

title: Account Settings
text:
Read how administrators manage account billing, profile, security, and branding.

In Account settings, you can manage account billing and usage, profile, security, regional, and branding settings.

title: Download a CSV File of Envelope or Template Information
heading_path: Download a CSV File of Envelope or Template Information
text:
Learn how account administrators download information about envelopes and templates in
CSV files to help them make decisions about actions to take on them.

As an account administrator, you can download
information about envelopes and templates. This information can help you make decisions about
what actions to take.

1. From the Accounts view, select Custody
   Transfer.
2. Select Transfer and select either
   Envelopes or Templates.

   All corresponding items in your account from the last three months are listed. Use the
   Filters settings to change the list of items shown by specifying
   a different date range or other criteria, such as the envelope sender or template
   name.
3. Select Download CSV.

   - For envelopes, in the Download CSV dialog box, select if the recipient information
     and envelope custom fields information should be included in the CSV file. These
     details are not used in the transfer process, but you may find them helpful for other
     purposes. For instance, you can use the envelope custom fields to help sort envelopes
     and identify the ones you want to transfer.
4. Select Download to download the CSV file. Example:

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/transferdownloadcsvfile.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1MzQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.py7QON8zA35Mi5qwAwUyKNbmEMkQpG9z_JxxR9yhdvo&_LANG=enus)

title: Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text:
Learn about Risk-Based Verification (RBV) and how you can configure it.

Risk-Based Verification selects the identity verification relevant for each recipient based on a real-time risk assessment. The real-time assessment analyzes recipients’ data and deduces a risk level that determines the identity verification method to apply. If the results indicate a low risk, recipients only need to identify using simplified verification methods, such as Phone Authentication. Conversely, high-risk score recipients must comply with advanced verification methods like liveness detection. Docusign works with a trusted partner (Socure) to perform risk checks. This partnership improves the security and simplicity of identity verification.

Important: Risk-Based Verification is only available to Docusign accounts hosted in the United-States.

title: Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text:
Identity Verification provides a default configuration for risk-based verification that meets the most common verification requirements. However, you can also create your configuration with customized parameters.

Risk-based verification configurations include the following parameters:

- Name and description
- Risk assessment
- Risk thresholds
- Stored data

title: Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text:
The name you give to your configuration is the name that appears to senders when they select an identity verification method for their envelope recipients.

The system does not translate the name and description. Senders see the text in its original language.

For more information about selecting an identity verification method for a recipient,
read the [To Add ID Verification for a Recipient](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=whh1578456529441.html&_LANG=enus) page.

title: Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text: The Risk assessment section shows recipients data that the trusted partner (Socure) analyzes to determine the appropriate risk level. The trusted partner has identified a set of signals that can either increase or decrease a participant’s risk score. For example, an email domain listed as high-risk can increase the risk score. Docusign administrators cannot change the risk assessment parameters.

title: Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text:
The Risk thresholds section shows the risk profiles assigned to each recipient based on their risk score. Each profile determines the identity verification method that will apply to its recipients. You can keep the ID Verification default methods. You can also customize the methods associated with each profile based on your specific security requirements and risk tolerance.

Risk-Based Verification configurations include the following risk profiles:

- Low risk. ID Verification automatically assigns low-risk profiles to low-friction identity verification methods, such as the default Phone Authentication method.
- Medium risk. By default, ID Verification requires that participants from the medium-risk category complete the Knowledge-based authentication method.
- High risk. ID Verification consider these participants to be high risk, and potentially fraudulent. By default, it requires them to verify their identity using the ID Verification with Liveness method.

title: Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text:
The Rejection Recipients section lists the triggers that you can activate to block recipients in the identity verification process. ID Verification provides the following criteria:

- Almost certain probability of fraud. Enable this option to deny access to recipients identified as likely fraudsters based on data analysis.
- Date of birth indicates COPPA review. Activating this option will automatically block recipients identified as under 13 years of age through data analysis.
- Email or IP address in sanctioned country. The trusted partner tracks any email address from a country listed in the OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) database. Activate this option to block recipients with such email addresses.
- Social Security Number has been reported as deceased. Enable this option to deny access to recipients using obsolete social security numbers.
- Global Watchlist Screening. The trusted partner checks if recipients are on the following watchlists:
  - AML (Anti-Money Laundering) list
  - OFAC list

  If this option is activated, ID Verification denies access to all recipients listed on the AML or the OFAC list. However, recipients on the PEP or Adverse Media lists but not on the AML or OFAC lists can still verify their identity.

By default, ID Verification activates all the available block criteria.

title: Risk-Based Verification Configuration
text: The Stored data section includes the data that ID Evidence can capture and store during the identity verification. The Risk assessment inputs tab allows you to log the data recipients provide to the trusted partner to assess their risk level. You can customize the recorded data and save information such as the address, the phone number, or the last four digits of the recipients’ social security number. Additional tabs may appear depending on the identity verification methods associated with each risk profile. For example, if you assigned the Knowledge-based authentication method to a risk profile, ID Verification displays a Knowledge-Based Authentication tab. From this tab, you can select the Knowledge-based authentication data to record, such as the address provided by the recipient or their final score on the questionnaire.

title: Docusign Single Sign-On Overview
text:
Read about how Docusign administrators can set up SSO at a global level to manage users in all their Docusign accounts.

Note: This guide is for Docusign organization administrators. This feature is only available with the Access Management with SSO add-on. Single Sign-On requires an organization in Docusign Admin..

With Single Sign-On (SSO), you can provision new users and enforce secure access management across all your corporate applications. SSO, also known as Federation, simplifies and secures user login with just one password for all your SSO-enabled applications. With Docusign Admin, you can set up and manage SSO at a global level to control all your Docusign accounts.

SSO enables your company to manage access to Docusign through an Identity Provider, such as Okta, Azure, Active Directory Federation Services, and OneLogin. With SSO, Docusign users must use the Company Log In option. When they enter their domain email address, authentication is handled by an Identity Provider (IdP).

Docusign Admin allows an administrator to manage users on their company's email domains. For example, suppose that every user at a company has an email address at the domain @myorganization.com. By proving ownership of myorganization.com, an administrator can manage the identity for Docusign users on that email domain. For example, a company can enable a security policy in a Docusign organization to require all users with their email address to authenticate with the corporate Identity Provider.

For more information about best practices and procedural guidelines, read the [Identify and Manage Your Company's External Docusign Accounts](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/km-guide-identify-manage-your-companys-external-docusign-accounts) whitepaper.

title: Create an Organization
text:
Learn how to create an organization and begin to manage multiple accounts.

1. Log in to a Docusign account that is enabled for Docusign Admin, and select the Admin tab from the
   top navigation bar.
2. On the Overview page in the Accounts view, select Get
   Started.

   ![The screenshot shows the Get Started button to create a new organization.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/The-screenshot-shows-the-Get-Started-button-to-create-a-new-organization.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU5NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Fe-zofl7mBeHI5XHkXCqSVrdVswS-nwtIFbRKW4VX4k&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Get Started button to create a new organization.")

   Note: If you do not see
   the Get Started section in your account, Docusign Admin is not enabled on the account. Contact Docusign Support for
   assistance.
3. Enter an organization name and an optional description in the fields, and select
   Next.
4. Link accounts to your organization. The account you used to create the organization is
   automatically linked. Select any additional accounts you and the other Docusign administrators want to manage centrally through your organization.

   The accounts for which you are an account administrator with All Administration
   Capabilities permissions are listed. You can link additional accounts at any time. See
   .
5. Select Create to finish creating your organization.

   Your organization is created.

title: Generate a Stamp Using Shachihata (Japanese Version Only)
heading_path: Generate a Stamp Using Shachihata (Japanese Version Only)
text:
Learn how administrators use Shachihata to generate stamps.

Docusign is integrated with Shachihata and allows you to generate stamps
based on a Japanese last name.

1. Select 印鑑の追加
   (CREATE STAMP).

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/ehanko1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwODYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.bsrT8DOfYKieP1JB8UT-9nukI7SvydqZVcJ0pKo5Q5w&_LANG=enus)
2. Select 取得 (Get).
3. Enter a Japanese name in the field and select 印鑑の取得
   (Get Stamps).![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/ehanko2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwODYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.bsrT8DOfYKieP1JB8UT-9nukI7SvydqZVcJ0pKo5Q5w&_LANG=enus)
4. Select one of the three example stamps generated by Shachihata.
5. Select 名前
   (Name) or 名前と日付
   (Name + Date).
6. Select 次へ
   (NEXT).
7. Review the stamp name and the phonetic information, which is optional, and select
   作成
   (CREATE).

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/ehanko3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwODYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.bsrT8DOfYKieP1JB8UT-9nukI7SvydqZVcJ0pKo5Q5w&_LANG=enus)
8. Verify that your stamp displays in the list of stamps.

title: View the Bulk Actions Log
text:
Learn how account administrators view details about bulk actions in the Bulk
Actions Log and can export the information to a comma-separated value (CSV)
file.

As an account administrator, you can view details about each bulk action job and its progress
from the Bulk Actions Log. The log displays the type of
action, its status, the name of the administrator who initiated the action, and the
date of the action. Optionally, you can download the information to a
comma-separated value (CSV) file.

1. From the Accounts view, select Bulk
   Actions.
2. (Optional) Select one of the options in the Actions menu
   to view information about the action or download the log information as a CSV
   file.
   - Select **Details** to review information associated with the action,
     including any error messages.
   - Select **Download CSV** to download the log information as a CSV
     file.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Japanese IDs
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Japanese IDs
text: Learn how to add identity verification configurations for Japanese IDs.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Japanese IDs
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations for Japanese IDs
text:
To add identity verification configuration for Japanese IDs,
you must respect the following requirements:

- Your account must subscribe to the ID Verification or ID Verification Premier
  packages.
- Your account must be located in Japan, which means the account is provisioned in
  the JP1 data center.

As an administrator, you must add an ID Verification configuration that supports Japanese IDs
to use as a part of your overall effort to comply with KYC (Know Your Customer) and
AML (Anti-money Laundering) regulations.

Docusign ID Verification with Japanese IDs is a configuration
for Japanese users to verify their identity with local KYC (Know Your Customer) and
AML (Anti-Money Laundering) regulations in mind. It uses image-processing technology
and biometric authentication to verify the identity automatically. This way,
Japanese users can quickly identify as part of an eKYC-compliant digital contracting
process.

Important: We recommend that you consult with your
legal and compliance teams to assess what may be required for eKYC compliance
specific to your organization.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity Verification.
2. Select Add Verification.

   The Add Configuration screen opens.
3. Select ID Verification with Japanese IDs
4. Select Next

   The Add ID Verification with Japanese IDs screen
   opens.
5. Enter a name for the configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   Note: The system does not translate the name and
   description. Senders see the text in its original language.
6. Select the maximum number of verification attempts.

   This number represents how often recipients can attempt to verify before the
   envelope is locked. By default, ID Verification locks the envelope when
   recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their identity. Recipients must contact
   the sender to unlock the envelope.
7. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.

   - Every access: recipients must identify every time
     they access an envelope.
   - Every access until role is completed: recipients
     must identify until they sign the envelope’s documents.
   - Once: recipients must only identify once,
     regardless of whether the result was pass or not pass.
8. (Optional) Select the type of IDs you want to accept in the dropdown
   menu.
9. (Optional) Select the additional data that you want ID Evidence to store for
   each ID verification.

   If you choose to store the selfie image in ID Evidence, the image remains
   available for only 90 days after verification. After 90 days, ID Evidence
   removes the access to the selfie from the proof file. Senders can no longer
   download the selfie.

   Also, if you choose to save the front and back side
   images of ID documents, ID Evidence stores both images except for Individual
   Number Card. In this case, ID Evidence only saves the front image for
   privacy reasons.

   Note: ID Verification can only
   store the data you check if it is available in the ID document the signer
   selected for verification. For example, if you check the box for Height
   data, but the ID does not include Height, we cannot store it.
10. Select Add.

    The configuration displays in the configurations list and is available
    for senders.

title: Settings to Allow Comments on Documents and Envelopes
heading_path: Settings to Allow Comments on Documents and Envelopes
text:
Read about how account administrators configure settings for users of an account to
comment on documents in envelopes. To facilitate transactions, administrators can allow comments
and specify how often users are notified when comments are received.

Comments can help users complete transactions by enabling recipients to ask and answer
questions about specific sections of documents in envelopes.

The settings for comments apply to all users of an account. You cannot change comments
settings for some users on an account and not for others.

Note: By default, comments are disabled for an
account. To enable comments and manage the settings for them, you must be an account
administrator with All Administration Capabilities. If the comments option is not available on
your account, contact Docusign Support to enable the feature.

For information about user preferences, see [Changing the Frequency of Comment Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ded1578456661259.html&bundleId=jux1643235969954&_LANG=enus).

For information about envelope settings, see [Disabling Comments on an Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ptb1578456497413.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus).

title: IAM Plans
text:
Docusign
Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) is an AI-powered platform that
enables you to connect and optimize every step of your business processes that involve
agreements.

As a new Docusign customer or trial user, you can review plan options and
purchase an IAM plan from the Plans and Pricing page.

As an administrator on a Docusign eSignature account, you can upgrade to an IAM
plan on the Subscription and Billing page.

As an administrator on a Docusign IAM account, you can manage or upgrade your
plan, purchase extensions, and add feature capacity to your plan on the
Plan and Billing page.

Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

Note: IAM Starter, IAM Standard, and IAM Professional plans are
currently available only for customers in the United States, Australia, Canada, Great
Britain, Germany, and France.

title: Account Discovery
text:
Read about how account administrators can use the Account Discovery feature to help new
users in the same domain to join existing eSignature accounts managed by the
administrator.

If Account Discovery is enabled for an eSignature account, new users signing up for a free or
trial account can choose from a list of paid accounts from the same domain name as their
account email address. This helps new users join the same account as their team or
department. When the new users log in, they will view an Account Discovery screen
similar to this example screen.

![The screenshot shows an example of the Account Discovery screen that appears to a new user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/account-discovery-request.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwMTMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.6ZaCFQOf_c0YoTR4XbkvL4amWMLL2ypEjUvpZEoNPNU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the Account Discovery screen that appears to a new user.")

The new user can select an account and request to be added to that paid
account. The Account Discovery screen appears once during the Free and Trial users’
onboarding flow.

The account administrators will receive an email notifying them that a user has
requested to join the account. Administrators receive only one email a day containing
all requests from that day. Administrators can choose to add new users to the correct
accounts or ignore the requests.

![The screenshot shows an example of the account discovery email that the account administrator receives.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/account-discover-email.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwMTMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.6ZaCFQOf_c0YoTR4XbkvL4amWMLL2ypEjUvpZEoNPNU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the account discovery email that the account administrator receives.")

The Account Discovery feature is turned on by default for qualifying accounts. Qualifying
accounts meet all of the following criteria:

1. Accounts are not listed as government or public sector accounts.
2. Account plan type allows more than one seat or user.
3. Account administrators have a company email address domain. Personal email address
   domains are not allowed.
4. Accounts do not have SSO enabled and do not have "Prevent unmanaged signups"
   selected.

Administrators can choose to [turn off the Account
Discovery feature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=irr1699904537705.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to turn off account discovery so that your eSignature account is not found by new Free or Trial users in a company’s domain.").

title: General Authentication Settings
text:
Read about the authentication settings administrators use to enforce authentication
requirements on senders and hide access codes in routing orders.

- **Enforce authentication requirements on sender when signing**: If the sender
  is also a recipient on an envelope, then any authentication requirements set on the envelope
  must be completed for them to view and sign.
- **Hide access code in the routing order**: When enabled, the access code will only be visible to the sender and the intended recipient. All other roles in the routing order will be unable to view the access code.

title: Document Date and Time Format
text:
Read about the settings that administrators use to specify the date and time format for
documents sent from an account.

These settings establish the date and time formats when signing documents sent from the
account.

- Current Date Format: This selects the date format applied
  to all Date Signed fields on the documents. This setting also affects the Date data
  validation formatting. For details, see [Data Validation for Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=jgd1578456357365.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus).

  Note: This effect on fields with Date
  data validation is new and applies only to fields added to envelopes and templates after
  this formatting change was implemented.
- Current Time Format: This selects the time format applied
  to all Date Signed fields on the documents. The time is added to the field after the date.
  If None is selected, the time is not added to the Date Signed field.

title: Envelope Expiration
text:
Read how administrators modify the default expiration period for envelopes.

(Default: 120 days; Values: 1-999 days; no warning)

By default, requests to sign or view expire 120 days after sending the envelope. You can modify
this value as needed. You can also add the option to send signers an expiration warning.

Docusign recommends that you enter a low value for the option Days
to warn signers before expiration, such as 3 days. Warning signers of an
expiring envelope improves the outcome of getting envelopes to complete instead of expiring.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/expirationsettings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1OTQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.apU0TGrI7pdrM9FvkxC4kKSWaBPRnZW-CdzQFIiHZGc&_LANG=enus)

When an envelope expires, the status changes to Voided and it can no longer be viewed or signed by recipients.

When an in-process envelope reaches six days to expiration, a warning icon displays and the
status shows "Expiring". Hover over the status to show the expiration date.

title: Add Envelopes and Seats for Plans Purchased Online
heading_path: Add Envelopes and Seats for Plans Purchased Online
text:
Learn how administrators can add more envelopes and seats for annual or monthly plans.

As an account administrator, you can purchase more envelopes in increments of 100 for annual
plans and 10 for monthly plans. New envelopes are shared among seats. Each purchase
of 100 or 10 envelopes includes one seat.

Note: This feature is available only for Standard or Business Pro Digital
eSignature plans that were purchased online.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

   The Plan and billing page opens. ![The screenshot shows the Plan and billing page for eSignature accounts.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/PnBUpgradePlanNewDesktop.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.wBUwsVd46sxUG2IT_ww8ukbaexh4npBh9ffkSYxKaas&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Plan and billing page for eSignature accounts.")
2. Select Add Envelopes & Seats.

   The Add envelopes and seats window opens. This
   example screenshot displays an annual plan. For monthly plans, you can select
   envelopes in quantities of 10.![The screenshot shows an example adding 100 envelopes and one seat on an annual plan.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/addenvelopes2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.wBUwsVd46sxUG2IT_ww8ukbaexh4npBh9ffkSYxKaas&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example adding 100 envelopes and one seat on an annual plan.")
3. Select the number of envelopes and seats that you want to purchase.

   Choose a number in increments of 100. Every purchase of 100 envelopes includes
   one seat. The total number of envelopes can be shared among all users.
4. Review the amount due for your purchase, and select
   Purchase.

   The purchase is completed and a copy of your receipt is sent to your
   email.
5. Select Done to close the confirmation window.

The additional envelopes and seats that you have purchased are
applied to your subscription immediately.

title: Use the Template Report to Find Templates Where a Removed User Is a Recipient or Signer
heading_path: Use the Template Report to Find Templates Where a Removed User Is a Recipient or Signer
text:
Learn how to use the template report to find templates that include a removed
user.

You can use the template report to locate the templates that have a removed user as a
recipient or signer. If a template includes a removed user as a recipient or a
signer, identify the templates that require updating and notify the template owner
to make the changes. For more information about reports, see the [Using Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=cnu1642812012214&topicId=wou1578456440763.html&_LANG=enus) topic.

1. Log in to your Docusign account, and select
   Reports on the global header navigation menu.
2. Navigate to Usage > Template Report.
3. Select a time period using the template creation date.
4. Download the report.

   Note: If you expect more than 50,000 responses, schedule
   the report or send it by email.
5. Open the downloaded file in a spreadsheet tool such as Excel.
6. Filter the Signer List and Other Recipient columns by the removed user to
   identify the templates that require updating.

title: Major Sections of Docusign Account Administration
heading_path: Major Sections of Docusign Account Administration
text:
Read about the major sections of Docusign account administration
settings. Depending on the account plan type, administrators might see only some sections and
not all.

Docusign Account settings include several major sections:

Note: Not all the sections described below are
available to all account types. The sections shown depend on the type of Docusign plan that you have.

[Account Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=nfw1583277366851.html&_LANG=enus "Read how administrators manage account billing, profile, security, and branding.") - Provides information on your plan and billing, account
profile, security settings, regional settings, and branding settings.

[Users and Groups Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=dct1583277369801.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators manage individual users, groups of users, and permission profiles for an account through the Users and Groups section.") - Provides information on managing users, groups of
users, and permission profiles.

[Signing and Sending](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jrx1583277385322.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how account administrators manage the default behavior and options for all documents sent from an account through the Signing and Sending section.") - Provides information on managing the default behavior
and various options for all envelopes sent from your account.

[Integrations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=cnn1583277397175.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators manage the Docusign Connect configurations and API integration keys for their accounts from the Docusign eSignature account administration web application.") - Provides information on managing the Connect
configurations and API integration keys for your account.

[Logs of Account Changes and Bulk Actions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=xpg1583277398727.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators review and download a history of changes to accounts and the results of bulk actions. Audit logs capture events and changes made to accounts such as settings, billing information, and plan type. Bulk actions logs provide details about bulk actions such as envelope transfers and user uploads.") - Provides information on working with the audit log for
the account.

[Automate Common Post-Signature Tasks with Agreement Actions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=hzy1594673444380.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators use Agreement Actions to create and manage business rules to determine what happens to completed envelopes. The rules can trigger actions, such as archiving to a cloud storage application, starting a Docusign CLM workflow, and exporting data to a spreadsheet.") - Provides information on automating tasks including archiving to cloud storage, starting a workflow, and exporting data to a spreadsheet.

title: Reporting Permission Access Levels
text:
View the levels of access that administrators can grant through the
Reporting permission.

The following table describes the level of access provided by the
Reporting permission.

| Administrative area | Page | Level of access |
| --- | --- | --- |
| N/A | Reports | Edit[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ply1661539719027.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_1) |

[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ply1661539719027.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_1) This permission includes access to usage reports, which otherwise are only
available to full account administrators.

title: Apps and Keys
text:
Read how account administrators view and manage API integration information for their
accounts from the Apps and Keys page. The Apps and Keys page provides
API information for your account, the integration keys associated with your account, and a way
to add integration keys.

The Apps and Keys page provides API information for your account, the
integration keys associated with your account, and a way to add integration keys.

An integration key (also known as an API key) is a unique GUID used to identify your API
integration. An integration key is required to authenticate your API calls with Docusign. Your
account can have as many test integration keys as you'd like. You can also have multiple keys
in production.

Note: The full functionality of this view is only
available in the Demo environment. After a key has been moved to the Production environment,
the account it is linked to is used to manage secret keys, RSA keypairs, and redirect
URIs.

For more information about using your integration key with the Docusign API, visit the [Docusign Developer Center](https://developers.docusign.com/).

title: Envelope Download
text:
Read about the signing setting for disabling the Docusign seal that is attached to each
agreement after download.

This setting is used with regional signature validators when you want to download envelopes
without the Docusign seal. This is because the Docusign seal does not meet local
requirements or it is not a listed and approved certificate.

Note: If you have questions about using this setting, contact Docusign Support.

For more information, see the [Docusign Seal](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=uqn1725403701960.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Docusign seals that show on every envelope you download and that you can turn off to respect some local regulations.") topic and the [Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=qgz1713823940160.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Docusign seals that show on every envelope you download and that you can turn off to respect some local regulations.") topic.

- Allow agreements to download without the Docusign seal: Disable the
  generation of the Docusign seal that is attached to each agreement after
  download.

  Important: Allowing agreements to
  download without the Docusign seal is under the responsibility of the account
  administrator. When this option is enabled and the last operation in the document is a digital signature, Docusign eSignature does not
  apply the Docusign seal to the agreement. However, if the last signature in the
  docuemtnt does not rely on a digital certificate, Docusign eSignature enforces
  the Docusign seal to the envelope upon download.

title: Permission Profiles
text:
Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a
number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to
individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to
multiple users simultaneously.

A permission profile is a group of settings that determines the behavior and actions
available for a user. Administrators assign a permission profile to a user when the user is
added to an account.

Permission profiles make it easier to manage permission settings for a large number of users.
Administrators can modify the profile settings, which are applied to all users with that
profile, instead of changing permission settings for each user individually.

Docusign eSignature account administration includes three default
permission profiles:

- DS Admin: can access all eSignature features and can manage all account settings and users
  for an account.
- DS Sender: can access to basic eSignature features. No administrator access. DS Senders
  can only send envelopes and use templates and PowerForms. They cannot use bulk send, create
  templates, or manage envelopes through the API.
- DS Viewer: can view and sign envelopes in eSignature. No access to sending envelopes.

Although you cannot modify or delete the default permission profiles, you can copy them to use as
templates for other permission profiles.

Note: If your account includes the Advanced
Administration module, you can use the delegated administration options to create permission
profiles that have limited administrator capabilities.

title: Electronic Seal Administration
text:
Read about electronic seal administration and delegation of seals to other
users.

Electronic seals allow signers to digitally seal documents with their signatures.
Electronic seals are applied on behalf of legal persons. The legal person seal is
applied on behalf of an individual who is sealing organizational documents with an
individual seal as part of their individual role within the organization. For example, a
VP of Sales applies an individual seal representing them as an individual to the
official organizational documents. Users can add electronic seals to documents if this
feature is enabled in the account and they have the required permissions. Read more
about using [Electronic Seals](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xcm1643837555908&topicId=isl1578456577247.html&_LANG=enus).

The administrator can delegate the use of an electronic seal to other users in the
Docusign account. This includes the ability to delegate basic
seals to users who are authorized to use them on the basis of their relationship to the
organization, but also delegate legal person seals to people who are not associated with
the seal. The administrator can delegate a legal person seal associated with User A to
be used by User B. The user associated with the seal (User A) and the account
administrator both receive a Docusign envelope that the administrator
is authorized to delegate the seal to another user or group in the Docusign account. If User A does not want the administrator to
delegate their legal person seal to User B, User A would need to contact the
administrator to remove the permissions.

Administrators can use groups to manage who can use the seal. The administrator is
responsible for creating a group that includes the seal owner and any delegated users
and linking this group to the seal. The administrator is responsible for preventing
unauthorized account members from using the seal.

A detailed audit trail is maintained for compliance purposes. The audit logs
capture every action related to seal delegation including the seal name, the group, the
user making changes, and the event date.

For more information about managing groups, see the [Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=cuv1583277363488.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators use groups to organize users into functional units for template sharing and other functions.") topic.

title: Turn Off KBA in an Existing Identity Verification Configuration
heading_path: Turn Off KBA in an Existing Identity Verification Configuration
text:
Learn how administrators can turn off KBA when editing an existing
configuration.

You can turn off KBA in an existing Identity Verification configuration if you want recipients
to identify themselves using methods other than KBA.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. On the configuration you want to edit, select Actions > Edit.

   The Edit Configuration screen opens.
3. In the Accepted IDs section, uncheck the
   Accept all recommended ID types, issuing countries and
   regions option and select Manage.

   The Manage accepted IDs screen
   opens.
4. Type “United States” in the search field.

   The Manage Accepted IDs screen displays the
   United States entry.
5. Uncheck the KNOWLEDGE-BASED AUTHENTICATION column.
6. Select Save.

   The Edit ID Verification screen
   opens.
7. Select Save.

ID Verification updates your configuration.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations for EU Qualified Electronic Signatures
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations for EU Qualified Electronic Signatures
text: Learn how to create identity verification configurations that use the Docusign ID Verification for EU Qualified verification method.

title: Add Identity Verification Configurations for EU Qualified Electronic Signatures
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configurations for EU Qualified Electronic Signatures
text:
To add an identity verification configuration for qualified electronic signatures (QES), your account plan must include ID Verification Premier. For more information, contact your Docusign representative.

You are an administrator responsible for configuring an identity verification process compliant with the European qualified electronic signature (eIDAS) regulation. This topic shows you how to add such a configuration and customize it with your organization's requirements.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.

   The Identity Verification screen
   opens.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration screen opens.
3. Choose ID Verification for EU Qualified and select
   Next.

   Note: With this ID verification method, the signature type
   is set to IDV - DS EU Qualified. You cannot edit it.

   The Add ID Verification for EU Qualifed page
   opens.
4. Enter a name for your configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   Note: You cannot translate the name and description of a configuration. Senders see the text in the language that the administrator wrote it.
5. Select the maximum number of verification attempts.

   This number represents how many times recipients can attempt to verify before
   the envelope is locked. If recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their
   identity, ID Verification locks the envelope. Users must contact the sender
   to unlock it.
6. (Optional) Configure protection for completed agreements.
   Select Recipient always verify to ask recipients to authenticate when they attempt to access an already signed document.

   For more information, read the [ID Verification for EU Qualified Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=con1b98f947-485d-4855-b66f-97000e633a3a.html&_LANG=enus "Read about Docusign ID Verification for EU Qualified configurations and how administrators can customize it to meet their organization’s needs.") page.

   Note: If you select the Recipient always verify option, consider allowing recipients to use their Identity Wallet to streamline verification. Go to step [8](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pyx1678128186236.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=pyx1678128186236__step_w0ab1b7b3c15_w1ab1b7b3_w2ab1b7_w3ab1) to configure the Identity Wallet options.
7. (Optional) In the Stored data from IDs section, select additional data from the ID document that you want ID Evidence
   to store. For example, you can store image captures, personal information, or document details.

   You can view a list of the data that is stored by default.
8. Choose your settings for the Docusign Identity Wallet:

   Note: If you selected the Recipient always verify option on step [6](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pyx1678128186236.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=pyx1678128186236__sms_reauth_option), consider allowing recipients to use their Identity Wallet to streamline verification.

   - Select Recipients can create or update a Docusign Identity
     Wallet to allow recipients to create or update their
     Identity Wallet.
   - Select Recipients can use their Docusign Identity
     Wallet to allow recipients to identify using their
     identity wallet.
9. Select Add.

The configuration appears in the configurations list and is
available for senders.

title: View Your Organization Administrators
text:
Learn how to view a list of the organization administrators associated with your account.

Account Administrators can view a list of all the organization administrators for the organization that their account is connected to. The list shows only the active full organization administrators.

Most accounts are managed by only one organization. Some accounts might be managed by one organization while the account subscription is managed by another organization. In these cases, organization administrators for both organizations are listed.

Note:

To view this feature, an organization administrator must open the Features page of the organization and enable Share Organization Administrator Contact Details in the features list.

1. From the Accounts view, select Account Profile.
2. View the list of organization administrators by name and email address.

   ![The screenshot shows the organization administrators for the account on the Account Profile page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/org-admin-list.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NzMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.vbKguXd9spJXE16vrX-WQ544wn_v-F7XJNQpsyxMMA8&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the organization administrators for the account on the Account Profile page.")

text:
Additional user guides related to Sending Settings include:
