title: Edit Custom Fields for Envelopes
text:
Learn how administrators edit custom fields for envelopes.

As an administrator, you can edit custom fields for
envelopes.

1. From the Accounts view, select Envelope Custom
   Fields.
2. Find the field that you want to edit, and select ACTIONS > Edit.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/editenvcustomfield.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU2MTksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.an5mnswvlSD5tHroScDqZoftwoCAIB-BgxRPfnQdXps&_LANG=enus)
3. In the Edit dialog box, change the field information as needed, and select
   Save.
4. In the Apply changes to template dialog box, select whether or not you want the change
   to be applied to any templates that use the field.

title: Multiple Docusign Account Administrators
text:
Read about the importance of having two full administrators on an account. Administrators
are alerted when an account has only one full administrator. To designate another user as an
administrator, assign full administrative permissions to the user.

If you have a paid account with two or more users, each time you log in, Docusign verifies that you have more than one active full administrator on the
account. If your account has only one full administrator, an information banner displays at
the top of the page.

Having multiple full administrators maintains continuous account access if an administrator
leaves the company or is otherwise unavailable. You can review a list of users and update
their assigned permission profile from the [Users
page](https://admin.docusign.com/authenticate?goTo=users). For more information about updating user permissions, see [User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ixb1583277365937.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators manage the users in an account through the Users page. You can add, edit, close, reset passwords, and modify sharing for your users.").

title: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
heading_path: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
text:
Read about the Docusign seals that show on every envelope you download and that you can
turn off to respect some local regulations.

title: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
heading_path: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
text:
Docusign places a tamper-evident digital seal on all envelopes signed on the
Docusign Signature Service.

The Docusign seal is placed on all envelopes when they are downloaded by any sender
or signer.

The Docusign seal helps to ensure that envelopes are not altered after they have
been signed. Docusign has developed a feature that allows
customers to disable the Docusign seal for envelopes that are signed using an
Advanced, Qualified, or any other x509 certificate.

title: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
heading_path: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
Why might it be a challenge for regional validators?
text:
In some regions around the world, local regulations and policies require the digital
certificate to meet their country-specific standards. In some instances, the
Docusign seal does not meet these requirements or is not a listed and approved
certificate type. As a result, customers in these regions who use [local validators](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xcm1643837555908&topicId=lzc1652878457364.html&_LANG=enus) will not have their documents approved while the Docusign seal is affixed to their documents. For documents in these regions to be validated by the local validators, they must contain a digital signature or digital seal rather than just electronic signatures.

title: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
heading_path: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
text:
The option to enable the removal of the Docusign seal is under the responsibility of the
account administrator.

After enabling the option, Docusign will not apply the Docusign seal as long
as the last operation in the document is a digital signature.

Important: You and your users should be aware that by
removing the Docusign seal, the signed document will not contain the Envelope ID
associated to the Docusign seal signature.

title: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
heading_path: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
Why must the last operation be a digital signature?
text:
Docusign is always looking to create a safe and secure signature process
while enabling our customers to conduct business in the global marketplace. Requiring the last operation in the document is digital signature ensures that the
document is in its final form, is secure, and the signature is affixed and encrypted
in the final form in which it appears. This permits the digital signature to act
similarly to the Docusign seal to assure the customer that the document is
tamper-proofed in its final format.

title: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
heading_path: Regional Signature Validators and the Docusign Seal
What if my last operation is not a digital signature?
text:
In that case, Docusign will enforce its Docusign seal to the
envelope upon download.

title: Stamps Permission Access Levels
text:
View the levels of access that administrators can grant through the
Stamps permission in combination with the Users and
Groups access levels.

The following table describes the level of access provided by the
Stamps permission in combination with each of the
Users and Groups access levels.

Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

| Administrative area | Page | Level of access: View Only | Level of access: Edit Groups Only | Level of access: Edit Users & Groups | Level of access: Edit Users, Groups, & Administrators |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N/A | Overview | View | View | View | View |
| Account | Plan and Billing | None | None | None | None |
| Account | Account Profile | None | None | None | None |
| Account | Security Settings | None | None | None | None |
| Account | Regional Settings | None | None | None | None |
| Account | Search Settings | None | None | None | None |
| Account | Brands | None | None | None | None |
| Account | Stamps | Edit | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Account | Updates | None | None | None | None |
| Account | Value Calculator | None | None | None | None |
| Users and Groups | Users | View | View | Edit[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=qjb1661539813627.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_1) | Edit[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=qjb1661539813627.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_2) |
| Users and Groups | Permission Profiles | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Groups | View | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Users and Groups | Signing Groups | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Audit Logs | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Bulk Actions | View | View | View | View |
| Auditing | Validations | None | None | None | None |

[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=qjb1661539813627.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_1) Edit access does not include the ability to edit users with "Allow All"
administrative permissions enabled, such as those with the DS
Admin permission profile.

[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=qjb1661539813627.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_2) Edit access does not include access to Shared Access or
Shared Envelopes unless the
Envelope Sharing permission is also
selected, and does not include access to Transfer
Envelopes or Transfer
Templates unless the Transfer
Envelopes permission is also
selected.

title: Update Your Account Subscription
text:
Learn how administrators can update account subscriptions including product quantity,
payment method, and billing information.

For account subscriptions that use automatic renewal, the current subscription will renew on
the scheduled date. As an account administrator, you can make the following changes
to your subscription:

- Edit the quantity of products in the subscription
- Edit the payment method, shipping information, and billing information

Note: Subscription management is not available for all customers. If you
must make changes to your subscription and this feature is not available, contact
Docusign Support. To add or remove seats, go to [Manage Seats in Your Subscription](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=kla1705531597804.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators can change the number of seats and envelopes in their subscriptions.")﻿.

1. In the Accounts view, select Plan and Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Subscription.

   The Renewals page opens.
3. Select Manage Renewal > Edit Your Subscription.
4. Edit the quantities of each product, and select
   Next.

   ![The screenshot shows editing the quantity of envelopes in a subscription.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/renewals2png.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.eH3m1tHdwq0fpG-R8At3EWYXyLp5cR_JEemk3gzmzKU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows editing the quantity of envelopes in a subscription.")
5. (Optional) Edit the billing details including payment method, shipping details,
   and billing details, and select Next.

   ![The screenshot shows the sections where you can edit your billing information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/renewals3png.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.eH3m1tHdwq0fpG-R8At3EWYXyLp5cR_JEemk3gzmzKU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the sections where you can edit your billing information.")
6. View the terms and conditions before submitting renewal updates.

   ![The screenshot shows the summary of the changes to your subscription.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/renewals4png.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.eH3m1tHdwq0fpG-R8At3EWYXyLp5cR_JEemk3gzmzKU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the summary of the changes to your subscription.")
7. Scroll down to the page and select the checkbox to confirm that you are the
   authorized user to submit changes.

   ![The screenshot shows the checkbox that you must select to authorize the changes.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/renewals4bpng.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.eH3m1tHdwq0fpG-R8At3EWYXyLp5cR_JEemk3gzmzKU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the checkbox that you must select to authorize the changes.")
8. Select Submit to make the changes to your
   subscription.

Your subscription is updated.

title: Custom Fields for Envelopes
text:
Read about how account administrators use custom fields in envelopes to classify,
record, and track information about documents. Custom fields are not visible to
recipients.

Envelope custom fields are used to classify, record, and track information about envelopes
sent for signature. The sender enters field values when preparing an envelope. Envelope custom
fields and their values are not shown to recipients during signing. However, the fields and
their values can be seen in the Certificate of Completion by all with access to it.
Certificate of Completion access can be disabled for your account. Contact Docusign Support for more information.

The values for an envelope custom field can be free-text entries, or selected from a list of
possible values. Envelope custom fields can be required or optional. Values are entered or
selected when envelopes are created.

Note: Envelope custom fields apply only to envelopes sent for signature. They are
not applicable for documents signed using the Sign a Document feature.

As a Docusign account administrator, you can use the Envelope Custom Fields
view to manage the envelope custom fields for your account.

Note: Changes to Envelope Custom Fields are captured in your account audit
log.

title: Resources Related to Document Retention and Purging
heading_path: Resources Related to Document Retention and Purging
text:
Read about additional resources for account administrators who want to learn more about
document retention and purging.

The following resources provide account administrators with more information about document
retention and purging:

- [Document Retention and Purging Whitepaper](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/km-guide-best-practices-for-docusign-document-retention-and-purging)
- [Document Retention and Targeted Envelope Purge: What you should know](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Document-Retention-Envelope-Purge-What-you-should-know-before-using-these-features)
- [Purging Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=kcb1583277378645.html&_LANG=enus "Administrators can search for and purge all completed, declined, or voided envelopes on the account sent from or signed by a specific email address. Envelopes purged in this way are immediately moved into the purge queue.")
- [Purge Envelopes - Managing Envelopes for Senders](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=edo1578456348066.html&bundleId=oeq1643226594604&_LANG=enus)

title: Turn Off Bank Login Identity Verification for a Specific Country or Region
heading_path: Turn Off Bank Login Identity Verification for a Specific Country or Region
text:
Learn how account administrators can turn off the bank login option if they want
recipients to identify themselves only using a driver license or a passport for a specific
country or region.

Recipients who need to identify themselves using an identity verification method can choose
between using a bank login, a driver license, a passport, or KBA. You can turn off
the bank login option if you want recipients to identify themselves only using a
driver license or a passport for a specific country or region.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. Search for the configuration you want to edit.
3. Select Actions >  Edit.
4. In the Accepted IDs section, uncheck the
   Accept all recommended ID types, issuing countries and
   regions option and select Manage.

   The Manage accepted IDs screen opens.
5. Search the countries for which you want to deactivate the Bank Login identity
   verification.
6. Uncheck the ELECTRONIC ID column.
7. Select Save.

   The Edit Configuration screen opens.
8. Select Save.

The configuration is updated.

title: Transfer Sent Envelopes
text:
Learn to transfer ownership of sent envelopes from your account to another account
member.

You can transfer your own sent envelopes from within your Docusign eSignature account
view.

1. From your Docusign account, select the
   Agreements tab in the global header.
2. Locate any of your draft or sent envelopes that you want to transfer to another account
   member.
3. To transfer a single envelope:
   1. Select the action menu for the envelope and select Transfer
      Ownership.
   2. Select the new owner for the envelope.

      ![The screenshot shows the Transfer Ownership section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/transferownership01.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1NDgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.O-O634UWVed62TEqMJTWAitdcAN82NuDcYOF52WzvDo&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Transfer Ownership section.")
   3. (Optional). To add yourself as a Receives a Copy recipient, select Add
      Original Owner as Carbon Copy. The envelope displays in your Inbox after
      the transfer is complete, and you are added to the envelope routing order as a
      completed copy received recipient.
   4. Select Transfer.
4. To transfer multiple envelopes:
   1. Mark the check box for each envelope that you want to transfer.
   2. Select the overflow menu and select Transfer
      Ownership.

      ![The screenshot shows Transfer Ownership of multiple envelopes.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/transferownership02.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1NDgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.O-O634UWVed62TEqMJTWAitdcAN82NuDcYOF52WzvDo&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows Transfer Ownership of multiple envelopes.")
   3. Select the new owner for the envelopes and select
      Transfer.

      The transfer is initiated. The envelopes may remain in your account for a brief
      time until the transfer is complete. Refresh your browser for an updated view of the
      transferred items.

title: Transfer Ownership of Envelopes and Templates Between Users
heading_path: Transfer Ownership of Envelopes and Templates Between Users
text:
Learn how account administrators use Custody Transfer to transfer envelopes and templates
from one user to another.

As an account administrator, you can transfer envelopes and
templates from one user to another by using Custody Transfer.

1. From the Accounts view, select Custody
   Transfer.
2. Select Transfer Envelope or Transfer
   Template.

   All corresponding items in your account for the last three months are listed. Use
   Filter to change the list of items shown by specifying a
   different date range or other criteria, such as the template owner or envelope
   sender.
3. Select the check box for the envelopes or templates that you want to transfer, and
   select Transfer Now.
4. Select the user that the items are being transferred to. Enter a user name or email
   address, and press Enter to view names that match the search
   criteria you entered. Select the name that you want, and select
   Transfer.
5. Select Confirm to confirm the transfer.

The job is placed in a queue for processing and listed in the
Bulk Actions log. This log shows a list of bulk actions for your account and the progress of
the actions. After the job is completed, the transferred items are moved to the new owner's
account.

title: Recipient Identification Using Knowledge-Based Authentication (KBA)
text:
Read about how recipients can use KBA to verify their identity.

In addition to passports and driver licenses, recipients in the U.S. can use knowledge-based
authentication (KBA) to verify their identity for access to envelopes. This feature is
available only to the recipients in the U.S.

Note: KBA will not be turned on by default for existing Identity
Verification configurations. This feature will be enabled by default only for the
Identity Verification configurations that are created after the feature is released. As
an account administrator, you can enable KBA on existing Identity Verification
configurations by editing an existing Identity Verification configuration and checking
Knowledge-based authentication (KBA) in the Other methods of
identity verification section.

title: View and Edit Permission Profiles
text:
Learn how account administrators modify the names of permission profiles and their
settings.

As an administrator, you can modify the names of
permission profiles and their settings.

1. From the Accounts view, select Permission
   Profiles.
2. Find the permission profile that you want to edit or view.

   Note: You cannot edit the
   DS Admin, DS Sender, and DS Viewer permission profiles.
3. Select Actions > Edit next to the permission profile.
4. In the Edit Permission Profile dialog box, change the permission
   profile name and settings as needed.

   See the [Permission Profile Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mfo1583277360424.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the administrator and user permissions that administrators use to define the actions available to account users that are assigned to the Permission Profile.") for information about the settings.
5. Select Save.

The changes to the permission profile are saved.

title: Add Signing Groups
text:
Learn how administrators create signing groups and optionally set up alternate email
addresses.

As an administrator, you can create signing groups and
optionally set up alternate email addresses.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signing
   Groups.
2. Select Add Signing Group.

   Tip: You can use the
   Copy option in the Actions dropdown to create a copy of an
   existing group and then modify the members as needed using the following steps.
3. Enter a name for the new signing group. The name must be unique for all signing groups in the account.
4. Select Save.
5. Locate the signing group that you created by searching or viewing the list.
6. Select Actions > Assign Users.
7. Locate the account users that you want to add by searching or viewing the list of all
   users.
8. Select the checkboxes next to the account users that you want to add to the Selected
   list.

   You can add up to 200 users in a signing group.
9. To add a signing group member who is not part of your account:

   1. Select Add User Manually.
   2. Complete the Name and Email fields.
   3. Select Add to add the member to the group's Selected list.
10. (Optional) To have all email notifications sent to a single email address, select the
    Use an alternate email address for notifications sent to this signing
    group option and enter the email address in the Email Address field. The
    signing group members will not receive notifications at their respective email addresses,
    but they can access the envelope from within their Docusign account. Or, if they have
    access to the group email, they can use the link in the group email notification.
11. Select Assign.

The assigned users are saved as members of the signing group.
The signing group is available to all members of the account.

title: Display Language Values
text:
Look up the code for the language the user views in the user interface.

The Language value is the default language for the user. The value can be any of the codes
shown below:

title: Display Language Values
text:
- Chinese Simplified = zh\_CN
- Chinese Traditional = zh\_TW
- Dutch = nl
- English = en
- French = fr
- German = de
- Italian = it
- Japanese = ja
- Korean = ko
- Portuguese = pt
- Portuguese Brazil = pt\_BR
- Russian = ru
- Spanish = es

title: Signing Experience Settings
text:
Read about how administrators use the In-session Landing Pages and Watermark Configuration settings to specify two key behaviors of the signing experience. These settings define where signers are routed in different situations and they ensure that in-process transactions clearly reflect an incomplete signing status.

These settings determine how the signing experience is shown to signers:

- In-session Landing Pages: Determine the web pages to which PowerForm (in-session) signers are routed for a variety of situations. These users will be sent to the specified destination URLs for each situation. If no URL is specified, users will be sent to a Docusign default destination URL.
- Watermark Configuration: The Watermark feature is designed to ensure that in-process transactions cannot be printed and signed on paper and that any saved or printed in-process documents display a watermark.

  When the Watermark feature is enabled and document signing is not complete, a watermark is displayed on each page of the documents when a printable version of the document is downloaded. If the document contains digital signatures, those signatures are stripped out.

  When all recipients have signed the document, the watermark is removed from the printable version of the document and digital signatures are visible.

  Important: If the Watermark feature is active and the Allow recipient to sign on paper option is enabled, the watermark does not display when viewing or downloading documents to sign.

- Auto-Navigation: This selects the document navigation option signing recipients see for their initial review of documents during a signing session. The selected option controls the navigation behavior as the recipient steps through the fields.

  If you use Radio Buttons on your documents, the options to navigate blank required fields are recommended for a smoother signing experience.

  The available auto-navigation options are as follows:

title: Signing Experience Settings
text:
If you use Radio Buttons on your documents, the options to navigate blank required fields are recommended for a smoother signing experience.

  The available auto-navigation options are as follows:

  - Page Only: With this option, there is no automatic navigation to the next field. The signer must manually navigate through the documents. The signer can scroll through pages, select Next, or use the document map on the right side of the browser to go to specific pages.
  - Navigate required fields: The initial navigation moves to the next empty or filled-in required field. Optional fields are skipped unless they contain invalid data. For example, if a zip code field is optional and contains an invalid entry, this field is required until corrected.
  - Navigate blank required fields: The initial navigation moves to the next empty required field. Optional fields are skipped unless they contain invalid data. For example, if a zip code field is optional and contains an invalid entry, this field is required until corrected.
  - Navigate all fields: The initial and subsequent navigation moves to the next required or optional field.
  - Page then Navigate required fields: The initial navigation goes to the top of the next page and then moves to the next empty or filled-in required field on the page. If there are no required fields on the page, the navigation stops at the top of the page and then goes to the next page when the signer selects Next.
  - Page then Navigate blank required fields: The initial navigation goes to the top of the next page and then moves to the next empty required field on the page. If there are no blank required fields on the page, the navigation stops at the top of the page and then goes to the next page when the signer selects Next.
  - Page then Navigate all fields: The initial navigation goes to the top of the next page and then moves to the next required or optional field on the page. If there are no required or optional fields on the page, the navigation stops at the top of the page and then goes to the next page when the signer selects Next.
- Allow senders to override: When selected, this option allows senders to disable the signing auto-navigation option for envelopes they send.
- Enable responsive signing: When selected, account administrators have the following default view options for responsive signing on mobile devices:

  - Optimize View for User’s Device: This is the default setting for most accounts. Docusign dynamically determines whether an agreement should open in the mobile-friendly (HTML) view or the desktop (PDF) view for mobile recipients, ensuring the best possible reading experience. Optimize View for User's Device supports two internal modes:

title: Signing Experience Settings
text:
uld open in the mobile-friendly (HTML) view or the desktop (PDF) view for mobile recipients, ensuring the best possible reading experience. Optimize View for User's Device supports two internal modes:

    - AI-assisted mode (default for most accounts): Leverages AI to evaluate document readability and automatically select the best experience for mobile recipients. If the document scores well for mobile readability, it opens in the mobile-friendly (HTML) view. If not, it opens in the desktop (PDF) view to preserve formatting and readability.
      - Accounts currently using Optimize View for User’s Device will automatically use the AI-assisted mode. Accounts that have opted out of AI features will continue using Basic (non-AI) mode.
      - The existing Enable AI tools in signing for sent agreements setting on the Signing Settings page determines whether Optimize View for User’s Device runs in AI-assisted or Basic mode. To disable AI-assisted mode and continue using Basic mode, unselect this setting.![The image shows the results of the combination of the Enable AI tools in signing for sent agreements setting and Enable responsive signing setting.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/responsive-sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUzNTEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.cDlz6uQe-IERElGKOq3w2mvGddTO9E7AXvAC-_db8f0&_LANG=enus "The image shows the results of the combination of the Enable AI tools in signing for sent agreements setting and Enable responsive signing setting.")
    - Basic (non-AI) mode: Analyzes the structure and complexity of the uploaded source document to predict whether it is likely to render well in mobile-friendly HTML. It does not evaluate the generated HTML output. Instead, the optimized view is determined solely based on the characteristics of the original document, unlike AI-assisted mode, which evaluates the rendered HTML directly to assess readability and quality. This mode remains available for accounts that prefer not to use AI features.
  - Open Only in Mobile-Friendly View: All documents will open with the mobile-friendly toggle set to ON, regardless of predicted quality.

    Note: In September 2025, accounts that have the Enable mobile-friendly as the default view setting enabled will be automatically updated to the Open Only in Mobile-Friendly View setting.
  - Open Only in Desktop View: All documents will open with the mobile-friendly toggle set to OFF (PDF view), regardless of predicted quality.

title: Signing Brand Company Name
text:
Read about how administrators display a company name that is associated with a brand in
the ERSD.

If your account uses multiple signing brands, you can specify a company name in the brand that
can be used in the ERSD, as in the following example. For more information about brands, see
the [Configure Brands](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=lfr1583277366660.html&_LANG=enus "Branding offers account administrators a way to customize the Docusign experience for both senders and recipients.") topic.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Signing_Brand01.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1NjUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.L7m3njIIe5l4OHpFCsogsw0gz2vgBSdW7-wp0R_Shts&_LANG=enus)

If you set the controlling option on the Legal Disclosure settings, each time that
the field for CompanyName is used in the ERSD, it will be replaced by the company name
specified in the brand. This substitution only works if you use the default legal disclosure
or if you apply the CompanyName the merge fields in a custom ERSD.

title: Deactivate Identity Verification Configurations﻿
text:
Learn how account administrators can deactivate identity verification configurations
without deleting it.

You can restrict an Identity Verification configuration from being used by deactivating it
instead of deleting it.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. Search the configuration you want to deactivate and select one of the following
   items in the Actions column:

   - If the configuration was created by an administrator on your account,
     select Edit > Deactivate.
   - If the configuration was created by Docusign, select View > Deactivate.
3. Select Deactivate in the confirmation message.

   Note: If backward compatibility is enabled on your account
   and a particular configuration is being used to ensure backward
   compatibility, it cannot be deactivated.

The configuration is now deactivated and cannot be
used.

title: Delete Document Custom Fields
text:
Learn how account administrators delete document custom fields when the fields are no
longer needed.

As an account administrator, you can delete document custom
fields when they are not needed.

1. From the Accounts view, select Document Custom
   Fields.
2. The page has a list of document custom fields for the account. Find the custom field
   that you want to delete and select Actions > Delete.

title: Reminders and Expirations - Account Defaults
text:
Read about the settings for the default reminder and expiration behavior for documents
sent from an account. Account administrators can determine the settings to be used on all
envelopes or allow users to specify the settings on the envelopes they send.

These settings are the default behavior for all envelopes sent from your account. As an
account administrator, you can choose to enforce these settings for all envelopes, or allow
users to modify the values for each envelope they send.

Note: When sending using the eSignature API, the API doesn't default to
these settings unless you explicitly set useAccountDefaults to True in your API call. If you
are sending using the API, refer to the [From the Trenches: Reminders and Expiration](https://www.docusign.com/blog/dsdev-trenches-reminders-expirations) article for more
information.

Note: Users can always use the [resend envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=hqt1578456337100.html&bundleId=oeq1643226594604&_LANG=enus) feature to send a manual reminder to
recipients whose turn it is to sign.

You must be an account administrator with All Administration Capabilities to manage these
settings. See [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") for details.

For information on how senders can modify reminder and expiration settings on each envelope
they send, see the [Set Advanced Options for Your Signers, Envelopes, and Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=tfm1578456367923.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus) topic.

title: Add Envelope Custody Transfer Rules
text:
Learn how to add envelope custody rules. As an account administrator, you can create
rules to automatically transfer envelopes when their status changes to Sent or
Complete.

Custody transfer rules can be set up to transfer envelopes from an individual user or group
of users when the envelope status changes to Sent or Completed. Envelopes can only be
transferred to users in the same account.

Note: You must be a Docusign account administrator with All Administration Capabilities to add custody transfer
rules.

1. From the Accounts view, select Custody
   Transfer.
2. Select Add Rule.

   The Add Custody Transfer Rule page displays.
3. In the From section:
   1. Select if the transfer is from a single user (From User) or from all the users in a group (From Group).
   2. Select the user or group that documents are transferred from. Multiple users or
      groups can be selected, but users and groups cannot be selected in the same
      rule.
4. In the To section:
   1. Select the user that the envelopes will be transferred to.
   2. Select the destination folder for the envelopes in the Folders list.
5. In the Transfer Time section:
   1. (Optional) You can clear the Add Original Owner as Carbon Copy setting to remove the original sender entirely from the envelope transaction. This option is selected by default and adds the original sender as a “Receives a copy” recipient for the envelopes they send. If you clear the setting, the original sender cannot access the envelopes or view the documents and will not receive notifications.
   2. Specify when the envelopes will be transferred: On Send or On Complete.
   3. (Optional) For On Send transfer rules, you can select the Transfer prior
      to first send option. When selected, ownership is transferred before the
      envelopes are sent and the new owner becomes the sender in all emails to recipients.
      The original creator will be added as a “Receives a copy” recipient only, provided the
      Add Original Owner as Carbon Copy option is selected.

      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/transfertime.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU0ODIsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.5YAdH1oQ5_ouWrVA5ymcLpAXbVIBTLhUw85_UrGJ_E0&_LANG=enus)

      Important: For On Send transfer rules, if the Transfer prior to
      first send option is not selected and all recipients are in the same
      routing order, the recipients receive the email invitation from the original sender.
      Subsequent email messages are from the user to which the envelope is transferred.

      If the Transfer prior to first send option is not
      selected and there are recipients in multiple routing orders, the recipients in
      the first routing order receive the email invitation from the original sender,
      while subsequent recipients in the routing order receive the email invitation from
      the user to which the documents are transferred.
6. Select Save. The rule is saved, enabled, and added to the
   Custody Transfer rule list on the Custody transfer page.

   Note: If the transfer From
   or To user is closed or the destination Folder is deleted, the transfer rule is
   automatically disabled and cannot be enabled until the error condition is
   corrected.

title: User Lists and Permission Profiles Downloads
heading_path: User Lists and Permission Profiles Downloads
text:
Read about how administrators download assigned permission profiles and permission
settings for account users as a comma-separated value (CSV) file. Administrators can download
just the permission profile name or a full list of permissions for users.

As a Docusign account administrator with User delegated permissions or
greater, you can download a list of the users on your account and their assigned permission
profiles. You can optionally include a full list of the permission settings and their values
through the Include user permissions option.

The downloaded comma-separated value (CSV) file includes the following information:

- Email ("UserEmail")
- Status ("Status")
- User First Name ("FirstName")
- User Last Name ("LastName")
- Job Title ("UserTitle")
- Company Name ("CompanyName")
- Permission Profile ("PermissionSet")
- Added Date ("AddedDate")
- API User Name ("APIUserName")
- Groups ("Group")

title: CSV File Format for User Updates
heading_path: CSV File Format for User Updates
text:
Read about the format of the comma-separated value (CSV) file administrators use to
upload updates to multiple users.

The comma-separated value (CSV) file requires a specific format for the user updates to be
successfully uploaded and applied.

title: CSV File Format for User Updates
heading_path: CSV File Format for User Updates
text:
The first line of the file is the header row, which defines the columns. The header row is
followed by rows of user data. Each row corresponds to a user.

Two columns are required in the CSV file: UserEmail and
APIUserName. Other columns are optional.

The following table contains the acceptable column header values. The header values
are not required to be in the order listed in the table and are not case-sensitive.
However, for the upload to be successful, the header value text in the CSV file must
match the value text.

The CSV file cannot contain columns with header values other than those listed. If
extra columns are present, the updates cannot be made.

**Acceptable column header values for the user updates CSV file**

| Header Row Value\* | Description |
| --- | --- |
| UserEmail | The user's complete email address. Required column. This value must match the existing email address for the user and cannot be changed. To update a user's email address, contact the user and request that they follow these steps in [Change Your Email Address](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=sdb1578456444133.html&_LANG=enus). |
| APIUserName | The unique user ID. This column is required. |
| FirstName | The first name of the user. |
| LastName | The last name of the user. |
| PermissionSet | The user's permission profile. The value for PermissionSet must match an existing permission profile for the account. This value is not case-sensitive. For example, for the default permission profile "DS Sender" you can enter "ds sender", "DS SENDER", and "DS Sender". |
| JobTitle | The job title of the user. |
| CompanyName | The user's company name. |
| Group | The user's assigned groups. The Group values must match existing Group names for the account. Additional Group columns can be added to the file to add users to more than one group.  You do not need to add users to the Everyone group, since all new users are automatically added to that group. |
| AddressLine1 | The user's address - first line. |
| AddressLine2 | The user's address - second line. |
| City | The user's city name. |
| StateRegionProvince | The user's regional location. |
| PostalCode | The user's postal code. |
| Phone | The user's phone number. |
| Language | The display language for the user's Docusign account. See the [list of language codes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wat1583277427901.html&_LANG=enus "See the values for the language codes that administrators use in the CSV upload file. Administrators specify the default language for users during bulk add and bulk update operations."). |

title: CSV File Format for User Updates
heading_path: CSV File Format for User Updates
text:
Add the user information in the lines below the header row. Use one row per user. Unless
you are using Microsoft Excel, you must separate each value with a comma (used as
the delimiter).

If you are using Microsoft® Excel® to create your file, you can enter the header values,
such as UserName and UserEmail, in different columns on the first line. Enter the
user information on subsequent lines, and save the file as a CSV file. You do not
need to add commas. Excel automatically adds the commas when you save the file.

See the example in the following section.

title: CSV File Format for User Updates
heading_path: CSV File Format for User Updates
text: ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/uploadupdateexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxODcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.jXWGiUgLz6t4mdqdFEFbPpX0C3DZHzbOJouoiejcc6M&_LANG=enus)

title: Email Archive Configuration View
text:
Read about the Email Archive Configuration view and how administrators use it to see
active, pending, and closed email archive addresses by account.

The [Email Archive Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=xjm1583277371373.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators can set up email addresses that automatically receive copies of Docusign signature requests and other email notifications for a sender’s account archiving and regulatory compliance purposes..") view shows a list of active, pending, and closed email
archive addresses associated with the account. This view allows you to add, close, and view
the history of the email archive addresses. An account can have up to five active or pending
email archive addresses.

When an email address is added to the list, Docusign sends a validation email to that email
address. The validation email contains a link that, when selected, activates the email
address. If the link is not selected, the email address remains in a pending status and email
copies are not sent to the email address.

To ensure that Docusign emails are not going to junk or spam folders for
the active email addresses, you can allow email from dse@docusign.net and the domains
@docusign.net and @docusign.com. Optionally, you can allow emails for specific IP addresses.
For more information about the current Docusign IP address ranges, see [Security for Docusign eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=esignature&_LANG=enus) in the Docusign
Trust Center.

title: Plan and Billing Information
text:
Read about how administrators review and modify subscription and billing information for accounts.

As the administrator on your Docusign eSignature account, you have access to certain billing and usage details in the Plan and Billing page. For example, you can see exactly how many envelopes you have sent and how many you have remaining for the current billing period. You can also make changes to billing-related information, such as your payment information.

Note: Some billing information is only available for certain entry-level accounts (Free, Personal, and Individual accounts). If your account type does not include usage details, you can also find much of the information using reporting. For more information, see the [Using Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=cnu1642812012214&topicId=wou1578456440763.html&_LANG=enus) topic.

Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

title: Update User Email Addresses
text:
Read about how organization administrators can bulk update user email addresses using a CSV file.

Docusign organization administrators with the Administrators and Users Administrators permission profiles with a claimed domain can update email addresses for users on their domain.

You can update email addresses for your users if the following conditions are met:

- The organization has claimed the domain. For more information, see the [Domains](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bzr1583359141662.html&_LANG=enus "Read about Step 1 of the process to set up and enable SSO for your Docusign organization and additional information about reserving domains.") topic.
- The user's email address is on the domain. For example, if your domain is www.example.com, the user's email would be user@example.com.
- If the organization has more than one claimed domain, you can also update the domain of the user to match another claimed domain.

Note: You cannot change an email address for a user on a domain you have not claimed.

To change a user's email address, download and populate the sample CSV from Users > Bulk Update > Download Update template. The UserEmail column will remain unchanged and should contain the current user's email address.

Add the UpdatedUserEmail column, and enter the new email address you want to use. After you complete the import, the user's email address is updated.

title: Set Document Retention Periods
text:
Learn how administrators modify the document retention policy. Administrators can specify
the number of days that documents are retained. They can also specify whether fields and
personally identifiable information are removed or redacted when documents are
purged.

As an administrator you can modify the document retention
policy. For example, you can specify the number of days that documents are retained. You can
also specify how personally identifiable information is treated when documents are
purged.

1. From the Accounts view, select Document
   Retention.
2. Select Enable document retention policy.
3. In the Keep for this many days field, enter the number of days
   you want to keep documents before sending them to the purge queue. For all envelopes sent
   from your account, the documents from each completed, voided, or declined envelope will be
   moved at the end of the retention period to a [purge queue](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=rlk1578456343945.html&_LANG=enus) (14 days under normal queue processing conditions), and then permanently removed from the system. The time is measured from the date that the envelope is completed, voided, or declined and is completed during overnight processing.

   Values: 1-9999

   Note: When you activate or change a retention policy, affected envelopes are added
   to the purge queue within 24 hours, under normal queue processing conditions. The policy
   is applied to all completed, voided, or declined envelopes in your
   account.
4. (Optional) If necessary, select Remove fields and metadata and Redact personally identifiable information.

   - When **Remove fields and metadata** is selected, Docusign deletes the envelope
     documents as well as any fields that were added to the documents and any API
     attachments that were added to the envelope from the system.
   - When **Redact personally identifiable information** is selected, Docusign redacts all personally identifiable information from the
     envelope, certificate of completion, and history. Fields that store personal data,
     such as name, physical address, email, and IP address, are replaced with the text
     “Redacted”. Time stamps for when actions took place are left intact, but you won’t be
     able to identify the person who took that action.

     Note: The
     process of redacting personal data cannot be undone. Each individual’s personal
     information on an envelope will be redacted from the envelope, rendering the audit
     log and certificate of completion untraceable.
   - If these options are not selected, when envelopes are purged, Docusign deletes only the envelope documents from the system.
5. Select Save.

   Document retention is set to remove your completed, voided, or declined documents from
   the Docusign system after the specified retention period and subsequent
   [purge queue](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=rlk1578456343945.html&_LANG=enus).

   Note: To disable the policy,
   clear the Enable document retention policy checkbox and select
   Save. Disabling an existing policy will remove any envelopes
   currently in the purge queue.

title: Signing Options Limits
text:
Read about how administrators limit the signature style options available to signers.
Signers have three options for signature styles. They can choose a pre-defined style, draw a
signature, or upload an image file. Administrators can also prevent recipients from changing
their name when they adopt a signature.

The three possible methods that recipients can use to sign are select a pre-defined style,
draw a signature, or upload an image file of their signature. To limit recipients' signing
options, you can choose to disable all but one of the three options (draw, upload, or select a
style).

The Lock Recipient Name option prevents recipients from changing their
name when adopting a signature. The spelling used by the sender for the recipient's name is
how they must sign the document.

title: Subscribe to Account Administrator Email Notifications
heading_path: Subscribe to Account Administrator Email Notifications
text:
Learn how to subscribe to account administrator email notifications. Account
administrators can choose the types of notifications to receive by email.

You can choose the types of notifications to receive by email.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Overview to view the Notifications section.
2. Next to Filters, select the Notification Settings icon.

   The Notification Preferences opens.
   ![The screenshot shows the Notification Preferences modal containing the notification types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/af.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Ij7EIbp-o4gBlt53Z3Zuc7IqLUZjGk_GKWNtHmabyNY&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Notification Preferences modal containing the notification types.")
3. Select the checkboxes for notification types that you want to subscribe
   to.
4. Select Save.

Your notification subscription settings are saved.

title: Download Permission Profiles Data
text:
Learn how account administrators download the data associated with permission profiles as
a comma-separated value (CSV) file.

Administrators can download a list of permission profiles
and the data associated with them as a comma-separated value (CSV) file.

1. From the Accounts view, select Permission
   Profiles.
2. Select Download Permission Profile.

The data associated with the permission profile is exported to a CSV file and saved to your
local Downloads folder.

title: Destination URLs for Post-Signing Navigation
text:
Read about how administrators can optionally use destination URLs to keep users on their
sites after they complete signing or decline to sign.

Note: These features are temporarily disabled for account plans purchased
on the web after November 20, 2024. For more information, contact Docusign
Support.

As an optional part of a signing brand, you can specify the web page displayed after certain
recipient actions, such as when they complete signing or decline to sign. If your organization
embeds the signing experience into another site, or uses PowerForms, this can be a useful
feature to keep your signers on your site, rather than have them navigate away to pages on the
Docusign domain. For more information about how destination URLs are used
with PowerForms, see [Branding Considerations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jbx1643062255110&topicId=plu1578456647555.html&_LANG=enus).

In addition to controlling the navigation, you can opt to include contextually relevant
parameters to your landing page. You can pass the envelope ID, the recipient's name or email
address, and any envelope custom fields defined for your account.

title: Reset User Passwords with the AI Agent
heading_path: Reset User Passwords with the AI Agent
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign AI Agent to reset user passwords, resend invitations, and reactivate users.

Note: This feature is currently available only to account administrators on digital plans, either paid or trial, in the US region and the English locale.

1. In the Accounts view, hover over the AI icon and select Try the Docusign AI Agent.

   ![The screenshot shows the Try the Docusign AI Agent option on the Overview page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/ai-agent-pw1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxNjUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.aq35LjQjIpPLaY7e_Xorhap8UZWKiPxL5yJhbR1LmB0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Try the Docusign AI Agent option on the Overview page.")

   The AI agent chat window opens.
2. Select the Reset a user’s password quick action.

   ![The screenshot shows the Reset a user's password quick action.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/ai-agent-pw2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxNjUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.aq35LjQjIpPLaY7e_Xorhap8UZWKiPxL5yJhbR1LmB0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Reset a user's password quick action.")
3. Follow the prompts from the AI agent and provide the user name and email address.

   You can provide multiple users in the same request.
4. Review the user results found by the AI agent, and confirm if you want to continue with the password reset.

   If the user is pending, closed, not found, or ineligible due to SSO, follow the alternative steps provided by the AI agent. For example, you might need to resend the invitation, reactivate the user, or add a new user.

title: View or Edit a User
text:
Learn how account administrators view or edit user information. Only users with the Active status are available to edit.

User details include contact information, permission profile, groups, and the API Username. View or edit users with the Active status. Users with the Closed or Pending status are view-only.

Note: The API Username is used in the OAuth JSON Web Token grant flow and other API methods. For more information about the OAuth JWT grant flow, visit the  [Docusign Developer Center](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt/).

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Locate the user that you want to view or edit.

   Note: Only users with the Active status are available to edit.
3. In the Action menu for the selected user, select View or Edit.

   The user information page opens.

   ![The screenshot shows the User Profile information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/view-user.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxNDMsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.0cdHTYkbPUhMJJylEElo69ZYtbCkh9e7zNvw2ZD-nPE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the User Profile information.")
4. Review the user details and update the entries as needed.
5. Select Save Changes after making updates.

The changes are saved and the Users page displays.

title: Delete Stamps
text:
Learn how administrators remove stamps that are not needed.

As an administrator, you can remove stamps that are no longer
needed.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Stamps.
2. Choose the stamp row, and select Actions > Delete.
3. Select Delete to confirm that you want to delete the
   stamp.

title: Envelope Transfer Permission Access Levels
text:
View the levels of access that administrators can grant through the Envelope
Transfer permission in combination with each of the Users and
Groups access levels.

The following table describes the level of access provided by the Envelope
Transfer permission in combination with each of the Users
and Groups access levels.

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| Users and Groups | Permission Profiles | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Groups | View | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Users and Groups | Signing Groups | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Signing Settings | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Sending Settings | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Identity Verification | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Email Preferences | None | None | None | None |
| Signing and Sending | Email Archive Configuration | None | None | None | None |
