[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=tes1661539690800.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_1) Edit access does not include the ability to edit users with "Allow All"
administrative permissions enabled, such as those with the DS
Admin permission profile.

[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=tes1661539690800.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_2) Edit access does not include access to Shared Access or
Shared Envelopes unless the
Envelope Sharing permission is also
selected.

title: Options for Envelope Recipients
text:
Read about the settings that administrators use to define the actions required for
recipients of envelopes. For example, administrators can specify whether a reason is
required when users decline to sign and specify the types of devices that can be used for
signing.

These settings provide options and actions related to envelope recipients.

- Require a reason when a recipient declines to sign: When
  selected, this option requires signers to provide a reason why they are declining to
  sign a document.
- Allow recipients to sign on paper: When selected, signers
  are allowed to choose to sign on paper instead of signing electronically. This
  setting controls the initial setting for templates and for envelopes that are not
  created from templates. If an envelope is created from a template, the template’s
  Allow recipients to sign on paper setting is used for the
  envelope. This account setting does not control envelopes created through the
  eSignature API.
- Allow senders to override sign on paper: When selected,
  allows senders to override the sign on paper setting when sending envelopes. See
  [Set Advanced Document Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tfm1578456367923.html&_LANG=enus) for more information about the sign on paper option.
- Allow recipients to sign on a mobile device: When
  selected, signers can use a mobile device to sign documents.
- Allow recipients to change signing responsibility: When
  selected, the signer can change the signing responsibility to another person or have
  the envelope signed by a delegate.
- Allow senders to override change signing responsibility: When
  selected, allows senders to override the change signing responsibility setting when
  sending envelopes. See [Set Advanced
  Document Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tfm1578456367923.html&_LANG=enus) for more information about allowing recipients to change signing responsibility.
- Allow recipients to edit documents: When selected, a user
  can choose to allow signers to edit document information using the Document Markup
  option. See [Allow Signers to Negotiate Changes with Document Markup](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=hgz1578456364517.html&_LANG=enus) for more information.
- Allow recipients to create a Docusign account: When
  selected, this option allows recipients to create their own Docusign account after they have finished signing.
- Allow offline signing on a mobile device: When selected,
  this option allows users to create, send, and sign documents using the Docusign mobile applications while offline.
- Allow recipients to sign documents offline on a mobile
  device: When selected, documents that are signed with the Docusign mobile app using offline signing are synchronized with
  this account.

  Note: Offline
  signing is only currently available for Docusign for iOS.
- Ask for confirmation if recipients try to leave an active signing
  session: When selected, a prompt appears if signers try to exit the
  browser, change the URL, or reload the signing session page but have not completed
  their signing session.
- Remind recipients to finish the signing session after they fill in
  required fields: When selected, a slide-up bar notifies the signer
  that they must select Finish after completing all tabs in a signing session.
- Select the signature pad type used by your organization:
  This option is used to select the signature pad used with your account. See [Signature Pads](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=emp1638237872959.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the additional hardware and software that is required to use signature pads for signing. The signing process is different with various signature pads.") for more information about using a signature pad
  with Docusign.

title: Options for Envelope Recipients
text:
dware and software that is required to use signature pads for signing. The signing process is different with various signature pads.") for more information about using a signature pad
  with Docusign.

  Select one of the following signature pad
  types:

  - None: This turns off the signature pad feature for
    your account.
  - Topaz: Enables your account to work with Topaz
    Systems, Inc™ signature pad devices.
  - ePad with IntegriSign: Enables your account to work
    with ePadLink ePad-ink with IntegriSign™ signature pad devices.
  - Topaz SigPlusExtLite: Enables your account to work
    with a Topaz Systems signature pad device with Chrome, Firefox, Opera, and
    Edge browsers. This selection requires installation of the SigPlusExtLite
    extension.

title: Organizations
text:
Read about how administrators manage multiple accounts at once by creating an
organization. Docusign administrators can manage users and settings for multiple accounts from
one central location.

To create an organization, you must be an account administrator with the All Administration
Capabilities permissions. The account you use to create the organization must be enabled with
Docusign Admin.

Note: If you do not see the
Get Started section in your Docusign account, Docusign Admin organization administration is not enabled on the account.
Contact Docusign Support for assistance.

When you create an organization, you are automatically the organization administrator, and
the eSignature account used to create the organization is the default account. An organization's default account is used for just-in-time
provisioning when new users are added.

After you create an organization, you can update the name and description at any time through
the Features page.

title: Assign Brands to Groups
text:
Learn how administrators assign brands to groups for group members to use when they
create envelopes and templates.

As an account administrator, you can assign brands to groups
for group members to use.

1. From the Accounts view, select Groups.
2. Locate the group that you want to assign a brand to by searching or scanning the list,
   and select it.
3. Select the Brands tab.
4. Select Assign Brand.

   The brands configured for your account are listed.
5. Select the checkbox next to the brands that you want to assign to the group, and select
   Assign.

All group members can now use the assigned brands on the
envelopes they send and templates they create.

title: Define Sending Settings
text:
Learn how account administrators can modify sending settings for documents sent from your
account.

As an administrator, you can modify sending settings for
documents sent from your account.

1. From the Accounts view, select Sending
   Settings.
2. Adjust your sending settings as needed.

   See the [Fields and Properties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=xgg1583277350154.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators configure optional fields and properties in Sending Settings.") topic for descriptions of the sending
   settings.
3. Select Save.

title: Export to Spreadsheet Action
text:
Read about how account administrators use the action Export to
Spreadsheet with agreement rules to export data from completed envelopes to
spreadsheets.

You can select a spreadsheet that is stored in a connected application to export and store
data from completed envelopes.

Agreement Actions supports several cloud storage providers: Google Drive, Microsoft SharePoint
Online, and Microsoft OneDrive for Business.

The first time you export to a spreadsheet, the columns headers are determined by the
first envelope that is evaluated by the associated rule. The sheet will use these
headers for future exports. For best results, create separate sheets for each
template to ensure that all data is captured.

The following data is exported to the spreadsheet:

- Standard envelope information, including the envelope subject, ID, and the
  sender name and email address
- The document name and ID
- Recipient name and email address
- Value entered in the document fields by the different recipients
- Template details, including the template name
- Value entered in the envelope custom fields by the senders and template
  creators

![The screenshot shows an example of the data exported to a spreadsheet.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/AAActions2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.sNt83EVSmis_aj1u0CkPbC9VKn-dfZAulflEQJafV-U&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the data exported to a spreadsheet.")

Note: The data is exported to a Raw
Data tab in the spreadsheet. A best practice is to leave this tab
untouched and rename and filter the data columns and values in a separate
tab.

title: Custody Transfer Rules for Envelopes
text:
Read about how account administrators use custody transfer rules to automatically
transfer ownership of envelopes after they are sent or completed.

In addition to manual transfer of envelopes, you can establish custody transfer rules to
automatically transfer envelope ownership from an individual user, or a group of users, to
another user when envelopes are sent or completed. When ownership is transferred, the
envelopes are removed from the original owner’s Sent list and added to the list for the new
owner. The original owner will only have access to the documents if the transfer rule adds
them as a carbon copy recipient.

For more information, see [Custody Transfer Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=xqx1583277378404.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how account administrators use the Custody Transfer page to manage rules that automatically transfer ownership of envelopes from one user to another.").

title: Turn Off the AI Tools for Signing Setting in Account Administration
heading_path: Turn Off the AI Tools for Signing Setting in Account Administration
text:
Learn how to disable the AI tools for signing in Account Administration to prevent account users from accessing AI-Assisted Summary and Extraction features.

You can choose to turn off the Enable AI tools in signing for sent agreements setting for your account users. When you turn off this setting, your account users cannot view or access the agreement summaries provided by the AI-Assisted Summary and Extraction feature.

Note:

- This setting allows administrators to turn off the feature for envelopes sent from the account. For any envelope received by users on the account, AI features may still appear if the sending account has them enabled.
- Any new AI features introduced into the Signing experience for sent agreements after this setting is turned off are also managed by this setting and are not auto-enabled.

1. In the Accounts view, select Signing Settings.
2. ![The screenshot shows the AI tools setting to turn off the feature for signers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/AI-tools-sign-settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUzODIsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.jKmUjseEVF06ED9x7pRkfC7I4Fs2eolx4scyjb76zJY&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the AI tools setting to turn off the feature for signers.")In the AI Tools in the Signing Experience section, uncheck the Enable AI tools in signing for sent agreements setting.
3. Select Save Changes.

The change to the setting is saved. Your account users cannot view or access the agreement summaries provided by the AI-Assisted Summary and Extraction feature.

title: Change the AI Data Controls Setting for an Account
heading_path: Change the AI Data Controls Setting for an Account
text:
Learn how to change the AI Data Controls setting for your account to control data sharing for AI model training.

The AI Data Controls setting provides additional control within the product to customers who have agreed to share their data for training Docusign AI models to deliver improved products. The AI Data Controls setting is an account-level setting. All data is aggregated and anonymized before being used for AI training. For more details, see the [AI Data Controls Setting FAQs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=dcs1708531444159.html&_LANG=enus) .

This setting is unavailable in some accounts. If the setting has not been rolled out to your account, your account's data will not be used to help improve Docusign AI models.

This setting is available for the following accounts:

- Accounts with Agreement Manager
- Accounts enabled with the Agreement Manager Trial
- Accounts with Workflow Builder
- CLM accounts (excludes CLM.CM accounts)
- Other accounts where an organization has agreed to share anonymized data for AI training in its Docusign service agreement.

This setting does not apply to developer, Sandbox, or demo accounts.

1. From the Accounts view, select AI Data Controls from the ACCOUNT section in the navigation pane on the left.

   The AI Data Controls page displays.![The screenshot shows the AI Data Controls page in the Accounts view.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/ai-data-setting.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUxMTAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Bzab5tQ6t7MqUXtUYJlBWVhuXXuRc0f3er9z9Q4K0Kg&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the AI Data Controls page in the Accounts view.")
2. Select the toggle to change the setting for Allow my anonymized data to be used to improve AI-assisted experiences.

   - Turn off the toggle to revoke consent to Docusign using your data for AI training.
   - Turn on the toggle to consent to Docusign using your data for AI training.
3. Select Save.

Your change to the AI Data Controls setting is saved.

title: Customize your Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
heading_path: Customize your Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
text: Learn how to copy and edit the read-only Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan configuration to meet your specific requirements.

title: Customize your Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
heading_path: Customize your Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration
text:
To customize the Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan configuration with your specific needs, you must have access to the read-only configuration. For more information about the requirements for using this configuration, read the [ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=con136df38a-18a8-4c57-9d7c-69d9e8e9d9cd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan read-only configuration. Learn about its configuration and the parameters that you can edit in your customized configuration.") page.

You are the administrator of a Docusign account hosted in Japan that already has access to the read-only Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan configuration. As your organization wants to implement its own configuration, you must copy the default configuration in order to customize it to fit your specific requirements. If you want to start setting the configuration from scratch, you may add it from the Add Configuration module.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity Verification.
2. Locate the read-only Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan configuration.
3. Select Create a Copy.
4. Customize the available settings for this configuration.

   For details on which settings you can change, read the [ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=con136df38a-18a8-4c57-9d7c-69d9e8e9d9cd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Docusign ID Verification with IC-chip Reading for Japan read-only configuration. Learn about its configuration and the parameters that you can edit in your customized configuration.") page.
5. Select Add.

   ID Verification saves your configuration.

title: View Feature Usage
text: As an administrator, you can review feature usage information to track feature status, usage, and increase feature quantity.

title: View Feature Usage
text:
1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and Billing.
2. View your reports in the Feature usage section.

   ![The screenshot shows the Feature usage section on the Plan and Billing page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/featureusage.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MTcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.wqaCV5RtMqf5tL4qcNv4GiRGE6en2yfY2L-IDacZ-E0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Feature usage section on the Plan and Billing page.")
3. Select view your reports for a detailed and filterable summary of your usage.

You can view your feature usage, learn more about each feature, and increase feature quantity.

title: View the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance read-only configuration
heading_path: View the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance read-only configuration
text: Learn how account administrators can view the parameters of the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration.

title: View the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance read-only configuration
heading_path: View the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance read-only configuration
text:
To view the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration, your account plan must include ID Verification, ID Verification Premier, or IAM for CX. For more information, contact your Docusign representative.

Note: ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance is a read-only configuration. You cannot edit its parameters.

You are an administrator responsible for configuring an identity verification process compliant with the 23 NYCRR Part 500 cybersecurity regulation. You want to access the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration to check that it fits your requirements. You may also want to review its parameters before you activate it.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity Verification.

   The Identity Verification screen opens.
2. Locate the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration.
3. Select View in the Actions column.

   The View ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance page opens and
   shows the configuration settings. For more information about the available parameters,
   read the [ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=con427cc6d7-6044-4e8b-aaff-a2a09cbf69ae.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration. This ID Verification configuration helps organizations to comply with the 23 NYCRR Part 500 cybersecurity regulation for U.S.-based recipients.") page.

   Note: You cannot edit the parameters of the
   ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance
   configuration.

title: View the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance read-only configuration
heading_path: View the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance read-only configuration
text: Activate the ID Verification for NYCRR500 Compliance configuration. For more information, read the [Activate Identity Verification Configurations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ett1690403409655.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how account administrators activate identity verification configurations.") page.

title: Add Identity Verification Configuration with CLEAR
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configuration with CLEAR
text: Learn how to add identity verification configurations supporting the CLEAR electronic ID

title: Add Identity Verification Configuration with CLEAR
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configuration with CLEAR
text:
You must have full account administrator or delegated permissions to enable CLEAR for your configuration. You must also have subscribed to the “ID Verification” or “ID Verification Premier” packages.

Note: CLEAR identity verification only applies to U.S. residents.

As an administrator, you must add an ID Verification configuration that supports the CLEAR electronic ID for U.S. residents.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity Verification.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration screen opens.
3. Select one of the following methods:

   - ID Verification
   - ID Verification with Liveness

   For more information about those ID Verification methods, read the [Verification of Government-Issued IDs for Simple and Advanced Signatures](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=zty1578456548276.html&_LANG=enus) and the [Verification of Government-Issued IDs with Liveness Detection for Simple and Advanced Signatures](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=qwg1688383833351.html&_LANG=enus) pages.
4. Select Next.

   The Add ID Verification page opens.
5. Enter a name for the configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   The system does not translate the name and description. Senders see the text in its original language.
6. Select the maximum number of verification attempts.

   This number represents how often recipients can attempt to verify before the envelope is locked. By default, ID Verification locks the envelope when recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their identity. Recipients must contact the sender to unlock the envelope.
7. Select the level of variation for name acceptance standards.

   Use this setting to match the recipient's name with the name in the ID. You can choose one of the following variations:
   - Maximum variation: Accepts additional variations of middle names, suffixes, equivalent names, and tolerates minor typing mistakes. The Maximum variation option is the default selection. It is not compatible with the IDV - DS EU Advanced signature type.
   - Moderate variation: Authorizes additional variations of middle names and suffixes.
   - Minimal variation: Authorizes minor variations of first and last names only.

   Note: For more information about this setting, see the [ID Name Matching](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=rhb1657542911402.html&_LANG=enus) page.
8. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.

   - Every access: recipients must identify themselves every time they access an envelope.
   - Every access until role is completed: recipients must identify themselves until they sign the envelope’s documents.
   - Once: recipients must only identify once.
9. Uncheck the Accept all recommended ID types, issuing countries, and regions option and select Manage.

   In the Americas tab, you can see that the CLEAR eID is selected by default.
10. Select Cancel.

    ID Verification closes the “Manage accepted IDs” page.
11. (Optional) Select the additional data that you want ID Evidence to store for each ID verification.

    Attention: If your configuration authorizes the use of Japanese Individual Number Cards and allows storage of data on the back of IDs, ID Evidence will store the 12-digit individual number showing on the back of Japanese Individual Number Cards. This data is personally identifiable information (PII) and considered sensitive. To avoid this situation, either unselect storage of the back of IDs or remove the Japanese Individual Number Card from the list of Accepted IDs for your configuration. Alternatively, use the Docusign ID Verification with Japanese IDs configuration. This ID Verification method respects data privacy by only allowing storage of the front of Japanese Individual Number Cards.
12. Choose your preferences for the Identity Wallet:

title: Add Identity Verification Configuration with CLEAR
heading_path: Add Identity Verification Configuration with CLEAR
text:
ese IDs configuration. This ID Verification method respects data privacy by only allowing storage of the front of Japanese Individual Number Cards.
12. Choose your preferences for the Identity Wallet:

    - Select Recipients can create or update a Docusign Identity Wallet to allow recipients to create or update their Identity Wallet.
    - Select Recipients can use their Docusign Identity Wallet to allow recipients to identify using their identity wallet.
13. Select Add.

    The configuration displays in the configurations list and is available for senders.

title: Download an Invoice
text: As an administrator, you can view, download, and print invoices for you accounts.

title: Download an Invoice
text:
1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types
2. In the Billing history section, you can select the transaction number to view, download, or
   print the invoice.

   Note: As the account administrator, you can also choose to complete a
   web form on the [Get Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/contactSupport?language=en_US) page to request a copy of your invoice.
   This form option is currently available in English, Spanish, French, Japanese, Portuguese, and
   German languages.

   ![The screenshot shows the Billing history section on the Plan and Billing page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Billinghistory01.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.S-KIv_BcPYFdBl07sb56p-wHZ72Q2y_-V8qOw8NsAHA&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Billing history section on the Plan and Billing page.")

   Note: Available options may vary based on your
   account plan and selected payment method. Your account plan might not support some options in
   this topic. For more information about which options are available for your account, review
   your account plan or contact Docusign Support.

You can view, download, and print invoices.

title: Account Security
text:
Read about settings that administrators use to add security to accounts and transactions
created by users. Administrators can define timeouts for web and mobile applications and
recipient sessions. They can also suppress certificate enforcement for testing
purposes.

These settings add increased security to this account and the signing transactions generated
by all users.

- Web App Session Timeout: Specify a timeout duration in
  minutes to help ensure that an account session does not remain open and active for an
  indeterminate period of time. This timeout setting includes web browser and mobile web
  browser sessions. The minimum allowed value is 1 minute and the maximum is 120 minutes. The
  default value is 20 minutes.
- Recipient Session Timeout: This session timeout is for
  signers. Specify a timeout value in minutes for increased security. The minimum allowed
  value is 1 minute and the maximum is 120 minutes. The default value is 20 minutes. The Recipient Session Timeout setting does not apply to Signing Groups. In this case, the first signer must release the envelope by selecting Finish Later before another signer in the Signing Group can access the envelope.
- Mobile App Session Timeout: This session timeout is for
  account users who use a mobile device and a Docusign mobile app to access the account.
  Specify a timeout value in minutes for increased security. The minimum allowed value is 1
  minute and the maximum is 120 minutes. The default value is 20 minutes.
- Suppress In Session Certificate Enforcement: In demo accounts, you
  can turn off in session certificate enforcement when developing and testing an embedded
  signing integration. Production accounts require in session certificate enforcement, so you
  cannot change or apply this setting in production accounts.

title: Permission Profiles Data in CSV Format
heading_path: Permission Profiles Data in CSV Format
text:
Read about the data administrators obtain when they download a list of permission
profiles for an account. The download is formatted as a comma-separated value (CSV) file.

As an administrator, you can download the full list of permission profiles on your account to
a comma-separated value (CSV) file. The download includes all permission profiles, regardless
of any filters or searches in effect, and contains the following details:

- Permission profile name
- Permission profile ID (useful for Account Server and integrations)
- Last modified date
- Number of users assigned to each permission profile
- List of privileges and values

**Example**: The following figure is a partial view of a sample download. Not all
privileges are shown.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/permissionsetcsv.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUyNDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.xYJ3CQzW5vS-RJSveSkaw0ytufWysKfwV88WlDF80cM&_LANG=enus)

title: Create and Edit Custom Electronic Records and Signature Disclosures
heading_path: Create and Edit Custom Electronic Records and Signature Disclosures
text:
Learn how administrators create custom legal disclosures.

The Custom Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure allows you to create a free-form disclosure using your own text and HTML formatting.

1. From the Accounts view, select Legal
   Disclosure.
2. Select Create Custom in the upper right corner. You are asked to
   confirm that you want to create a custom disclosure. Select OK to
   continue.

   If you have already selected to use a custom disclosure, you are taken to the
   Configure a Disclosure page when you select Legal Disclosure.
3. Select the Signer Language for the disclosure.

   Note: When you save the
   changes, only the disclosure for the currently selected Signer Language is saved. Docusign will not automatically translate your custom disclosure and you
   must create a disclosure for each language used by your signers.
4. Edit your custom disclosure text and HTML formatting in the Enter
   HTML field as needed. HTML is required to maintain the display formatting.

   Important: Javascript, XML, and CSS style tags cannot be used to edit this
   field.

   If this is the first time you are editing the disclosure, the Enter HTML field shows
   the default English (US) Docusign disclosure. You can use this as a
   guideline for your custom disclosure or select Clear to remove
   the disclosure text.
5. To review the changes you have made, select Preview. Your
   current disclosure for the selected language is shown for review. This is the same view
   that your recipients see.

   Select Done to close the preview.
6. Select Save to save the disclosure changes.

title: Delete Identity Verification Configurations
text:
Learn how account administrators delete identity verification configurations that are no
longer needed for recipient verification.

When you delete an Identity Verification configuration, the configuration is immediately
removed for senders.

Note: An account must have at least one configuration. If there is only one
configuration, it cannot be deleted.

Note: You can only delete custom configurations that have been created
by an administrator on your account. Configurations created by Docusign”
cannot be deleted. You can deactivate them if necessary.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. Search the configuration you want to remove.
3. Select Actions >  Delete.
4. Select Delete in the confirmation message.

The configuration is removed.

title: Set the Default Signature Type
text:
Learn how to set the signature type that eSignature selects by default when sending
envelopes.

title: Set the Default Signature Type
text:
Ensure you can access the account administration settings before
configuring the default signature type.

As an account administrator, you want to set the default signature type to apply to signers
when senders have not specified any while configuring the envelope.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signature
   Types.
2. Select Edit.

   The Manage Signature Type List screen opens.
3. Select the signature type you want to set by default and move it to the top of
   the list.

   A confirmation message shows on the Signature Types
   screen.

The signature type you selected is now at the top of the list. It
shows the Default label, indicating it is the new default choice.

title: Add Multiple Users through Bulk Actions
heading_path: Add Multiple Users through Bulk Actions
text: Learn how account administrators add a number of users at one time through bulk actions.

title: Add Multiple Users through Bulk Actions
heading_path: Add Multiple Users through Bulk Actions
text:
To use a bulk action to add multiple users to an account at one time, you must have a comma-separated value (CSV) file with the user information properly formatted. You can create a CSV file according to the information described in [CSV File Format for User Additions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=joi1583277428502.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the format required for the comma-separated value (CSV) file that administrators use to upload new users in a bulk action."), or you can download a sample CSV file and add the user information to it. See [Download a Sample CSV File](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=iab1652809474209.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how administrators download a sample comma-separated value (CSV) file they can use to upload users in bulk. The pre-formatted sample CSV file can save administrators time by eliminating the need to create and format a file. Administrators populate the sample file with the new user content and upload it to the Docusign account.").

As an administrator, you can add multiple users to an account simultaneously through the Add Users bulk action.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Select Bulk Add.
3. Select Upload File in the Add Users dialog box.
4. Select Submit and confirm that you want to process the file.

   Note:  An account can only have one in-process CSV file at a time.

   The system processes the file and checks for errors in the format and user information. The system sends an activation email to the newly added users.
5. Select the icon in the Actions column to update the job status.

title: View Plan and Billing Information
text:
Learn how administrators review and make changes to subscription type and billing information.

As an administrator, you can review plan type, usage data, and the billing information for your accounts.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. View the following information about your account. Access to this information can vary due to your plan type and payment method.

   - **Account Plan Type and Account ID**: Lists which plan type you're subscribed to and your account ID.
   - **Billing Information**: Shows how much you are paying for your account, including your next payment due date and an invoice and payment history. Lists your current payment method and access to update your credit card information.
   - **Usage Data**: Shows details on usage data for services that can impact your billing, such as phone authentication, the number of seats in use, and the number of envelopes used and remaining.

     Note: Usage data is only available on Free, Individual, and Personal accounts.
   - **Billing History**: View and download invoices for your account.

title: Topics Related to Configuring and Using Brands
heading_path: Topics Related to Configuring and Using Brands
text:
Read topics about configuring and using brands that are helpful to administrators.

Administrators that are interested in learning more about configuring and using brands can
read the following topics:

- [Assign Signing Brands to Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=iby1583277366335.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators assign brands to groups for group members to use when they send envelopes or create templates.") - Use the groups functionality to manage which signing brands are available to your account members when they send envelopes and create templates.
- [Advanced Configuration for Branding](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=cno1583277333030.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced configuration options available to administrators that want to customize the signing experience. Administrators can customize destination URLs, header and footer links, and signing brand languages.") - Specify destination URLs, header and footer links, and customize resource files.
- Create a PowerForm - PowerForms inherit the signing branding specified on the associated template.

title: Additional Information for Claiming Domains
text:
Read about additional information for organization administrators who are claiming
domains.

**Domain DNS entry**

- **TXT or CNAME token must remain in the domain's DNS entry**. For as long as you want
  to reserve the domain for your Docusign organization, the token must
  remain in place. Docusign periodically checks the DNS to ensure that
  claims are valid, and removal of a token would prevent your users from accessing Docusign.
- **The process of updating DNS entries varies by vendor.** You might need to coordinate
  with your network administrator to make this change. Also, it may take up to 72 hours for
  DNS changes to propagate over the internet. Therefore, coordinating ahead of time will
  ensure timely deployment of Single Sign-On.
- **Optional - perform a sanity check to confirm the DNS change is active using one of the
  following methods**.
  - For Windows users, open the command prompt and enter these commands:

    `nslookup –q=txt [myorganization.com]`

    `nslookup –q=CNAME [Guid].[myorganization.com]`

    Where *[myorganization.com]* is the domain that you are checking.
  - For Mac users, open the terminal and enter these commands:

    `dig txt
    [myorganization.com]`

    `dig CNAME
    [Guid].[myorganization.com]`

    Where
    *[myorganization.com]* is the domain that you are checking.

title: Email Preferences
text:
Read about how administrators set default email notification preferences for new users
when they are added to an account. Users can change these settings in their My
Preferences. API User settings apply to envelopes sent
using an API integration.

These settings are the default email notification preferences for all new users added to your
account. Users can change these settings in their preferences, as described in the [Notification Preferences](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=dhc1578456326388.html&bundleId=jux1643235969954&_LANG=enus) guide. Changes to these defaults do not affect existing users.

The API User settings apply to envelopes sent using an API integration.

title: Docusign Seal
text:
Read about the Docusign seals that show on every envelope you download and that you can
turn off to respect some local regulations.

When a user downloads an agreement, eSignature adds a digital Docusign seal to ensure
that the document hasn’t changed after signing. Account administrators can turn off
Docusign seal generation for agreements whose last signature is a digital signature. This final
digital signature acts similarly to the Docusign seal. It ensures that the document is
in its final form and secure. It also guarantees that the signature is affixed and
encrypted in the final form in which it appears.

Important: Allowing agreements to download without the Docusign
seal is under the responsibility of the account administrator. When this option is
enabled and the last operation in the document is a digital signature,
Docusign eSignature does not apply the Docusign seal to the agreement. However, if the
last signature in the document does not rely on a digital certificate, Docusign
eSignature enforces the Docusign seal to the envelope upon download. For more
information about disabling the Docusign seal, see the [Envelope Download](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=poe1725403720043.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the signing setting for disabling the Docusign seal that is attached to each agreement after download.") topic.

If you have questions about the setting to
remove the Docusign seal, contact Docusign Support.

Despite the intended security benefits of Docusign seals, there are scenarios
where they can cause confusion. For example, local regulations and policies can require
digital certificates to meet specific standards. Submitting agreements with Docusign
seals to validators in these regions may result in document rejection. Local validators
reject the agreements because the Docusign seal does not meet local requirements or is
not a listed and approved certificate. Local validators in these regions can validate
those documents only if they contain a digital signature or digital seal rather than
electronic signatures.

Important: You and your users should be aware that by removing
the Docusign seal, the signed document will not contain the Envelope ID associated to
the Docusign seal signature.

title: Disable Comments on Envelopes
text:
Learn how account administrators disable comments on documents and envelopes. Senders
and recipients of envelopes sent before comments are disabled can still view comments they
are authorized to see.

Administrators can disable comments on envelopes for
users of an account.

Note: When you disable comments, senders and
recipients of envelopes that had prior comments can still view the comments and
replies they are authorized to see.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Comments.
2. Clear Enable comments in envelopes sent from this
   account.
3. Select Save.

Account users cannot post comments on envelopes sent from
the account.

title: Create an Agreement Rule to Create Another Envelope
heading_path: Create an Agreement Rule to Create Another Envelope
text:
Learn how to create an agreement rule that creates and sends another
envelope.

title: Create an Agreement Rule to Create Another Envelope
heading_path: Create an Agreement Rule to Create Another Envelope
text:
You must create a connection to the eSignature application before
you create the new rule to create another envelope. Next, create the beginning of an
agreement rule and select the condition scope of Any or
All. For more details, see [Connect an Application](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=cnw1598646827061.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how administrators connect applications to Docusign for use with Agreement Rules.") and [Create an Agreement Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wqg1596767500566.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how account administrators create agreement rules to automate post-signing tasks with completed envelopes. To create rules, administrators specify the conditions for the rule and the actions to take when the conditions are met.").

You can create an agreement rule that
creates another envelope from a template. You can create follow-up envelopes based
on specific parameters and conditions of a completed envelope. You can save the
newly created envelope as a draft for review or choose to send it
automatically.

1. In an agreement rule you created, select Template for
   the condition.

   Note: You must select Template for the condition if you want to use
   Create another envelope for the action type.
2. Select Create another envelope for the action type.
3. Select eSignature for the app.
4. Select a template for the new envelope.
5. Select either Save to drafts to save a draft of the
   template when the conditions are met or Send
   automatically to automatically send the new envelope after the
   conditions are complete. The default setting is Save to
   drafts.

   The Map roles between templates section is completed with the names of the original
   envelope and the new envelope. If the new template includes any required roles,
   they display as text instead of as a dropdown. The red asterisk indicates they
   are required.
6. Select a role from the original envelope to map to its new related role.

   The sender of the envelope is the administrator that created the connection to the
   eSignature application.
   ![The screenshot shows the Create another envelope action.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Create-another-envelope-action.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4ODAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.35OzZlH11MFZzv-RyTOZxuNyzB7ghY-f0XAyL7UOE_g&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Create another envelope action.")
7. (Optional) Select ADD ANOTHER ROLE.

   Adding another role adds a new line with a dropdown for each template.
8. Select the role from the dropdown to complete the role mapping. Roles that are
   already mapped are disabled.
9. (Optional) Add another type of action.

   You can add only one Create another envelope action in a rule.
10. Select Save.

The new agreement rule is saved.

title: Remove Users from Accounts
text:
Learn how administrators close users to remove them from accounts and groups they were assigned to and remove associated permission profiles. If necessary, administrators can reactivate closed users.

To remove users from accounts, you must close them. Closed users cannot send new envelopes or access their account. As an administrator, you can also remove users from signing groups they belong to.

Note: Closed users will lose access to any completed documents in their account. However, recipients can still complete envelopes that were sent by users before they were closed. Before closing a user, it is recommended to transfer the ownership of the envelopes or ask the user to download all of their completed documents. After removing a user, you can [find templates where a removed user is a recipient or signer](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jux1679004702413.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to find templates that include a removed user.").

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Locate the user whose account you want to close.
3. Select Close from the Actions dropdown menu for the selected user.
4. (Optional) If you also want to remove the user from any signing groups the user belongs to, in the Close User dialog box, select Remove user from all signing groups. If you leave users in signing groups, you can always [edit the groups later](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jca1583277369608.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators can set up and manage Signing Groups and alternate emails for the members of the group. Any member of the Signing Group can open and act on the document fields addressed to the group.") to remove them. If you do not remove them, they can continue to receive any notifications sent to the signing groups they belong to.
5. Select Close to confirm that you want to remove the user from the account.

The user's status changes to Closed and the user record is grayed out. Depending on the filters currently applied to your view, you may not see the closed record.

title: Edit Identity Verification Configurations
text:
Learn how account administrators update configurations for identity verification.

When you modify an existing configuration, it is
immediately updated for senders.

Note: You can only edit custom
configurations that have been created by an administrator on your account. Configurations
created by Docusign cannot be edited. If you need to make changes, you can
create a copy and then edit the copy of the configuration.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. For the configuration you want to edit, select Actions > Edit.
3. Update the information as necessary, and select Save.

The configuration is updated.

title: Envelope Sharing Between Account Users
text:
Read about how administrators permit users to see and take action on envelopes that
belong to other users on the account.

Envelope sharing permits users on the same account to see and take action on shared
envelopes.

The ability to share envelopes between your account users is helpful for scenarios such as
coverage during a leave of absence, delegated management of Docusign transactions, or other
collaborative workflows. As a Docusign administrator, you can set up envelope sharing and
allow users to see and act on envelopes that belong to another Docusign user.

Sharing is enabled on an individual user basis. When a user's envelopes are shared, the users
who were granted access can see and act on all of that user's envelopes.

Note: Shared envelopes is now in maintenance mode and will no longer be
updated. While you can continue to use shared envelopes, shared access also allows users to
share draft envelopes, move the shared envelopes, create custom folders, and view which users
have access to the envelopes. For more information, see [Manage Shared Access to Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=imk1656607611659.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators manage settings for shared access to envelopes among users.").

See [Shared Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=xbn1578456279661.html&_LANG=enus) for more information about how users see and act on envelopes shared with them.

Note: All changes made to sharing settings are recorded in the audit log.

title: Switch Your IAM Plan
text:
Learn how to upgrade or downgrade your IAM plan.

As an administrator of an existing IAM account, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan, and
add or edit feature capacity to your subscription.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   The Subscription and Billing page opens.![The screenshot shows the Subscription and billing page for an IAM Professional account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/IAM-Sub-and-billing-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ4OTksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.S21e-wxSWzy4sp21RgM-G1SrdbWMfMa3RNUn3RkOdmE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Subscription and billing page for an IAM Professional account.")

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Your Subscription > Switch Plan.

   The Switch your plan page opens.
3. (Optional) Select the Annual or
   Monthly toggle to change your billing cycle.
4. Choose the plan that you want, and select Select
   Plan.

   If you are upgrading your plan, the Confirm your subscription
   change page opens. If you are downgrading your plan, the
   Edit your subscription page opens.
5. To upgrade a plan, review your changes, and select
   Confirm.

   Your plan is upgraded and available immediately.
6. To downgrade a plan:
   1. Review your plan and edit by selecting from available options.
   2. (Optional) Select the Annual or
      Monthly toggle to change your billing
      cycle.
   3. (Optional) Select Add Capacity or edit the
      feature capacity total to add additional capacity.
   4. Select Next. The Confirm your
      subscription change page opens.
   5. Review your changes, and select Confirm.
   6. Your plan is changed. The changes apply on your renewal date.

You have switched your IAM plan.

title: Audit Logs
text:
Read about the types of changes to accounts that are captured in the audit logs.
Account administrators can view changes to account settings, billing information, plan type,
users, and more. They can filter the information by date range and download the selected
information to a comma-separated value (CSV) file for further processing.

The audit logs provide an easy way for account administrators to see changes to account
settings, billing information, plan type, and users. Administrators can filter the information
by date range and download the selected information to a comma-separated value (CSV) file for
further processing.

Note: The audit logs contain data for account changes
starting from June 1, 2015. Data before that date is not available in the audit logs.

**Types of changes**

The audit logs provide a record of the following types of changes to an account:

- Account settings
- User settings
- Billing credit card and billing address information
- Account plan changes
- Permission profiles (does not include creation or deletion of permission profiles)
- Envelope sharing settings
- Branding changes
- Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure changes
- Document Custom Fields
- Envelope Custom Fields

A maximum of 100 events are shown on the Audit Logs page. To view any
additional events, export the CSV file.

**Information displayed**

The information displayed in the audit logs includes the following:

- **Event Date:** The date and time the account change was made. The date and time shown
  uses the time zone setting for your browser and will vary depending on your browser
  settings.
- **User:** The name and email address of the user who changed the setting. Usually, this
  is an administrator. This column shows the user as "Docusign System" to
  indicate a change made to your account by a Docusign technical process.
  This occurs when a new user activates their account, or if there is a change in your account
  plan where features are added or changed. This column shows the user as "Docusign System User" to indicate a change made by an internal Docusign employee if you requested a change to your account. Contact Docusign Support if you have any questions about a change completed by one
  of these users.
- **Description:** The category for the changed setting. For categories that have
  multiple entries, such as Users, the name of the entry is also included in the description.

  *Example:* If you change something for user John Jones, the description will
  show "User Setting Change - John Jones".
- **Field:** The name of the field that was changed.
- **Old Value** and **New Value:** The old and new values for the field.

  In some cases, the Old Value field might be blank. This is expected and only means that
  there was no old value for the field.

  For fields that use a check box, a value of True means that the check box is selected.
  False means that the check box is not selected.

title: Introduction to Docusign Account Administration
text:
Learn about the Docusign account settings interface for
administrators who manage multi-user accounts.

Docusign provides an easy and intuitive interface to manage account
settings and users.

title: Introduction to Docusign Account Administration
heading_path: Get started with a video tour
text: To get started, watch this video about how to access Docusign account administration and manage your account:

title: User Personal Information
text:
Read about how administrators control whether users can edit their name.

This option controls the changes account members can make to their personal account
information in their My Preferences view.

- **Allow users to edit their name**. When selected, account members can edit
  their name in their My Preferences, Personal Information section. If you need to control
  user names in your Docusign account, clear this option to restrict users
  from making any changes, as described in the user topic [Change Your Name](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=lbn1578456452537.html&bundleId=jux1643235969954&_LANG=enus).

title: Transfer of Envelopes and Templates Between Users
heading_path: Transfer of Envelopes and Templates Between Users
text:
As an account administrator, you can manually transfer envelopes or templates between
users. When you transfer ownership of envelopes or templates, the transferred items are removed
from the original owner’s account and moved to the new owner's account. Transfer envelopes and
templates from either the Users page or the Custody Transfer page.

As an account administrator, you can manually transfer envelopes or templates between users.
When you transfer ownership of envelopes or templates, the transferred items are removed from
the original owner’s account and moved to the new owner's account.

Note: The Transfer Custody feature must be enabled for your account to be able to
transfer ownership of envelopes and templates.

title: Start Workflow Action
text:
Read about how account administrators use the action Start
Workflow with agreement rules to automate CLM workflow tasks with completed
envelopes. For example, administrators can generate a document, send a document for
signature, and create a folder structure.

eSignature accounts that are linked to a Docusign CLM.DS or Docusign CLM.CM account can kick off a workflow when envelopes are
complete.

Note: If you do not have access to a Docusign CLM.DS or
Docusign CLM.CM account, contact Docusign Support for more information.

Docusign CLM Workflow is a powerful tool to help your business automate processes
that require repetitive human action. You can include tasks in your CLM workflows,
such as generating a document, sending a document for signature or for review,
creating a folder structure, and much more.

For more information about creating and managing workflows, see the following references:

- [Agreement Actions in CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=trr1605121275463.html&bundleId=pxt1643324456371&_LANG=enus)
- [Workflow Designer](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=kuq1584137317950.html&bundleId=yks1643320936212&_LANG=enus)
- [Workflow Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=fat1590675875893.html&bundleId=yks1643320936212&_LANG=enus)

title: Default Language Values for CSV Upload File
heading_path: Default Language Values for CSV Upload File
text:
See the values for the language codes that administrators use in the CSV upload file.
Administrators specify the default language for users during bulk add and bulk update
operations.

Use the following codes to specify the default language for users during bulk update and bulk
add operations. The language code is specified in the CSV file used to upload the user
information.

title: Default Language Values for CSV Upload File
heading_path: Default Language Values for CSV Upload File
text:
- Chinese Simplified = zh\_CN
- Chinese Traditional = zh\_TW
- Dutch = nl
- English = en
- French = fr
- German = de
- Italian = it
- Japanese = ja
- Korean = ko
- Portuguese = pt
- Portuguese Brazil = pt\_BR
- Russian = ru
- Spanish = es

title: Archive To Action
text:
Read about how account administrators use the action Archive To with agreement rules to archive completed documents to cloud storage.

Completed documents are saved to the selected cloud storage provider, such as a shared drive in Google Drive. You can determine how documents are archived.

- **Customize envelope settings:** Select Include Certificate of Completion to save the certificate in the same folder as the signed document. For more information about the Certificate of Completion, see [History and Certificate of Completion.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=rld1600726136178.html&_LANG=enus)

  Select Combine all envelope contents into a single ZIP file if you want to archive all documents in an envelope as a single .zip file.
- **Customize file name and folder name/path :** Customize the file name and location. Type the + sign and select one of the envelope fields to use a dynamic value to name your documents, for example the name of the template used or the recipient names.

  Note: You can also enter the name of the destination folder at the beginning of the file name to save the documents in a specific folder. For example, **Offer Letters/FY21/** saves the documents in the FY21 subfolder in the Offer Letters folder.

  Note: When archiving to Box, the file paths are case-sensitive. If you receive an error that an item with the same name already exists, then the folder might already exist with a different capitalization. Correct the capitalization or choose a different location.

  Note: If you add an envelope custom field to a file and folder path in an agreement rule, and any input to the field is not found when the rule runs, this field displays as "-" in the file and folder path. To continue using this rule, complete the value for the field or delete the envelope custom field from the file and folder path.

![The screenshot shows an example of the archive options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Archive-Options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4NzIsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.vAZx7JACrIXt2qKh7snc69SJ_ZsAukWFfKsFi5h3CSk&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the archive options.")

title: Rename a Group
text:
Learn how administrators edit the names of groups.

As an account administrator, you can edit the name of
groups.

1. From the Accounts view, select Groups.
2. Find the group that you want to rename.

   Note: You cannot edit the
   Everyone and Administrators groups.
3. Select Actions > Rename Group.
4. In Group Name, change the group name and select Save.

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users
text:
Read about how organization administrators can use a CSV file to add multiple
users.

Your CSV import file is made up of a header row with the column headers and a row of user or
account data for each user that you want to add to an account. Only new users can be
imported. Any changes to existing users will be ignored. To make changes to existing
users, see [Bulk Update Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jwk1593209395373.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can bulk update existing users in one or more accounts.").

To ensure your CSV is properly formatted, download the CSV template from the Add Users dialog
box to create your new CSV file.

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users
The header row for add users
text:
The first line of the file is the header row that defines each of the columns. The header
values are not required to be in the order listed and are not case-sensitive, but
the text must match the listed values.

Important: Your CSV file must contain these required
columns: AccountID, FirstName, LastName, UserEmail, and eSignPermissionProfile. The
rest of the header values are optional.

Note: For the user's name, you can use either the
FirstName and LastName columns together, or the UserName column. Your spreadsheet
should only contain one of these. If both the FirstName and LastName columns and the
UserName column are present in your CSV, the values entered in the UserName column
take precedence.

The EnableCLM and CLMPermissionProfile columns are only applicable for organizations
with the CLM product. If your organization doesn't have a CLM account, leave these
columns blank.

Note: The LoginPolicy and AutoActivation
columns only apply to organizations with a reserved domain and Single Sign-On (SSO)
through an Identity Provider. For more information, see the [Docusign Single Sign-On Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=njp1583359138365.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how Docusign administrators can set up SSO at a global level to manage users in all their Docusign accounts.") topic.

The acceptable column header values for an Add Users CSV file are:

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users
The header row for add users
text:
us "Read about how Docusign administrators can set up SSO at a global level to manage users in all their Docusign accounts.") topic.

The acceptable column header values for an Add Users CSV file are:

| Header Row Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| AccountID | The 32 character API Account ID of the user's account in your organization. This can be found in the API and Keys section of the account. **Required column**. |
| AccountName | The name of the user's account in your organization. The account name must match the Account ID provided. |
| FirstName | The user's first name. **Required column**. |
| LastName | The user's last name. **Required column**. |
| UserName | The user's full name. This can be used instead of FirstName and LastName. This is useful for languages that place family names before given names. **Required column.** If this column is used instead of FirstName and LastName, it is required. |
| UserEmail | The user's complete email address. **Required column**. |
| eSignPermissionProfile | The user's permission profile for the eSignature product. This value must match an existing permission profile for the account. This value is not case-sensitive. **Required column**. |
| EnableCLM | Grants the user access to the CLM product. If you grant a user access to CLM, you must also assign them a Permission profile for that product with the CLMPermissionProfile column.  - TRUE - The user has access to CLM. - FALSE - The user does not have access to CLM. |
| CLMPermissionProfile | The user's permission profile for the CLM product. This value must match an existing permission profile for the account. This value is not case-sensitive. If you assign a user a CLM permission Profile, you must also grant them access to the CLM product with the EnableCLM column. |
| UserTitle | The user's job title. |
| CompanyName | The user's company name. |
| Group | The user's assigned groups. The Group values must match the existing Group names for the account. Additional Group columns can be added to the file to add users to more than one group.  You do not need to add users to the Everyone group, since all new users are automatically added to that group. |
| AddressLine1 | The user's address - first line. |
| AddressLine2 | The user's address - second line. |
| City | The user's city name. |
| StateRegionProvince | The user's regional location. |
| PostalCode | The user's postal code. |
| Phone | The user's phone number. |
| Language | The user's display language for their Docusign account. See the [list of language codes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=rga1593054537902.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the code for the language the user views in the user interface."). |
| LoginPolicy | The user's login policy. Valid values include the following:   - None - The user is created with no policy assigned. - FedAuthRequired - The user must log in with an Identity   Provider. - FedAuthBypass - The user may log in with an Identity   Provider or their Docusign username and password.   For more information about login policies, see the [Setting a User Login Policy](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zah1583359147774.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can set a user login policy. When SSO is enabled for an organization, all account users follow the policy settings specified for the domain.") topic. |
| AutoActivate | For domain users, new users can be activated automatically for domain accounts using SSO by setting the value to TRUE.  The user is activated automatically once the import is complete. Memberships activated in this way will not receive an activation email. |

The access code option (adding an access code for authentication during user
activation) cannot be used with this bulk action.

title: Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users
heading_path: Build a CSV to Bulk Add Users
text:
In the lines of the file below the header row, add the user information with commas
used as the delimiter (separator) between each value.

If you are using Microsoft® Excel® to create your file, you can enter the header
values (FirstName, LastName, UserEmail, etc.) in different columns on the first
line, enter the user information on subsequent lines, and save the file as a CSV
file. You do not need to add commas. Excel will automatically add commas when you save
the file.

title: Users and Groups Permission Access Levels
heading_path: Users and Groups Permission Access Levels
text:
View the levels of access that administrators can grant through the Users and
Groups permission.

The following table depicts the level of access provided by each of the Users and
Groups access levels.

| Administrative area | Page | Level of access: View Only | Level of access: Edit Groups Only | Level of access: Edit Users & Groups | Level of access: Edit Users, Groups, & Administrators |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N/A | Overview | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Users | View | View | Edit[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wfj1661539843707.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_1) | Edit[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wfj1661539843707.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_2) |
| Users and Groups | Permission Profiles | View | View | View | View |
| Users and Groups | Groups | View | Edit | Edit | Edit |
| Users and Groups | Signing Groups | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Audit Logs | None | None | None | None |
| Auditing | Bulk Actions | View | View | View | View |
| Auditing | Validations | None | None | None | None |

[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wfj1661539843707.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_1) Edit access does not include the ability to edit users with "Allow All"
administrative permissions enabled, such as those with the DS
Admin permission profile.

[2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wfj1661539843707.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_2) Edit access does not include access to Shared Access or
Shared Envelopes unless the
Envelope Sharing permission is also
selected, and does not include access to Transfer
Envelopes or Transfer
Templates unless the Transfer
Envelopes permission is also
selected.

title: Reduce the Number of Seats in Your Subscription
heading_path: Reduce the Number of Seats in Your Subscription
text:
Learn how administrators can reduce the number of seats in a subscription. Seat
reductions are applied on the subscription renewal date.

Administrators can reduce the number of seats and envelopes in a subscription.

Note: When an administrator removes a user from an account, the
total number of seats and envelope capacity in the subscription is unchanged.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Your Subscription > Edit Subscription Details.

   For eSignature accounts, scroll to the bottom of the page to view Manage Your
   Subscription in the Manage subscription details section.

   ![The screenshot shows the Manage Your Subscription button in the Manage subscription details section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Manage-Sub-CTA-Experiment.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.UAJbcLeDJslazocZ-3TXGy6Sj2ooKtbmVynr9HCY6Q4&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Manage Your Subscription button in the Manage subscription details section.")

   The Edit your subscription window opens. ![The screenshot shows the Edit your subscription window for an eSignature Standard subscription.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Edit-your-subscription-window.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.UAJbcLeDJslazocZ-3TXGy6Sj2ooKtbmVynr9HCY6Q4&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Edit your subscription window for an eSignature Standard subscription.")
3. Select Edit Quantity.

   The Edit seats and envelopes window opens.![The screenshot shows the Edit seats and envelopes window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Edit-seats-and-envelopes.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.UAJbcLeDJslazocZ-3TXGy6Sj2ooKtbmVynr9HCY6Q4&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Edit seats and envelopes window.")
4. Select the - button to reduce the number of seats in
   your plan.

   Each seat includes 10 envelopes on a monthly plan or 100 envelopes on an
   annual plan. The total number of envelopes can be shared among all users.
5. Select Next.

   The Confirm change window opens.
6. Review changes to your envelopes and select Confirm
   Change.

   The What led to this change window
   opens.
7. Select a reason for changing the number of seats, and select
   Next.

   The Confirm your subscription window opens.![The screenshot shows the Confirm your subscription window with a eSignature Standard subscription.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Confirm-your-subscription-window.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.UAJbcLeDJslazocZ-3TXGy6Sj2ooKtbmVynr9HCY6Q4&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Confirm your subscription window with a eSignature Standard subscription.")
8. Review your current subscription and your new subscription reflecting the new
   number of seats, and select Confirm.
9. Select Done to close the confirmation window.

Your changes are saved as pending. The seat reductions are applied
to your subscription on your subscription renewal date.

title: Shared Access Events in Audit Logs
heading_path: Shared Access Events in Audit Logs
text:
Read about how administrators view changes to shared access settings for users in an
account.

As an administrator, you can view the changes made in your account. The Audit Logs page shows a
log entry for every time the shared access settings are changed in an account. On the
Audit Log page, you can also filter the events, and you can export the data to a .csv
file.

The audit logs fields use the following terminology for shared access events:

- AccountId: The account of the principal user.
- AgentUser: The user who is taking the action on behalf of another user
  or is accessing another user’s envelopes.
- Permission: The permission assigned, which is either Send or Manage.
- PrincipalUser: The user whom the action was taken on behalf
  of or whose envelopes are being accessed.
- UserAuthorizationId: The ID of the authorization event of access
  granted for an agent to the envelopes of a principal user.

![View Shared Access Events in Audit Logs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Audit_logs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUyMjEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.0BMCeUD1mgKwq8VZNm0le5iz1XusaJuZpHGbRBKhIq0&_LANG=enus "View Shared Access Events in Audit Logs.")

title: Seat Request Management
text:
Read about how administrators can review and process user requests to join an account.

When users request to join an account through the Account
Discovery feature, account administrators receive a notification email. The email
notifies administrators to review and approve or deny the request on the
Users page. The Users page includes a
User Requests section that shows the new requests with options
for administrators to confirm or deny the request. Requests older than 30 days are
removed from the list.

The administrator can respond to the request using the following options:

- Add the user to the account: if the administrator selects
  Confirm, the user is added to the
  Users list. Approved users are assigned a seat on the
  account in pending status and are assigned the DS Sender permission. The requesting
  user receives an email to activate a seat on the account.
- Decline the user request: if the administrator wants to decline the
  request, the administrator selects Deny. The requesting user
  will be removed from the list.

![The screenshot shows the Users page with new requests waiting to be reviewed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/seatmgmt1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.l6ljZ_luGY1gNN7VQVBohq5C4eX4PlMF6yjrErl-W6s&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Users page with new requests waiting to be reviewed.")

title: Create an Agreement Rule
text:
Learn how account administrators create agreement rules to automate post-signing tasks with completed envelopes. To create rules, administrators specify the conditions for the rule and the actions to take when the conditions are met.

You can automate actions taken on completed envelopes by creating rules. To create a rule, set the conditions for your rule and the actions that are taken when the conditions are met.

Note: Agreement Action rules do not work if documents in the template were replaced during the envelope preparation process. Instead, create a new template with the correct documents. Configure a new rule to use this new template in the condition.

1. From the Accounts view, select Rules in the AGREEMENT ACTIONS section.
2. Select ADD RULE.
3. Enter a name for your rule.

   Note: The Enable rule option is selected by default. A selected rule becomes active after you save it. You can disable the rule to prevent it from applying to actions.
4. Select the scope of the conditions that affect the rule.

   Condition types:
   - **All:** All conditions are true for the created rule.
   - **Any:** Any aspect of the conditions is true for the created rule.
5. Select your first **If** condition and select who or what the rule applies to.
