   ![The screenshot shows an example of a condition.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/aacreate1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4NzAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.-K1vEm_JL0aCX04LBjB-X6GT26xJSQAEoURKgpnz1Vw&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of a condition.")

   - **Document Custom Field:** The rule applies when a value is entered or selected in a specific document custom field. Select the document custom field from the list and enter the value you'd like to check for.
   - **Envelope Custom Field:** The rule applies when a value is entered or selected in a specific envelope custom field. Select the envelope custom field from the list and enter the value you'd like to check for.
   - **Recipient:** The rule applies to the selected recipient. You can enter either a full or partial email address, for example, both joe.sample@docusign.com or @docusign.com.
   - **Sender:** The rule applies to the selected sender. Select the name of the sender.
   - **Template:** The rule applies to the intended template, which you choose by selecting the type or name. When choosing a template name for a rule, ensure that the template name for the actual template does not include spaces or special characters around it. Template names with spaces or special characters can prevent the rule from triggering properly.

   Note: Document custom fields and envelope custom fields display a list of all eligible fields on the account. The account must have fields available to use these conditions.
6. Select the filter criteria for the conditions.

   - **Is:** The rule applies only when the value is present.
   - **Is not:** The rule applies only when the value is not present.
   - **Contains:** The rule applies only when the value is present in some form.
7. If you need another condition, select **Add Another Condition**.

   Note: You can add up to 10 conditions to an agreement rule.
8. Select one of the following actions:

   - Archive to: Archive completed documents to a cloud storage provider.
   - Start Workflow: Begin a workflow in Docusign CLM.DS or Docusign CLM.CM (formerly SpringCM).
   - Export to Spreadsheet: Export the envelope data to a spreadsheet.

title: Create an Agreement Rule
text:
nts to a cloud storage provider.
   - Start Workflow: Begin a workflow in Docusign CLM.DS or Docusign CLM.CM (formerly SpringCM).
   - Export to Spreadsheet: Export the envelope data to a spreadsheet.

   ![The screenshot shows an example of an agreement rule.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/aacreaterule2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU4NzAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.-K1vEm_JL0aCX04LBjB-X6GT26xJSQAEoURKgpnz1Vw&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of an agreement rule.")
9. (Optional) Add another action.

   Note: You can add up to three actions per rule.
10. Review the rule, and select SAVE.

The rule is saved.

title: Set Up Custom Help Contacts
text:
Learn how administrators provide users with a point of contact to help them get answers
to questions about their account or get IT help.

Administrators and IT resources can provide guidance and contact information to eSignature
account end users. This information helps senders answer account questions by putting them
in contact with an administrator, an IT help desk, or another internal resource that
supports Docusign for your company.

1. From the Accounts view, select Account
   Profile.
2. In the Custom Help Contact section, enter a message to users.
3. Enter the name and contact information for an administrator on the account, a company help desk, or another IT resource.
4. Select Save.

The information is displayed when users select the
(?) Help icon in their eSignature account.
![The screenshot shows an example of a custom help message in the Help menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/customhelpmessage.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NzgsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.qx50Wb7Glvia0si8jLOc5NtfIzKib4ryv2xN4SmFlDs&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of a custom help message in the Help menu.")

title: Time Zone, Date Format, and Language
heading_path: Time Zone, Date Format, and Language
text:
Read about how administrators set the time zone, date format, default language, and
date signed information for an account through Regional Settings.

Note: To manage the regional settings, you must be an
administrator with All Administration Capabilities. For more information, see [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.").

As an administrator, you can specify the time zone, date and time format, default language,
and signed date behavior for account users. The settings are located on the Regional
Settings page.

- Time Zone: The time zone, date, and time format settings are used to
  record the date and time of events listed in the Envelope History and the Certificate of
  Completion.

  Note: Changes to these settings affect Envelope History.
  Events listed in Envelope History reflect the new values. The audit events in the
  Certificate of Completion remain unchanged and reflect the settings that were in place
  when the event occurred.

  By default, these settings apply to all users on the
  account. If you allow users to set their own time zone and date format, users can adjust
  the settings in the My Preferences menu, under Regional
  Settings.

  Note: If you disable Allow
  users to set their own Time Zone and Date Format, changes made by users
  revert to the default settings for the account.
- **Default Language:** The default language is applied to users and brands that are
  added to the account. Changes to the default language do not affect existing users. Users
  can change their preferred language in the My Preferences menu, under
  Regional Settings.
- **Date Signed:** You can add the Date Signed field to a document
  to record the date and time that the document was signed. You can specify the language used
  for the signing month and the time zone that are displayed in the Date
  Signed field.
  - Recipient: Defer to the language, date format, and time zone
    settings of the recipient account.
  - Account default: Use the default settings defined by the
    account.

  Note: You define the default account date and time format
  settings for Date Signed on the Signing
  Settings page under Document Formatting. For more
  information, see [Document Date and Time Format](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=okg1583277354516.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the settings that administrators use to specify the date and time format for documents sent from an account.").

title: View or Remove Shared Access with a User
heading_path: View or Remove Shared Access with a User
text:
Learn how administrators manage shared access to envelopes among users.

As an administrator, you can view and manage the shared access with a specific user. The
Shared with [User Name] page shows the list of users that the current user shares
access to their envelopes.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Users.
2. Locate the user that you want to manage sharing settings for, and select Actions > Manage Shared Access.
3. Select Shared with [User Name].

   The users that shared access to their envelopes with this user are
   listed.
4. (Optional) Select View next a user name that has the
   shared access for this user.
5. In the list for the shared access for the user you selected, select
   Remove to remove the shared access to their envelopes
   for the user you are managing.
6. Select Remove on the confirmation message.

   The shared access is removed for the user.

title: Customize Sending Brand Header and Footer Links
heading_path: Customize Sending Brand Header and Footer Links
text:
Learn how administrators customize or hide links included in the sending
brand.

Note: These features are temporarily disabled for account plans
purchased on the web after November 20, 2024. For more information, contact Docusign Support.

You can customize or hide the links in the sending brand in the Docusign
applications. You can add up to three footer links.

Note: To customize header and footer links for a signing brand, see [Advanced Configuration for Signing
Brands](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=cno1583277333030.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced configuration options available to administrators that want to customize the signing experience. Administrators can customize destination URLs, header and footer links, and signing brand languages.").

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Brands.
2. Select the Sending tab.

   Your current sending brand configuration displays.
3. Select Header and Footer Links.
4. In the Customize Links page, you can add up to three links to the footer by specifying
   text and URLs for Footer Link 1, 2, and 3.

   ![The screenshot shows the Footer Link 1 section of the Customize Links page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/footerlinksadd.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNTIsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.P-Bkge9hCF4qljuzNiJKH32u-pp3UhUHGyv_O6Sv7UM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Footer Link 1 section of the Customize Links page.")
5. (Optional) To hide a link, select the Hide link toggle next to
   the link.

   If the footer link is blank, with no text or URL, it will not display to your users.
   Using the Hide link option is useful if you have completed the link information, but you
   want to hide it without deleting the information.
6. Select Save.

Your link changes are saved. Users will see the new
links the next time they log in to Docusign.

title: Increase the Number of Seats in Your Subscription
heading_path: Increase the Number of Seats in Your Subscription
text:
Learn how administrators can increase the number of seats and envelopes in a
subscription. Additional seats are applied to your subscription immediately.

As an administrator, you can increase the number of seats and envelopes in a subscription.
Additional seats are applied to your subscription immediately.

1. From the Accounts view, select Plan and
   Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Your Subscription > Edit Subscription Details.

   For eSignature accounts, Manage Your Subscription is in
   the Manage subscription details section.

   The Edit your subscription window opens.![The screenshot shows the Manage subscription details section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Manage-Sub-CTA-Experiment.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Ob0Q5AelS6WpG5FcCJpZORC19Zh3Cd4aa6eSvgBMiQM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Manage subscription details section.")
3. Select Edit Quantity.

   The Edit seats and envelopes window opens. ![The screenshot shows the Edit seats and envelopes window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Edit-seats-and-envelopes.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Ob0Q5AelS6WpG5FcCJpZORC19Zh3Cd4aa6eSvgBMiQM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Edit seats and envelopes window.")
4. Select the + button to increase the number of seats in
   your plan. Each seat includes 10 envelopes on a monthly plan, or 100 envelopes
   on an annual plan. The total number of envelopes can be shared among all users.
5. Select Next.

   The Review your subscription changes window opens.
6. Review your current subscription and your new subscription reflecting the new
   number of seats.
7. Select Continue to Purchase.

   The Confirm your order window opens.![The screenshot shows the Confirm your order window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Confirm-your-order.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NDcsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.Ob0Q5AelS6WpG5FcCJpZORC19Zh3Cd4aa6eSvgBMiQM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Confirm your order window.")
8. Select Purchase.

   The purchase is completed and a copy of your receipt is sent to your
   email.
9. Select Done to close the confirmation window.

The additional seats are applied to your subscription
immediately.

title: Add Seats, Envelopes, and Add-Ons for Plans Purchased from a Sales Representative
heading_path: Add Seats, Envelopes, and Add-Ons for Plans Purchased from a Sales Representative
text:
Learn how to add additional seats, envelopes, and add-ons to your existing subscriptions.

This feature is available to eligible global customers who have purchased an eSignature plan from a Docusign sales representative. If you don’t see the option to edit products directly in your account, it might be due to your subscription type or contracting region.

Note: Some customers may temporarily be unable to access product purchase and product usage information. Usage information will be available once the transition is complete within two business days.

If you need to add capacity immediately or for additional help, visit the Docusign [Support Center](https://support.docusign.com/) or contact your Docusign account team.

1. From the Account view, select Plan and Billing.

   Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.
2. Select Manage Subscription.

   The Subscription and usage page opens.
3. Select Add More or Edit Products to view your current order details and add more quantity to a product.![The screenshot shows the Subscription and usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/SSAC_sub_v2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.gqM1g4oAqN9bZsJfu3Ic5D8YKay3NtlDqZCM7zwD7jY&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Subscription and usage page.")

   The checkout page opens.
4. Enter the quantity to add to your product. As you increase the number of envelopes or seats, your plan services will automatically adjust to match the changes.
5. (Optional) Expand the Your other products section to add quantities for any of your other products.![The screenshot shows the checkout page where you can add more quantity to a product.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/SSAC_checkout.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5MjQsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.gqM1g4oAqN9bZsJfu3Ic5D8YKay3NtlDqZCM7zwD7jY&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the checkout page where you can add more quantity to a product.")
6. Review your new order summary. Select the certification checkbox to confirm that you are authorized to make this purchase for your account.
7. Select Submit to complete your purchase.

Your purchase is completed and will be available to use right away while your order is processed. Within 24 hours, your subscription and pricing updates will be fully processed, and you’ll be charged according to your current billing cycle settings.

For additional help, visit the Docusign [Support Center](https://support.docusign.com/) or contact your Docusign account team.

title: Default Mode for Signature Adoption
text:
Read about the signing modes that administrators can choose as a default for the
Adopt Your Signature dialog box.

This option sets which of the available signing modes is shown first when a signer is
adopting their signature or initials. If you prefer an option, but have not ruled out the
others, you can set the preferred option to display when the Adopt Your Signature dialog box
displays. Recipients can still select any of the other signing modes configured for the
account.

- Start on adopt shows the Select Style mode, with the different signature styles.
- Start on draw shows the Draw mode, where the signer draws their signature or initials.
- Start on upload shows the upload mode, where the signer can upload an existing file with their signature or initials.

Separate settings are available for Docusign web app and mobile web signers.

title: Access Codes
text:
Read about how access codes provide an additional level of security for administrators.

Use these options to enable and set requirements for the format of access codes used for an
additional measure of recipient security, and to enable the automatic Access Code Generator.

- **Enforce access code rules:** When selected, the access code rules can be entered and are enforced when a sender adds an access code requirement during sending. The access code rules are only enabled when this option is selected. The access code rules are:
  - **Minimum access code length:** This sets the minimum length for an access code. It can be
    6–50 characters.
  - **Access codes must include at least:** This sets the characters required (letter, number,
    and special character) for an access code. One or more options can be selected. A
    special character is any keyboard character that is not a letter, number, or space. The
    following characters are not allowed: < and > and the character combination
    "&#". The & or # characters can be used separately.
- **Enable Access Code Generator:** When selected, senders can use the Access Code Generator to automatically generate and copy access codes.

title: Fields and Properties
text:
Read about how account administrators configure optional fields and properties in Sending Settings.

As an administrator, you can enable optional fields and their properties for the sending settings of an account.

- Default font for fields: Defines the default font, size, and color for fields on documents sent from your account.
- Enable formula field (calculated field): When selected account senders can add Formula fields to documents. Formula fields dynamically calculate a result based on recipient-entered values in other fields. For more information, see [Calculated Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=crs1578456361259.html&_LANG=enus).
- Enable conditional fields: When selected the conditional logic field property is enabled for the account. For more information, see [Conditional Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=hew1578456460132.html&_LANG=enus).
- Enable conditional routing: When selected, senders will be able to route envelopes based on envelope data. For more information, see [Conditional Routing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=aqm1578456487908.html&_LANG=enus).
- Allow admins to share custom fields: This allows administrators to share custom fields they created with other users in the account.
- When an envelope is sent, write the initial value of the fields for all recipients: When selected, the initial value in a field is written to the document for all recipients when the envelope is sent.
- Allow SMS delivery to recipients: If selected, senders are able to choose to send signing notifications by SMS in addition to the standard email notifications. You can further customize individual user or group access to this feature with [permission profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.").
- Allow fax delivery to recipients: This allows senders to send documents through Docusign and have recipients receive the documents by fax.
- Enable envelope types dropdown: When selected, the Envelope Types dropdown displays for senders when they create or update an envelope or template. The envelope type data is stored as an envelope custom field to use for reporting, searching, and classifying agreements based on envelope type. This setting is enabled by default. For more information about using envelope types, see [Envelope Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tvp1724803837658.html&_LANG=enus). For information about finding envelopes using the envelope type, see [Search for Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=veh1578456417384.html&_LANG=enus).
- Enable signer attachment field: If selected, account senders can add the Signer Attachment field to a document. You can further customize individual user access to this feature with [permission profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.").

  If this field is enabled for the account, you can select the following options:

title: Fields and Properties
text:
dual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.").

  If this field is enabled for the account, you can select the following options:

  - Allow signers to add multiple attachments per field: If selected, a signer can add multiple attachments to a Signer Attachment field.
  - Append attachments to the document with the attachment field: If selected, when a signer uploads an attachment, the document is inserted into the envelope directly after the document with the Signer Attachment field. If this option is not selected, any signer attachments are included at the end of all documents in the envelope. Furthermore, with this option, the attachment becomes a permanent part of the envelope. The signer cannot remove the attachment after uploading it. Also, if the envelope is ever copied (as described in [Copy Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=dwd1578456423420.html&_LANG=enus)), any signer attachments are included in the new envelope.
- Document Visibility: This option enables and sets how the Document Visibility feature is configured for the account.

  If this option is selected, the additional Allow sender to specify document visibility: option is available. When selected, the sender can adjust document visibility.

  The Document Visibility options are:

title: Fields and Properties
text:
s option is selected, the additional Allow sender to specify document visibility: option is available. When selected, the sender can adjust document visibility.

  The Document Visibility options are:

  - Off: Document Visibility is not active for the account.
  - Must sign to view, unless a member of sender's account: With this option Document Visibility is enabled for all envelopes sent from the account and any member of the sending account can view all the documents that are sent together.
  - Must sign to view, unless sender: With this option Document Visibility is enabled for all envelopes sent from the account and only the sender can view all the documents that are sent together.
  - Sender can set "must sign to view, unless a member of sender's account": With this option, the sender has the option of enabling Document Visibility for the documents that are sent together. When enabled, all the documents that are sent together are still visible to any member of the sending account.
  - Sender can set "must sign to view, unless sender": With this option, the sender has the option of enabling Document Visibility for the documents that are sent together. When enabled for an envelope, all the documents that are sent together are only visible to the sender.
- Enable draw new signature for each signature or initial field: When selected, this option allows a sender to require that signers draw their signature and initials in each signature and initial field in the sent documents. This option can be set separately for each signer. See [Require Recipients to Draw Each Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=jyu1578456488969.html&_LANG=enus) for information about using this option when sending.
- Allow senders to require signers to upload a new image to sign or initial: When selected, senders can select this option to require signers to upload a new image to sign or initial each time they sign a document. See [Require Recipients to Upload a New Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=rsc1578456490941.html&_LANG=enus) for information about using this option when sending.
- Prevent users from forwarding completed envelopes: When selected, the 'Forward' option for completed envelopes is hidden in the sending experience.
- Enable signature stamp field: When selected, senders on the account can see the Stamp field and add it to documents and templates.
- Enable including the Envelope ID on the document: When selected, senders can stamp documents with the envelope ID, as described in [Set Advanced Options for Your Signers, Envelopes, and Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tfm1578456367923.html&_LANG=enus). If the additional option to Include Envelope ID by default: is also selected, then the advanced option to stamp documents is selected by default for all envelopes sent from the account. There are three possible states to configure for your account:
  - Default: ON Sender: Can Set

    To include the envelope ID by default and allow senders to turn it off, leave both options selected.

    ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/envelopeid1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.OavRnflRcJ5ctSxkLIETpIB_YIWPZjwGaz7LCCqh-Uo&_LANG=enus)
  - Default: ON Sender: Cannot Set

    To enforce the envelope ID stamp for all envelopes sent from the account, clear the Enable including the Envelope ID on the document setting, and leave the Include Envelope ID by default setting selected.
  - Default: OFF Sender: Can Set

title: Fields and Properties
text:
all envelopes sent from the account, clear the Enable including the Envelope ID on the document setting, and leave the Include Envelope ID by default setting selected.
  - Default: OFF Sender: Can Set

    To allow senders to turn the envelope ID stamp on, select the Enable including the Envelope ID on the document setting, and clear the Include Envelope ID by default option.
- Automatically replicate information in fields with the same data label: This option sets how information is replicated for fields with the same Data Label. This applies to Check box, Company, Text, Drop Down, Name, Note, and Title fields.

  Important: Changing this setting will affect envelopes that have been sent but not yet completed.

  There are two possible selections:

  - on an individual document: The option only applies to fields with the same Data Label for a single file.
  - on all documents in an envelope: The option applies to fields with the same Data Label for all files.
- Allow sender to download form data: This allows senders to download the data in a document to a CSV file.
- Enable custom email and language for each recipient: If selected, senders have the option to specify a custom email message and select the display language for each recipient on an envelope. See [Recipient Language and Custom Message](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=wxf1578456494201.html&_LANG=enus) for more information about using this option. You can further customize individual users' access to this feature using [permission profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.").
- Allow senders to add recipients from this account's User Directory: If selected, senders will be able to search against the names and email addresses of all active users on the account and add those users as recipients.
- Enable signing order by default: If selected, all new envelopes have the Set signing order option selected by default. For any envelope, the sender can clear the option and turn off the signing order, to use parallel routing. See [Set a Signing Order](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=kcb1578456479440.html&_LANG=enus) for more information about how to set and use a signing order.
- Enable envelope copy with field data: If selected, senders have the option to create a copy of an envelope and retain any information entered into fields when the envelope was created.
- Enable PDF/A Conversion and Compliance: If selected, all new uploaded documents in envelopes are saved as PDF/A, an ISO-standardized version of PDF designed for the archiving and long-term preservation of electronic documents. This setting is only visible if it is enabled for your account plan. For more information about this setting, see [PDF/A Conversion and Compliance](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=jbc1666049037612.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators can enable PDF/A conversion and compliance for documents in the account.").
- Docusign Feedback: If selected, senders on the account will receive occasional surveys within the Docusign application to collect their feedback on the sending experience.

title: Email Archive Configuration
text:
Read about how administrators can set up email addresses that automatically receive
copies of Docusign signature requests and other email notifications for a
sender’s account archiving and regulatory compliance purposes..

This feature allows an account administrator to set up email addresses that automatically
receive copies of Docusign signature requests and other email notifications
for a sender’s account archiving and regulatory compliance purposes. When the option is
enabled and at least one email address is active, a copy of each Docusign
system-generated email sent to a sender or recipient for the account is also sent to the
active email archive addresses. See the [Email Archive Configuration View](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=shy1583277370204.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Email Archive Configuration view and how administrators use it to see active, pending, and closed email archive addresses by account.") topic for more
information on “active” email addresses.

Important: Email addresses where non-archival, general business
activity may occur are not suitable and should not be used for email archiving with this
feature. Customers are strongly advised to implement special archival email
addresses with heightened access controls due to the increased sensitivity associated with
notifications delivered to the archival email address using this feature.

Note: This feature is only available for certain
account plans.

title: Delete Envelope Custom Fields
text:
Learn how account administrators delete envelope custom fields when they are no longer
needed.

As an account administrator, you can delete custom fields from
envelopes when they are not needed.

1. From the Accounts view, select Envelope Custom
   Fields.
2. Find the field that you want to delete, and select Actions > Delete.

The field is removed from the account.

title: Close Email Archive Addresses
text:
Learn how administrators close archived email addresses to prevent Docusign system-generated emails from going to the address.

When an email archive address is closed, Docusign stops sending copies of
the Docusign system-generated emails to the email address.

1. From the Accounts view, select Email Archive
   Configuration.
2. In the [Email Archive Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=xjm1583277371373.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators can set up email addresses that automatically receive copies of Docusign signature requests and other email notifications for a sender’s account archiving and regulatory compliance purposes..") list, find the email address you want to close.
3. In the Actions dropdown for the email address, select Close.

   The email address is closed, and the status and history for that address are updated.

   Closed email addresses can be reactivated.

title: Signature Types
text:
Read about signature types in Docusign eSignature and how you can manage the signature
types available to senders.

Electronic signatures are a common part of modern agreements. However, there are circumstances
where digital signatures are necessary for businesses, regions, or specific use cases.
These provide extra assurance by using digital certificates to verify identity.

title: Signature Types
text:
A digital signature is an electronic signature with a digital certificate to
authenticate the signer’s identity. Digital certificates indicate that signers have
completed extra steps to confirm their identity. Signers use digital certificates to
issue digital signatures on documents. A document’s digital signature proves the
signer’s identity and the document’s integrity.

Docusign eSignature provides its own set of digital signatures. It also partners with
Trust Service Providers (TSP), integrating their signatures into the eSignature
workflow. TSPs integration allows you to meet local standards, no matter where you
are.

title: Signature Types
text:
A signature type is a digital signature applying to a specific use case or country.
For example, DS Email is a signature type covering all the primary requirements for
digital signatures. If you need enhanced verification of the signer’s identity in
the European Union, you can also choose the DS EU Advanced signature type.

When senders create envelopes for signature, they select a signature type for each recipient.
The signature types available to a sender depend on the account configuration.
Account administrators can use the Signature Types page to
choose the signature types available for an account.

Table 1. signature type properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | Name of the signature type as it shows to senders in the Signature Type list when creating an envelope. |
| Jurisdiction | Area or legal authority that enforces the rules for using a signature type. Jurisdiction decides which laws apply to digital signatures. Therefore, it ensures that signatures are valid and accepted in a particular area or country. |
| Level | Degree of assurance that a digital signature is a reliable indicator of the signer's identity and the integrity of the signed document. For example, countries in the European Union support the following levels of signatures: simple, advanced and qualified. Other countries may support similar levels with distinct names. |
| Device | Appliance containing the digital certificates that eSignature uses to generate signatures. Signers usually store their certificates on the cloud or using a physical device like a smart card or a USB key. |

See the [eSignature Legality Guide](https://www.docusign.com/products/electronic-signature/legality/global) page for more
information on the supported jurisdictions and levels of signature.

title: Signature Types
text:
The Signature Types page lists all the signature types available to
senders when they create envelopes. The first signature type in the list applies by
default on envelopes unless the sender selects a distinct signature type. From this
page, administrators can add, remove or change the position of signature types in
the list.

title: Administrator Email Notification Subscriptions
text:
Read about how administrators can subscribe to account administrator email notifications
so that they can stay up to date with account events and product news.

On the account settings Overview page, you can view all of your notifications. You can also
choose to receive these notifications by email. Subscribing to notifications keeps you
informed of the notifications and captures this information for you in an email.

You can select the types of notifications to receive by email. At any time, you can
change your selections or unsubscribe if you no longer want to receive notifications by
email.

title: Activate the Identity Verification for FINTRAC Compliance
heading_path: Activate the Identity Verification for FINTRAC Compliance
text:
Learn how account administrators activate the Docusign ID Verification
for FINTRAC compliance configuration.

To use the FINTRAC configuration in templates or envelopes, activate the Docusign ID Verification for FINTRAC compliance configuration. The
FINTRAC configuration is available in all eSignature accounts. To maintain
compliance, the Docusign ID Verification for FINTRAC compliance
configuration cannot be copied or edited.

Note: It is your responsibility to save the data related to a FINTRAC
configuration for a minimum of five years. You can save the data by downloading the
PDF or using the API to collect the data and then store it outside of Docusign.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.
2. Search the ID Verification for FINTRAC compliance
   configuration and select Actions > Activate.
3. Select Activate in the confirmation message.
4. To test the configuration, create a new template or envelope.
5. In Identity Verification, select Docusign ID Verification for
   FINTRAC compliance.

title: Logs of Account Changes and Bulk Actions
heading_path: Logs of Account Changes and Bulk Actions
text:
Read about how account administrators review and download a history of changes to
accounts and the results of bulk actions. Audit logs capture events and changes made to accounts
such as settings, billing information, and plan type. Bulk actions logs provide details about
bulk actions such as envelope transfers and user uploads.

As an account administrator, you can review changes to an account and the progress or results
of bulk actions for a specific period of time from the Audit Logs and
Bulk Actions Logs. You can download the information as a
comma-separated value (CSV) file for detailed processing and analysis.

Review changes to an account for a specific date range from the Audit
Logs page. The audit logs include changes made to account settings, billing
information, plan type, and more.

Review a history of bulk actions from the Bulk Actions Log page. The
bulk actions logs provide details about and the progress of actions such as user uploads and
envelope transfers.

title: Custody Transfer Rules
text:
Read about how account administrators use the Custody Transfer page to manage rules that
automatically transfer ownership of envelopes from one user to another.

Docusign account administrators can add and manage rules to automatically
transfer ownership of envelopes between users in the same Docusign account
from the Custody Transfer page.

Note: The Transfer Custody feature must be enabled for your account to be able to
transfer ownership.

To transfer envelopes or templates manually, see [Transfer
Envelopes and Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=vyz1583277376194.html&_LANG=enus "As an account administrator, you can manually transfer envelopes or templates between users. When you transfer ownership of envelopes or templates, the transferred items are removed from the original owner’s account and moved to the new owner's account. Transfer envelopes and templates from either the Users page or the Custody Transfer page.").

title: Transfer Shared Envelopes
text:
Learn how Administrators transfer ownership of shared envelopes from one account member
to another.

You can transfer envelopes belonging to other account members if their envelopes are shared
with you.

Note: Shared envelopes is now in maintenance mode and will no longer be
updated. While you can continue to use shared envelopes, shared access also allows users to
share draft envelopes, move the shared envelopes, create custom folders, and view which
users have access to the envelopes. For more information, see [Manage Shared Access to Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=imk1656607611659.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators manage settings for shared access to envelopes among users.").

1. From your Docusign account, select the
   Agreements tab in the global header.
2. Select Shared Envelopes and select the user whose envelopes you
   want to transfer.
3. Locate the envelopes that you want to transfer by filtering, searching, or scanning the
   list.
4. To transfer a single envelope:
   1. Select the action menu for the envelope and select Transfer
      Ownership.
   2. Select the new owner for the envelope.

      ![The screenshot shows the Transfer Ownership section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/transferownership01.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.NGlSdqL2ZJpmTs3313g6yfta66Tj1I6Z8xDnGguS5OE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Transfer Ownership section.")
   3. (Optional). To add the current owner of the envelope as a Receives a Copy
      recipient, select Add Original Owner as Carbon Copy. The
      envelope displays in their Inbox after the transfer is complete, and they are added to
      the envelope routing order as a completed copy received recipient.
   4. Select Transfer.

      The transfer is initiated. The envelope may remain in the original owner's account
      for a brief time until the transfer is complete. Refresh your browser for an updated
      view of the transferred item.
5. To transfer multiple envelopes:
   1. Mark the check box for each envelope that you want to transfer.
   2. Select the overflow menu and select Transfer
      Ownership.

      ![The screenshot shows Transfer Ownership of multiple envelopes.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/transferownership02.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.NGlSdqL2ZJpmTs3313g6yfta66Tj1I6Z8xDnGguS5OE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows Transfer Ownership of multiple envelopes.")
   3. Select the new owner for the envelopes and select
      Transfer.

      The transfer is initiated. The envelopes may remain in the original owner's
      account for a brief time until the transfer is complete. Refresh your browser for an
      updated view of the transferred items.

title: Transfer Envelopes and Templates Between Users
heading_path: Transfer Envelopes and Templates Between Users
text:
Learn how administrators transfer envelopes and templates from one user to another from
the Users page.

As an account administrator, you can manually transfer envelope or templates from one user
to another from either the Users page or the Custody Transfer page.

1. From the Accounts view, select Users.
2. Locate the user whose items you want to transfer.
3. Select Actions and select either Transfer
   Envelopes or Transfer Templates.
4. Select the items that you want to transfer by selecting the check box on the
   corresponding rows. Use the Filters settings to change the list of
   items shown.
5. Select Transfer Now.
6. Type a name or an email and press the Enter key to generate a filtered list of
   users.
7. Select the user to transfer the items to and select Transfer.
8. (Optional) Check Add Original Owner as Carbon Copy to add the
   original sender as a Receives a copy recipient for the envelopes. If you do not select
   this option, the original sender loses all access to the transferred items.
9. Select Confirm to confirm the transfer.

   The job is placed in a queue for processing and listed in the Bulk Actions log. This
   log shows a list of bulk actions for your account and the progress of the actions.
10. When the transfer is complete, the transferred items are available in the new owner's
    account. You can select Details to review the transfer information
    and download a copy of the CSV file with the transfer information.

title: Configure the Authentication Failure Redirect Page
heading_path: Configure the Authentication Failure Redirect Page
text:
Learn how account administrators can specify the page where users are redirected if they
are not able to successfully complete the identity verification process.

Note: These features are temporarily disabled for account plans
purchased on the web after November 20, 2024. For more information, contact Docusign Support.

As an account administrator, you can specify the page where users are redirected if they are
not able to successfully complete the identity verification process. If not
specified, users are redirected to the default ID Verification authentication
failure page. You define the URL in the branding options.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Brands.
2. Edit the brand you want to configure.
3. Select Destination URLs.
4. Enter the URL for the Authentication Failure, and select
   Save.

   ![Setting the Authentication Failure Redirect URL](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/configauth01.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU3ODksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.0yp8PlmYLnokUgPM0CO0jPcJSOBm9PncmEN11wqvcLs&_LANG=enus "Setting the Authentication Failure Redirect URL")

The URL is saved as the redirect page for users that fail to
authenticate.

title: Add Signature Types
text: Learn how to add signature types for your senders.

title: Add Signature Types
text:
Make sure you complete the following prerequisites before adding
signature types:

- Your Docusign account gives you access to the account
  administration settings.
- Your Docusign account gives you access to one or more
  signature types.

As an account administrator, your organization requests that you add new signature types for
senders. Therefore, senders can access additional signature types that fit best with
their business cases.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signature
   Types.
2. Select Edit.

   The Manage Signature Type List screen opens.
3. Select Add Type.

   The Add Signature Types screen opens.
4. Choose the signature types of your choice.

   Disabled checkboxes correspond to signature types already in your signature
   type list. You cannot select them again.
5. Select Add [N] Types.

   A confirmation message shows on the Manage Signature Type
   List.
6. Select Save.

   A confirmation message shows on the Signature Types
   screen.

The signature types you added are now available to senders
when they create envelopes.

title: Add Signature Types
text:
- [Change the Order of Signature Types Available to Senders for Envelope Creation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=naj1686300753902.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to change the order of signature types in the list available to senders.")
- [Set the Default Signature Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=uio1686302663212.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set the signature type that eSignature selects by default when sending envelopes.")

title: Password Security
text:
Read about the options available to administrators to set password rules for accounts. Administrators can specify a minimum number of characters, type of characters allowed, and expiration behavior. Depending on the needs of the organization, administrators can customize the options to fit their specific security requirements.

The Docusign system allows users a set number of login attempts with an incorrect password. For account security, if a user enters the wrong password too many times, the system locks the user out temporarily. Users can attempt to log in again after a brief waiting period.

Note: When administrators update password rules, the new rules apply when users either create a new password or update an existing password. Until either scenario exists, users can log in with their existing passwords created according to the previous rules.

The **Password Rules** option allows administrators to set the following rules for passwords used by account users to access Docusign.

- **Basic** – The minimum password length is 6 characters with no other password requirements.
- **Medium** – The minimum password length is 7 characters and must have one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one number or special character.
- **Strong** – The minimum password length is 9 characters and must have one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character.
- **Custom** – You can customize the length and age of passwords. The age is the minimum number of days after a password is set before it can be changed. You can specify whether uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters must be included in the password.

  Note: Angle brackets ("<" ">") and spaces are never allowed in passwords.

Note: Docusign removed the Password Questions option and recommends using a secondary phone number or email for account recovery instead. As of January 2026, using security questions and answers (SQSA) is discontinued for existing customers.

title: Connected Apps
text:
Read about how organization administrators manage the applications that can access your
Docusign organization. Applications are authorized for all domain users and
access is limited by the permissions you specify.

Manage the applications that can access your Docusign organization.
Connected applications are authorized for all domain users and access is limited by the
permissions you specify.

You can authorize any application for which there is an existing integrator key in any account that is linked to your organization.

To authorize an application for your domain users, you must first claim a domain for your Docusign organization. To learn more about claiming domains, see the [Claim a Domain](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=gso1583359141256.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to prove ownership of a domain as part of claiming the domain for an organization.") topic.

Note: For more information about integrator keys, see
[Apps and Keys](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pmp1583277397015.html&_LANG=enus "Read how account administrators view and manage API integration information for their accounts from the Apps and Keys page. The Apps and Keys page provides API information for your account, the integration keys associated with your account, and a way to add integration keys."). For more information about using your
integrator key with the Docusign API, visit the [Docusign
Developer Center](https://www.docusign.com/developer-center).

title: Locate and Purge Envelopes for a Signer or Sender
heading_path: Locate and Purge Envelopes for a Signer or Sender
text:
Learn how to find and permanently delete envelopes. Administrators can filter envelopes
and remove those that are not needed.

You can use the Purge option to remove
completed, declined, and voided envelopes permanently.

1. From the Accounts view, select Document
   Retention.
2. Select the Targeted Purge tab.
3. Select Filter to search for the sender or signer.
4. Select the appropriate filters, and select Apply.

   Sender: Filter to view envelopes that belong to a specific
   sender on the account. If your account has fewer than 10 users, you will see a sender
   list instead of a search box.

   Recipient or Envelope Subject:  Filter based on a recipient's
   name, email address, or the subject of an envelope.

   Envelope Status: Filter based on the status of the envelope.
   Only completed, declined, and voided envelopes are available to purge.

   Date Range: Select a range of the last 24 hours, 7 days, 30 or
   Custom to specify a range.
5. Select the envelopes to be purged, then select Add to Purge
   Queue.
6. (Optional) If necessary, select Remove fields and metadata and
   Redact personally identifiable information.

   - When **Remove fields and metadata** is selected, Docusign deletes the envelope
     documents as well as any fields that were added to the documents and any API
     attachments that were added to the envelope from the system.
   - When **Redact personally identifiable information** is selected, Docusign redacts all personally identifiable information from the
     envelope, certificate of completion, and history. Fields that store personal data,
     such as name, physical address, email, and IP address, are replaced with the text
     “Redacted”. Time stamps for when actions took place are left intact, but you won’t be
     able to identify the person who took that action.

     Note: The
     process of redacting personal data cannot be undone. Each individual’s personal
     information on an envelope will be redacted from the envelope, rendering the audit
     log and certificate of completion untraceable.
   - If these options are not selected, Docusign deletes only the envelope documents from
     the system.
7. Select Purge in the confirmation message to move the selected
   envelopes into the purge queue.

   The Purge Status of the envelopes changes to Purge Soon. See [Purge Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=edo1578456348066.html&bundleId=oeq1643226594604&_LANG=enus) for more information about how purging works.![The screenshot shows a document added to the purge queue.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/documentretent01.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1MTYsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.upNyWYbNUvoOgGBSf4z1osRigJ5mj6E4n4TbFRG1rNQ&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows a document added to the purge queue.")

title: Define Signing Settings
text:
Learn how administrators define the signing behavior for an account.

As an administrator, you can define the signing behavior for
an account.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signing
   Settings.
2. Adjust your signing settings as needed.

   See [Signing Experience Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=rgk1583277353500.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use the In-session Landing Pages and Watermark Configuration settings to specify two key behaviors of the signing experience. These settings define where signers are routed in different situations and they ensure that in-process transactions clearly reflect an incomplete signing status.") for descriptions of the signing
   settings.
3. Select Save.

title: Signature Adoption and Format
text:
Read about the settings administrators use to configure signature modes and styles for
signers.

These settings configure the signature modes and styles for signers.

- Signature Adoption Configuration: This option is used to
  set the signing options for the account. This includes the default signing modes, limiting
  signature styles for adopting a signature, locking a recipient’s name for signing and, for
  drawn signatures or initials, setting the minimum pixels required to adopt a signature. For
  a detailed description of the configuration options, see [Signature Adoption Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=dsz1583277358168.html&_LANG=enus "Read the signing options administrators can specify for an account through the Signature Adoption Configuration view. For example, administrators can specify signing modes, limit signature styles, and specify a minimum pixel size for drawn signatures.").
- The selected formats will be applied to all signatures:
  This option controls the appearance of signatures for signers.
  - Add Docusign frame: A frame with extra information is placed
    around the signer’s signatures and initials to make it easy to recognize the signature
    was created by Docusign. This extra information appears on the
    documents, including the Certificate of Completion, sent from the account.

    Note: The frame is not applied if the signer is using the Docusign mobile app to sign.
  - Do not add frame: Only the signer’s script name or initials are
    shown.

  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  |  |
  | Sample signature and initials with the frame | Sample signature and initials without the frame |

  With the Docusign frame selected, the signature is easily
  recognized as a Docusign signature.

  If you expect to send
  documents to others who understand that the Docusign signature indicates
  a secure document handling process, and that they can rely on the document to be tamper
  proof, that there is an online backup, then you should enable the Docusign frame to indicate to others that the signature is from Docusign.

  If you expect to send documents to others who are not familiar with Docusign, and do not expect extra layers of security, but perhaps just
  require faxed in documents, you can use the Do not add frame option so that these
  recipients are not confused by the additional information.

title: Upload Stamps
text:
Learn how administrators upload stamps to be used in place of signatures for groups or
individuals.

As an administrator, you can upload stamps to be used in place
of signatures for groups or individuals.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Stamps.
2. Select Create Stamp.
3. Select Choose File to open a file chooser, or drag an image file
   to the dialog box. The file should be a standard image file such as GIF, JPG, PNG or IPX.
   IPX is a commonly used image type for stamps in Japan.

   When you select an IPX image, you see a message stating that stamps with the same
   serial number will not be added.

   Use the available options to modify the image:

   - Rotate the image
   - Change the size of the image
   - Crop the image to a square or circle
4. Select Next when you are ready to upload the image.
5. Enter a stamp name in Stamp Name, and select
   Create.

The stamp is created and displays in the list on the Stamps
page.

title: Resource Files
text:
Read about how administrators that have XML and HTML skills customize resource files to
control text elements in an account. Administrators can customize one or both of the resource
files available for Docusign eSignature. One file is used for signing and the
other file is used for email messaging.

Note: These features are temporarily disabled for account plans purchased
on the web after November 20, 2024. For more information, contact Docusign
Support.

Resource files are an optional method to control the text elements in your account signing
view and for emails sent by your account. If the resource file option is not enabled for an
account, contact Docusign Support for more information and assistance.

Important: Customizing resource files is an advanced
branding configuration option which can significantly impact your account, and should be done
only by someone with expertise in XML and HTML. The resource files are subject to change
without notice. If you customize your resource files, after each release, Docusign recommends you review any changes and update your custom files as
needed.

Docusign eSignature uses two resource files: one for signing and one for
email messages. In addition to setting text elements, the signing resource file also controls
some of the dialog boxes displayed in the signing view.

To use resource files, you will download the appropriate resource file for the information
you want to change, modify the resource file, and then upload your modified file to the Docusign system. When you upload a modified resource file, only the
differences between your modified file and the original file are saved by the system. An
account can only upload one of each type of custom resource file for each signing brand, so
all changes for all languages must be included in the uploaded files.

You can modify and upload one or both (signing and email) of the resource files to customize
them for your account. If you do not upload a modified resource file, the system will use the
default information in the original resource file.

For more information about specific resource files, see the following guides:

- [Signing Resource File](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=docusign-signing-resource-file-guide&topicId=docusign-signing-resource-file-guide.pdf&_LANG=enus)
- [Email Resource File](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=sjd1688145913931&topicId=vxn1688146331549.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Security Settings
text:
Read about how administrators use Security Settings to affect authentication options.
These settings determine how users access the account and how recipients access all documents
sent from the account.

Security Settings affect authentication options for:

- How your users access the account
- How recipients access all envelopes sent from the account

These settings apply to all users on the account and all of the envelopes sent from the
account. For settings affecting access to envelopes, changes apply to any new envelopes
created.

You must be an administrator with All Administration Capabilities to manage these settings.
See [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") for
details.

title: Set Default Signing Brands
text:
Learn how administrators configure a signing brand to use as a default for envelopes and
templates created for an account. Optionally, the configuration can allow senders to modify the
brand.

As an administrator, you can configure a default signing brand
for envelopes and templates that are created for an account. Optionally, you can allow senders
to modify the brand.

1. From the Accounts view, select Brands to
   view the Signing tab.
2. Locate the brand that you want to set as the default.
3. Select the menu icon and select Set as Default.

The selected brand becomes the default signing brand
that is used by envelopes and templates that are created in your account.

title: Setting a User Login Policy
text:
Read about how organization administrators can set a user login policy. When SSO is
enabled for an organization, all account users follow the policy settings specified for the
domain.

Note: This guide is for Docusign
organization administrators. Single Sign-On requires and organization in Docusign Admin.

When SSO is enabled for an organization, all account users follow the policy settings
specified for the domain. For example, an organization’s email domain is myorganization.com.
If the Docusign administrator requires all users to authenticate with an
Identity Provider, then all users with an @myorganization.com email address are required to
authenticate with their corporate credentials.

Note: It is also possible to prevent users from
logging into personal Docusign accounts while at work. For more information, see [Prevent Access to Personal Docusign Accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mee1606866850692.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how organization administrators can prevent their users from accessing personal Docusign accounts at work.").

There might be cases where using the default policy might not be appropriate for all users. Some examples of this are:

- The Docusign administrator might want to retain the ability to maintain a username and password in Docusign. This is helpful for cases where the administrator needs to access Docusign to make updates to the organization’s SSO configuration without going through the Identity Provider.
- An application or integration user cannot log in through an Identity Provider because that particular application does not currently support SSO.

For these cases, Docusign administrators have the option of setting login policies on a per user basis.

Important: This login policy setting deliberately
overrides any policies that are enforced on the email domain. Administrators should use this
as an exception rather than a standard rule for all users.

title: Replace a Brand With the Resource File of Another Brand
heading_path: Replace a Brand With the Resource File of Another Brand
text:
Learn how administrators can replace an existing brand using the XML resource file
exported from another brand.

You can replace an existing brand using the XML
resource file exported from another brand. Replacing a brand is a fast way to copy the
same brand across multiple accounts. For example, you might want to replace brands in a
production environment with a brand created in a demo environment. Replacing a brand
maintains any templates and groups associated with the exported brand.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Brands to view the Signing
   tab.
2. Locate the brand that you want to use as the replacement brand.
3. Select the menu icon and select Export.

   An XML file of the brand is downloaded to your file system.
4. Locate the brand that you want to replace.
5. Select the menu icon and select Replace.
6. (Optional) Before replacing the brand, you can download a backup copy of your
   current brand by selecting the link in the information message.
7. Select Upload File and select the XML file that you
   exported from the brand you want to use as the replacement brand.
8. Select Replace Brand.

The brand is replaced.

title: Transfer Envelopes and Templates in Bulk with a CSV File
heading_path: Transfer Envelopes and Templates in Bulk with a CSV File
text:
Learn how account administrators use a CSV file to transfer envelopes and templates in
bulk.

As an account administrator, you can transfer envelopes and
templates in bulk by using a comma-separated value (CSV) file.

1. Open the [downloaded CSV file](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ngj1583277448218.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how account administrators download information about envelopes and templates in CSV files to help them make decisions about actions to take on them.") and remove all data
   except the EnvelopeID or Template ID column with each ID for the items you want to
   transfer.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/transfercsvupload.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1MzksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.F1f_9vb2wI5M5nE2f17STmrUcs7m3ZAA_QskJW_vBOE&_LANG=enus)
2. From the Accounts view, select Custody
   Transfer.
3. Select Transfer and select either
   Envelopes or Templates.
4. Select Transfer Using CSV.
5. In the Transfer Using CSV dialog box:
   1. Select Upload .CSV File and select the prepared CSV
      file.
   2. Select the user that the items are being transferred to and select
      Transfer.
   3. (Optional for envelopes only) Check Add Original Owner as Carbon
      Copy to add the original sender as a “Receives a copy” recipient for the
      envelopes. If you do not select this option, the original sender loses all access to
      the transferred items.

      ![The screenshot shows the Upload CSV window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/transfercsvuploadfinish.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjU1MzksInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.F1f_9vb2wI5M5nE2f17STmrUcs7m3ZAA_QskJW_vBOE&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Upload CSV window.")
6. Select Confirm to confirm the transfer.

   The transfer job is placed in a queue for processing and listed in the Bulk Actions
   log. This log shows a list of bulk actions for your account and the progress of the
   actions.

   When the transfer is complete, you can select Details
   to review the transfer information and download a copy of the CSV file with the transfer
   information. After the job is completed, the transferred items are moved to the new
   owner's account.

title: Differences in Signing with a Signature Pad
heading_path: Differences in Signing with a Signature Pad
text:
Read about how the signing process differs with various signature pads.

The signing process is different with various signature pads. There are no changes to the
process for sending envelopes.

Note: The first time a signer
uses an ePadLink device, the signer must allow activation of the plugin for the
device.

1. The signer selects the first
   Sign or Initial tag on the
   document. The Adopt Your Signature window opens.
2. The signer selects one of the following options:
   - On a Topaz device, the signer selects Select a Signature
     Style to use a pre-set signature or initial style.
   - On an ePad device, the signer selects Start
     Capture.
3. The signer uses the signature pad device to draw their
   signature or initials.

   The signer selects Clear to reset
   the signature or initial block and start over.
4. The signer selects one of the following options:
   - On a Topaz device, the signer selects Adopt and Sign
     or Adopt and Initial to save the signature or
     initials.
   - On an ePad device, the signer selects OK.
5. After adopting their signature or initials, the signer
   only needs to select a Sign or Initial
   tag to add the signature or initials.

   If the Sign In Each
   Location option is enabled for the envelope, the signer must
   sign or initial each Sign or
   Initial tag.

title: What can you configure for signing and sending brands?
heading_path: What can you configure for signing and sending brands?
text: Read about the ways that account administrators can style signing and sending brands.

title: What can you configure for signing and sending brands?
heading_path: What can you configure for signing and sending brands?
text:
Signing brands offer control over the styling of the email notifications sent to recipients and the signing view. You can add a custom logo and specify a color theme to add your company branding to these interactions. You can set a default signing brand for all envelopes sent from your account. Senders can select a signing brand to apply to the envelopes they send. Signing brands are also applied and saved with templates.

If your account includes PowerForms, the forms inherit the signing brand used on the associated template.

Advanced configuration options are available to customize destination URLs, links, and resource files. These options are covered in [Advanced Configuration for Signing Brands](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=cno1583277333030.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced configuration options available to administrators that want to customize the signing experience. Administrators can customize destination URLs, header and footer links, and signing brand languages.").

title: What can you configure for signing and sending brands?
heading_path: What can you configure for signing and sending brands?
text: The sending brand provides control over your Docusign account styling. You can specify a single sending brand for all account members, and add a custom logo and specify a color theme to apply your company branding. You can also specify a brand for signing request emails sent by Docusign to your signers.

title: Signing Groups Overview
text:
Read about how administrators can set up signing groups and alternate emails for the
members of the group. Signing groups allow any member of the group to act on documents sent to
the group. Alternate emails allow email notifications to go to another email address, rather
than directly to the group members.

When the signing group option is used, group members that open and sign the documents are tracked in the history and certificate of completion. The members of a signing group are not required to have a Docusign account.

Once a signing group is set up, it is available for use by any account sender when adding
recipients to an envelope. The group can be added in the same manner as any other recipient.
See this [Signing Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=zgn1578456447934.html&bundleId=gav1643676262430&_LANG=enus) topic for more information about sending envelopes
with a Signing Group.

title: Signing Groups Overview
heading_path: Adding an alternate email for notifications
text:
You can choose to add an alternate email address for the signing group and have all email
notifications sent to that email address, rather than directly to the group members. When
you use an alternate group email, only the recipients who are also members of the signing
group can view and act on envelopes sent to the group email. In addition, with an alternate
email, members must have a Docusign account to view and act on the envelope. If members
access the documents from the group email notification, they must first sign in to their
Docusign account.

title: Set Watermarks
text:
Learn how administrators set a watermark on in-process documents to indicate their
incomplete signing status.

Administrators can configure a message and style for a
watermark. When the document signing is incomplete, each page in a printable version of
the document clearly displays a watermark.

1. From the Accounts view, select Signing
   Settings.
2. Select Watermark Configuration to go to the Watermark
   page.
3. Select Apply watermarks to documents that are still in
   progress.
4. Select the appearance options for the watermark: font style, font color, font
   size, transparency percentage, and orientation.
5. Type the watermark message. The maximum length of the watermark message is 50
   characters.
6. Select Preview to see an example of how the watermark
   will display if the document is printed before completely signed. Review the
   information and make any necessary changes.
7. Select Save to save the watermark settings.

The watermark settings are saved.

title: Customize Themes for Sending Brands
text:
Learn how administrators customize the logo and color scheme for a brand.

As an administrator, you can customize the logo and color
scheme for a brand.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Brands.
2. Select the Sending tab.

   Your current Sending brand configuration displays.![The screenshot shows the sending brand customization section on the Brands page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Brands-sending.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNDEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.22VqkCR7yd2mrqdqviqLaJIK9iVgxMA63of0RnEXv2g&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the sending brand customization section on the Brands page.")
3. Select Edit Your Theme to open the brand customization view.
4. Make your logo and color changes, and select Apply Brand.

   ![The screenshot shows the sending brand customization view.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/Customize-a-Sending-Brand-window.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjUwNDEsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.22VqkCR7yd2mrqdqviqLaJIK9iVgxMA63of0RnEXv2g&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the sending brand customization view.")

Your sending brand is saved. Account users will see the
change when they log in or refresh their active browser session.

title: Edit Custom Fields from Documents
text:
Learn how administrators edit custom fields for documents.

As an administrator, you can edit custom fields for
documents.

1. From the Accounts view, select Document Custom
   Fields.
2. The page has a list of document custom fields for the account. Find the custom field
   that you want to edit.

   In the same row of the custom field, select Actions > Edit.
3. Edit the custom field as needed.
4. Select Save to save the changes.

title: Manage Your Subscription to Account Administrator Email Notifications
heading_path: Manage Your Subscription to Account Administrator Email Notifications
text:
Learn how to update or discontinue your subscription to email notifications. Account
administrators can choose the types of notifications to receive by email.

You can change the types of notifications to receive or
unsubscribe from receiving notifications by email.

1. From the Accounts view, select
   Overview to view the Notifications section.
2. Next to Filters, select the Notification Settings icon.

   The Notification Preferences opens.
3. Select the checkboxes for notification types that you want to subscribe to.
4. Clear all of the checkboxes to unsubscribe from all notifications.
5. Select Save.

Your changes to your notification subscription settings are
saved.

title: Supplemental Documents
text:
Read about actions administrators can require signers to take regarding supplemental
documents. Administrators can specify that signers view, accept, or read the supplemental
documents to complete the signing process.

If supplemental documents are enabled for your account, you can specify the default recipient
actions that signers must complete in order to finish signing. Selection here makes an action
required for all supplemental documents sent from the account.

The settings you choose here are applied to all permission profiles on your account. With the
