[Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously."), you can
control users' access to the supplemental documents feature and whether they can override the
default actions when sending supplements. See the [Send Supplemental Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=nfs1578456470024.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus) guide for
more information.

The options are as follows:

- **Signers must view supplemental documents**: Recipients must select the
  View button on the supplement strip to open the supplement view,
  which lets them read through the document, and download or print it.
- **Signers must accept supplemental documents**: Recipients must select the
  Accept button on the supplement strip.
- **Signers must read supplemental documents**: Recipients must scroll to the
  bottom of the supplement. This option is only available if both must view and must accept
  options are selected.

Supplemental document interactions are logged in both the History and Certificate of Completion for an envelope.

title: Access to Docusign Account Administration
text:
Read about how account administrators access the account settings. Access is limited to
those with full administrative or delegated permissions.

Administrators can open Docusign account administration settings from the
Docusign account and by direct access. To access account administration
settings, you must have either full account administrator or delegated permissions.

Docusign recommends having at least two full account administrators on accounts with two or
more users. For more information, see the [Multiple Docusign Account Administrators](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=qgo1583277323320.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the importance of having two full administrators on an account. Administrators are alerted when an account has only one full administrator. To designate another user as an administrator, assign full administrative permissions to the user.") topic.

title: Security Settings Permission Access Levels
text:
View the levels of access that administrators can grant through the Security
Settings permission.

The following table describes the level of access provided by the Security
Settings permission.

Note: The Plan and Billing page appears with the title Subscription and Billing for some account types.

| Administrative area | Page | Level of access |
| --- | --- | --- |
| N/A | Overview | View |
| Account | Plan and Billing | None |
| Account | Account Profile | None |
| Account | Security Settings | Edit[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zun1661539748163.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fntarg_1) |
| Account | Regional Settings | None |
| Account | Search Settings | None |
| Account | Brands | None |
| Account | Stamps | None |
| Account | Updates | None |
| Account | Value Calculator | None |

[1](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zun1661539748163.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=fnsrc_1) This permission excludes Recipient Session Timeout and
Capture voice recording during phone
authentication.

title: Envelope Custody Transfer Rules
text:
Read about how account administrators use custody transfer rules to automatically
transfer ownership of envelopes to users of an account.

Custody transfer rules are set up to transfer envelope ownership from an individual user, or a
group of users, to another user automatically when envelopes are sent or completed. When
ownership is transferred, the envelopes are removed from the original owner’s account and
added to the new owner's Manage page. The original owner will only retain access to the
envelopes if the transfer rule adds them as a carbon copy recipient.

The Custody Transfer page provides a summary view of all envelope custody transfer rules for your account. The summary view shows who, a user or group, envelopes are transferred from, who they are transferred to, the destination folder for the transfer, when the transfer happens, and if the transfer rule is enabled.

Custody transfer rules cannot be modified. After rules are added, they can only be disabled,
enabled, or removed.

Note: To learn more about envelope custody transfer, see
the [Best Practices for Envelope Custody Transfer](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/km-guide-best-practices-for-envelope-custody-transfer) guide.

title: Create Custom Fields for Documents
text:
Learn how account administrators create custom fields that users can select from the
Fields palette when they prepare documents and add recipient fields. If
your account is integrated with Salesforce and you have a Salesforce Connect configuration set
up, you can relate custom fields to Salesforce fields.

As an account administrator, you can create custom fields
that can be added to documents. Users can select custom fields from the
Fields palette when they prepare documents and add recipient
fields.

Note: If your account is integrated with Salesforce and you
have a Salesforce Connect configuration set up, you can relate this custom field to a
Salesforce field. See the Docusign for Salesforce Administrator Guide for
more information about relating custom fields to Salesforce objects.

1. From the Accounts view, select Document Custom
   Fields.
2. Select Add Document Field.
3. Enter a name for the custom field.

   Tip: Use a descriptive
   name to help users identify it in the Fields palette.
4. Select the type of field that you want to create from the Type
   menu, for example, Date Signed, Drop Down or
   Radio Button.

   Set the properties for the custom field. The properties available depend on the type
   of field that you select. Custom field properties are generally the same field properties
   for the field type selected. Information about additional custom field properties is
   provided below:
   - If you select Drop Down as the field type, you must provide
     options for the list that is presented to the recipient. Enter the options in the
     Options box, separating each with a semi-colon (;). You are
     limited to 2000 characters in the list, including the semi-colon separators.
   - Most fields allow you to set an Initial Value for the field.
     The sender and recipient can change this information, unless the Read
     Only option is selected.
   - To share the field with all account users, select Shared.
   - If you select Text Field as the field type, you can select a
     validation setting such as Email, Date,
     or Zip to constrain user input to an assigned format.
   - If you select Note as the field type, the Include
     in email option is available. Select this option to have the note
     display in the email sent to the recipient.
   - The AutoPlace Text field allows you to add the custom field
     to all instances of a word or characters in a document.

     Type the string of characters in the AutoPlace Text field.
     When the custom field is added to a document, the custom field is attached to all
     occurrences of the characters in the document. See [Add Fields Automatically with AutoPlace](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ytr1578456405118.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus)
     for more information about using AutoPlace.
5. Select Save to save your custom field.

   Your new custom field displays in the list. If you shared the custom field, all
   account users see it in the Fields palette when they prepare a
   document and add recipient fields.

title: Create an Identity Verification Configuration with Only KBA
heading_path: Create an Identity Verification Configuration with Only KBA
text:
Learn how account administrators create configurations for identity verification using
only KBA.

You can create a configuration with knowledge-based authentication (KBA) as the only option if
you want recipients to identify themselves with only KBA.

1. From the Accounts view, select Identity
   Verification.

   The Identity Verification screen
   opens.
2. Select Add Configuration.

   The Add Configuration screen opens.
3. Choose Knowledge-Based Authentication and select
   Next.

   The Add Knowledge-Based Authentication page
   opens.
4. Enter a name for your configuration. Optionally, add a description.

   Note: The name and description are not translated. Senders
   see the text in the language it is written.
5. Select the maximum number of verification attempts.

   This number represents how many times recipients can attempt to verify before
   the envelope is locked. If recipients are unsuccessful in verifying their
   identity, by default, the envelope is locked and users must contact the sender
   to unlock it.
6. Select one of the levels of variation for name acceptance standards when
   matching the name of the recipient with the name in the ID:

   - Maximum variation: Allows additional variations
     of middle names, suffixes, equivalent names and tolerates minor typing
     mistakes. This is the default selection. The Maximum variation option is
     not compatible with the IDV - DS EU Advanced signature type.
   - Moderate variation: Allows additional variations
     of middle names and suffixes.
   - Minimal variation: Allows minor variations of
     first and last names only.

   Note: For more information about this setting, see [ID
   Name Matching](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=rhb1657542911402.html&_LANG=enus).
7. Select how often recipients must verify when accessing an envelope.

   If you select Every access or Every access
   until role is completed, you can turn on Allow SMS
   authentication after one successful verification. The recipient
   provides a mobile phone number to complete this type of authentication.
8. Select Add.

ID Verification adds your new configuration.

title: Add Custom Messages to the Banner
heading_path: Add Custom Messages to the Banner
text:
Learn how administrators communicate information by adding custom messages to the top
banner in the sending experience.

Administrators can add a custom message that displays in the top banner of the sending
experience. This message can be used to relay announcements or other important
information. Optionally, you can add a longer message that displays when a user
selects the banner message.

**Example Custom Message Title**
![The screenshot shows an example of the custom message title.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/The-screenshot-shows-an-example-of-the-custom-message-title.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.P_Tcp6Whv3i6l4n--3D5mIktN3c0NAlFsEsS4fREFRU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example of the custom message title.")

**Example Custom Message**
![The screenshot shows an example message that opens after selecting the custom message title.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/The-screenshot-shows-an-example-message-that-opens-after-selecting-the-custom-message-title.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.P_Tcp6Whv3i6l4n--3D5mIktN3c0NAlFsEsS4fREFRU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows an example message that opens after selecting the custom message title.")

1. From the Accounts view, select Account
   Profile.
2. Select the Enable Message toggle.
3. In the Notice field, enter a message title that is fewer
   than 150 characters.

   Note: The default message
   title is "Update from your administrator" and has been translated for all
   supported languages within the eSignature sending experience. If you edit or
   replace this title, it will not be translated for your end users and will
   display as it is written.
4. Optionally, in the Custom Message field, enter a custom
   message that is fewer than 500 characters.

   You can use HTML to format the text and add links in the custom message. HTML
   tags for images and videos are not allowed.

   Note: If you do not add a
   custom message, your users will only view the message title from the
   Notice field. This can be useful when you only
   want to share a brief message. Add longer messages in the Custom
   Message field.

   ![The screenshot shows the custom message fields in the Account Profile page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pik1583277475390/page/images/custom-message-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicGlrMTU4MzI3NzQ3NTM5MCJ9.P_Tcp6Whv3i6l4n--3D5mIktN3c0NAlFsEsS4fREFRU&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the custom message fields in the Account Profile page.")
5. Select Save.

   The custom message is saved and is displayed for all users on the
   account.

title: Default Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure Customization
heading_path: Default Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure Customization
text:
Read about factors that account administrators must consider when creating custom
disclosures.

Important: If you choose to create a custom disclosure, you
will not be able to change back to the default disclosure once you save the custom disclosure.
Additionally, Docusign will not automatically translate your custom
disclosure. You must create a disclosure for each language used by your signers.

Note: If you have elected to use a disclosure, each
time you change the disclosure content, all unsigned recipients of outstanding documents will
be required to accept a new version. You can edit the information in the disclosure even if
acceptance of the disclosure is not required.
